gmw_00004221.name: Id3
gmw_00004221.overview: Id3 is a Lyman-break starburst galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 2.77 [arXiv:1405.4139]. It has a star formation rate of 60 solar masses per year, a stellar mass of 1.2 billion solar masses, and is surrounded by several nearby galaxies [arXiv:1405.4139].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Id3 as the probable GRB 051008 host, a z≈2.77 Lyman-break starburst galaxy with moderate global extinction, high star formation, and nearby galaxies at similar redshift.

quotes.quote: Galaxies Id1, Id2, Id4, and Id5 lie within a 10 arcsecond radius circle around the probable host galaxy of GRB 051008 (Id3) and we will also study these galaxies below. We found a photometric redshift of z = 2.77_ 0.20 ^ +0.15 (95 confidence level) for the host galaxy. In case of the galaxy Id3 the best-fit is the LMC reddening law.

The Le Phare package with the PEGASE2 library also gives the best fitted values of the host physical parameters: star-formation rate SFR _ PEGASE2 60 M _ / yr and the stellar mass of the galaxy M 1.2 10^9 M _ . Also the Id3 galaxy is less dusty than an average LBG and 10 times less massive.
It is a rather typical Lyman-break galaxy located at the redshift of z_ phot = 2.77_ 0.20 ^ +0.15 . The host galaxy has a SFR of 60 M_ / yr typical for LBGs.

gmw_00004222.name: SHiZELS-8
gmw_00004222.overview: SHiZELS-8 is a rotation-dominated high-redshift galaxy characterized by a turbulent disk with three kiloparsec-sized clumps [arXiv:1906.06245, arXiv:2011.00686]. It features a compact core in the F140W band, an extended ultraviolet morphology, and a flat radial metallicity gradient [arXiv:2011.00686].

sources.summary: The paper finds SHiZELS-8 has marginal CO(2-1) emission, low metallicity, possible larger molecular gas content than SHiZELS-19, and dynamically complex misaligned H and CO kinematics.

quotes.quote: The SHiZELS-8 H observation Swinbank2012a suggests that this galaxy is consistent with being a turbulent rotating disk hosting three kpc-sized clumps Swinbank2012b . Unfortunately the SHiZELS-8 CO(2-1) observations have too low S/N to allow a detailed dynamical characterization. This galaxy has 50 lower velocity-integrated CO(2-1) flux density than SHiZELS-19, but its emission seems more extended, i.e, it has a lower CO surface brightness.

Two pieces of evidence support the idea that SHiZELS-8 is a dynamically complex system. Firstly, the H and CO dynamics show that both components rotate in the same direction but have position angles offset by 100-120 deg (see Fig. fig:SHiZELS8_tapered ), which is in contrast to the negligible offset in the H and CO dynamics seen in SHiZELS-19 (Fig. fig:maps ). Second, our previous SINFONI observation shows a flat radial [ N ii ] /H metallicity gradient Swinbank2012a .

sources.summary: The paper describes SHiZELS-8 as a rotation-dominated, clumpy high-redshift galaxy undetected by ALMA, with low flat metallicity, extended UV morphology, and a compact F140W core.

quotes.quote: SHiZELS-8 : This galaxy is dominated by rotation and present clumpy features Swinbank2012a, Gillman2019 . None of the three H clumps is detected by ALMA. Its metallicity is relatively low ( 12+ ( O/H)<8.3 ) and the metallicity gradient is flat Swinbank2012a . A simple dynamical description using carbon monoxide is presented by Molina2019 .
The HST F606W image shows an extended UV morphology, while the F140W image shows a compact core in the galaxy. The clumpy structures seen in the H map are not recovered by the F606W image.

gmw_00004223.name: SHiZELS-19
gmw_00004223.overview: SHiZELS-19 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy with a dense interstellar medium [arXiv:1906.06245]. Although previously classified as a disturbed dispersion-dominated disk, it exhibits strong asymmetries and a velocity gradient that make its structural classification uncertain [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SHiZELS-19 with resolved ALMA and H data, finding dense molecular gas, lower SFR surface density than similar high-redshift systems, and a depletion-time gradient.

quotes.quote: In the case for SHiZELS-19, we are able to easily detect the source in the high-resolution datacube. Thus, for this galaxy, we set the spectral channel width to 25 km s ^ 1 aiming to minimize spectral resolution effects in the derivation of the kinematic parameters. We show the spatially integrated spectrum for each galaxy in Fig. fig:fig1 .

For this galaxy, we derive a median _ 10 _ SFR =-0.5 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 and _ 10 _ H_2 = 3.0 0.6 M_ pc ^ 2 values. These estimations indicate that SHiZELS-19 has a somewhat denser ISM compared with local star-forming galaxies. On the other hand, the median _ H_2 value is consistent with molecular surface density estimations from galaxy-integrated observations of BzK and `typical' star-forming galaxies at similar redshifts Daddi2010,Tacconi2013 , but SHiZELS-19 presents lower _ SFR values compared to these systems. We derive a median _ dep =2.3 1.2 Gyr for this galaxy, with the pixel-by-pixel distribution between 0.003-5 Gyr.

sources.summary: ID10 is a SHiZELS galaxy previously classified as a disturbed dispersion-dominated disk; this paper finds a velocity gradient but strong asymmetries and classifies it as uncertain.

quotes.quote: ID10 This galaxy is part of the SHiZELS survey Swinbank_2012, Molina_2017, Gillman that map the H emission line with VLT/SINFONI. The ALMA observations of the CO(2-1) line used here were previously analyzed by Molina_2019 , who classify ID10 as a disturbed dispersion-dominated disk. Here, we confirm the presence of a smooth gradient in the velocity field, but the strong disturbances and asymmetries in both PVDs, the systematic residuals in the channel maps, and the presence of bright emission only on one side of the major-axis (see arrow in Fig. fig:pv3 ) lead us to classify ID10 as uncertain.

gmw_00004224.name: HE 1353 1917
gmw_00004224.overview: HE 1353 1917 is an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:1905.10385] hosting a low-power active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1910.07865]. The active nucleus drives a fast, multiphase gas outflow [arXiv:1905.10385, arXiv:1905.10383] via a radio jet that moves directly within the plane of the galactic disk [arXiv:1905.10383, arXiv:1910.07865].

sources.summary: The paper presents HE 1353 1917 as an edge-on AGN host where multiwavelength data reveal an ENLR, extra-planar fountain gas, and a compact jet-driven multiphase outflow.

quotes.quote: We combine extensive spatially-resolved multi-wavelength observations, taken as part of the Close AGN Reference Survey (CARS), for the edge-on disc galaxy HE 1353 1917 to characterize the impact of the AGN on its host galaxy via outflows and radiation. Multi-color broad-band photometry is combined with spatially-resolved optical, NIR and sub-mm and radio observations taken with VLT/MUSE, Gemini-N/NIFS, ALMA and the VLA to map the physical properties and kinematics of the multi-phase inter-stellar medium (ISM). We detect a biconical extended narrow-line region (ENLR) ionized by the luminous AGN oriented nearly parallel to the galaxy disc, extending out to at least 25 kpc. The extra-planar gas originates from galactic fountains initiated by star formation processes in the disc, rather than an AGN outflow, as shown by the kinematics and the metallicity of the gas.

Nevertheless, a fast multi-phase AGN-driven outflow with speeds up to 1000 km/s is detected close to the nucleus at 1 kpc distance. A radio jet, in connection with the AGN radiation field, is likely responsible for driving the outflow as confirmed by the energetics and the spatial alignment of the jet and multi-phase outflow. Evidence for negative AGN feedback suppressing the star formation rate (SFR) is mild and restricted to the central kpc. But while any SFR suppression must have happened recently, the outflow has the potential to greatly impact the future evolution of the galaxy disc due to its geometrical orientation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HE 1353 1917 as an exceptional nearby AGN host with a global [C ii] excess, an edge-on disk, and a massive AGN-driven multiphase outflow.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of the highest global [ C ii ] excess with respect to the FIR luminosity in the nearby AGN host galaxy HE 1353 1917. This galaxy is exceptional among a sample of five targets because the AGN ionization cone and radio jet directly intercept the cold galactic disk. As a consequence, a massive multiphase gas outflow on kiloparsec scales is embedded in an extended narrow-line region.

Based on the FIR and AGN-subtracted extinction-corrected H luminosity, we expected HE 1353 1917 to be the faintest target in [ C ii ] , but it turned out to be the brightest source of our sample. While all galaxies have similar metallicities, stellar masses, and AGN bolometric luminosities, the obvious dissimilarity is the edge-on orientation of HE 1353 1917. The [ C ii ] line width of 760 60 km s ^ 1 is broader than the CO(1-0) line for cold molecular gas outflow 210 km s ^ 1 , but narrower than the ionized gas outflow 1020 km s ^ 1 (see Fig. fig:spectra ).

sources.summary: The paper cites HE 1353-1917 as a low-power AGN where a jet moving in the disc drives a multiphase outflow and affects gas kinematics and excitation.

quotes.quote: In Husemann19a , a rich set of multi-wavelength observations are presented of the low-power AGN HE 1353-1917 . In this object, the jet is moving in the plane of the disc and is driving a multi-phase outflow over a region of about 1 kpc. Using observations in several wavebands, Husemann19a were able to show that it is most likely that the plasma jet is responsible for this.

In HE 1353--1917 and IC 5063, the highest excitation and the most extreme kinematics do not occur at the core, but along the entire jet where it appears to be interacting strongly with the ISM. One reason for these differences could be the large disparity in jet power between HE 1353--1917 and IC 5063 on the one hand and \ on the other. In addition, the fact that in HE 1353--1917 and IC 5063 the orientation of the jet is such that the jet moves in the plane of the gas disc, while in \ the jet is not aligned with the disc, may play a role in explaining the differences.

gmw_00004225.name: DDO 22
gmw_00004225.overview: DDO 22 is a dwarf galaxy observed at a high inclination [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. It features a region of high neutral hydrogen column density on its southern side, as well as a misalignment between its kinematic and neutral hydrogen major axes [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133].

sources.summary: DDO 22 is described as a high-inclination dwarf with southern high-column-density HI and misaligned kinematic and HI major axes.

quotes.quote: DDO 22: The HI axial ratio suggests that this galaxy is seen at a high inclination. A region of high HI column density is present in the southern side of the galaxy. The kinematic and HI major axes appear to be misaligned.

gmw_00004226.name: DDO 43
gmw_00004226.overview: DDO 43 is a rotationally supported, late-type dwarf irregular galaxy located in the NGC 2841 Group [arXiv:1803.01998, arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a face-on orientation with an extended HI disk containing fifteen detected HI holes [arXiv:1803.01998, arXiv:2006.01735] [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133].

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 43 as a late-type dwarf irregular with an extended HI disk, rotational support, and a dense compact dark-matter halo suppressing small-scale spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We select five late-type dwarf irregular galaxies, namely, DDO 154, NGC 3741, DDO 43, NGC 2366 and DDO 168 in which we study the constraining influence of dominant dark halo on the formation of small-scale spiral arms. Two of these (DDO 154, NGC 2366) are a part of the THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Wal08 , whereas DDO 43 and DDO 168 are a part of the LITTLE (Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes) THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Hun12 . Our sample galaxies have the following characteristics: These have a large HI disk (extending to several optical radii), and in the inner regions as well as in the outer regions the larger part of the baryonic contribution comes from HI gas, and not stars. The galaxies are rotationally supported. We find that for these low luminosity galaxies, the corresponding dark matter halo is dense and compact, i.e., the central density ( _0 ) is few times 10^ 2 M _ pc ^ 2 and the core radius (R _ ) is less than two-three times the exponential disk scalelength (R _ d ).

sources.summary: The paper places DDO 43 in the NGC 2841 Group and reports 15 H I holes, mostly Type 2 and Type 3, with one largest Type 1 hole.

quotes.quote: DDO 43 DDO 43 (PGC 21073, UGC 3860) is located 7.8 Mpc away in the Ursa Major constellation. Although it is a member of the NGC 2841 Group, it is relatively isolated from the other members. Our estimate of the scale height is about 470 pc. identified four apparent \ holes in the ISM of this galaxy. We detected 15 holes with most of them being Type 2 and Type 3.

sources.summary: DDO 43 is described as face-on, with a regular velocity field, rapidly rising rotation curve, and roughly perpendicular kinematic and optical major axes.

quotes.quote: DDO 43: The HI axial ratio indicates a face-on orientation. Parallel velocity contours in a regular velocity field mark a rapidly rising rotation curve. The kinematic and optical major axes are roughly perpendicular.

gmw_00004227.name: DDO 46
gmw_00004227.overview: DDO 46 is a gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1504.06673, arXiv:2303.05104] located approximately 6.1 Mpc away [arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a thin-disk kinematic structure [arXiv:1504.06673], an oblate dark matter halo [arXiv:2303.05104], and 18 neutral hydrogen holes [arXiv:2006.01735].

sources.summary: The paper measures DDO 46 stellar and gas kinematics, finding limited stellar spectroscopy, a central vertical velocity dispersion, maximum rotation speed, and a thin-disk-like kinematic ratio.

quotes.quote: However, due to poor weather conditions during our observing run for DDO 46, we were able to extract only one useful data point from the morphological major axis. We determined a central stellar velocity dispersion perpendicular to the disk, _ z,0 , of 13.5 8 \ for DDO 46 and < _ z,0 > of 10.7 2.9 \ for DDO 168. We then derived the maximum rotation speed in both galaxies using the LITTLE THINGS \ data.

We corrected the observed \ rotation speeds for asymmetric drift and found a maximum velocity, , of 77.4 3.7 and 67.4 4.0 for DDO 46 and DDO 168, respectively. Thus, we derived a kinematic measure, / _ z,0 , of 5.7 0.6 for DDO 46 and 6.3 0.3 for DDO 168. Comparing these values to ones determined for spiral galaxies, we find that DDO 46 and DDO 168 have / _ z,0 values indicative of thin disks, which is in contrast to minor-to-major axis ratio studies.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 46 as a literature example of a declining rotation curve, but says low inclination, low signal-to-noise, and unphysical mass implications make it unreliable.

quotes.quote: We also reproduce in Fig. fig-rc three rotation curves from the 2015AJ....149..180O analysis of the LITTLE THINGS survey 2012AJ....144..134H : those of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3.

Unfortunately neither DDO 46 nor LSB F564-V3 appear in the 2017MNRAS.466.4159I analysis: the former was excluded due to its low inclination (and thus large and uncertain correction to the rotation velocity), while the reason for the exclusion of the latter is unclear.

Furthermore, inspection of figs. A.49 A.7 in 2015AJ....149..180O reveals that the declining part of the rotation curves of DDO 46 and LSB D564-8 occurs in regions of very low signal-to-noise ratio. Finally, we note that the rotation curves of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3 cannot be interpreted as tracers of the enclosed mass, as they fall more steeply than the Keplerian limit (illustrated by the slope of the thin grey lines in Fig. fig-rc ): if taken at face value, the declining rotation velocities in the outer parts of these galaxies imply a (nonsensical) declining cumulative dynamical mass profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO46 as a gas-rich dwarf whose q decreases rapidly with radius and whose halo falls in the oblate, gas-rich dwarf regime.

quotes.quote: A clear trend is that q does not vary significantly beyond the R_ 25 radius (Figure 1). For the 6 large galaxies that have M(*)>10^ 9 M_ , q is close to constant in the outer disk region, except for NGC 3184 which shows a spike in q at large radii. However, this spike maybe due to errors in _ H i measurement as the column density decreases at the disk edge. A similar trend is present for the less massive galaxies ( M(*)<10^ 9 M_ ), where M81dfwA shows a similar spike in q at the disk edge. The dwarf galaxy IC1613 definitely has q 1 around R_ 25 and 0.5 < q < 1.0 beyond that. This is an exceptional case for the dwarfs. The gas rich dwarf DDO46 is the only galaxy that shows a significant decrease in q from q=0.5 at R_ 25 to 0.3 at 2R_ 25 , which indicates a fairly rapid change in halo shape with increasing radius.

Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

It is interesting to note that the gas rich galaxies (9 of them) all have oblate halos, with q<0.6 but the stellar dominated galaxies have a range of q values with q 0.2 (very oblate) to q 1.25 (prolate). Perhaps the most important result from this study is that the gas rich galaxies all have oblate halos at R_ 25 (Fig. 3). There are 9 gas rich galaxies that have M(gas)/M(baryon>0.5 and they are all dwarf galaxies : Holmberg1, Holmberg2, M81dwfA, DDO46, DDO53, DDO75, DDO187, F564-V3 and UGC8833. The q varies between 0.11 in DDO187 to 0.55 in Holmberg2. The stellar dominated galaxies, however, have a variety of q values ranging from q=0.21 in IC10 to q=1.25 for NGC5194 at R_ 25 .

sources.summary: DDO46 is discussed as a gas-rich LITTLE THINGS dwarf with high H I column density and a non-declining disk dynamical mass curve.

quotes.quote: These studies show that large column densities of H i exist in regions where there is hardly any stellar disk mass, good examples being the gas rich dwarfs DDO63 and DDO46 in the LITTLE THINGS survey hunter.etal.2012 .

The remaining 3 galaxies, DDO46, DDO63 and DDO187 are gas rich dwarfs and are all part of the Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes; The H i Nearby Galaxy Survey ( LITTLE THINGS ) survey hunter.etal.2012 which is a survey of nearby, gas rich irregular/dwarf galaxies.
This is especially true in the case of the large galaxies NGC628, NGC6946 and NGC4214 and the dwarfs DDO46 and DDO187, all of which clearly have non-declining disk dynamical mass curves.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 46 as a 6.1 Mpc dwarf containing 18 H I holes, more than half Type 3, with estimated scale height and disk length.

quotes.quote: DDO 46 DDO 46 (PGC 21585, UGC 3966) is situated 6.1 Mpc away with V-band magnitude 14.7 . The galaxy has 18 \ holes and more than 50 of those holes are Type 3. The estimated scale height and \ disk length are about 150 pc and 3 kpc respectively.

sources.summary: DDO 46 has a U-shaped high-column-density HI ridge, a velocity field symmetric about the minor axis but not the major axis, and an outer flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: DDO 46: The HI distribution shows a U-shaped high-column density ridge. The velocity field is reasonably symmetric with respect to the kinematic minor axis, but asymmetric with respect to the major axis. The rotation curve is almost flat at the edge of the HI distribution.

gmw_00004228.name: NGC 2537
gmw_00004228.overview: NGC 2537, also known as the Bear Paw Galaxy, is classified as a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. It features a U-shaped high-column-density neutral hydrogen ridge [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133] and hosts a major starburst region in its main body [arXiv:astro-ph/0203128].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2537 as having a just-resolved radio continuum source aligned with strong H emission, consistent with optically thin thermal emission from a major starburst region.

quotes.quote: A just resolved source coincides with strong H emission in the main body of NGC 2537 (Stil 1999). Higher-frequency flux-densities S_ 4.8 = 18 4 \ mJy at 6.3 cm and S_ 25 =11 4 \ mJy (Klein et al. 1984) agree well with our value in Table cont-sample-tab and suggest a flat radio spectrum, i.e. optically thin thermal emission as suggested by the strong H emission. The greater distance of NGC 2537, and the observed flux-density suggest a total excitation parameter u 1050 pc cm ^ 2 , corresponding to the output of some 2800 O6 stars. This suggests a major burst of star formation within the kiloparsec-sized region. It is tempting to speculate that the companion UGC 4278 bears responsibility for this.

sources.summary: NGC 2537 is described as a blue compact dwarf/Bear Paw Galaxy with a U-shaped high-column-density HI ridge, a flattening rotation curve, nearby UGC 4278, and no HI detection of NGC 2537A.

quotes.quote: NGC 2537: The HI distribution consists of a U-shaped high column-density ridge. The velocity field, irregular in the north, exhibits the characteristic spider shape of a flattening rotation curve, also evident in the XV map. NGC 2537 is sometimes classified as a Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy and is also known as the Bear Paw Galaxy (Schorn 1988). UGC 4278 is a nearby companion. NGC 2537A occurring just east of NGC 2537 on PSS plates is not visible in the HI data.

gmw_00004229.name: DDO 52
gmw_00004229.overview: DDO 52 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the NGC 2841 Group [arXiv:2006.01735]. Its neutral hydrogen is primarily distributed in a low-column-density disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133] that contains 17 detected disk holes [arXiv:2006.01735].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 52 as the most distant LITTLE THINGS galaxy, an isolated NGC 2841 Group object with 17 detected H I disk holes.

quotes.quote: DDO 52 DDO 52 (PGC 23769, UGC 4426), which lies about 10.3 Mpc away, is the most distant galaxy in the LITTLE THINGS sample. It is an isolated galaxy in the NGC 2841 Group (NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is operated by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology, under contract with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. ). The calculated scale height of the galaxy is about 240 pc, and the \ disk extends to 4.9 kpc from the galactic center. We detected 17 \ holes in the disk according to our selection criteria.

sources.summary: DDO 52 is described as having most HI in a low-column-density disk, a regular velocity field, a possible flattening rotation curve, and high rotational-to-random velocity.

quotes.quote: DDO 52: Most of the HI is in a low-column-density disk, with a peak N_ HI = 1.5 10^ 21 2 . The velocity field is regular with a hint of a flattening rotation curve. The XV diagram shows a rather high ratio of rotational to random velocity, despite its small amplitude.

gmw_00004230.name: DDO 123
gmw_00004230.overview: DDO 123, also known as UGC 7534, is a face-on dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1908.07807, arXiv:astro-ph/0203133] with a sub-solar metallicity [arXiv:1908.07807, arXiv:2301.09953]. Its neutral hydrogen gas is distributed evenly throughout the disk, and its rotation curve rises to the edge of this gas disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133].

sources.summary: UGC 07534 is described as the low-luminosity dwarf irregular host of SN 2018hna, with a 12.82 Mpc distance, outer-disk supernova location, and sub-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: SN 2018hna was discovered by Koichi Itagaki on 2018 October 23.9 UT (JD 2458414.3) in the galaxy UGC 07534. SN 2018hna lies 31 E and 30 N from the nucleus of the host galaxy. A spectrum obtained by Hiroshima One-shot Wide-field POLarimeter (HOWPol, 2008kawabata ) on 2018 October 24.8 UT, displayed a prominent P-Cygni profile of H categorizing it as a Type II SN (TNS 2933).

A distance modulus of = 30.52 0.29 mag (12.82 2.02 Mpc) is estimated for the host galaxy UGC 07534 from the mean of the Hubble flow distances provided in NASA Extragalactic Database (NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu ) and the Tully-Fisher relation 2013akarachentsev . SN 2018hna occurred in the outskirts ( 2.5 kpc from the center) of the low-luminosity ( M_B -17.1 mag) dwarf irregular galaxy (IBm) UGC 07534. The luminosity-metallicity relation of 2004tremonti yields an oxygen abundance of 8.14 0.02 for UGC 07534. This indicates a sub-solar metallicity ( 0.3 Z_ ) of the host environment of SN 2018hna and is consistent with the occurrence of 1987A-like SNe in late-type galaxies (Sc or later; 2012pastorello ) having sub-solar metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 7534 as the irregular dwarf host of SN 2018hna, giving its subsolar metallicity, heliocentric redshift, Tully-Fisher distance, and negligible host extinction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of SN 2018hna, UGC 7534, is an irregular dwarf galaxy, which was classified as an IBm-type galaxy according to the third reference catagloue of bright galaxies (RC3.9, 1991rc3..book.....D). The metallicity of UGC 7534 was found to be subsolar (Z 0.3 Z, 2019ApJ...882L..15S). The helliocentric velocity of UGC 7534 gives a redshift of 0.002408 1999PASP..111..438F. The distance to this galaxy was estimated to be 10.50 Mpc through the Tully-Fisher relation 2013AJ....145..101K.

Given a measurement error of 25 2013AJ....145..101K, we apply a distance of 10.50 2.63 Mpc, and a distance modulus of = 30.11 0.54 mag. Since there is no significant Na I D absorption in optical spectra of SN 2018hna at the redshift of the host, we adopt a Galactic reddening of E(B-V) = 0.01 mag 2011ApJ...737..103S and neglect extinction in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: DDO 123 is described as a face-on galaxy with evenly distributed HI, velocity-field irregularities tied to low signal-to-noise regions, and a rotation curve rising to the HI edge.

quotes.quote: DDO 123: The HI is distributed evenly throughout the disk of this face-on galaxy. Irregularities in the velocity field coincide with low signal-to-noise regions. The rotation curve rises out to the edge of the HI disk.

gmw_00004231.name: DDO 165
gmw_00004231.overview: DDO 165 is a dwarf galaxy in the M81 Group [arXiv:2006.01735] that features a central, low-density neutral gas hole surrounded by a ring-like structure [arXiv:1910.04167]. Most of its neutral hydrogen is located in this ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133], and the galaxy likely experienced a large-scale interstellar medium blow-out in the last 100 Myr [arXiv:1705.04692, arXiv:1910.04167].

sources.summary: The paper reports no diffuse X-ray emission in DDO 165, notes the observations are least sensitive, and argues warm gas remains in the disc though a recent large-scale blow-out is likely.

quotes.quote: DDO 165 From Figure fig:ddo165_xraycontour , no diffuse X-ray emission is detected within the main disc of the galaxy. DDO 165 is the farthest galaxy in the sample and also has the shortest combined exposure time of 24 ksec, making these X-ray observations the least sensitive of the sample. However, based on deep H imaging, the warm ionized gas is contained with the \ disc (McQuinn et al. \ in prep) suggesting that it is unlikely that DDO 165 has a hot gas outflow of significance. These observations do not rule out a starburst-driven galactic wind in the recent past; modelling of the energetics of star formation suggest it experienced a large scale blow-out of the \ disc in the last 100 Myr Cannon2011a, Cannon2011b .
No outflow was detected in DDO 165; this galaxy has likely experienced a large-scale blow-out of the ISM in the last 100 Myr Cannon2011a, Cannon2011b .

sources.summary: DDO 165 is described as HI-ring dominated, with a highly irregular velocity field, a southeast-to-northwest maximum velocity gradient, and an XV-map rotation fit dominated by ring emission.

quotes.quote: DDO 165: Most of the HI is located in a ring. The velocity field is highly irregular. The maximum velocity gradient is along a line from SE to NW in position angle 120^ 140^ . The rotation velocity was fitted to the XV map assuming solid-body rotation. However, the emission in the XV map is mainly due to the ring.

sources.summary: The paper says DDO 165 has a central neutral-gas hole, likely prior ISM blow-out, ring-like H I and H-alpha emission, and possible foreground cirrus contamination.

quotes.quote: DDO 165 From Figure fig:ddo165 , the \ map shows the neutral gas has a central, low-density hole surrounding by a broken, ring-like structure of material. A previous study of the \ concluded that the galaxy likely experienced a blow-out of the ISM in the recent past Cannon2011a . The majority of \ emission is coincident with this ring-like \ distribution, and with a similar spatial extent. The higher surface brightness \ emission regions have circular, bubble-like appearances and coincide with higher \ densities.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 165 as an M81 Group dwarf with three good-quality holes, one of each type, compared with earlier detections.

quotes.quote: DDO 165 DDO 165 (Mailyan 82, PGC 45372 or UGC 8201) is an M81 Group dwarf at a distance of 4.6 Mpc with a V-band magnitude of 15.6 . detected seven \ holes in DDO 165. According to our criteria, we have only three `good quality' holes, one of each type. The largest hole we found is in agreement with whereas the other two holes are listed by them as a single hole.

gmw_00004232.name: DDO 166
gmw_00004232.overview: DDO 166 is a face-on dwarf galaxy characterized by a ridge of high-column-density neutral hydrogen extending over its eastern side [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. Despite its small inclination, the galaxy exhibits a strong velocity gradient and S-shaped isovelocity contours on its eastern side [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133].

sources.summary: DDO 166 is described as face-on, with an eastern high-column-density HI ridge, a strong velocity gradient despite small inclination, and S-shaped eastern isovelocity contours.

quotes.quote: DDO 166: A ridge of high-column-density HI extends over the eastern side of this face-on galaxy. The velocity field has a strong gradient, in spite of the small inclination suggested by the HI isophotes. The isovelocity contours are twisted into an S-shape at the eastern side of the galaxy.

gmw_00004233.name: DDO 168
gmw_00004233.overview: DDO 168 is a rotationally supported dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1803.01998] located in the Canes Venatici I group [arXiv:2006.01735]. It features a thin-disk kinematic structure [arXiv:1504.06673], a slow-rotating neutral hydrogen bar [arXiv:1908.00023], and two northern disk holes [arXiv:2006.01735].

sources.summary: The paper measures DDO 168 along four position angles and derives its stellar velocity dispersion, rotation speed, and thin-disk-like kinematic shape indicator.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the stellar and gas kinematics of DDO 46 and DDO 168 from the LITTLE THINGS survey and determine their respective / _ z,0 values. We used the Kitt Peak National Observatory's Mayall 4-meter telescope with the Echelle spectrograph as a long-slit spectrograph, which provided a two dimensional, 3 long slit. We acquired spectra of DDO 168 along four position angles by placing the slit over the morphological major and minor axes and two intermediate position angles. However, due to poor weather conditions during our observing run for DDO 46, we were able to extract only one useful data point from the morphological major axis. We determined a central stellar velocity dispersion perpendicular to the disk, _ z,0 , of 13.5 8 \ for DDO 46 and < _ z,0 > of 10.7 2.9 \ for DDO 168.

We corrected the observed \ rotation speeds for asymmetric drift and found a maximum velocity, , of 77.4 3.7 and 67.4 4.0 for DDO 46 and DDO 168, respectively. Thus, we derived a kinematic measure, / _ z,0 , of 5.7 0.6 for DDO 46 and 6.3 0.3 for DDO 168. Comparing these values to ones determined for spiral galaxies, we find that DDO 46 and DDO 168 have / _ z,0 values indicative of thin disks, which is in contrast to minor-to-major axis ratio studies.

sources.summary: The paper presents DDO168 as a standard-SFDM example where the minimum superfluid mass is constrained at the galaxy center rather than the outer rotation-curve radius.

quotes.quote: This is illustrated in Fig.  fig:smallfMDM-DDO168 for DDO168. For the smallest boundary condition value = -0.06 shown in Fig.  fig:smallfMDM-DDO168 , at R = R_ max , both /m and _* are not close to the minimum values that _ SF > 0 allows. However, at R = 0 , the minimum values are reached (i.e., _ SF vanishes). Thus, the boundary condition = -0.06 corresponds to the minimum possible allowed by our condition _ SF > 0 . But now the constraint comes from R = 0 rather than R = R_ max .

When does not grow sufficiently, the condition (0) > 0 gives an additional constraint. This is what happens for DDO168 as illustrated in Fig.  fig:smallfMDM-DDO168 . This, then, is the reason why two-field SFDM allows smaller superfluid masses (i.e. smaller ) compared to standard SFDM.

sources.summary: The paper identifies an H I bar in dwarf irregular DDO 168, measures a slow pattern speed, and links the result to dark-matter-halo dynamical friction.

quotes.quote: We examine the total intensity maps of the VLA LITTLE-THINGS galaxies and identify an bar in the dwarf irregular galaxy DDO 168 which has a dense and compact dark matter halo that dominates at all radii. This is only the third galaxy found to host an bar. Using the kinematic data, we apply the Tremaine-Weinberg method to estimate the pattern speed of the bar. The bar is found to have an average pattern speed of 23.3 5.9 . Interestingly, for the first time, we find that the observed pattern speeds of the bar in the two kinematic halves are different.

We estimate the bar radius to be 1 kpc and the dimensionless ratio, R_L/R_b to be 2.1 indicating a `slow' bar in DDO 168. Our results confirm the proposition that the dynamical friction with the halo slows down a rotating bar in a galaxy dominated by dark matter halo from inner radii.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 168 as a late-type dwarf irregular with an extended HI disk, rotational support, and a dense compact dark-matter halo suppressing small-scale spiral structure.

quotes.quote: We select five late-type dwarf irregular galaxies, namely, DDO 154, NGC 3741, DDO 43, NGC 2366 and DDO 168 in which we study the constraining influence of dominant dark halo on the formation of small-scale spiral arms. Two of these (DDO 154, NGC 2366) are a part of the THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Wal08 , whereas DDO 43 and DDO 168 are a part of the LITTLE (Local Irregulars That Trace Luminosity Extremes) THINGS (The HI Nearby Galaxy Survey) survey by Hun12 . Our sample galaxies have the following characteristics: These have a large HI disk (extending to several optical radii), and in the inner regions as well as in the outer regions the larger part of the baryonic contribution comes from HI gas, and not stars. The galaxies are rotationally supported. We find that for these low luminosity galaxies, the corresponding dark matter halo is dense and compact, i.e., the central density ( _0 ) is few times 10^ 2 M _ pc ^ 2 and the core radius (R _ ) is less than two-three times the exponential disk scalelength (R _ d ).

sources.summary: DDO 168 is described with two central very high HI column-density regions, a velocity-gradient position angle changing across the disk, and peculiar structure resembling other amorphous galaxies.

quotes.quote: DDO 168: Two very high HI column density regions ( N_ HI 6 10^ 21 2 ) occur near the center of the galaxy at = 13^h12^m16^s.8 , = 46^ 11'0'' and = 13^h12^m15^s.8 , = 46^ 11'30'' . The position angle of the velocity gradient changes over the disk by approximately 20^ . The peculiar structure of DDO 168 is not unique. Similar very-high column density regions combined with twisted velocity fields have been observed in other `amorphous galaxies' such as NGC 1140 (Hunter et al. 1994) and IZw 18 (Viallefond et al. 1987, Van Zee et al. 1998).

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 8320 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This irregular galaxy is located 14 away from UGC 8308, forming a probable pair of dwarf galaxies. Bresolin et al. (1993), Hopp Schulte-Ladbeck (1995), and Tikhonov Karachentsev (1998) have resolved it into stars and estimated it distance to be 3.3 Mpc, 3.9 Mpc, and 4.0 Mpc, respectively. The WFPC2 photometry gives a TRGB magnitude corresponding to a distance of 4.33 0.49 Mpc. The derived TRGB distances of UGC 8320 and UGC 8308 agree with each other within the uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper treats DDO 168 as the lone dwarf consistent with extrapolated Tully-Fisher behavior, attributing this to its high central H I column density and concentrated gas distribution.

quotes.quote: DDO 168 is the only dwarf galaxy in the rotation-curve sample that is consistent with the extrapolation of the T-F relation, thereby breaking the pattern established by the other dwarfs. However, its central HI column density N_ HI =6.3 10^ 21   cm^ 2 is exceptionally high. In Section  model-sec we have shown that galaxies with a centrally concentrated HI distribution have linewidths 20 to 30 lower than those with a homogeneous HI distribution. If we consider the maximum rotation velocity instead of the linewidth, DDO 168 is no longer different from the other fast rotators (see Fig.  TFdisc ). We thus conclude that DDO 168 would have a linewidth comparable to those of the other dwarfs, if it had a smoother HI distribution.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 168 as a Canes Venatici I Group member with two northern H I disk holes and an estimated scale height.

quotes.quote: DDO 168 DDO 168 (PGC 46039, UGC 8320) is another Canes Venatici I group member also known as PGC 46039 and UGC 8320. It is located 4.3 Mpc away with V-band magnitude 15.7 . The galaxy has two holes, both located in the northern part of the \ disk. The \ scale height of the galaxy is estimated as 210 pc.

sources.summary: The paper says DDO 168 lacks a declining rotation curve in reanalysis and has irregular kinematics plus a nearby companion, undermining its use as a clean mass tracer.

quotes.quote: We also reproduce in Fig. fig-rc three rotation curves from the 2015AJ....149..180O analysis of the LITTLE THINGS survey 2012AJ....144..134H : those of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3.

They did not find a declining rotation curve for DDO 168, and pointed out several kinematic irregularities -- a clear bar in both the stellar and gas distributions, a `very irregular' outer disc, possibly warped, and possible signs of significant amounts of inflowing/outflowing gas -- which may account for the differences. DDO 168 also has a companion, DDO 167, at a projected separation of only 33 kpc 2015AJ....149..196J .

Finally, we note that the rotation curves of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3 cannot be interpreted as tracers of the enclosed mass, as they fall more steeply than the Keplerian limit (illustrated by the slope of the thin grey lines in Fig. fig-rc ): if taken at face value, the declining rotation velocities in the outer parts of these galaxies imply a (nonsensical) declining cumulative dynamical mass profile.

sources.summary: The paper reports that DDO 168 has outer surface-brightness excess, central deficit, color-profile flattening, and low-level isophotal changes unlikely to be sky-subtraction artifacts.

quotes.quote: DDO 168 : shows an excess in its surface brightness profiles with respect to a pure exponential at large radii as well as a deficit in the central regions. Its colour profile displays a clear flattening from the point at which the luminosity excess becomes visible in the profiles. But the error envelopes are large at those points both in the radial profiles and colour profiles. It is to be noted though that for this galaxy, the measured sky counts after subtraction were marginally negative around the galaxy.

gmw_00004234.name: DDO 185
gmw_00004234.overview: DDO 185 is a dwarf galaxy with a high-inclination neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. It exhibits concentric, elliptical density contours [arXiv:2404.16247], an asymmetric velocity shear [arXiv:2404.16247], and non-circular motions likely caused by an inner star-forming region [arXiv:1103.4215].

sources.summary: UGC8837 is excluded from later scalar-field trends because its fitted length scale is anomalously small and the authors attribute the result to low-quality, mismatched kinematic data.

quotes.quote: One problematic situation is UGC8837, which clearly deviates from the general trend for what concerning the value of scalar field length L , resulting it very small. Even if the fit of our model to data is very good, we do not consider anymore this galaxy in the following discussion because its results are strongly related to the low quality of data. As discussed in , the H are of good quality, but do not correspond very well to the HI profile, with a large difference in their systemic velocity probably due to the interference of inner star formation region and there located non-circular motions. A further error source can be also the inclination, being this galaxy almost edge-on, the most problematic configuration in this case.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 08837 as an observed comparison to a simulated system, emphasizing similar H I morphology, asymmetric velocity shear, and possible catalog-selection ambiguity.

quotes.quote: We select UGC 08837 for comparison because it is similar in size and HI mass to m11c, and its rotation curve is qualitatively similar to the reconstruction shown for m11c. Both m11c and UGC 0887 appear to have have concentric, elliptical density contours, as well as a clear (albeit asymmetric) velocity shear. As discussed in the previous subsection, these HI features are indicators of diskiness, and thus fitness for RC analysis. However, as shown in Figure fig:ang_mom , m11c does not form a disk; its apparent velocity gradient is a result of viewing angle and the resolution and Gaussian blur used to generate HI maps that are comparable to real observations. The purpose of this comparison is not to state that UGC 08837 has similar strong magnetic fields to m11c, or even that it is a poor candidate for RC analysis, but simply to highlight the visual similarities between UGC 08837 and m11c. If UGC 08837 is included in RC analysis for SPARC-like catalogs, then it is plausible that a galaxy like m11c could be as well.

sources.summary: DDO 185 is described as a high-inclination HI disk with regularly spaced isovelocity contours consistent with a rising rotation curve, and as a Tully-Fisher calibrator.

quotes.quote: DDO 185: The HI column density map suggests a disk seen at a high inclination. The isovelocity contours are regularly spaced, consistent with the rising rotation curve shown by the position-velocity map. DDO 185 (=Holmberg IV) was used as a calibrator galaxy for the Tully-Fisher relation by Kraan-Korteweg et al. (1988).

gmw_00004235.name: G1
gmw_00004235.overview: G1 is an edge-on disk-like galaxy [arXiv:1701.04272]. It acts as a main gravitational lensing galaxy located at a redshift of 0.742 [arXiv:1701.04272].

sources.summary: The paper treats G1 as the main lensing galaxy in SDSS J1515+1511, discussing its faint contaminated spectrum, assumed redshift, mass scale, extinction, microlensing, and disk-like morphology.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, the main lensing galaxy (G1) is so faint and close to the bright quasar that it is not feasible to accurately extract its spectrum through the GTC data. However, assuming the putative redshift z_ G1 = 0.742, the GTC and LT spectra of the distant quasar are used to discuss the macrolens magnification, and the extinction and microlensing effects in G1. The new constraints on the time delay and macrolens magnification ratio essentially do not change previous findings on the mass scale of G1 and external shear, while the redshift of the lensing mass is found to be consistent with the assumed value of z_ G1 . This is a clear evidence that G1 is indeed located at z_ G1 = 0.742.
Moreover, 2016MNRAS.458....2R found that G1 is an edge--on disk--like galaxy.

gmw_00004236.name: G2
gmw_00004236.overview: G2 is an early-type galaxy located in a gravitational lens field [arXiv:1701.04272]. Spectroscopic data from the Gran Telescopio Canarias places the galaxy at a redshift of 0.541, where it is estimated to contribute only a small fraction of the field's external shear [arXiv:1701.04272].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G2 as a secondary galaxy in the lens field, measures its redshift from GTC spectra, and estimates its dark-matter shear contribution as small.

quotes.quote: From the GTC data we also obtain the redshift of two additional objects (the secondary galaxy G2 and a new absorption system) and discuss their possible role in the lens scenario.

However, we were lucky because our GTC--OSIRIS--R500R long--slit exposures included light from the secondary galaxy G2 (see Section sec:intro and the top sub--panel inside Figure fig:f4 ), and thus, we extracted the G2 spectrum and measured its redshift. Since G2 is 16 away from the A image, we used the IRAF/APEXRACT package to get an initial spectrum, and then the riz -SDSS magnitudes of the galaxy to peform an accurate flux calibration. The final spectrum is consistent with an early--type galaxy at z_ G2 = 0.541 (see Figure fig:f4 ).
Thus, G2 may account for only a small fraction of the external shear in Table tab:t8 , while it could be responsible of a slight increase in z_ l see discussion in 2010ApJ...708..995G .

gmw_00004237.name: SRGA J 005751.0 + 210846
gmw_00004237.overview: SRGA J 005751.0 + 210846 is identified with the edge-on galaxy LEDA 1643776 [arXiv:2104.09390]. Its high X-ray luminosity indicates the presence of an active galactic nucleus, which is heavily obscured by absorption in the host galaxy's interstellar gas [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J 005751.0+210846 is identified with edge-on galaxy LEDA 1643776; its weak optical lines and high X-ray luminosity indicate a hidden AGN with absorption likely in interstellar gas.

quotes.quote: SRGA J005751.0 + 210846 This X-ray source was discovered in the 4--12 keV band by the \ telescope of the \ observatory and, at the same time, was not detected in softer X-rays by the \ telescope of the same observatory. A probable optical counterpart of the X-ray source is the galaxy LEDA 1643776 that falls into the \ position error circle (Fig. fig:spec0057 ). The galaxy is oriented edge-on to the observer. Previously, a spectrum has already been obtained for it during the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (release 12, sdssdr12 ), from which its redshift was measured ( z=0.04798 0.00002 ).

Nevertheless, a high X-ray luminosity ( 5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 in the 4--12 keV band from the \ data) of the object leaves no doubt that this is an AGN. The weakness of the observed lines probably stems from the fact that we observe the galaxy LEDA 1643776 edge-on, so that the optical radiation from the active nucleus (and, in particular, from the narrow-line region) is almost completely absorbed in the interstellar gas of the galaxy. The non-detection by the \ telescope in combination with the 4--12 keV flux measured by the \ telescope (Fig. fig:spec0057 ) allows a lower limit (at 90 confidence) to be placed on the absorption column density: >10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . Much of this absorption may arise in the interstellar gas of the galaxy and not in the gas–dust torus surrounding the SMBH.

gmw_00004238.name: SRGA J 043209.6 + 354917
gmw_00004238.overview: SRGA J 043209.6 + 354917 is identified with a 2MASX/WISEA galaxy whose near-infrared colors indicate the presence of an active nucleus [arXiv:2104.09390]. Optical line ratios and hydrogen emission line fluxes classify the object as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with slight X-ray absorption [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J043209.6+354917 is identified with a 2MASX/WISEA galaxy whose colors imply an active nucleus; optical line ratios classify it as a Seyfert 1 with slight X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: SRGA J043209.6 + 354917 This X-ray source is first mentioned under the name 1WGA J 0432.1 + 3549 in the catalogue of sources discovered in the soft X-ray energy band during the \ pointed observations wgacat . It is also present in the catalogue of sources detected during the \ slew observations xmmsl2 . However, the nature of this object so far has remained unknown. The source was reliably detected by both \ and \ telescopes of the \ observatory. The X-ray source is reliably identified with the galaxy 2MASX J 04320796 + 3549287 = WISEA J 043207.95 + 354928.8 (Fig. fig:spec0432 ), whose colors in the near infrared ( W1-W2=0.68 ) wright10 point to the presence of an active nucleus.

The object is classified from the ratios log( [ O III ] 5007/ H )>0.77 and log( [ N II ] 6584/ H )=0.19 0.11 (Fig. chart:bpt ) and the fluxes in the broad and narrow H and H components as a Seyfert 1 galaxy. A slight absorption is detected in the object's X- ray spectrum (Fig. fig:spec0432 ): 3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00004239.name: SRGA J 045049.8 + 301449
gmw_00004239.overview: SRGA J 045049.8 + 301449 is a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy identified with LEDA 1896296 [arXiv:2104.09390]. The galaxy exhibits a broad component only in its Balmer H-alpha line and shows appreciable absorption in its X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J045049.8+301449 is identified with LEDA 1896296/WISEA J045048.00+301502.8 and classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy with appreciable X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: SRGA J045049.8 + 301449 This object was discovered in hard X-rays (the source SWIFT J0450.6 + 3015) by the BAT instrument of the Neil Gehrels \ observatory swiftbat and is present in the catalogue of point X-ray sources detected by the XRT telescope of the same observatory evan20 . However, its nature so far has remained unknown. The source was reliably detected by both \ and \ telescopes of the \ observatory. The X-ray source is reliably identified (Fig. fig:spec0450 ) with the galaxy LEDA 1896296 = WISEA J045048.00 + 301502.8 ( W1-W2=0.38 ).

From the position on the BPT diagram (Fig. chart:bpt ) and the presence of a broad component only in the Balmer H line, the object can be classified as a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy. An appreciable absorption is detected in the object's X-ray spectrum (Fig. fig:spec0450 ): 4 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00004240.name: SRGA J 152102.3 + 320418
gmw_00004240.overview: SRGA J 152102.3 + 320418 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.114 [arXiv:2104.09390]. Its infrared color indicates the presence of an active nucleus, and optical line ratios classify the object as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J152102.3+320418 is identified with WISEA J152101.83+320414.6; its infrared color suggests an active nucleus and optical line ratios classify it as Seyfert 2.

quotes.quote: SRGA J152102.3 + 320418 This X-ray source is present in the catalogue of point X-ray sources detected by the XRT telescope of the Neil Gehrels \ observatory evan20 , but its nature so far has remained unknown. The source was reliably detected by both \ and \ telescopes of the \ observatory. The X-ray source is reliably identified (Fig. fig:spec1521 ) with the galaxy (SDSS data) WISEA J152101.83 + 320414.6, whose infrared color ( W1-W2=1.20 ) points to the possible presence of an active nucleus.

The redshift of the galaxy determined from these 14 lines is z=0.11425 0.00031 . The ratios log( [ N II ] 6584/ H )=-0.61 0.03 and log( [ O III ] 5007/ H )=0.88 0.05 . From the position on the BPT diagram (Fig. chart:bpt ) and the absence of broad lines, the object is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy.

gmw_00004241.name: SRGA J 200431.6 + 610211
gmw_00004241.overview: SRGA J 200431.6 + 610211 is an active galactic nucleus identified with a 2MASX/WISEA galaxy [arXiv:2104.09390]. Based on its infrared color, emission lines, and the absence of broad lines, it is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.05866 [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J200431.6+610211 is identified with a 2MASX/WISEA galaxy whose color suggests an active nucleus; seven emission lines give z=0.05866 and Seyfert 2 classification.

quotes.quote: SRGA J200431.6 + 610211 This X-ray source was discovered during the \ all-sky survey: 2RXS J 200433.8 + 610235 2rxs . However, its nature so far has remained unknown. The source was detected by both \ and \ telescopes of the \ observatory. The X-ray source is reliably identified (Fig. fig:spec2004 ) with the galaxy 2MASX J 20043237 + 6102311 = WISEA J 200432.40 + 610230.8 , whose infrared color ( W1-W2=0.89 ) points to the possible presence of an active nucleus.

The redshift was determined from seven lines: z = 0.05866 0.00013 . The ratios log( [ [ O III ] 5007/ H )=1.04 0.03 and log( [ N II ] 6584/ H )=0.00 0.06 . From the position on the BPT diagram (Fig. chart:bpt ) and the absence of broad lines, the object can be classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy.

gmw_00004242.name: SRGA J 224125.9 + 760343
gmw_00004242.overview: SRGA J 224125.9 + 760343 is an active galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.2834 [arXiv:2104.09390]. Its infrared color, Fe II emission, and narrow broad Balmer components identify the object as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J224125.9+760343 is identified with WISEA J224125.79+760353.8; its infrared color, emission-line redshift, narrow broad Balmer components, and Fe II emission indicate a narrow-line Seyfert 1.

quotes.quote: SRGA J224125.9 + 760343 This X-ray source was discovered during the \ all-sky survey: 2RXS J224124.5 + 760346 2rxs , but its nature so far has remained unknown. The source was detected by both \ and \ telescopes of the \ observatory. The X-ray source is reliably identified (Fig. fig:spec2241 ) with the infrared source WISEA J 224125.79 + 760353.8 , whose infrared color ( W1-W2=0.97 ) points to the possible presence of an active nucleus.

The redshift determined from six emission lines is z=0.2834 0.0003 . The narrow-line flux ratio is log( [ O III ] 5007/ H )=0.66 , while the ratio log( [ N II ] 6584/ H ) is difficult to estimate due to the line merging. The relative narrowness of the broad Balmer line components ( FWHM (H )<2000 km s ^ 1 ) and the presence of a noticeable Fe II emission suggest that this object is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy.

gmw_00004243.name: SRGA J 232446.8 + 440756
gmw_00004243.overview: SRGA J 232446.8 + 440756 is an irregular galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.046 [arXiv:2104.09390]. Its infrared color and optical emission line ratios indicate the presence of an active nucleus, classifying the object as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2104.09390].

sources.summary: SRGA J232446.8+440756 is identified with an irregular 2MASX/WISEA galaxy; its infrared color and optical line ratios show an active nucleus classified as Seyfert 2.

quotes.quote: SRGA J232446.8 + 440756 This X-ray source was discovered in the 4--12 keV band by the \ telescope of the \ observatory and, at the same time, was not detected in softer X-rays by the \ telescope. The X-ray source can be identified with the irregular galaxy 2MASX J 23244834 + 4407564 = WISEA J 232448.36 + 440756.5 (Fig. fig:spec2324 ). Its redshift is known: z=0.04634 2mass , while its infrared color ( W1-W2=0.83 ) points to the presence of an active nucleus. However, the galaxy has not yet been classified as an AGN from optical spectroscopy.

The redshift was determined from five emission lines and is z = 0.04624 0.00020 , consistent with the previously measured value by . The ratios log( [ N II ] 6584/ H )=-0.46 0.07 and log( [ O III ] 5007/ H )= 0.86 0.07 . From the position on the BPT diagram (Fig. chart:bpt ) and the absence of broad lines, the object can be classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy.

gmw_00004244.name: KK 246
gmw_00004244.overview: KK 246 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located within the Local Void [arXiv:0705.4139, arXiv:1103.5798]. It features an extremely extended neutral hydrogen disk that is tilted relative to its stellar distribution [arXiv:1103.5798, arXiv:2106.02672], and it exhibits a high dynamical-to-baryonic mass ratio [arXiv:1103.5798, arXiv:1205.6934].

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 461-36, alias KK 246, as a lonely galaxy with an HST TRGB distance, Local Void location, and high peculiar velocity constraining void flow.

quotes.quote: An HST observation provides a TRGB measurement for the lonely galaxy ESO 461-36 = KK 246 2006AJ....131.1361K . The distance given in that reference is probably too great, primarily because the reddening estimate that was used 1998ApJ...500..525S is too low. Using the procedures described by 2007ApJ...661..815R we find a distance of 6.4 Mpc. Though closer than previously suspected, the galaxy still lies well into the Local Void. This galaxy has an observed V_ LS =443 \ resulting in V_ pec =-30 \ with H _0=74 .

ESO 461-36 has a peculiar velocity toward us in the Local Sheet rest frame as do galaxies on almost the opposite side of the sky in the Leo Spur. However in the rest frame established by galaxies with known distances in the Local Supercluster we are moving toward the Leo Spur and away from ESO 461-36. With respect to the LSC, ESO 461-36 has a very high peculiar velocity.

sources.summary: The paper presents KK 246 as an isolated Local Void dwarf irregular with an extremely extended, misaligned H i disk, anomalous gas, high dark-matter content, and possible gas accretion signatures.

quotes.quote: We have found that KK 246, the only confirmed galaxy located within the nearby Tully Void, is a dwarf galaxy with an extremely extended H i disk and signs of an H i cloud with anomalous velocity. It also exhibits clear misalignment between the kinematical major and minor axes, indicative of an oval distortion, and a general misalignment between the H i and optical major axes. We measure a H i mass of 1.05 0.08 10^8 M_ , and a H i extent 5 times that of the stellar disk, one of the most extended H i disks known. We estimate a dynamical mass of 4.1 10^9 M_ , making this also one of the darkest galaxies known, with a mass-to-light ratio of 89. The relative isolation and extreme underdense environment make this an interesting case for examining the role of gas accretion in galaxy evolution.
KK 246, also referred to as ESO 461-036, is a dwarf irregular galaxy residing a few Mpc within the Tully Void, an extremely large void at least 23 Mpc in radius Tully2008 .

sources.summary: The paper describes KK 246 as an isolated Local Void dwarf with a stellar distribution tilted relative to its H I cloud, possibly formed by a merger of two dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The dwarf galaxy KK 246 seems to have evolved in splendid isolation in its lonely position in the Local Void (Topic topic:LVoid ). So why is the distribution of stars in KK 246 tilted from the H I cloud that surrounds it ? A first thought is that two dwarfs merged to form KK 246. To explore this it would be helpful to know the streaming motions of the stars in this galaxy to compare to the redshift pattern in the HI. Are the rotation axes of stars and H I not the same?

sources.summary: The paper models KK 246 as an isolated Local Void dwarf whose rotation curve and high mass discrepancy remain consistent with MOND.

quotes.quote: Within the framework of modified Newtonian dynamics (MOND) we investigate the kinematics of two dwarf spiral galaxies belonging to very different environments, namely KK 246 in the Local Void and Holmberg II in the M81 group. A mass model of the rotation curve of KK 246 is presented for the first time, and we show that its observed kinematics are consistent with MOND.

The isolated galaxy KK 246, on the other hand, has a maximum velocity consistent with MOND (see also Milgrom 2011), despite having a very high mass discrepancy: its dynamical-to-baryonic mass ratio is 15.
The previous analyses of KK 246 are not as detailed as the one presented here, because the whole set of data necessary to perform a mass decomposition based on the rotation curve was unavailable.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 461 36 as a lonely dwarf in the Local Void with a measured TRGB distance, H I velocity, and peculiar motion that contributes to the local void-flow interpretation.

quotes.quote: A good TRGB distance and HI velocity exists for the lonely dwarf galaxy ESO 461 36 2006AJ....131.1361K . This galaxy has the peculiar velocity 216 \ indicated by the vector in Fig. 2. This galaxy is chasing us out of the void. It's motion is additive to the orange vector of Fig. 1 so it's motion with respect to the rest frame given by our 3000 \ sample is over 500 .

gmw_00004245.name: CSWA 19
gmw_00004245.overview: CSWA 19 is a high-redshift, gravitationally lensed galaxy [arXiv:1805.01484]. Its systemic redshift is determined from emission and photospheric absorption features, and it exhibits sub-solar abundance ratios alongside enhanced aluminum covering fractions [arXiv:1805.01484].

sources.summary: The paper uses CSWA 19 in the lensed-galaxy outflow sample, noting enhanced Al III covering, subsolar abundance ratios, and a systemic redshift from emission and absorption features.

quotes.quote: \ covering fractions are on average higher than for the low ions, and this difference is statistically significant within individual velocity channels in some cases (notably for CSWA 19 and CSWA 39).
Sub-solar [ / ] in CSWA 19 and CSWA 164 likewise suggests moderate depletion e.g., howk1999a , although it could also reflect an odd-even nucleosynthetic effect.
CSWA 19 We adopt the redshift of CSWA 19 measured from \ 1907,9, which is consistent with the Fe ii * 2365 fine structure emission line as well as photospheric absorption.

gmw_00004246.name: CSWA 128
gmw_00004246.overview: CSWA 128 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy [arXiv:1805.01484]. Its systemic redshift has been measured using the O III emission feature [arXiv:1805.01484].

sources.summary: The paper discusses CSWA 128 as a lensed galaxy affected by blended absorption, with high-quality MMT coverage and a systemic redshift from O III.

quotes.quote: 1402 in CSWA 128 and 1260 in CSWA 39 are blended with strong features arising from lower redshift systems (Figure fig:intervening ), and we therefore do not use these in subsequent analysis.
Additionally CSWA 128 has a high quality MMT spectrum reaching the necessary blue wavelengths described by stark2013 .

CSWA 128 We adopt the redshift of CSWA 128 measured from O iii ] 1661,6, which is the only feature for which we are able to measure a reliable systemic redshift although the significance of the fit is modest (7 ).

gmw_00004247.name: CSWA 164
gmw_00004247.overview: CSWA 164 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1805.01484] that exhibits star formation [arXiv:1805.01484]. Its systemic redshift is measured from photospheric absorption lines [arXiv:1805.01484], and it displays sub-solar abundance ratios that suggest moderate depletion [arXiv:1805.01484].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CSWA 164 with multi-transition absorption fitting, H I constraints, subsolar abundance ratios, and a systemic redshift from photospheric absorption.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:c164_si2 shows an example of this method applied to \ in CSWA 164.
Sub-solar [ / ] in CSWA 19 and CSWA 164 likewise suggests moderate depletion e.g., howk1999a , although it could also reflect an odd-even nucleosynthetic effect.
CSWA 164 We measure the redshift of CSWA 164 from a combined fit to the photospheric absorption lines Si iii 1294, Si iii 1417, S v 1501, N iv 1718, and C iii 2297.

gmw_00004248.name: UGC 5889
gmw_00004248.overview: UGC 5889 is a gas-rich, low-surface-brightness dwarf spiral galaxy located in the Leo I group [arXiv:astro-ph/0502357, arXiv:astro-ph/0107100]. It is a companion to the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 3377 and exhibits a very quiet, low rate of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0502357, arXiv:astro-ph/0107100].

sources.summary: The paper includes UGC 5889 in its MUSE sample and says its low number of points and observational limitations obscure several resolved correlations.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

Figure fig:SFR-sigma_9 shows an overall correlation between _ SFR _ * . Observational limitations do not allow for decisive conclusions in the cases of ESO 59-01, UGC 5889 and UGC 8041. Moreover, the different behaviours within NGC 4592, observed qualitatively in Fig. fig:amazing , lead to significant scatter and no statistical evidence for an overall correlation in this galaxy. It can be seen that the trends are not the same for all galaxies. NGC 1796, NGC 4592, UGC 8041 appear to have the tightest correlations in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper studies UGC 5889 as a resolved low-surface-brightness dwarf, deriving its distance, stellar photometry, gas-rich quiet nature, and recent star-formation history from HST color-magnitude diagrams.

quotes.quote: In this paper we focus on the study of a resolved LSB galaxy: UGC 5889 (NGC 3377A). This dwarf galaxy is a member of the Leo I group. Due to its similar redshift and resulting proximity -- only 20 Kpc in projected distance (Sandage Hoffman, ), it is thought to be a companion of the large elliptical NGC 3377. With M _ H_I / L _B 0.3 M _ / L _ , it is gas rich (Knezek et al. ). It is classified as a low surface brightness dwarf having an average surface brightness inside the Holmberg radius of B=23.97 mag arcsec ^ 2 .

Thus, despite the dense environment where it is located, UGC 5889 appears to be a very quiet dwarf galaxy. The star formation history of UGC 5889 is unknown. This paper deals with the determination of the SFR and the SFR history from the analysis of colour--magnitude diagrams (CMDs).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3377A as a low-surface-brightness dwarf spiral companion candidate where 83 young star groupings trace star formation despite a very low star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377A NGC 3377A is an almost face-on dwarf spiral galaxy, about 7 north-west from the E6 giant elliptical NGC 3377, in the Leo I (M96) group. The radial velocity difference of 120 km/s (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991, hereafter RC3) between these two galaxies suggests that NGC 3377A may be a companion of the giant elliptical. According to Tonry et al. (1997), NGC 3377A is about 10.7 Mpc distant, implying a linear separation from NGC 3377 of only 20 kpc.

Sandage et al. (1991) and Knezek et al. (1999) showed that this very low surface brightness galaxy has various peculiarities. In particular, in spite of its high M _ HII /L _B ratio ( 0.30 in solar units, which is a typical value for a Sc type spiral), it has a very low star formation rate, about 0.003 M _ /yr, so that star formation can continue for more than a Hubble time (Knezek et al. 1999). The application of our algorithm to this galaxy led us to the identification of 83 YSGs, whose positions in the galaxy are shown in Fig. 1 and whose main characteristic parameters are given in Table 2.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5889 as having the weakest sample-wide star formation and an NSC dominated by old low-metallicity stars, implying GC-accretion growth.

quotes.quote: Out of the galaxies in our sample, UGC 5889 shows the least amount of star formation across the MUSE field of view and its NSC is located in a region with a very low SFR density. Furthermore, the NSC spectrum shows weak emission lines and the kinematic analysis revealed an non-significant offset of v = 9.1 3.1 km s ^ 1 . The NSC SFH shows predominantly old ages at low metallicity. This component constitutes 91 of the mass, but also a minor young and metal-rich component is present. Hence, we conclude that this NSC was formed predominantly through the GC accretion channel.

gmw_00004249.name: SPT-CL J2106-5844 BCG
gmw_00004249.overview: The SPT-CL J2106-5844 BCG is a highly luminous brightest cluster galaxy that coincides with its cluster's northwestern mass and X-ray peak [arXiv:1910.04775]. It hosts a compact radio core associated with an active galactic nucleus, a possible bent radio jet, and a tentative dust component [arXiv:2101.02216].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SPT-CL J2106-5844 BCG as the galaxy associated with an extended radio complex, compact radio core, possible bent AGN jet, and tentative dust component.

quotes.quote: The radio observations provide evidence for an extended radio complex, with an overall size of 400 kpc and dominated by a large radio galaxy associated with the BCG. The overall morphology of the signal is observed to consist of an extended radio structure covering roughly 500 kpc around a compact radio core that dominates the flux density and is coincident with the BCG. The morphology of the radio emission around the BCG suggests that it may be associated with a radio jet from the central AGN with mildly bent arms Sakelliou2000 .

Unresolved source The fitting algorithm further identifies an unresolved (point-like) positive source in the direction of SPT-CL J2106-5844, whose position is coincident with the bright compact component dominating the low-frequency radio emission from the cluster and consistent with the coordinates reported by Song2012 for the BCG. We measure a positive curvature of the spectrum towards high frequencies (see Fig. fig:indexc ), suggesting the presence of a dust component within the cluster central galaxy (see Fogarty2019 or Romero2020 for examples of clusters with a dusty BCG).

sources.summary: The paper identifies SPT2106’s BCG by coordinates, includes it despite extreme luminosity, and links it to the northwestern mass and X-ray peak.

quotes.quote: The northwestern substructure coincides with the BCG and X-ray peak while the southeastern one agrees with the location of the number density peak.

The luminosity and number density maps are made by selecting cluster member candidates, which meet the following criteria: 24 < F606W < 26 and 1.4 < F606W F814W < 2.2 (see also Figure  fig:cmd ); the brightest cluster galaxy BCG; Song2012 at (RA,DEC)=(21:06:04.66,-58:44:28.32) is manually included (not initially selected by the criteria because of its extreme luminosity F606W  22 ).
The northwestern mass peak position agrees with that of the X-ray peak, which also coincides with the BCG whereas the southeastern peak seems to be associated with the number density peak.

gmw_00004250.name: RX J1131-1231
gmw_00004250.overview: RX J1131-1231 is a quadruply lensed quasar system with a source redshift of 0.658 [arXiv:1506.02097, arXiv:2105.08690]. The foreground lensing galaxy is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.295 [arXiv:1506.02097].

sources.summary: The paper applies an elliptical power-law lens model to RX J1131 1231, estimating the lens galaxy redshift, axis ratio, Einstein radius, slope, and time-delay behavior.

quotes.quote: In this section, we apply our formalism to the quadruple lens RX J1131 1231. The lensing galaxy is an elliptical at redshift 0.295 and the source redshift is 0.658. The large separation ( 3 arcsec) makes study of the system relatively simple (see Cla06 ).

Indeed the galaxy and images fall approximately on the curve, with the maximum distance from the hyperbola being about 0.025 arcsec. This should be compared with the positional accuracy of about a few mas (see Table tab:RXJ ). Our simple model appears to provide a reasonable approximation.

For the case of RX J1131 1231, we obtain B=2.65 which corresponds to =1.14 . Using eqs. ( eq:iso ) and ( eq:deltatr2 ) we have 1 + q^B 1 - q^B C B with C 1 2 T _ ^2 _ i=1 ^n r_i^2 = 3.99 which depends entirely on the observed image positions and time delays of a quadruple lens. Finally we can deduce the axis ratio by q C - B C + B ^ 1 / B = 0.72 This value differs by 5 of the previous derived value ( q 0.69 ), and thus rather robust. For the Einstein radius we deduce _E = 1.61^ which differs about 3 from the previous model ( _E=1.57^ ).

sources.summary: The paper models RX J1131-1231 as a quadruply lensed quasar, comparing Gaia magnitude anomalies in images C and D with worst-case microlensing excursions.

quotes.quote: Quad Lens RX J1131-1231 The quad RX J1131-1231 was the thirteenth and last ranked GraL system described in 1. We have used Keeton's lensmodel program ( 2001astro.ph..2340K ) to model HST positions for the four images as a singular isothermal sphere with external shear. Minimizing the RMS residuals of Gaia magnitudes from the model predictions, we find that image C, the faintest of the cusp images, is 0.34 magnitudes fainter than predicted. This is only 1/4 of the _ - excursion shown in Figure fig:delta++_delta-- . Image D, isolated on the far side of the lens, is 1.20 magnitudes brighter than predicted.

gmw_00004251.name: SPT0103-45
gmw_00004251.overview: SPT0103-45 is a strongly lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy that exhibits a ring-shaped continuum morphology [arXiv:2109.03450]. The galaxy displays a smooth velocity gradient across its structure, which supports a scenario where it is a rotating disk [arXiv:2109.03450].

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT0103-45 as a strongly lensed dusty star-forming galaxy with ring-like continuum morphology, counter-images, velocity gradients, and source-plane evidence for a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: SPT0103-45 is ring-shaped, which is characteristic of strongly lensed sources Spilker16 . The brightest region is located in the northwest (henceforth, the gem ) and another bright region can be seen in the southeast. They correspond to two counter-images of the same region of the source (see Sect. sec: lens_modelling ).

We can see a smooth velocity gradient across the source, consistent for both the lines. Emission at blueshifted velocities is close to the inner diamond caustic, while the most redshifted emission is near the outer caustic. The results thus favor a scenario in which SPT0103-45 is a rotating disk.

gmw_00004252.name: SPT2357-51
gmw_00004252.overview: SPT2357-51 is a compact, strongly lensed dusty galaxy [arXiv:2109.03450]. Its disturbed source-plane kinematics and an offset between the continuum and the main gas emission region suggest a possible merger [arXiv:2109.03450].

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT2357-51 as a compact strongly lensed dusty galaxy whose line profiles, disturbed source-plane kinematics, and gas-continuum offset favor a possible merger.

quotes.quote: SPT2357-51 is also gravitationally lensed and has a very compact continuum emission compared to the other sources. The compact emission is due to the small Einstein radius of the foreground lens galaxy ( _ E 0.2^ , Spilker16 ). The resolved structure of SPT2357-51 is difficult to see visually in our default continuum image using all the line-free channels and a Briggs weighting with a robustness parameter of 0.5.

The geometry of this source is more disturbed than the two other sources. While, there is a velocity gradient for the velocities corresponding to the blue component, the geometry associated to the red component ( > 0 km s ^ 1 ) is much more disturbed. The kinematics from our source-plane reconstruction are only marginally consistent with the expectation for rotation, though it is hard to constrain the emission region for the red component because the signal is emitted very close to caustics. Similar to the image-plane kinematic analysis (Sect. sec:kinematics 2357 ), we also find that the continuum is offset from the main region of gas emission. Our source-plane reconstruction thus favors the scenario of a disturbed kinematics possibly linked to a merger.

gmw_00004253.name: NGC 5829
gmw_00004253.overview: NGC 5829 is a gas-rich, late-type spiral galaxy classified as a grand-design system [arXiv:1904.03150]. Located at a redshift-based distance of approximately 78 Mpc, it features two non-identical spiral arms and a roughly constant metallicity in its outer regions [arXiv:1904.03150].

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 42 as NGC 5829, a gas-rich late-type grand-design galaxy with asymmetric arms, bright peripheral regions, and roughly constant outer metallicity.

quotes.quote: Arp 42 is the poorly studied system, consisting of Sc-type galaxy NGC 5829 and the irregular-looking IC4526 observed at the projected distance of 2.3 , or about three times of its optical radius R_ 25 = 0.7 . Indeed, the systemic velocities V_ GSR of NGC 5829 and IC 4526 are 5759 and 13732 respectively HYPERLEDA http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ , Makarov2014 , evidencing that these galaxies are not interacting ones. Since there are no other objects of comparable brightness in the vicinity, hereafter we will consider Arp 42 as a single object NGC 5829, assuming its redshift-based distance to be 78 Mpc.
It is a gas-rich late-type galaxy. Its total mass of neutral hydrogen is about 2 10^ 10 (Hyperleda; see also Casasola2004 ). NGC 5829 is a Grand Design-type galaxy, its two arms look non-identical.

For the second cut, which crosses the periphery of NGC 5829, the metallicity remains roughly constant 12+ (O/H) 8.4 . Oxygen abundance does not show any change when the slit passes through the BER1 and BER2.

gmw_00004254.name: NGC 2535
gmw_00004254.overview: NGC 2535 is a peculiar spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0109231] that features a bright, eyelid-shaped oval of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0610421] and a long tidal tail [arXiv:1904.03150, arXiv:astro-ph/0109231]. It is embedded in an extended gaseous disk [arXiv:1904.03150] and has been classified as both an H II galaxy and a LINER [arXiv:astro-ph/0610421].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2535 as the larger Arp 82 galaxy, with H II/LINER classifications, an ocular star-forming oval, strong line emission, and high star formation.
