quotes.quote: Based on optical spectroscopy, NGC 2535 and 2536 have been classified as both H II galaxies kee85 or as a Low Ionization Emission Line Region (LINER) and an H II galaxy respectively dah85 . NGC 2535, has a bright oval of star formation shaped like an eyelid. This type of ocular structure has been observed in other galaxies, e.g. IC 2163 and NGC 2207 elm06 , and is presumably the result of large-scale gaseous shocks from a grazing prograde encounter.

Clump 13, in the very center of NGC 2535, is clearly visible in the IRAC and MIPS images. It is also visible in the , R, and NUV images, however, it almost disappears in FUV. This could be the result of reddening. The L(IR)/L(FUV) ratio (see 4.2) is a measure of the total UV opacity. L(IR)/L(FUV) is greatest for knot 13, consistent with a large reddening.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2535 as the main Arp 82 spiral, with a tidal tail, extended gaseous disk, regular rotation, and a smooth bridge velocity field.

quotes.quote: Arp 82=VV 009 is the system of M51-type. It consists of a luminous spiral galaxy with the well defined two-armed spiral structure (NGC 2535), one arm of which connects or overlaps with the close satellite (NGC 2536). In addition, NGC 2535 possesses a long tidal tail as the continuation of the north spiral arm at the opposite side from the satellite.

Both galaxies NGC 2535/36 in the system Arp 82 are embedded in the large envelope evidently associated with the main galaxy. The extended gaseous disc is too large to be the result of the observed interaction, and most probably it existed before the galaxies became close Kaufman1997 . The slit PA=147 also crosses the region of splitting of the spiral arm of NGC 2535 about half-way between galaxies, clearly visible in GALEX image (see Section sec:disc ).
Our observations confirm the regular rotation of NGC 2535 and a smooth velocity distribution along the bridge, connecting two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper gives image-based morphology notes for KPG156A, including classification, color information, structural features, and its role within a binary-galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: KPG156A . The galaxy is classified as SA() pec. Our sharp/filtered and (B-I) images show a bright central nucleus surrounded by two knotty arms forming an inner ring structure. The arms extend far from the center forming: 1) a bridge to its companion and 2) a very long tail. They may be tidally-generated by the interaction. In addition, faint filamentary structures are seen almost tangent to the ring.

gmw_00004255.name: NGC 2536
gmw_00004255.overview: NGC 2536 is a barred spiral satellite galaxy classified as SBbc pec [arXiv:astro-ph/0109231] that is interacting with the larger galaxy NGC 2535 [arXiv:1904.03150]. The galaxy exhibits strong star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0610421], non-circular gas motions [arXiv:1904.03150], and an older central stellar population [arXiv:1904.03150].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2536 as the Arp 82 satellite, with higher bridge-adjacent oxygen abundance, strong non-circular gas motion, and old central stellar populations.

quotes.quote: Arp 82=VV 009 is the system of M51-type. It consists of a luminous spiral galaxy with the well defined two-armed spiral structure (NGC 2535), one arm of which connects or overlaps with the close satellite (NGC 2536). Both galaxies NGC 2535/36 in the system Arp 82 are embedded in the large envelope evidently associated with the main galaxy. Similarly found stellar mass for NGC 2536 is 8.7 10^9M_ .

In contrast, there is a strong non-circular gas motion in the satellite galaxy -- both in the central part and in the spiral arm adjacent to the bridge. The same is correct for the central region of NGC 2536 which definitely has a large age. As it follows from the abundance profile for PA=147 there is a jump of (O/H) at about 0.2 dex between the bridge and the off-center regions of the main body of a small galaxy NGC 2536 (the abundance of gas in the satellite is higher than in the adjoining regions closer to the main galaxy).
The same conclusion follows from the spectral cut at PA=342 : the oxygen abundance in the satellite is higher than in the neighbour extended emission "island" located closer to the main galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper gives image-based morphology notes for KPG156B, including classification, color information, structural features, and its role within a binary-galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: KPG156B . The galaxy is classified as SB(rs) pec. However, in our sharp/filtered and (B-I) images no clear ringed structure is appreciated. We notice instead, a bright and somehow elongated nuclear region with two diffuse and opened spiral arms resembling a integral sign. We classify this galaxy as SBbc pec. The estimated (B-V)_ T ^ 0 colour is representative of Sbc-Sc types.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2536 as Arp 82's smaller companion, with H I gas, H II classification, greater reddening, young clumps, and strong star formation.

quotes.quote: The total H I mass of Arp 82 is 2.3 10^ 10 , of which 2.3 10^ 9 \ is associated with NGC 2536 (the smaller companion) kau97 , typical of Low Surface Brightness (LSB) galaxies in the sample of one04 .

Figure 15 plots the ages of the clumps against distance from NGC 2536. The symbols are the same as before, with the blue symbols being the broadband ages and the red symbols are the ages. The mean broadband ages of NGC 2536, the bridge region, the spiral region, and the tail region are 30 Myr, 5 Myr, 15 Myr and 8 Myr respectively. The mean \ ages of NGC 2536, the bridge region, the spiral region, and the tail region are 9 Myr, 11 Myr, 9 Myr, and 13 Myr, respectively.
From this figure it can be seen that the SFR is greatest in the spiral region of NGC 2535 and in NGC 2536, with much less star formation in the bridge and tail regions.

gmw_00004256.name: UGC 4457
gmw_00004256.overview: UGC 4457 is a highly luminous, mildly inclined Sc-type spiral galaxy located approximately 147 Mpc away [arXiv:1904.03150]. It is part of an M51-type interacting system, featuring a companion satellite at the end of its southeastern spiral arm and a thin, curved tidal tail extending from the opposite arm [arXiv:1904.03150].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 4457 as the luminous main Arp 58 galaxy, with M51-type morphology, a tidal tail, regular rotation, and disturbed companion-side gas motions.

quotes.quote: Arp 58 = UGC 4457 = VV 413 also belongs to the M51-type systems. Its main component is the Sc-type two-arm mildly inclined galaxy. The satellite is observed at the end of the SE-spiral arm at a distance of about one optical diameter D_ 25 1 arcmin.

The second spiral arm at the opposite side from the satellite has a faint extension which forms a thin curved tidal tail. Systemic velocity of the main galaxy is close to 11000 , and the distance adopted in this paper is 147 Mpc. UGC 4457 is very luminous galaxy: its corrected B- magnitude is B_ tc = 14.24 (HYPERLEDA database), which corresponds to luminosity 6.6 10^ 10 L for the adopted distance.

A symmetric gas velocity profile of UGC 4457 allows to restore its rotation velocity curve. Unlike UGC 4457, the satellite does not reveal a regular rotation: its half faced to the main galaxy demonstrate a zero LOS velocity gradient which is evidently the result of gravitational interaction.

gmw_00004257.name: SDSS J022248.95-045914.9
gmw_00004257.overview: SDSS J022248.95-045914.9 is a massive red central galaxy with a stellar mass reaching 10^11.6 solar masses [arXiv:2012.08798]. Although its high mass and quenched star formation are typical of elliptical galaxies, morphological analysis reveals that it is actually a disk galaxy [arXiv:2012.08798].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J022248.95-045914.9 as a massive quenched red central disk galaxy whose low-Sersic morphology challenges formation models.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report a massive red central disk galaxy SDSS J022248.95-045914.9 with stellar mass reaching 10^ 11.6 M_ . The galaxy was discovered during our study of physical properties of massive galaxies in the CMASS sample of the Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey (BOSS) survey in the XMM-LSS region . We perform Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) fitting on multi-band data of the galaxy from FUV to K_s collected by Moutard et al. using Le PHARE .

From both the photometry and the spectrum, we can conclude that the galaxy is very massive and its star formation has been quenched. These are the typical features of a massive elliptical galaxy. However, our analysis of the morphology for the galaxy surprisingly shows that it is a disk galaxy.

gmw_00004258.name: Swift  J1644 + 5734
gmw_00004258.overview: The host galaxy of Swift J1644+5734 is a compact, spheroid-dominated galaxy [arXiv:1509.08945] located at a redshift of z=0.354 [arXiv:1605.06461]. It is a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1605.06461] with a bulge-dominant structure [arXiv:1507.08843] and a stellar population dominated by relatively old stars [arXiv:1509.08945].

sources.summary: The paper describes the host galaxy of Swift J1644+57 as bulge-dominant, derives late-time host fluxes for SED fitting, measures stellar population properties, and estimates the mass of its central black hole.

quotes.quote: We study the host galaxy properties of the tidal disruption object, Swift J164449.3+573451 using long-term optical to near-infrared (NIR) data. First, we decompose the galaxy surface brightness distribution and analyze the morphology of the host galaxy using high resolution HST WFC3 images. We conclude that the host galaxy is a bulge-dominant galaxy that is well described by a single S e rsic model with S e rsic index n=3.43 0.05 . Adding a disk component, the bulge to total host galaxy flux ratio (B/T) is 0.83 0.03 , which still indicates a bulge-dominant galaxy.

Our long-term NIR light curves reveal the pure host galaxy fluxes 500 days after the burst. We fit spectral energy distribution (SED) models to the multi-band fluxes from the optical to NIR of the host galaxy and determine its properties. The stellar mass, the star formation rate, and the age of stellar population are (M_ /M_ ) = 9.14^ +0.13 _ 0.10 , 0.03^ +0.28 _ 0.03 M_ /yr, and 0.63^ +0.95 _ 0.43 Gyr. Finally, we estimate the mass of the central super massive black hole which is responsible for the tidal disruption event.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the host as a compact, spheroid-dominated galaxy detected in many bands, with old stellar light, younger emission lines, and a measured stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The galaxy is detected in 12 photometric bands from 0.45--4.5 microns (see Table host_tab ), with limits at both shorter and longer wavelengths (although in practice these limits are not yet constraining). From this we can derive the physical properties of the host galaxy based on template fitting to the available spectral energy distribution (SED) shown in Figure galsed . Considering the Binary Population and Spectral Synthesis (BPASS) library of models eldridge09 , we find the SED to be well reproduced by a relatively old dominant stellar population (age = 3.2 10^9 years), although the emission lines clearly indicate the presence of a younger population as well (Fig. galsed ). Importantly, the fitting also provides a much more robust determination of the stellar mass than was previously possible, since earlier attempts were significantly contaminated at red wavelengths by transient light. Specifically we find a stellar mass of M_* = 5.5 10^9 M _ .

Despite its luminosity appearing very similar to the LMC ( M_B 18 ), the morphological properties of the host of Swift J1644+57 are rather different. The core of the galaxy is barely resolved by the HST IR observations, although is reasonably resolved in the optical. The galaxy has little ellipticity e 0.1 and is very concentrated, with R_ 20,50,80 =0.077,0.184,0.388 = 0.39,0.92,1.95 kpc at z=0.354 .

sources.summary: The paper studies Swift J1644+5734 as a jetted tidal-disruption source in a star-forming galaxy, finding no VLBI proper motion and limiting the apparent jet speed.

quotes.quote: The source Swift  J1644 + 5734 is the first-known transient that has extremely luminous non-thermal emission from radio to ray wavelengths bur11, blo11, zau11 after a star was disrupted by the gravity of a supermassive black hole see a recent review by kom15 . The tidal disruption event (TDE) took place in a star-forming galaxy at z=0.354 lev11 . The evolution of the X-ray light curve up to 500 days roughly followed a t^ 5/3 power law decline as expected for the fallback rate of tidally disrupted material e.g. lod09 .

The complex behaviour of the radio lightcurves is indicative of a forward shock driven by a jet with a fast core plus slow sheath structure mim15 . If the non-thermal emission came from a highly anisotropic component, such as a relativistic jet formed in Swift  J1644 + 5734, the high luminosity up to 10^ 48  erg s ^ 1 in X-rays could be naturally explained as a result of strong beaming effects e.g. bur11 . The existence of a relativistic jet in Swift  J1644 + 5734 may be confirmed directly via measuring its apparent jet speed.

Interestingly, there was no apparent superluminal motion observed in Swift  J1644 + 5734 during our EVN observations. According to our VLBI proper motion measurements and the redshift of Swift  J1644 + 5734, the apparent jet speed corrected for time dilation was _ app =(1+z)d_ a _ t =0.16 0.09c , where is the light speed and the angular-diameter distance d_ a =1006  Mpc at z=0.354 with the cosmological parameters H_0=70  km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m =0.28 , _ =0.72 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Swift J164449.3+573451 as a MAXI-detected TDE example and candidate, measuring its redshift, X-ray luminosity, variability amplitude, peak timing, and decay slope.

quotes.quote: As an example, Figure detection_ex shows the MAXI /GSC significance map around Swift J164449.3+573451 (hereafter Swift J1644+57), a transient source detected by this method. This object is significantly detected in the data of 30 days during the outburst (left panel), while it is not in the 37-month data (right panel).

On the basis of positional coincidence, we identify 3 TDE black candidates in the MAXI transient source catalog from the literature, Swift J1112.2-8238 (hereafter Swift J1112-82; ), Swift J1644+57 Bur11 , and Swift J2058.4+0516 (hereafter Swift J2058+05; ). They are located at z= 0.89 , z= 0.354 , and z= 1.1853 , and the (isotropic) luminosities in the 4--10 keV band are estimated to be 10 ^ 47.1 erg s ^ 1 , 10 ^ 46.6 erg s ^ 1 , and 10 ^ 47.5 erg s ^ 1 , respectively.

The second characteristic is that the decay light curves are consistent with a power-law profile of t^ 5/3 , where t is time since the onset time of each TDE. We note that the time of the flux peak, t_ p , is delayed from the TDE onset time by approximately 80 days for NGC 4845, 20 days for Swift J1644+57 and Swift J2058+05, and 5 days for Swift J1112-82. Hence, we set the central day of the bin showing the highest flux as t_ p , and estimate the TDE onset time by correcting for these offsets. We confirm that the 10-days light curves in the 3--10 keV band follow power-law profiles, as shown in Figure tde_lc . A power-law fit to the light curve over 90 days after the peak flux is found to be acceptable in terms of a ^2 test, yielding the best-fit index of 1.86^ +0.45 _ 1.55 , 1.94^ +0.39 _ 0.48 , 1.92^ +0.58 _ 0.73 , and 2.00^ +1.14 _ 1.25 for Swift J1112-82, Swift J1644+57, Swift J2058+05, and NGC 4845, respectively.

gmw_00004259.name: NGC 2663
gmw_00004259.overview: NGC 2663 is a nearby elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2207.06713] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1610.01174, arXiv:2207.06713] and highly-collimated radio jets spanning 355 kpc [arXiv:2207.06713]. It is situated in a rarefied X-ray environment [arXiv:2207.06713], and its circumnuclear region shows no signs of stellar rotation [arXiv:1610.01174].

sources.summary: The paper reports 355 kpc radio jets around the nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 2663, describing a rarefied X-ray environment and possible southern-jet recollimation.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present the discovery of highly-collimated radio jets spanning a total of 355 kpc around the nearby elliptical galaxy , and the possible first detection of recollimation on kiloparsec scales. The small distance to the galaxy ( 28.5 Mpc) allows us to resolve portions of the jets to examine their structure.

In addition, for the fit parameters of the mekal model, the elemental abundances were frozen to solar values and the redshift was frozen to our adopted redshift value for NGC 2663 (that is, z=0.00701 ).

In summary, we found that: Radio observations with ASKAP , MWA and ATCA reveal two oppositely directed radio jets that span 355 kpc around the otherwise typical red-and-dead elliptical galaxy . X-ray observations with eROSITA, \ and \ show that \ is located in a rarefied environment, with no diffuse emission. The jet is remarkably linear over its entire length, which is also consistent with a low density environment on Mpc scales. The southern jet shows at least one possible example of narrowing and simultaneous brightening of the jet. This is possibly indicative of recollimation of the jet by external, environmental pressure.

sources.summary: The paper reports no stellar rotation in NGC 2663, locates its nucleus using broad H emission, finds no velocity-dispersion peak, and treats its dispersion map as complex.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1399, NGC 2663 and NGC 3136, where we did not detect any stellar structure in rotation, the heliocentric radial velocities shown in Table tab_mapa_vel_radial_stellar were extracted from the position of the nuclei of these galaxies. In NGC 2663, we set the position of the nucleus by the image of the red wing of the broad component of H (see paper II). This component is emitted by the broad line region (BLR), which is directly associated with AGNs in galaxies.

We do not see any evidence of peaks in the velocity dispersion maps of NGC 1380, NGC 2663, and NGC 3136. In NGC 1404, we detected a sigma-drop in the central region. This feature has already been reported by 1998A&AS..133..325G . We will revisit the sigma-drop detected in NGC 1404 in Section ngc1404_case . The nuclear peaks detected in ESO 208 G-21, IC 1459, IC 5181, and NGC 4546 should correspond, in part, to unresolved stellar rotation. This interpretation is not new at all (see e.g., 1987IAUS..127...17K,1988ApJ...327L..55F ). First, the peaks of velocity dispersion agree, within the errors, with the positions of the kinematic centre and also with the positions of the AGNs of these four objects. Also, note that in IC 1459, IC 5181 and NGC 4546, the shape of the peaks are elongated perpendicularly to the direction of the rotation. The 1D profiles confirm this statement, since the velocity dispersion decreases slowly along this direction. A possible interpretation for this characteristic is that it is caused by a stellar structure in rotation. Along the plane of rotation, the sigma peak has a width limited by the PSF. On the perpendicular direction it may not be limited by the PSF as the size of the structure may go beyond it. In NGC 7097, the elongated structure detected in the velocity dispersion map is a possible consequence of the complexity of the stellar kinematics discussed in Section stellar_radial_velocities . Also the elongation detected in IC 1459, IC 5181, and NGC 4546 may be the result of a more complex setup of their stellar kinematics. For NGC 1380, one would expect a peaky structure, on the basis of the radial velocity map, somewhat similar to those of ESO 208-G21, IC 5181, IC 1459 or NGC 4546. Instead, for unknown reasons, it shows an irregular structure with a tendency to increase towards the centre. 2007ApJ...671.1321G , using data from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) , analysed the stellar kinematics of NGC 1399, where we detected a nuclear peak, but no sign of stellar rotation. They showed that the stellar orbits are predominantly tangential near the SMBH. However, our data do not have enough spatial resolution to detect the decrease of the observed velocity dispersion that results from this anisotropy in the nuclear region. Circumnuclear velocity dispersion of the sample galaxies To obtain a representative value of the circumnuclear velocity dispersion of the sample galaxies, we calculated _ * within a radius R_ * as _ * _ i=1 ^ N_p I_i _i^2+V_i^2 _ i=1 ^ N_p I_i , where I_i is the intensity of the stellar continuum in the spaxel i and N_p is the total number of spaxels within R_ * . The values of _ * and R_ * are shown in Table tab_disp_vel_stellar . The statistical errors for _ * were estimated with a MC simulation and are 1 km s ^ 1 . Maps of the Gauss Hermite h_3 moment The maps of the h_3 moment are shown in Fig. cinematica_estelar_1 . The profiles extracted along and perpendicularly to the directions of the stellar rotations detected in the radial velocity maps are shown in Fig. perfil_stellar_kin_1 . The most remarkable feature of the h_3 maps is the anti-correlation between this parameter and the radial velocities in the galaxies where we detected stellar structures in rotation. LOSVDs with signatures of asymmetry are related to rotating stellar structures embedded in the spheroidal structure, which is common for some E galaxies 1994MNRAS.270..325C,2010gfe..book.....M . These asymmetric profiles have already been detected for IC 1459 by 1988ApJ...327L..55F . Indeed, these authors interpreted this result as a kinematically cold disclike component that is embedded in a kinematically hot structure, although they described the LOSVD profile as a sum of two Gaussian functions. In NGC 1404, the anti-correlation between h_3 and the radial velocity is probably a consequence of the KDC also being embedded in the spheroidal structure of this object. Comparison of the GMOS-IFU results with other data sets Comparison with long-slit data In order to check the consistency of our results, we compared the stellar kinematics extracted from the GMOS data cubes with other data sets presented in the literature. The kinematics of the galaxy IC 1459 were previously analysed by 2002ApJ...578..787C using long-slit data from the HST and from ground-based spectroscopy. In Fig. perfil_comparacao_ic1459 , we show 1D profiles of the radial velocity, velocity dispersion and h_3 moment (only ground-based results) along the major-axis of IC 1459 extracted from the GMOS data cube and from both long-slit spectrographs. One may see that the results from GMOS seem to be consistent with the data presented by 2002ApJ...578..787C . The differences are probably caused by the fact that these measurements have distinct spatial resolutions. For NGC 1399 and NGC 1404, we obtained ground-based long-slit data from 1998A&AS..133..325G along the major-axis of these objects. Fig. perfil_comp_fornax shows the 1D profiles of the kinematic parameters extracted from GMOS and from the long-slit data. Again, one may see a consistency between the GMOS and long-slit measurements. Moreover, the GMOS results of both galaxies match the stellar kinematics IFU data presented by 2014MNRAS.441..274S . A direct comparison between Fig. perfil_comp_fornax and fig. A2 from 2014MNRAS.441..274S confirms this statement. It is worth mentioning that both long-slit data and the IFU data from 2014MNRAS.441..274S do not have enough spatial resolution to resolve the KDC in NGC 1404. In fact, the differences between all results seem to be caused by distinct spatial resolutions among the data sets. Comparison with SAURON data cubes The galaxy NGC 4546 is also part of the SAURON sample and of the ATLAS 3D project. So, we decided to analyse their data cube of this object 2011MNRAS.413..813C The data cube was downloaded from the ATLAS 3D project website at http://www-astro.physics.ox.ac.uk/atlas3d/ mainly to compare the circumnuclear velocity dispersion measurements. We built kinematic maps for this data cube using exactly the same procedures described in Section ppxf_meth . We assured that the kinematic centres of both data cubes have the same radial velocity. In Fig. perfil_comparacao_atlas , we compare the 1D profile of the kinematic maps taken from the GMOS data cubes and from the SAURON data cubes along the direction of the stellar rotations. We used the same PA from Table tab_mapa_vel_radial_stellar for this extraction. Again, the differences that are seen in the 1D profiles are probably caused by the fact that the data sets do not have the same spatial resolution. Indeed, the 1D profile of the velocity dispersion shows that both measurements agree for > 1.0 arcsec, where the effects of the seeing of the GMOS data cube are less pronounced. Also the h_3 results seem to reasonably match each other. We may also compare if the values measured for _ * agree for both data cubes. For the SAURON data cube, we found _ * = 241 12 km s ^ 1 (assuming that the systematic error for the SAURON data cubes is 5 , as stated by 2006MNRAS.366.1126C ), while we found _ * = 254 1 km s ^ 1 using the GMOS data cube. Although both measurements agree within the errors, we explore some possibilities to explain this difference between them: since the spatial sampling of data cubes from the ATLAS 3D project is 0.8 arcsec, we resampled the GMOS data cubes to the same spaxel size. We built kinematic maps using such a configuration and we obtained _ * = 253 1 km s ^ 1 . Also the spectral resolutions of both data cubes are different ( _ inst = 30 km s ^ 1 in = 5577 \ for this GMOS data cube and, from 2004MNRAS.352..721E , _ inst = 108 km s ^ 1 for the SAURON data cube). So, we modified the spectral resolution of the GMOS data cubes to _ inst = 108 km s ^ 1 , but maintaining the spaxels with sizes of 0.2 arcsec. With this configuration, _ * = 243 1 km s ^ 1 . Using spaxels with sizes of 0.8 arcsec and a spectral resolution _ inst = 108 km s ^ 1 for the GMOS data cubes, we calculated _ * = 257 1 km s ^ 1 . Although the values of _ * for the GMOS data cube with spaxels of 0.2 arcsec and _ inst = 108 km s ^ 1 and for the SAURON data cube match each other, the spectral resolution does not seem to be, alone, the cause of the systematic differences for the GMOS data cubes. The point here is that the kinematic maps for the GMOS data cubes with such a configuration is noisier than the same results with any other configuration. Moreover, the GMOS data cube with exactly the same instrumental configuration as the SAURON data cube provides _ * = 257 1 km s ^ 1 , which is almost the same result found for the original GMOS configuration. Another parameter is the seeing of the observation, but it does not seem to affect the measurements made with GMOS. We found _ * = 251 1 km s ^ 1 using the GMOS data cube in its original configuration, but with a FWHM of the PSF degraded to 1.4 arcsec (the original PSF has a FWHM = 0.71 arcsec). One possible source of systematic errors in the _ * measurements using GMOS data cubes is the wavelength range where ppxf is applied. To test this, we fitted each spectrum of the GMOS data cube of NGC 4546 with the same wavelength range as the SAURON data cube, which is from 4800 to 5250 . We found _ * = 243 1 km s ^ 1 . We also applied ppxf from 5250 to 6100 . For this setup, we obtained _ * = 223 1 km s ^ 1 . These differences between the values may come from the fact that GMOS observations made with the R831-G5322 grating is noisier in the blue region of the spectra. Also, the spectral resolution is constant in wavelength but not in velocity. It seems that, depending on the wavelength range, the values for the _ * parameter may vary by 10 , at least for NGC 4546. For IC 1459, for example, which was observed with the B600-G5323 grating (that provides a better response on the blue region of the spectra), we obtained _ * = 319 1 km s ^ 1 for the 4800 \ to 5250 \ range and 322 1 km s ^ 1 for the 5250 \ to 6100 . Both measurements are very close to _ * = 325 1 km s ^ 1 , obtained when we fitted all the wavelength range of the data cube. It is worth mentioning that only these two gratings were used in all sample galaxies (see paper I). A systematic investigation about this issue will be revisited in a future paper, but, for now, we suggest that the errors caused by applying ppxf to different wavelength ranges may be as large as 10 . NGC 1404 was classified as a fast rotator by 2014MNRAS.441..274S . They used an IFU with a spaxel of 1 arcsec ^2 and a FOV of 25 28 arcsec ^2 . By comparing our results with those obtained by 2014MNRAS.441..274S , we conclude that NGC 1404 has a KDC with an extension of 200 pc that is counter-rotating with respect to the external structure of the galaxy. Indeed, the presence of a decoupled structure in NGC 1404 was suggested by 2014MNRAS.441..274S due to the detection of two peaks in their velocity dispersion map. 2008IAUS..245..303G have also reported this counter-rotating KDC. 2006MNRAS.373..906M found KDCs with diameters of about one-hundred pc in some ETGs. These features were detected in fast rotating galaxies and are counter-rotating with respect to their external stellar components. According to these researchers, the host galaxies of these compact KDCs are among the youngest ETGs of the SAURON sample. Moreover, these KDCs are usually (but not always) younger than the overall population of their host galaxies. Although this result might suggest a link between counter-rotation and enhanced star formation, 2006MNRAS.373..906M showed that it is more likely to detect compact KDCs in young galaxies due to the effect of luminosity-weighting. By means of a simple model, they demonstrated that when a KDC of a galaxy gets older, its mass-to-light ratio increases and it becomes harder to distinguish the KDC from the background rotation field. The results of their model revealed that KDCs older than 5 Gyr are barely seen in the radial velocity maps. In other words, it is easier to detect a younger KDC, but they may have a broad range of ages. Indeed, 2006MNRAS.373..906M showed in their fig. 16 that, among six galaxies with KDCs with intrinsic sizes of 100 pc, in five the central luminosity-weighted ages of these objects are between 0.5 and 4 Gyr. Only one galaxy has such a compact structure with a luminosity-weighted age of 18 Gyr. Probably, NGC 1404 would be similar to these objects studied by 2006MNRAS.373..906M , since the estimated age of the central region of NGC 1404 is about 5 8 Gyr 2008IAUS..245..303G . We detected a drop in the velocity dispersion map of NGC 1404. Moreover, the velocity dispersion profile rises more steeply along the direction of the stellar rotations. This may be a consequence of the superposition of the two stellar components in counter-rotation. Indeed, 2007MNRAS.379..418C , using three-integral Schwarzschild models, showed that such a configuration predicts two peaks in the velocity dispersion along the rotation of both stellar components. This result for NGC 1404 resembles that of the 2 galaxies (those objects that shows two off-centred peaks in the velocity dispersion maps along the major axis of the galaxy), as defined by 2011MNRAS.414.2923K , although it is worth mentioning that the definition of such a class of objects given by these authors claims that the separation of the peaks of the maximum values of the velocity dispersion should be, at least, half of the effective radius. However, the data from the ATLAS 3D project, analysed by 2011MNRAS.414.2923K , does not have enough spatial resolution to detect velocity dispersion peaks with a separation of a few arcsec. Thus, a systematic study with seeing-limited data would be of great value in order to better understand objects with the same characteristics. Another hypothesis is that the drop is not related to the KDC. In this case, we may speculate the existence a star-forming region that is kinematically cold and dominates the stellar light within a projected radius smaller than the PSF of the data cube of this object ( 1 arcsec, see paper I). According to 2006MNRAS.369..853W , kinematically cold gas is able to form stars in the centre of barred late-type galaxies. They showed that a star formation rate of 1 M _ yr^ 1 is enough to maintain kinematically cold stars, and thus a drop. The reason is that, although there are mechanisms that make stars kinematically hot (e.g., gravitational disturbances), the nuclear region is fueled again with kinematically cold stars. However, NGC 1404 is a non-barred ETG. One possible scenario suggested by 2006MNRAS.369..853W is that the star formation rate is quite low, possibly undetectable. In this case, the drop may fade away, but over a long time-scale ( 100 Myr). Therefore, an in-depth study of the stellar populations of NGC 1404 is necessary to verify this hypothesis ( 2008IAUS..245..303G do not give much detail of their analysis of the stellar populations of NGC 1404). In addition, a detailed modelling of the stellar kinematics would be interesting, since it may confirm if this is either related to a kinematically cold component or associated with the KDC. Stellar kinematics may provide information about the formation history of galaxies. Fast and slow rotators, for example, have different formation paths 2014MNRAS.444.3357N . However, to assert a global characteristic for the stellar kinematics, data cubes with a FOV that englobes, at least, the region within one effective radius of the galaxies are necessary. The problem is that such data cubes have a spatial resolution that is limited by the size of the fibre (diameters of 2 arcsec). Observations using seeing-limited IFU data are, thus, very important in order to access information about the circumnuclear (scales of 100 pc) properties of the stellar kinematics. There are features that are only seen in such scales, as in the case of NGC 1404 [ but see also 2006MNRAS.373..906M ] . The point here is that an analysis with seeing-limited data cubes may provide complementary information about the stellar kinematics of a galaxy as a whole. Another interesting issue is that, with such data cubes, one may look for off-centred AGNs in a reliable way. Comparing the position of the kinematic centre with the position of the AGNs and also with the position of the photometric centre of the stellar structure may reveal that the SMBH is not always at the centre of a galaxy (see e.g. 2014ApJ...796L..13M ). Thus, the main subject of the present paper is to provide stellar kinematics results from seeing-limited data cubes for the sample galaxies. A more complete analysis in order to search for general characteristics of the stellar kinematics from the circumnuclear region of massive ETGs demands more data and is not possible with the present sample. Below we present the main findings of the present paper. We detected stellar structures in rotation embedded in a kinematically hot stellar structure (spheroid) in the circumnuclear region of seven galaxies of the sample. The other three objects do not seem to possess any sign of stellar rotation within a radius of 100 pc from the nucleus. The galaxy IC 5181 seems to have a small variation of the PA of the stellar structure in rotation with radius. This may be a consequence of a non-axisymmetric potential within the central region of the galaxy. It is worth mentioning that an evidence of a non-axisymmetric potential for this galaxy is also seen in the gas kinematics (paper III). In six galaxies, we detected a nuclear peak in the velocity dispersion maps. A fraction of this peak in four galaxies is likely to be associated with the stellar rotation in a region that is not fully resolved. In NGC 1380, NGC 2663, NGC 3136, and NGC 7097, the velocity dispersion maps are more complex and require a careful analysis for a reliable interpretation.

gmw_00004260.name: Fermi J1544-0649
gmw_00004260.overview: Fermi J1544-0649 is a low-power, "blue" BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1802.01105, arXiv:2001.11772] located within the host galaxy 2MASX J15441967-0649156 at a redshift of z=0.171 [arXiv:2001.11772]. The source features a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1802.01105] and has exhibited multiple X-ray and gamma-ray flaring episodes [arXiv:1802.01105, arXiv:2001.11772].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Fermi J1544-0649 as a radio-weak, low-power BL Lac with flat radio spectrum, borderline radio loudness, intermediate Ca break, and blazar-like SED.

quotes.quote: On May 15th (2017) Fermi /LAT detected a transient, not associated with any known gamma-ray source. Named as Fermi J1544-0649, it showed clear detection for two consecutive weeks, with a peak on May 21. It was reported by on June 11, together with a follow-up observation by Swift /XRT, measuring an enhancement in X-ray emission at a position corresponding to the optical transient detected on May 25 (ASASSN-17gs = https://wis-tns.weizmann.ac.il/object/2017egv AT2017egv ).

The spectral index ( , adopting the convention S= ^ ) calculated between 4.85 GHz and 14.60 GHz shows values >-0.5 for all epochs, suggesting an orientation of the jet near the line of sight, typical of blazars. This confirms the estimate at lower frequencies using pre-burst flux densities from the literature, giving an =-0.16 0.21 between 150 MHz (TGSS, 66.6 8.4 mJy) and 1.4 GHz (NVSS, 46.6 1.5 mJy).
Finally, the overall SED fitting, from radio to ray band, is typical of low-power, ``blue'' BL Lac.

sources.summary: The paper treats Fermi J1544-0649 as a newly awakened BL Lac object with GeV and X-ray flares, hard spectra, radio steadiness, and a z=0.171 host galaxy.

quotes.quote: Multi-wavelength observations of the BL Lac object Fermi J1544-0649: one year after its awakening label1 label1 MWL observations of Fermi J1544-0649

We report observations of a transient source \ from radio to . \ was discovered by the Fermi-LAT in May 2017. Follow-up Swift-XRT observations revealed three flaring episodes through March 2018, and the peak X-ray flux is about 10^3 higher than the ROSAT all-sky survey (RASS) flux upper limit. Optical spectral measurements taken by the Magellan 6.5-m telescope and the Lick-Shane telescope both show a largely featureless spectrum, strengthening the BL Lac interpretation first proposed by Bruni18 .

The host galaxy, 2MASX J15441967-0649156, was found to be at a spectroscopic Redshift of z=0.171, using the MDM 2.4m Hiltner telescope on the night of 2017 June 14 UT Chornock17 . The persistent radio source at the same position, NVSS J154419-064913, has a flux density of 46.6 mJy at 1.4 GHz in 1996/1997 NVSS_survey .

gmw_00004261.name: NGC 6877
gmw_00004261.overview: NGC 6877 is an elliptical companion galaxy to NGC 6876, the dominant elliptical galaxy in the Pavo group [arXiv:0810.3577]. The galaxy contains central nonstellar emission consistent with polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission and may be in an early premerger phase with its neighbor [arXiv:0810.3577].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6877 as NGC 6876's elliptical companion, detecting central nonstellar emission but no extra X-ray point sources, and noting a possible premerger phase.

quotes.quote: Nonstellar emission, consistent with PAH emission, is also found in the central region of elliptical galaxy NGC 6877, companion to the dominant Pavo group elliptical galaxy NGC 6876.
The companion elliptical galaxy, NGC 6877, ( 1 4 to the east with radial velocity difference of v_r 300 ) may be in an early premerger phase with NGC 6876.

Thus X-ray point sources, such as low-mass X-ray binaries that may be associated with NGC 6876 or NGC 6877 are difficult to distinguish from the elliptical galaxies' hot diffuse X-ray gas halos. We found no additional X-ray point sources associated with either NGC 6877 or NGC 6868.

gmw_00004262.name: NGC 6754
gmw_00004262.overview: NGC 6754 is a galaxy featuring transient spiral arms that rotate at a similar speed to its gas [arXiv:1610.00440]. The galaxy hosts hundreds of H II regions [arXiv:1511.01495] and exhibits gas radial migration [arXiv:1610.00440], though monitoring has revealed no UV or X-ray transient events [arXiv:1601.03739].

sources.summary: The paper uses VLT/MUSE data to map NGC 6754’s gas kinematics and abundances, finding streaming motions and radial migration tied to transient spiral structure.

quotes.quote: In this letter we analyse the gas content in NGC 6754 with VLT/MUSE data to characterise its 2D chemical composition and H line-of-sight velocity distribution. We find that the trailing (leading) edge of the NGC 6754 spiral arms show signatures of tangentially-slower, radially-outward (tangentially-faster, radially-inward) streaming motions of metal-rich (poor) gas over a large range of radii. These results show direct evidence of gas radial migration for the first time.

We compare our results with the gas behaviour in a N body disk simulation showing spiral morphological features rotating with a similar speed as the gas at every radius, in good agreement with the observed trend. This indicates that the spiral arm features in NGC 6754 may be transient and rotate similarly as the gas does at a large range of radii.

sources.summary: NGC 6754 had a complete HII-region census from the MUSE data, yielding 423 regions and prior measurements of a radial oxygen-abundance gradient, mixing scale, and possible azimuthal abundance variation.

quotes.quote: presented a complete census of the Hii regions in the galaxy NGC 6754 using the same observations presented here. These were used to derive the radial oxygen abundance gradient and estimate the typical mixing scale length. Evidence of an azimuthal variation in the oxygen abundance was found, which may be connected with radial migration.
After these cuts, the number of Hii regions that we are left with is: IC 1158 (292), NGC 6754 (423), ESO 362-18 (91), NGC 7742 (323), NGC 7469 (169), NGC 2906 (241).

sources.summary: The paper monitored NGC 6754 weekly with Swift from April to September 2014, collecting 27 XRT and UVOT epochs and finding no UV or X-ray transient events.

quotes.quote: NGC 6754 The weekly monitoring of the galaxy NGC 6754 ranged from the first week of April 2014 to the end of September 2014. Good data have been collected for 27 epochs by both the XRT and the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC6754 ). We found no transient events in the UVOT data, as well as in the XRT data.

gmw_00004263.name: source m1
gmw_00004263.overview: Source m1 is a broad-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of 1.5758 [arXiv:2211.04244]. It hosts a moderately accreting supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 10^7 solar masses [arXiv:2211.04244].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source m1 as a broad-line AGN in cluster XDCPJ0044.0-2033 and derives its X-ray luminosity, bolometric luminosity, black hole mass, and Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the analysis of the spectrum of source m1 shows that the H emission line is broad ( FWHM>1500 km/s, see Figure fig:fit spettri m1 panel). Source m1 is therefore classified as a broad line AGN (BLAGN, see Section Analisi A1 for more details). Finally, from the analysis of the YJ-band spectra, an H 4861 line is observed for sources m1 and n1 , confirming their redshift (see Figure fig:spettri H beta ).

From the derived values, and assuming a redshift of 1.5758 (see Section sub:spettroscopia NIR ), the intrinsic, unabsorbed luminosity is measured to be Log [ L_ [ 2-10 keV ] ^ X ] = 43.4^ +0.3 _ 0.4 in the [ 2-10 keV ] rest-frame band. These results are consistent within 1 with those found in , where an aperture of 1.5 arcsec is used for the extraction of the spectrum.

We then estimated the BH mass of m1 using the virial formula applied to the Broad Line Regions (BLRs). Therefore, we conclude that source m1 hosts a relatively small ( 10^ 7 M_ ) and moderately accreting ( _ Edd 0.2 ) SMBH.

sources.summary: The paper reports G23.6644-0.0372 as a resolved VLBA/EVN source and says its structure supports the prior conclusion that it is an intrinsically compact extragalactic source affected by scatter broadening.

quotes.quote: G23.6644-0.0372 (M 1) M 1 was observed for two epochs separated by 5 months. Yang2022 , Y22 hereon, had observed this source in 2010 using the European VLBI Network (EVN) and found that the source showed an extended structure at EVN resolution. We thus imaged the target only with the shortest baselines using a Gaussian taper and down-weighting baselines longer than 20 M .

The second epoch of observations had sparse uv coverage as compared to the first epoch because the Los Alamos and Owens Valley dishes failed to take data for a large part of the experiment. We thus concatenate the two epochs of observations to improve uv coverage and detect a resolved source.

In the case of M 1, the extended structure of the source as seen in the VLBA agrees with the conclusion of Y22, wherein they suggest that this target is an intrinsically compact extragalactic source and appears resolved due to scatter broadening.

sources.summary: The paper finds this galaxy’s stellar and H dynamical models agree outside 4 kpc but diverge centrally, possibly because AGN emission biases central density estimates.

quotes.quote: For one galaxy, COS4 25353-M1 139825 (bottom right), the models agree within their uncertainties from 4 kpc to (at least) 10 kpc, while in the central 3 kpc the stellar model yields higher dynamical masses relative to the H model. This galaxy is seen almost face on, with a difference between the H kinematic major axis and the F814W position angle of 26.5^ . This is also one of the objects with strong [ NeV ] emission in the central region. We speculate that emission from the AGN could bias the light-weighted estimates of the central density for both models.

gmw_00004264.name: JADES-GS-z7-LA
gmw_00004264.overview: JADES-GS-z7-LA is a UV-faint Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of z=7.278 [arXiv:2302.12805, arXiv:2306.05295]. It has a low stellar mass, a bursty star formation history [arXiv:2302.12805], a close companion source [arXiv:2302.12805], and resides within a strong surrounding galaxy overdensity [arXiv:2306.05295].

sources.summary: JADES-GS-z7-LA is a z=7.278 Ly emitting galaxy with extreme Ly equivalent width, optical-line confirmation, low dust/neutral gas, high ionization ratios, a close companion, and bursty low-mass SED.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a remarkable Ly emitting galaxy at z=7.2782 , JADES-GS+53.16746-27.7720 (shortened to JADES-GS-z7-LA), with rest-frame equivalent width, EW _0 (Ly ) =388.0 88.8 \ and UV magnitude 17.0 . The spectroscopic redshift is confirmed via rest-frame optical lines [ O ii ] , H and [ O iii ] in its JWST/NIRSpec Micro-Shutter Assembly (MSA) spectrum. The Ly line is detected in both lower resolution ( R 100 ) PRISM as well as medium resolution ( R 1000 ) G140M grating spectra. The line spread function-deconvolved Ly full width at half maximum in the grating is 383.9 56.2 km s ^ 1 and the Ly velocity offset compared to the systemic redshift is 113.3 80.0 km s ^ 1 , indicative of very little neutral gas or dust within the galaxy. We estimate the Ly escape fraction to be >70 .

JADES-GS-z7-LA has a [ O iii ] / [ O ii ] ratio (O32) of 11.1 2.2 and a ( [ O iii ] + [ O ii ] )/H ratio (R23) of 11.2 2.6 , consistent with low metallicity and high ionization parameters. Deep NIRCam imaging also revealed a close companion source (separated by 0.23'' ), which exhibits similar photometry to that of JADES-GS-z7-LA, with a photometric excess in the F410M NIRCam image consistent with [ O iii ] +H emission at the same redshift. The spectral energy distribution of JADES-GS-z7-LA indicates a ``bursty'' star formation history, with a low stellar mass of 10^ 7 M _ .

sources.summary: JADES-GS-z7-LA is described as a very faint z=7.28 Lyman-alpha emitter with high escape fraction, extreme equivalent width, and a strong surrounding overdensity.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-z7-LA is, on the other hand, a very UV-faint ( = 16.7 ) galaxy in the GOODS-S field with a near-unity escape fraction yielding an extremely large EW of 400 90 implying that it lies within a very large ionized bubble Saxena2023 . Both of these strong LAEs exhibit very high EWs of 1500 placing them in the top 17 and 7 of the EW distribution, respectively, given their UV luminosities (see sec:OIIIHb_EW_results ).

It is immediately clear from Fig. fig:LAE_nearbyMaps that the surface density of z 7.0-7.6 galaxies is unusually high near the GOODS-S LAE JADES-GS-z7-LA. In particular, about 1 arcmin East of this very UV-faint LAE lies a complex of six distinct z 7.0-7.6 galaxies with a maximum separation of 23 arcsec from one another. The surface density of z 7.0-7.6 galaxies in this very small (0.073 arcmin ^2 ) region of GOODS-S is 87.3 galaxies/arcmin ^ 2 , a factor of 70 higher than that on average over the deep imaging region covering JADES-GS-z7-LA.

Given its near-unity Ly escape fraction and relatively small velocity offset ( 120 km/s), JADES-GS-z7-LA is expected to reside in a very large ( R 3 physical Mpc) ionized bubble Saxena2023 . As discussed in Saxena2023 , this object cannot have created such a large bubble on its own given its very low luminosity ( = -16.7 ), implying that the local ionizing photon budget must be dominated by neighboring galaxies.

gmw_00004265.name: z8 GND 5296
gmw_00004265.overview: The high-redshift galaxy z8 GND 5296 is a bright, highly star-forming Lyman-alpha emitter located at a redshift of approximately 7.5 [arXiv:1905.08812, arXiv:2306.05295]. It resides in an overdense region surrounded by several fainter candidate galaxies [arXiv:2306.05295] and likely inhabits a large ionized bubble [arXiv:2009.10092, arXiv:2208.01660].

sources.summary: The paper reports MOSFIRE spectroscopy of z8 GND 5896, measuring CIII] emission, refining the systemic redshift, and inferring low metallicity and strong ionization from young stars or AGN.

quotes.quote: \ (n e z8 GND 5896, also known as FIGS GN1 1292) is a bright (H _ 160 = 25.38, Finkelstein et al. \ in prep), highly star-forming galaxy at z_ Ly = 7.5078. The brightest detection in our H band sample of high-redshift galaxies, \ was first spectroscopically confirmed via \ emission by F13. Figure fig:hband shows a portion of the HST /WFC3 F160W image of the CANDELS/GOODS-N field, including the MOSFIRE slit positions from the MOSFIRE 2013, 2014, and 2017 observing runs.

We present Keck/MOSFIRE H band spectroscopy targeting \ 1907,1909 in a z=7.5056 galaxy previously identified via \ emission. We detect strong line emission at 1.621 0.002 m with a line flux of ( 2.63 0.52 ) 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . We tentatively identify this line as [ 1907, but we are unable to detect 1909 owing to sky emission at the expected location. This gives a galaxy systemic redshift, z_ sys =7.5032 0.0003 , with a velocity offset to \ of \ = 88 27 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes z7 GND 42912 as a bright, massive z=7.51 Ly emitter in Pair A, close to z7 GND 6330 and capable of forming a pMpc-scale ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: Particularly, z7 GND 42912 at z=7.51 and z7 GND 6330 at z=7.55 are in close proximity with each other with a 1.55 pMpc physical separation, marked as ``Pair A" in the right panel of Figure fig:lae_clustering . To clarify, our discussion on LAE pairs in this section must be distinguished from the conventional definition of galaxy pairs in the context of galaxy-galaxy interactions. Instead, we discuss LAE pairs which overlap their individual ionized bubbles each other, forming contiguous ionized areas. Along the LOS, the two galaxies are separated by 1.53 pMpc while in the transverse direction, they are separated by mere 52 7 (0.27 pMpc).

Specifically, z7 GND 42912 at z=7.51 (in Pair A) and z7 GND 16863 at z=7.60 (in Pair B) are massive ( M_ * >10^9M_ ) and bright in their UV ( M_ UV =-21.6 and 21.2 ), which could be enough to form 1 pMpc size ionized bubbles around them. As listed in Table tab:MOSFIRE_LAE_spectra , their Ly luminosities are also bright ( > 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) enough to form 1 pMpc size ionized bubbles, based on the model (see their Figure 15).

sources.summary: The paper treats z8 GND 5296 as a UV-bright z=7.51 Lyman-alpha emitter and quantifies nearby candidate galaxies and overdensity around it.

quotes.quote: The galaxy z8 GND 5296 is a UV-bright ( = 21.5 ) system in the GOODS-N field with a most recent EW measurement of 33 4 from Jung2020 . JADES-GS-z7-LA is, on the other hand, a very UV-faint ( = 16.7 ) galaxy in the GOODS-S field with a near-unity escape fraction yielding an extremely large EW of 400 90 implying that it lies within a very large ionized bubble Saxena2023 . Both of these strong LAEs exhibit very high EWs of 1500 placing them in the top 17 and 7 of the EW distribution, respectively, given their UV luminosities (see sec:OIIIHb_EW_results ).

We also identify five candidate z 7.0-7.6 galaxies lying near ( < 1.5 arcmin separation) the UV-bright LAE in GOODS-N (z8 GND 5296), all located roughly to the southeast (see Fig. fig:LAE_nearbyMaps ). Considering the smallest rectangular area that encloses all six of these objects, we obtain a surface density of 3.6 galaxies/arcmin ^ 2 which is approximately 4 higher than the average over the full JADES/GOODS-N footprint with F335M. These five relatively nearby objects have inferred EWs of 500--1500 and UV magnitudes of 20.0 18.4 , indicating that they are all substantially ( 4 ) fainter than z8 GND 5296.

sources.summary: The paper uses z7 GND 42912 as an EoR LAE test case: predictions agree at 1 sigma in one comparison, it may inhabit an ionized bubble, and larger-N matching underpredicts its emission.

quotes.quote: In five out of ten cases (ID z7 GND 18626, z7 GND 44088, z7 GND 42912, z7 GND 22233, and z7 GND 39781) the 1 interval of our predictions overlapped with the 1 interval of the observational measurement, indicating moderate agreement. For strong emitters (observed > 20 \ ), our prediction overlapped with observation five out of eight times. Furthermore, two strong emitters (z7 GND 42912 and z7 GND 16863), postulated by jung20 to inhabit ionized bubbles, had observed equivalent widths greater than or equal to the majority of our predicted distributions, as one might expect for sources with little IGM attenuation.

ID z7 GND 42912 offers a good example of how challenging predicting \ emission can be. As N increases, the peak of the predicted distribution shifts from agreeing well with the observation to under-predicting it.

gmw_00004266.name: NGC 1534
gmw_00004266.overview: NGC 1534 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1412.3856, arXiv:1806.09255] that contains a dust lane [arXiv:1412.3856] and exhibits low-power star formation [arXiv:1806.09255]. The galaxy is surrounded by giant, low surface-brightness remnant radio lobes [arXiv:1412.3856, arXiv:1806.09255] but shows no active compact core [arXiv:1806.09255].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1534 as a rare, dusty disk/lenticular host of very low surface-brightness relic giant radio lobes spanning about 700 kpc.

quotes.quote: Recent observations with the Murchison Widefield Array at 185 MHz have serendipitously unveiled a heretofore unknown giant and relatively nearby ( z = 0.0178 ) radio galaxy associated with NGC 1534. The diffuse emission presented here is the first indication that NGC 1534 is one of a rare class of objects (along with NGC 5128 and NGC 612) in which a galaxy with a prominent dust lane hosts radio emission on scales of 700 kpc. We present details of the radio emission along with a detailed comparison with other radio galaxies with disks. NGC1534 is the lowest surface brightness radio galaxy known with an estimated scaled 1.4-GHz surface brightness of just 0.2 mJy arcmin ^ 2 . The radio lobes have one of the steepest spectral indices yet observed: =-2.1 0.1 , and the core to lobe luminosity ratio is <0.1 .

At the centre of these two diffuse patches we see a relatively bright 13.7 mag galaxy at a redshift of 0.0178, NGC 1534: see Table  tab:NGC1534_optical . The galaxy has an obvious disk component with a dust lane as well as a dominant nuclear bulge. 1991rc3..book.....D classify it as SA0/a?(rs) edge-on: a lenticular, with the possibility of being a spiral.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1534 as a lenticular galaxy surrounded by remnant radio emission, with no active compact core, low-power star-formation emission, and possible drift from old lobes.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the large-scale radio emission surrounding the lenticular galaxy NGC 1534 with the Australia Telescope Compact Array and Murchison Widefield Array. We find no significant compact emission from the nucleus of NGC 1534 to suggest an active core, and instead find low-power radio emission tracing its star-formation history with a radio-derived star-formation rate of 0.38 0.03 M _ yr ^ 1 . The spectral energy distribution of the extended emission is well-fit by a continuous injection model with an `off' component, consistent with dead radio galaxies.

NGC 1534 is a lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z=0.017816 dpd+91 and is part of the HDC 269 and LDC 292 galaxy groups chm+07 . hje+15 serendipitously discovered remnant radio emission surrounding NGC 1534 with the MWA.

If NGC 1534 is indeed the host, then we see from the offset position of the optical galaxy and the thinnest point of the radio emission that NGC 1534 must have moved from its old lobes, leaving the radio plasma to gradually diffuse and lose energy to the intergalactic medium.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1534 as a GLEAM radio-image target, showing that differential background subtraction connects and enhances its extended radio emission and modestly improves signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: We selected a patches in the sky from image cut-outs at an observing bandwidth of 147-154 MHz for NGC 1534 and 208-216 MHz for Abell 3565. Figure fig:obser shows us the main targets (NGC 1534 and Abell 3565) before ( top ) and after ( bottom ) the background subtraction.

For NGC 1534 the radio emission appears to originate from two non-related sources, however, following the differential operation we observe a connection and enhancement in the extent of the emission. The signal-to-noise ratio improves by a factor of about 1.3 and 1.5 for NGC 1534 and Abell 3565, respectively, as compared to the original target images.

gmw_00004267.name: UCG 7408
gmw_00004267.overview: UCG 7408 is a gas-rich blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1007.3936] with an extended, asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution that lacks ordered rotation [arXiv:1007.3936]. Observations of its extended disk reveal a narrow absorption feature [arXiv:1409.3987] and a high fraction of warm atomic gas [arXiv:1409.3987].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7408 as a gas-rich blue dwarf foreground galaxy with extended, asymmetric HI emission and possible 21 cm absorption affected by beam-filling emission.

quotes.quote: In order to understand the effect of the large GBT beam size on the survey, we imaged the \ distribution in the foreground galaxy J122115+454838 (UGC 7408) with the VLA under program AY190 on 28 June and 16 July 2008.

We detected \ emission from three foreground galaxies: J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC 7408), J141629.25+372120.4, and J160659.13+271642.6, thus suggesting the presence of gas-rich galaxies in our sample. Figure  hi_emm shows the smoothed GBT \ spectra for these galaxies. The spectra are presented in terms of the optical velocity of the galaxies, which show very good correspondence with the distribution.

All the three galaxies are ``blue" (as defined in Section  sec:optical ) although they vary in luminosity and stellar mass. While J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC7408) is a gas rich dwarf galaxy of luminosity 0.003 L_* and \ mass of 1.4     10 ^8  M_ , J141629.25+372120.4 and J160659.13+271642.6 are 0.1L_* galaxies and have \ masses of 2.3     10 ^9  M_ and 8.1     10 ^9  M_ , respectively. The VLA D-configuration image of \ emission associated with UGC 7408 (green contours), overplotted on an SDSS -band image, is presented in the left panel of Figure  VLA_HI_image . The FIRST image (beam size = 5 ^ .40 5 ^ .40) of the background QSO J122106.87+454852.1 with its lobes is shown in blue in (enlarged version in the right panel) and its D-configuration continuum image (beam size = 52 ^ .17 38 ^ .11) is shown with white contours. The sightlines (sightline (SL)  12, 13, and 14) to the background continuum source probes the galaxy at impact parameter of 3.2, 2.8, and 2.6 kpc. The \ distribution in this galaxy is extended, extending 2-3 times beyond the optical size of the galaxy. The \ is asymmetric and extends 5 ^ .6 to the southeast of the galaxy. The Westerbork observations of neutral Hydrogen in Irregular and SPiral galaxies WHISP Survey; swaters02 also imaged this galaxy. The subsequent analysis by swaters09 found that its \ distribution is clumpy and lacking ordered rotation.

sources.summary: The paper detects a narrow H I absorber in UCG 7408's extended disk, inferring a low cold-gas fraction and suppressed condensation caused by low metallicity.

quotes.quote: We detected a narrow absorption feature associated with UGC 7408 in our VLA B-configuration spectra towards sightline 2. The absorption feature peaks at a velocity of 444.5 and was measured to have a full-width at half maximum (FWHM) of 1.1 . The width of the feature is comparable with our spectral resolution. No significant absorption features were seen towards sightlines 1 and 3.

Our results suggest that the process of condensation is suppressed in UGC 7408. We found that a large fraction of atomic gas in the extended disk of this galaxy is warm with temperature much larger than a few 100 K. Therefore, we conclude that most of the atomic gas in the extended disk of UCG 7408 failed to condense into the atomic gas clouds of 100-50 K (typically found in the Milky Way ISM).

gmw_00004268.name: J141629
gmw_00004268.overview: J141629 is a blue foreground galaxy with a luminosity of approximately 0.1 L* [arXiv:1007.3936]. It possesses a large neutral hydrogen reservoir with an estimated mass of 2.3 billion solar masses, though observations show no cold-gas absorption at its disk edge [arXiv:1007.3936, arXiv:1409.3987].

sources.summary: The paper reports HI emission from J141629.25+372120.4, identifies it as a blue 0.1 L* foreground galaxy, estimates its HI mass, and treats the sightline absorption as a nondetection.

quotes.quote: We detected \ emission from three foreground galaxies: J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC 7408), J141629.25+372120.4, and J160659.13+271642.6, thus suggesting the presence of gas-rich galaxies in our sample. Figure  hi_emm shows the smoothed GBT \ spectra for these galaxies. The spectra are presented in terms of the optical velocity of the galaxies, which show very good correspondence with the distribution.

All the three galaxies are ``blue" (as defined in Section  sec:optical ) although they vary in luminosity and stellar mass. While J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC7408) is a gas rich dwarf galaxy of luminosity 0.003 L_* and \ mass of 1.4     10 ^8  M_ , J141629.25+372120.4 and J160659.13+271642.6 are 0.1L_* galaxies and have \ masses of 2.3     10 ^9  M_ and 8.1     10 ^9  M_ , respectively.

In the other two galaxies with \ emission (J141629.25+372120.4 and J160659.13+271642.6), the expected \ diameter estimated using the relationship shown in Figure 7 of swaters02 are 30.7 and 60.2 kpc, respectively. Thus, the sightlines, SL  22 and 23 are expected to pierce through the \ distribution in these systems. Although we detect dips in the GBT spectra, it is unclear if there is real absorption or are overlapping individual emission features mimicking absorption. Hence, we consider these two cases as nondetections.

sources.summary: The paper reports no H I absorption toward J141629 sightlines, despite its large H I reservoir, implying unusually low cold-gas content near the disk edge.

quotes.quote: We did not detect any absorbers toward the sightlines 4 and 5 probing galaxy J141629 at impact parameters of 16.2 and 32.4 kpc and sightline 6 probing galaxy J160659 at an impact parameter of 23.3 kpc. The limiting optical depths and column densities estimated from 3 noise in the spectra are presented in Table 5. Both these galaxies have large reservoirs ( Log [ M_ HI ] = 9.4 and 9.9 respectively) and hence are expected to have extended distributions. The empirical \ mass to size relationship derived for dwarf galaxies from the WHISP Survey swaters02 predicts that the distribution in J141629 and J160659 have radii, R _ HI , of 15.3 and 30.1 kpc, respectively.

Similarly, sightline 5 probes J141629 at the edge of its disk ( =16.2 kpc and R_ HI =15.3 kpc) and the upper limit on its column places it an order of magnitude below that expected at R_ HI . This suggest that cold-to-average column densities could be lower than 10 towards these sightlines.

gmw_00004269.name: J160659
gmw_00004269.overview: J160659 is a blue foreground galaxy with a luminosity of 0.1 L* [arXiv:1007.3936]. It contains a large reservoir of neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1409.3987] with an estimated HI mass of 8.1 billion solar masses [arXiv:1007.3936].

sources.summary: The paper reports HI emission from J160659.13+271642.6, describes it as a blue 0.1 L* foreground galaxy with a high HI mass, and treats the probed sightline as a nondetection.

quotes.quote: We detected \ emission from three foreground galaxies: J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC 7408), J141629.25+372120.4, and J160659.13+271642.6, thus suggesting the presence of gas-rich galaxies in our sample. Figure  hi_emm shows the smoothed GBT \ spectra for these galaxies. The spectra are presented in terms of the optical velocity of the galaxies, which show very good correspondence with the distribution.

All the three galaxies are ``blue" (as defined in Section  sec:optical ) although they vary in luminosity and stellar mass. While J122115.22+454843.2 (UGC7408) is a gas rich dwarf galaxy of luminosity 0.003 L_* and \ mass of 1.4     10 ^8  M_ , J141629.25+372120.4 and J160659.13+271642.6 are 0.1L_* galaxies and have \ masses of 2.3     10 ^9  M_ and 8.1     10 ^9  M_ , respectively.

In the other two galaxies with \ emission (J141629.25+372120.4 and J160659.13+271642.6), the expected \ diameter estimated using the relationship shown in Figure 7 of swaters02 are 30.7 and 60.2 kpc, respectively. Thus, the sightlines, SL  22 and 23 are expected to pierce through the \ distribution in these systems. Although we detect dips in the GBT spectra, it is unclear if there is real absorption or are overlapping individual emission features mimicking absorption. Hence, we consider these two cases as nondetections.

sources.summary: The paper finds no H I absorption toward J160659 inside its expected extended disk, suggesting either patchy cold gas or much warmer atomic gas.

quotes.quote: We did not detect any absorbers toward the sightlines 4 and 5 probing galaxy J141629 at impact parameters of 16.2 and 32.4 kpc and sightline 6 probing galaxy J160659 at an impact parameter of 23.3 kpc. The limiting optical depths and column densities estimated from 3 noise in the spectra are presented in Table 5. Both these galaxies have large reservoirs ( Log [ M_ HI ] = 9.4 and 9.9 respectively) and hence are expected to have extended distributions. The empirical \ mass to size relationship derived for dwarf galaxies from the WHISP Survey swaters02 predicts that the distribution in J141629 and J160659 have radii, R _ HI , of 15.3 and 30.1 kpc, respectively.

This implies that J160659 is probed by sightline 6 ( =23.3 kpc) within the R _ HI =30.1 kpc. However, no was observed in absorption. The 3 limit on the absorption column density towards this sightline is 3.6 10^ 18 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00004270.name: 2MASX J04463790-1013349
gmw_00004270.overview: 2MASX J04463790-1013349 is a face-on spiral galaxy that features a large-scale bar [arXiv:2006.02454]. Its nucleus is a weak active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2307.13674] with compact emission lines [arXiv:2307.13674] and mid-infrared emission dominated by the host galaxy [arXiv:2006.02454].

sources.summary: The paper describes AT2019qiz's host as a weak AGN candidate whose nucleus shows changing narrow-line diagnostics, compact emission lines, and negligible intrinsic X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, there are no spectra of the host galaxy (2MASX J04463790-1013349) taken before the flare. Both studies suggest that the host galaxy harbours a weak AGN based on Baldwin-Phillips-Terlevich (BPT) diagrams. Early X-ray detections suggest accretion started promptly in this event qiz20 , though the high hardness ratio may indicate that the X-ray emission originates from the pre-existing AGN qiz21 . However, note that the hardness ratio varied during outburst, implying that the TDE did affect the X-ray emission.

We extracted the spectrum of the host galaxy nucleus using a circular aperture with a diameter of 1 FWHM(stellar PSF). The integral field of MUSE also allows us to check for spatial extent. The host starlight is of course very extended, but the emission line source is compact. Comparing extractions with 1 arcsec and 2 arcsec apertures changed the [ OIII ] flux by 15 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J04463790-1013349 as AT2019qiz's face-on barred spiral host, analyzes its nucleus, and reports WISE colors consistent with galaxy-dominated rather than AGN-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: It is coincident with the centre of the galaxy 2MASX J04463790-1013349 (a.k.a. WISEA J044637.88-101334.9).

The host of AT2019qiz is a face-on spiral galaxy. A large-scale bar is visible in the PanSTARRS image (Figure fig:ps1 ). French2020 analysed Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) images of four TDE hosts and identified bars in two. While central bars (on scales 100 pc) can increase the TDE rate by dynamically feeding stars towards the nucleus Merritt2004 , there is no evidence that large-scale bars increase the TDE rate French2020 . Given the proximity of AT2019qiz, this galaxy is an ideal candidate for HST or adaptive optics imaging to resolve the structure of the nucleus.
The host of AT2019qiz has W2-W3=1.8 mag, consistent with other spiral galaxies. Thus the emission detected by WISE is dominated by the galaxy, not an AGN.

gmw_00004271.name: NGC 5496
gmw_00004271.overview: NGC 5496 is a highly inclined, blue disk galaxy characterized by low surface brightness [arXiv:1308.4406, arXiv:1910.04596]. It is a low-metallicity system that exhibits an intrinsically low dust-to-gas ratio and weak carbon monoxide emission [arXiv:1308.4406, arXiv:1910.04596].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5496 as a blue low-surface-brightness edge-on disk and a persistent outlier with low dust-to-gas and low dust-to-CO ratios.

quotes.quote: Two notable outliers persist through each of the plots in Figure fig:corr_fir_shi , which are SDP 1 (with the lowest \ flux) and NGC 5496 (with the highest). These two are also outliers in the CO-- \ correlation (Figure fig:corr_fir_sco , last panel), with SDP 1 having a very high / \ ratio, and NGC 5496 having low / \ and / \ ratios (the latter being an upper limit). NGC 5496, which has the lowest / M_ HI , is another edge-on disk galaxy but is very blue and has low surface brightness (unlike NGC 5746 for example). Due to the high inclination, the \ flux has been corrected by a factor of 1.32 for self-absorption; however without this correction this object remains a significant outlier, so it appears to have an intrinsically low dust/gas ratio. Figure fig:mdust_lco_lhi shows that it also has a low stellar mass and low metallicity (see also discussion below).

sources.summary: NGC 5496 is one of the BADGRS with local metallicity measurements and weak CO emission; its outer disk shows low R_31 and a narrow higher-transition line in an inclined disk.

quotes.quote: For NGC 5496, UGC 9215 and UGC 9299 metallicities were taken from deVis2017CE , who used the SDSS and GAMA spectra and strong line ratios, and are quoted in the Pettini2004 O3N2 calibration.

The errors on the high R_ 21 >1 ratios in NGC 5496 O1,O2 and UGC 9299 are large enough that they are still consistent with a `normal' R_ 21 =0.7-1.0 value, however UGC 9215 O1 appears to be significantly excited with R_ 21 =2.11 0.58 .

This is especially evident in the NGC 5496 O2 region, where the \ line is very much narrower than the \ line. This is a highly inclined galaxy and the large sight-line through the disk exacerbates this effect.

gmw_00004272.name: SDP 1
gmw_00004272.overview: SDP 1 is a highly luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1308.4406]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed visual morphology indicative of a merger, and it displays an atypically high dust-to-neutral hydrogen ratio [arXiv:1308.4406].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP 1 as an atypical luminous AGN with disturbed morphology, high dust-to-HI ratio, and outlier behavior in CO and infrared correlations.

quotes.quote: Two notable outliers persist through each of the plots in Figure fig:corr_fir_shi , which are SDP 1 (with the lowest \ flux) and NGC 5496 (with the highest). These two are also outliers in the CO-- \ correlation (Figure fig:corr_fir_sco , last panel), with SDP 1 having a very high / \ ratio, and NGC 5496 having low / \ and / \ ratios (the latter being an upper limit). There are several outliers in the / M_ HI ratios, in particular SDP 1 (with the highest / M_ HI ) and NGC 5496 (with the lowest). SDP 1 is a spectroscopically identified AGN, has a disturbed visual morphology (see Figure fig:spectra ) and a high luminosity ( =4.0 10^ 11 ), hence it is atypical of the sample. It is also possible that the \ self-absorption is underestimated for this source (in which case it should have a lower / M_ HI ) -- the correction in Section sec:hiselfabs is based on morphology and inclination alone, but this is likely to be unreliable for mergers.

gmw_00004273.name: AM 2229-735
gmw_00004273.overview: AM 2229-735 is a peculiar galaxy system undergoing a minor merger [arXiv:1911.03595]. The interacting galaxies exhibit tidal arms and a connecting material bridge, serving as a progenitor for a future polar-ring galaxy [arXiv:1911.03595].

sources.summary: The paper models AM 2229-735 as a minor merger and likely polar-ring progenitor whose simulated tidal stream can settle into a polar disk-like structure.

quotes.quote: We used N-body+hydrodynamics simulations to reproduce the dynamics of the observed system AM 2229-735 that is a minor merger whose interaction signals are those of a progenitor for a polar ring galaxy. Our simulations reproduce the global characteristics of interaction observed in the system such as arms and a material bridge connecting the galaxies. As a merger remnant, we found a quasi-stable and self gravitating planar tidal stream with dark matter, stars and gas orbiting in a plane approximately perpendicular to the main galaxy disk leading in the future to a polar ring galaxy.

From its first report in ``A CATALOGUE OF SOUTHERN PECULIAR GALAXIES AND ASSOCIATIONS'' Arp1977, Arp1987 , the system AM 2229-735 have been studied by several authors, this has allowed to collect many data that will be useful to configure initial conditions for the simulations and to compare their results with observations to get clues about its evolution and fate.

gmw_00004274.name: SG
gmw_00004274.overview: SG is a satellite galaxy in the AM 2229-735 system that is connected to the main disk galaxy by a material bridge [arXiv:1911.03595]. It has a radial disk scale length of 1.0 kpc/h and an inclination of 41.75 degrees [arXiv:1911.03595].

sources.summary: The paper defines SG as the satellite galaxy in AM 2229-735, fits its disk properties, and traces stripped satellite material forming the polar stream.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:AM shows an image of the system, one can see a main disk galaxy (hereafter MG) at its centre, a satellite galaxy (hereafter SG), a material bridge that connects both galaxies, a special luminous region (hereafter SR) on this bridge and other objects that we assume are field objects. Fitting the disk isophotes to an exponential profile we got radial disk scale lengths for both MG 3.37 kpc/h and SG 1.0 kpc/h . The mean PA obtained this way are 1.74^ for MG and 59.57^ for SG. The mean inclination, in the case of AM 2229-735 is 63.55^ for MG and 41.75^ for SG.

The figures are presented in the coordinates (x',y',z') , with centre at the centre of mass of MG, of the particles in the stream after rotations such that the angular momentum of the stream points along the z' axis, thus, we are showing its face-on and edge-on views.

gmw_00004275.name: NGC 7421
gmw_00004275.overview: NGC 7421 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy featuring an inner resonance ring [arXiv:1912.07452]. It exhibits an asymmetric, off-center neutral hydrogen disk that has been affected by both a past tidal interaction and subsequent weak ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1912.07452, arXiv:1506.04399].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7421 as a barred spiral with an inner ring, off-center HI, possible tidal tails, and weak ram-pressure effects.

quotes.quote: NGC 7421 It is a late-type barred spiral with an inner resonance ring Buta1995 . It has the smallest relative velocity ( v_ rel = -9.59 km s ^ 1 ) with respect to IC 1459. It is the third smallest, R_ ,g =5.26 kpc, and less massive, 2.44 10^ 10 M _ , galaxy. The colour composite VST image (Figure fig:ngc7421sp , left panel) shows the small inner bar and the South-East light asymmetries due to the spiral arms and the outer envelope of the galaxy, that are consistent with the off-centre of the HI disk. The recent study made by Serra2015 has shown that the HI asymmetry in the opposite direction of the star-forming optical disk might be the signature of ram pressure stripping.

According to Denes2014 , this galaxy is a HI-deficient galaxy, M _ HI =(1.1 0.3) 10^ 9 M _ Serra2015 . However according to Ryder1997 and Ryder2000 , the tenuous hot intragroup medium is not able to produce a significant ram pressure stripping and hence tidal interactions might produce a couple of tails. The conclusion is that the galaxy had a previous tidal interaction, maybe with NGC 7418, which affected the stellar disk and the HI gas, and a subsequent weak ram pressure stripping, as happened for IC 5273, which dislocated the HI gas.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 7421 as a ram-pressure-stripped group member whose low-column-density H I is pushed eastward, matching a compressed star-forming side of the optical disk.

quotes.quote: In contrast with this result, an earlier study by based on shallower data from the \ Parkes All Sky Survey barnes2001 finds that the group members IC 5264 (a dusty early type), IC 5269B, IC 5269C, NGC 7418 and NGC 7421 (all late types) are \ deficient. This disagreement is most likely due to the large uncertainty on the \ deficiency of individual galaxies. More precise indications about the occurrence of gas removal in IC 1459 may come from the resolved study of the \ morphology with interferometry. This has been done for a few galaxies in the group. discuss signatures of ram pressure stripping in NGC 7421 using an \ image obtained with the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA).
