Although XID-312 is just above our selection criteria in the L_X-L_B plane, the galaxy has been reported to be X-ray variable in the recent study by young2012 , who concluded that the observed variability cannot be due to binary populations or ultraluminous sources but rather is best explained by an accreting black hole. Its X-ray hardness ratio, defined as HR=(H-S)/(H+S), where S and H are the soft (0.5-2 keV) and hard (2-8 keV) band net counts is HR=-0.2 xue2011 . The observed hardness ratio suggests some absorption by neutral gas with a density of about N_H 10^ 22 cm^ 2 see Figure 9 in silverman2005 .

gmw_00004299.name: XID-476
gmw_00004299.overview: XID-476 is a low-mass galaxy with an early-type host morphology [arXiv:1305.3826]. It is identified as a candidate active galactic nucleus hosting a low-mass black hole estimated at 250,000 solar masses [arXiv:1305.3826].

sources.summary: The paper presents XID-476 as one of three low-mass X-ray AGN candidates; its host is modeled as early type and its X-ray excess supports a low-mass black hole interpretation.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present three candidate AGNs in low-mass galaxies, selected to have a high ratio of X-ray to optical luminosity as detailed below. If confirmed, these galaxies would be among the lowest-mass host galaxies known see also barth2008,reinesetal2011 . Two of them (XID-476, XID-231) are detected in the shallower E-CDF-S coverage lehmer2005 and the third (XID-312) within the central 4 Ms area xue2011 with more than 100 counts in the full band (0.5-8 keV). In Figure  fig:optical , we show postage-stamp HST/ACS images of the three low-mass galaxies while indicating the X-ray centroid and an effective search radius for an optical counterpart that demonstrates the likelihood of these associations.

The morphological analysis of XID-476 using GALFIT to fit a S e rsic model shows that its host galaxy is an early type galaxy based on the best-fit S e rsic index ( n_ Sersic,F850LP =3.3 ). We do not find a point source (Host/Nuclear > 40) in the center of the galaxy. Since we do not have a broad emission line to use to estimate the BH mass, we convert the stellar bulge mass into a BH mass using the relation from haeringrix2004 , yielding a BH mass of 2.5 10^5 M_ .

gmw_00004300.name: XID-231
gmw_00004300.overview: XID-231 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus candidate with a stellar mass of log M_* = 8.3 [arXiv:1305.3826]. The host galaxy exhibits a peculiar irregular morphology where a single Sérsic model is insufficient to describe the structure, and no distinct bulge or disk component can be identified [arXiv:1305.3826].

sources.summary: The paper presents XID-231 as a low-mass X-ray AGN candidate with the lowest stellar mass in the set, peculiar irregular morphology, and excess X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present three candidate AGNs in low-mass galaxies, selected to have a high ratio of X-ray to optical luminosity as detailed below. If confirmed, these galaxies would be among the lowest-mass host galaxies known see also barth2008,reinesetal2011 . Two of them (XID-476, XID-231) are detected in the shallower E-CDF-S coverage lehmer2005 and the third (XID-312) within the central 4 Ms area xue2011 with more than 100 counts in the full band (0.5-8 keV). In Figure  fig:optical , we show postage-stamp HST/ACS images of the three low-mass galaxies while indicating the X-ray centroid and an effective search radius for an optical counterpart that demonstrates the likelihood of these associations.

XID-231 has the lowest stellar mass of all three candidates (log M _ * =8.3 ) and shows the most peculiar host among all three galaxies. We find no sign of a point source (Host/Nuclear > 40) from our two-dimensional imaging analysis. Although the best-fit single S e rsic index is n=2.4, the residuals are significant and suggest that a single S e rsic model is insufficient to describe the host morphology. Adding further S e rsic models to the fit reduces the residuals but we cannot identify a prominent bulge or disk component even by fixing some of the parameters.

gmw_00004301.name: BST1047+1156
gmw_00004301.overview: BST1047+1156 is an extremely gas-rich, low surface brightness dwarf object located in the Leo I galaxy group [arXiv:1807.11544]. It features two tidal tails, contains young stellar populations, and is embedded within diffuse gas connecting M96 to the HI Leo Ring [arXiv:1807.11544].

sources.summary: The paper reports BST1047+1156 as an extremely diffuse, gas-rich Leo I object with faint optical/UV starlight, young populations, tidal tails, and ambiguous tidal-dwarf or field-LSB origins.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of diffuse starlight in an extragalactic HI cloud in the nearby Leo I galaxy group. We detect the source, dubbed BST1047+1156, in both broadband optical and GALEX ultraviolet light. Spanning 2 kpc in radius, it has a peak surface brightness of =28.8 , making it the lowest surface brightness object ever detected via integrated light. Although the object is extremely gas-rich, with a gas fraction of f_g=0.99 , its peak HI column density is well below levels where star formation is typically observed in galaxies.

Nonetheless, BST1047+1156 shows evidence for young stellar populations: along with the detected UV emission, the object is extremely blue, with B-V=0.14 0.09 . The object sports two tidal tails and is found embedded within diffuse gas connecting the spiral galaxy M96 to the group's extended HI Leo Ring. The nature of BST1047+1156 is unclear.

gmw_00004302.name: H0111-149
gmw_00004302.overview: H0111-149 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248]. Its broad-band spectrum exhibits a canonical primary power law, a neutral iron line, and absorption in excess of the galactic value [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248].

sources.summary: The paper identifies H0111-149 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy whose BeppoSAX spectrum requires excess absorption, has a canonical primary power law, and shows a neutral iron line.

quotes.quote: H0111-149 (MKN 1152) Although marginally evident in the ratio reported in figure 1(), H0111-149 requires absorption in excess to the galactic value at the 90 confidence level: an ionized absorber ( N_ H =1.61^ +0.55 _ 0.47 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 , 1.12 ^ +1.68 _ 0.68 erg cm s ^ 1 ) or a cold one partially covering the source (N _H =4.2 ^ +1.0 _ 1.0 10 ^ 21 cm^ 2 , C _ f =0.40^ +0.30 _ 0.16 ) fit equally well the data ( ^2_ warm =81 for 77 d.o.f versus ^2_ cold =82 for 77 d.o.f.). If instead we introduce in the fits both a cold and warm absorber fully covering the source as done in H0557-525 and used by Perola et al. (2002), the fit is slightly better ( ^2 =79.3 for 76 d.o.f.) and the values for the cold absorber is N_ H =1.19^ +0.81 _ 0.59 10 ^ 20 cm^ 2 while the warm one has N_ H =2.66^ +1.17 _ 2.49 10 ^ 21 at cm^ 2 and =20^ +34 _ 14 erg cm s ^ 1 . In Table 3 we report the best fit parameters relative to this last case. No soft excess is required by the broad band fit. The primary power law is canonical ( = 1.71^ +0.15 _ 0.10 ) and a neutral iron line at E_ line =6.5 keV is detected with high confidence (90 ) with an EW=125 eV .

gmw_00004303.name: H1846-786
gmw_00004303.overview: H1846-786 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248]. In the 0.1-10 keV band, its X-ray spectrum is best fit by a canonical power law, showing no evidence of extra absorption, a soft excess, or a statistically required iron line [arXiv:astro-ph/0310248].

sources.summary: The paper treats H1846-786 as a possibly contaminated Seyfert observation, finding a canonical 0.1-10 keV power law, no extra absorption or soft excess, and weak iron-line constraints.

quotes.quote: H1846-786 (IRAS F18389-7834) As discussed in section 2.1 the PDS data of H1846-786 could be contaminated by other Seyfert galaxies present in the field of view, therefore we first analysed only the LECS and MECS data. In the 0.1-10 keV band a simple canonical power law is the best fit to the data (see figure 1(d)). This result indicates no need for extra absorption or the presence of a soft excess. Also the presence of an iron line is not statistically required by the data; in any case fixing the line energy at 6.4 keV as expected in the case of the iron K _ fluorescence line, we obtain an upper limit on the equivalent width of 200 eV again compatible with the values seen in Seyfert 1s. We also tried to put some constrain on the reflection and high energy cut-off taking into consideration the PDS data and assuming first that non contaminating sources were present in the field of view.

gmw_00004304.name: And VIII
gmw_00004304.overview: And VIII is a tidally distorted dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M31 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309254]. It contains several planetary nebulae, globular clusters, and a small amount of associated neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0309254], though it may alternatively represent part of a stellar stream [arXiv:astro-ph/0406146].

sources.summary: The paper presents And VIII as a newly detected, tidally distorted M31 dwarf satellite with planetary nebulae, globular clusters, associated H I, Local Group dwarf-like luminosity, and an elongated structure.

quotes.quote: And VIII is tidally distorted, with length 10 kpc and width a few kpc. It contains 5--12 planetary nebulae (PNe) and 1--3 globular clusters, and has a velocity of --204 km/s with respect to M31, some 350 km/s away from M32's velocity. There are also 4 10^5 solar masses of HI, well-separated from the disk, at the same position and velocity. The satellite has luminosity of 1.2--2.4 10^8 , and a central surface brightness of order _V=24 . Both these values are typical of Local Group dwarf galaxies.

We conclude that it is a tidally disrupting dwarf satellite of M31. There are two HI features associated with And VIII which contain of order 4 10^5 solar masses, about 0.1 of its total luminous mass. And VIII appears to be a dSph galaxy with a small amount of associated HI.

sources.summary: The paper identifies And VIII as a plausible stream progenitor candidate because its position and radial velocity match the orbit, while noting uncertainty over whether it is a satellite galaxy.

quotes.quote: The recently discovered And VIII mor03 is an attractive possibility as a progenitor: Both its location and radial velocity ( 0^ , 0.5^ ; v_ rad =-204 km s ^ 1 with respect to M31) are consistent with those of the giant southern stream and of our determined orbit (see Fig. fig:sim_orb1 ). Future observations need to confirm if And VIII is a satellite galaxy or, as has been recently suggested iba04 , simply a part of the stellar stream.

In conclusion, our analyses lead us to expect that: (i) The stream should turn around slightly beyond the edge of field `1'; (ii) The stream should widen around this turning point and the line -of-sight velocity dispersion should exhibit significant variations along the stream; (iii) There are possible associations between the post-pericenter part of the stream and the northern spur or the eastern high-metallicity feature. In the latter case, this eash high metallicity feature should lie well in front of the disk; and (iv) the kinematic data on And VIII are consistent with this feature being associated with the orbit of the giant southern stream.

gmw_00004305.name: NGC 3278
gmw_00004305.overview: NGC 3278 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.2111] classified as type Sa [arXiv:1808.00977]. It exhibits an elevated specific star formation rate [arXiv:1808.00977] and features an off-center atomic gas distribution that suggests a recent inflow of gas [arXiv:1808.00977].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3278 as the nearby spiral host of SN 2009bb, gives its velocity and distance, and models its radio emission when measuring the supernova.

quotes.quote: Supernova SN 2009bb was discovered by the Chilean Automatic Supernova Search Program CHASE; Pignata+2009a, Pignata+2009b on 2009 March 29.9 UT, in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 3278 . The radial velocity of NGC 3278 is 2964 \ Paturel+2003 , and in what follows we will use a round distance of 40 Mpc for the galaxy and the supernova. SN 2009bb is in a region of high star-formation, approximately 4.2 kpc from the center of the galaxy in projection Levesque+2010 .

The presence of a small amount of extended emission due to the galaxy NGC 3278 is apparent. We estimate the contribution from NGC 3278 to be 240 100 \ at our resolution of 3.42 2.03 (FWHM). The subtraction of the galaxy component of the radio emission was not straightforward.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3278 as SN 2009bb's spiral host, finding offset atomic gas, low gas masses for its SFR, elevated sSFR, and properties consistent with recent gas inflow.

quotes.quote: exploded within a spiral galaxy type Sa devaucouleurs91 NGC 3278 ( ESO 317-G 043 , PGC 031068 ) at a redshift of 0.009877 0.000123 strauss92 . It has an inclination to the line of sight of 41 deg hyperleda .

The atomic gas distribution of is not centred on the optical galaxy centre, but instead around a third of the atomic gas resides in the region close to the SN position. This galaxy has a few times lower atomic and molecular gas masses than predicted from its star formation rate (SFR). Its specific star formation rate ( sSFR SFR / ) is approximately two to three times higher than the main-sequence value, placing it at the higher end of the main sequence towards starburst galaxies.
The gas properties of are consistent with a recent inflow of gas from the intergalactic medium, which explains the concentration of atomic gas close to the SN position and the enhanced SFR.

gmw_00004306.name: COSMOS-18812
gmw_00004306.overview: COSMOS-18812 is a high-redshift, star-forming emission line galaxy [arXiv:2305.07781]. Although the 4364 auroral line was detected at greater than 2̃ significance, sky-line contamination prevented constraints on its electron density and O+ abundance, while its nitrogen abundance remains consistent with theoretical predictions [arXiv:2305.07781].

sources.summary: COSMOS-18812 is one of two targets with a significant 4364 detection, but sky-line contamination prevents density and O+ constraints; its nitrogen abundance remains consistent with expectations.

quotes.quote: Auroral-line Detections The 4364 line was detected at greater than 2 significance in only two targets: COSMOS-18812 and GOODS-N-8240. For COSMOS-18812, the 3727,3730 line doublet fell onto a pair of sky lines, so it was not possible to constrain the electron density or the O ^+ abundance. Within their respective limits and uncertainties, all three of the stacks as well as GOODS-N-8240 and COSMOS-18812 have nitrogen abundances consistent with this prediction.

gmw_00004307.name: COSMOS-19439
gmw_00004307.overview: COSMOS-19439 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2305.07781]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy, combining MOSFIRE and MOSDEF data, were used to predict its auroral-line flux [arXiv:2305.07781].

sources.summary: The paper combines new MOSFIRE and existing MOSDEF spectra for COSMOS-19439, predicts its auroral-line flux, and concludes the observations did not reach enough depth.

quotes.quote: For COSMOS-19439 and COSMOS-19753, there were existing observations from the MOSDEF survey with 2 hours in each band. We therefore combined the deep MOSFIRE spectra with existing MOSDEF observations for these two targets, thereby increasing the total exposure time by 2 hours. For two of the individual auroral-line targets where there were existing MOSDEF spectra (COSMOS-19439 and COSMOS-19752), we predicted the 4364 flux based on 5008 flux as well as T_e predictions.

For COSMOS-19439 and COSMOS-19753, we predicted auroral \ line fluxes in the ranges of 0.8-1.9 10^ 18 and 2.1-5.0 10^ 18 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 respectively. Since the 2 upper limits are above our lowest line flux predictions, this suggests that the observations did not reach the required depth in the 10 combined hours of integration.

gmw_00004308.name: GOODS-N-6699
gmw_00004308.overview: GOODS-N-6699 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2305.07781]. It exhibits a 6302 emission line detection and a possible emission feature near 4364 [arXiv:2305.07781].

sources.summary: The paper finds a possible emission feature near 4364 for GOODS-N-6699, notes uncertainty in its association, and reports a significant 6302 detection.

quotes.quote: Upon visual inspection, we report the presence of emission lying a few angstroms blueward of the 4364 line in two of the targets: GOODS-N-6699 and GOODS-N-14595. For GOODS-N-6699, there is an emission feature detected at > 3 significance when centering a Gaussian profile at 4360 . It is uncertain whether this emission is associated with the 4364 line though it appears at a similar wavelength.
Including the filler targets, a total of nine galaxies yielded significant ( > 2 ) 6302 detections, with two of the auroral \ targets (COSMOS-19753 and GOODS-N-6699) yielding detections.

gmw_00004309.name: GOODS-N-8240
gmw_00004309.overview: GOODS-N-8240 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2305.07781]. It has a well-constrained oxygen abundance equivalent to 21 percent of the solar value, alongside a slightly enhanced nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio [arXiv:2305.07781].

sources.summary: GOODS-N-8240 has a tentative 4364 detection, a well-constrained oxygen abundance, and a nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio enhanced relative to its oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: We tentatively detect 4364 emission in GOODS-N-8240 since the Gaussian fit to the line profile places the significance of this detection at the > 2 level. For the individual \ auroral targets, only GOODS-N-8240 has a well-constrained oxygen abundance of 12+ (O/H)=8.02^ +0.24 _ 0.17 , corresponding to 21 of the solar oxygen abundance. Within their respective limits and uncertainties, all three of the stacks as well as GOODS-N-8240 and COSMOS-18812 have nitrogen abundances consistent with this prediction.

However, for GOODS-N-8240, we find that its N/O ratio of ( N/O)= -0.99^ +0.22 _ 0.23 is slightly enhanced given its oxygen abundance of 12+ ( O/H)=8.02^ +0.24 _ 0.17 . Because the stacks are consistent with the local N/O vs. O/H relation, there does not appear to be strong evidence for an evolution in the N/O ratio between z=0 and z 2 based on this sample, though GOODS-N-8240 does represent an outlier in this regard.

gmw_00004310.name: GOODS-N-14595
gmw_00004310.overview: GOODS-N-14595 is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2305.07781]. It exhibits an elevated star formation rate that is offset high by approximately 0.6 dex from empirical fits [arXiv:2305.07781].

sources.summary: GOODS-N-14595 is offset high in star formation rate and shows an uncertain feature near the 4364 auroral line, motivating physical quantities from a single-Gaussian fit.

quotes.quote: These two empirical fits are shown as dashed and dotted black lines respectively, and they agree with the five GOODS-N galaxies at z 1.7 with the exceptions of GOODS-N-18462 and GOODS-N-14595, which are offset in log(SFR) by roughly -0.3 dex and +0.6 dex respectively. The emission-line feature adjacent to 4364 in GOODS-N-14595 appears to be more closely centered on the expected central wavelength for the auroral oxygen line, with a significance of just 0.38 when centering on 4360 . Since it is unclear to what extent the emission in GOODS-N-14595 can be attributed to 4364 , we report the determination of physical quantities from the single-Gaussian fit to 4364 .

gmw_00004311.name: NGC 4567/8
gmw_00004311.overview: NGC 4567/8, also known as VV 219, is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of two Sc-type spiral galaxies [arXiv:1005.3057, arXiv:2201.02270]. The system is classified as an early-phase minor merger with a global dust temperature of 20 K [arXiv:1005.3057, arXiv:2201.02270].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4567/8 as an Sc galaxy pair, deriving asymmetric dust temperatures, a 20 K global temperature, and a global dust mass from Herschel data.

quotes.quote: This Letter focuses on NGC 4501, which is an Sbc galaxy and has an optical diameter (major axis) of 7.23 , and the galaxy pair NGC 4567/8, which are both of Sc type with optical diameters of 2.92 \ and 5.1 , respectively (values taken from GOLD Mine; goldmine ).

For NGC 4567/8, the dust temperature distribution is also asymmetric, with higher temperatures in NGC 4568 that are coincident with peaks in the GALEX FUV. For both NGC 4501 and NGC 4567/8, the global temperature is 20 K (and also for NGC 4567 and NGC 4568, individually; we included an additional uncertainty in the measurements to account for the overlap in emission from the two galaxies).

For NGC 4501 and NGC 4567/8, we find global dust masses of (1.2 0.3) 10^ 8 M_ and (8.6 1.8) 10^ 7 M_ , respectively ( (1.7 0.4) 10^ 7 M_ and (6.6 1.5) 10^ 7 M_ for NGC 4567 and NGC 4568 individually, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper maps SFE distributions in VV 219, finds a mass ratio above three from CO and H I data, and concludes it is an early-phase minor merger.

quotes.quote: Figures fig_SFEmol (b) and fig_SFEgas (b) show the distributions of SFE _ mol and SFE _ gas in VV 219.
Since VV 219 has a mass ratio larger than 3 estimated from both CO and H I data, VV 219 is also a minor merger.
We conclude that Arp 84 and VV 219 can be regarded as an early phase of a minor merger.

gmw_00004312.name: NGC 5515
gmw_00004312.overview: NGC 5515 is a Seyfert galaxy that exhibits double-peaked narrow-line emission in its optical spectrum [arXiv:1406.5113]. Although this emission suggested the presence of dual active galactic nuclei, high-resolution radio observations detected only a single, compact radio source at its center [arXiv:1406.5113].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5515 as a double-peaked Seyfert, detecting one compact radio source and arguing that a single AGN better explains the radio and optical data than dual AGN.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert galaxy NGC 5515 has double-peaked narrow-line emission in its optical spectrum, and it has been suggested that this could indicate that it has two active nuclei. We observed the source with high resolution Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) at two radio frequencies, reduced archival Very Large Array data, and re-analysed its optical spectrum. We detected a single, compact radio source at the position of NGC 5515, with no additional radio emission in its vicinity. The optical spectrum of the source shows that the blue and red components of the double-peaked lines have very similar characteristics. While we cannot rule out unambiguously that NGC 5515 harbours a dual AGN, the assumption of a single AGN provides a more plausible explanation for the radio observations and the optical spectrum.

Our non-detection of any additional compact source in the field of view implies an upper limit of the radio power P 2 10^ 20 W Hz ^ 1 . According to e.g. and , AGN have high-luminosity cores, with power exceeding 2 10^ 21 W Hz ^ 1 , therefore we can rule out the existence of another radio-emitting AGN in the field of view. We can thus conclude that there is no dual radio-emitting AGN in the centre of NGC 5515.

gmw_00004313.name: A1689 BCG
gmw_00004313.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy A1689 BCG is a giant elliptical cD galaxy [arXiv:1709.08642] with a large core radius of 3.8 kpc [arXiv:1710.01322]. This core structure suggests the galaxy may host a central supermassive black hole exceeding 20 billion solar masses [arXiv:1710.01322].

sources.summary: The paper studies A1689 7.1, a strongly lensed z=4.866 galaxy, using VLT spectroscopy and photometry to measure nearby IGM absorption, young stellar age, low dust, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The subject of this paper is the Ly forest in the spectrum of an unusually bright high-redshift galaxy at z = 4.866 (Frye et al. 2002, 2007). The galaxy is situated behind the massive galaxy cluster Abell 1689 ( z = 0.183 ), which magnifies the starlight of this background object by a factor of 10.3 by the effect of gravitational lensing (Broadhurst et al. 2005). Multicolor Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging of one of the faint lensed images, hereafter designated A1689 7.1 , is shown in Figure 1.

A1689 7.1 is imaged in several bands, as follows: i_ 775 = 23.10 0.01 (HST ACS), J_ 110 =23.10 0.02 (HST NICMOS), H = 23.45 0.38 and K_s = 23.45 0.35 (Son of Isaac Instrument on ESO New Technology Telescope), and P_ 3.6 =23.3 0.02 , and P_ 4.5 = 23.34 0.03 (IRAC on Spitzer Space Telecope). The observing details for these data are discussed elsewhere (Frye et al. 2007). From these data we set initial constraints on the galaxy age and dust extinction. Our high quality J_ 110 , P_ 3.6 and P_ 4.5 points indicate only a modest H I Balmer series continuum break at rest-frame 4000 and thus a young underlying stellar population dominated by O stars with a minimum age of t 10 Myr (Figure 4).

Figure fig_bc shows our best fit model to the joint variation of the parameters of dust extinction, age, and mass, with values: E(B-V) = 0.0^ +0.02 _ 0.00 , t = 100_ 14 ^ +14 Myr, and M^* = 7.3 0.70 10^8 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Abell 1689 arc ID 31.1 as a lensed high-redshift galaxy with many emission lines, direct oxygen abundance, Ly emission, and weak absorption.

quotes.quote: Abell 1689 arc ID 31.1 The arc ID 31.1 is adopted from limousin07 . For this target, a wealth of emission lines are observed as listed in Table  tab:a9.1 . From the strongest emission lines, which are not affected by telluric absorption, and also excluding we find the redshift z=1.8339 0.0003 . The last column in Table  tab:a9.1 lists the line fluxes after correcting for intrinsic extinction as described in Section  sect:ebv_gas . The \ line profile is double-peaked with a much brighter red than blue component, which is the characteristic signature of resonance scattering of photons in an outflowing medium (see Fig.   fig:Lyafit ).

We detect the temperature-sensitive [ O iii ] 4363 line. Many other rest-frame UV emission lines are identified in the Abell 1689 ID 31.1 spectrum. In particular we draw attention to the narrow emission lines from the C iv 1548,1550 doublet shown in Fig.  fig:A31.1_CIV .

sources.summary: The paper models the A1689 BCG/cD light profile, finding an unusually large 3.8 kpc core and inferring that its central black hole may exceed 2 x 10^10 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Finally, our \ fit to the light profile of the A1689 BCG implies a break radius of 3.8 kpc, among the largest known; we discuss implications of the sizable core and extensive GC population for the supermassive black hole in light of scaling relations. Our preferred model used n=7 for the \ index of the BCG and gave a break radius R_b=3.8 0.2 kpc, at the extreme high end found for break radii in core galaxies.

From our best \ fit, we obtain a core radius R_b=3.8 0.2 kpc for the A1689 cD, which would imply an extraordinary SMBH mass (4 2) 10^ 10 . However, the above empirical scaling is not constrained for R_b > 1 kpc or > 2 10^ 10 . Thus, the safest conclusion is simply that likely exceeds 2 10^ 10 \ in the A1689 BCG if its core has become depleted by the same physical process as in more nearby core galaxies for which has been measured.

For A1689, there are no other luminous nuclei or obvious dust features within the cD's large break radius. Because sizable extrapolations of the scaling relations are required in both cases, we simply conclude that >2 10^ 10 , if the core in this galaxy grew through the same processes that depleted the cores in nearby galaxies with measured SMBH masses.

sources.summary: The paper blindly recovers the multiply imaged Abell 1689 lensed galaxy at z=4.89, citing spectral confirmation, lens magnification, and its unlensed Lyman-alpha luminosity.

quotes.quote: We blindly recovered a z=4.89 galaxy in Abell 1689 that was discovered originally by fry02 . This object is multiply imaged, and the fry02 spectrum shows a strong break across the line and metal absorption lines in the continuum redward of , confirming the redshift. We estimate the magnification of this source at a factor of 7.2, within the range of 3--14 suggested by the coarser modeling of fry02 . The unlensed luminosity in the \ line is (7.4 0.7) 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy S18 as the central cD giant elliptical in Abell 1689, models its extended envelope, and tests whether it affects Fundamental Plane fits.

quotes.quote: Galaxy S18 is a cD, a giant elliptical with a typical extended envelope which is very well fit by an exponential component. Thus, its total surface-brightness distribution is best fit by a \ model.

The central cD galaxy S18 could be considered an ``outlier'' for its peculiar surface brightness distribution, but we find consistent results regardless of whether or not S18 is included in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper lists Abell 1689 among four BCGs below the Salpeter IMF comparison line, but finds averaged stellar-population models can reconcile its mass-to-light estimates.

quotes.quote: Discussion on constraining the IMF From Figure ML_ML , we can see that, for a Salpeter IMF, the majority (ten) of the BCGs fall above the 1-to-1 line, but there is a subset of four BCGs below the 1-to-1 line (Abell 754, 1689, 1763 and 1942). The BCGs above the line (with _ DYN > _ POP ) are better described by the bottom-heavy Salpeter, or an even `heavier', IMF due to a higher fraction of low mass stars. For relatively old stellar populations (as expected for passively-evolving, massive early-type galaxies), only stars with masses below 1M _ are present (see MartinNavarro2015b, Lyubenova2016 ). This limits stellar populations based IMF analysis of massive early-type galaxies to its low-mass end. As seen from Figure ML_ML , a Kroupa IMF (which has fewer stars with masses below 0.5M _ than a Salpeter IMF) describes the four BCGs below the 1-to-1 line better, and is closer to reconciling the stellar mass-to-light ratios measured from stellar population and kinematic properties.

The BCG in Abell 1689 has _ DYN of 3.60 0.09 , which is not notably lower than what we expect for passively evolving BCGs, and Figure ML_ML_AVERAGE shows that if we take the average of two stellar population method/models (ULySS/Vazdekis/MILES and FIREFLY/MaStar/E-MaStar) then the error bars on _ POP are larger and both the Salpeter and Kroupa IMF reach the 1-to-1 line reconciling the dynamical and stellar population estimates.

gmw_00004314.name: D036
gmw_00004314.overview: D036 is a compact dwarf merger candidate that exhibits a tadpole-like morphology [arXiv:2212.07022]. The galaxy features a short, faint, one-sided tail-like structure and is located in a nearly isolated environment at the edge of the small group CGCG 092-005 [arXiv:2212.07022].

sources.summary: The paper describes D036 as a compact dwarf merger candidate with tadpole-like morphology, a one-sided faint tail, and a nearly isolated location near CGCG 092-005.

quotes.quote: Given their compact nature, D036 and D075 are visually similar to tadpole galaxies identified by Straughn15 in Hubble Deep Field. D036 has a short faint tail-like structure that shows one-side elongation. All, except D047, are located in a nearly isolated environment. The D036 is located at the edge of small group CGCG 092-005 at a 445 kpc sky-projected distance from the group center.

gmw_00004315.name: Q 0528 250
gmw_00004315.overview: Q 0528 250 is a high-redshift blazar and quasar [arXiv:1501.07210, arXiv:0801.3682]. Its spectrum features a molecular hydrogen absorption system at redshift z = 2.811, which is resolved into at least two components that do not completely cover the background source [arXiv:1501.07210, arXiv:astro-ph/0309817].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the blazar Q 0528 250 spectrum, finding partial coverage by a molecular hydrogen absorber, residual saturated-line flux, and a robust two-component absorption model.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the molecular hydrogen absorption system at z_ abs =2.811 in the spectrum of the blazar Q 0528 250. We demonstrate that the molecular cloud does not cover the background source completely. The partial coverage reveals itself as a residual flux in the bottom of saturated H _2 absorption lines. This amounts to about (2.22 0.54) of the continuum and does not depend on the wavelength.

This value is small and it explains why this effect has not been detected in previous studies of this quasar spectrum. However, it is robustly detected and significantly higher than the zero flux level in the bottom of saturated lines of the Ly forest, (-0.21 0.22) per cent. The presence of the residual flux could be caused by unresolved quasar multicomponents, by light scattered by dust, and/or by jet-cloud interaction. The H _2 absorption system is very well described by a two-component model without inclusion of additional components when we take partial coverage into account.

sources.summary: The paper identifies this as the first DLA with detected H2, with UVES resolving the earlier single component into two and detecting rotational levels up to J=5.

quotes.quote: Q 0528 2505, =2.811 : This is the first DLA system in which H _2 was detected Levshakov85 . The detection of H _2 was readdressed in more detail by Foltz88 , Srianand98 and Srianand05 . The single H _2 component seen in a low resolution CASPEC spectrum Srianand98 is resolved into two components in a UVES spectrum Srianand05 . Rotational levels up to J=5 are detected.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0528--250 as a quasar sightline with a z=2.811 H2-bearing cloud; higher-resolution UVES spectra reveal complex asymmetric H2 profiles that prevent more accurate constraints.

quotes.quote: With some modifications, such measurements were performed for the z = 2.811 H _2 bearing cloud toward the quasar PKS 0528--250 in and setting the limit of | / |< 1.8 10^ 4 (1 ). Later on higher resolution spectra of this QSO obtained with the VLT/UVES revealed that the H _2 profiles at z = 2.811 are complex and consist of at least two subcomponents with v 10 km s ^ 1 . Lines arising from the low rotational levels with J = 0 and J = 1 show a red-side asymmetry -- contrary to those arising from J = 2 and J = 3 where a blue-side asymmetry is clearly seen. This complexity makes impossible higher accuracy measurements of / in this system.

gmw_00004316.name: HLSJ091828.6+514223
gmw_00004316.overview: HLSJ091828.6+514223 is a strongly lensed submillimeter galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.2429 [arXiv:1201.2908]. It is a highly luminous infrared source containing a massive reservoir of molecular gas [arXiv:1201.2908].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HLSJ091828.6+514223 as a strongly lensed z=5.2429 submillimeter galaxy with high FIR luminosity, massive molecular gas, H2O emission, and broad ionized gas flows.

quotes.quote: During our Herschel Lensing Survey (HLS) of massive galaxy clusters, we have discovered an exceptionally bright source behind the z=0.22 cluster Abell 773, which appears to be a strongly lensed submillimeter galaxy (SMG) at z=5.2429. This source is unusual compared to most other lensed sources discovered by Herschel so far, because of its higher submm flux ( 200mJy at 500 ) and its high redshift. The dominant lens is a foreground z=0.63 galaxy, not the cluster itself.

The source has a far-infrared (FIR) luminosity of L _ FIR = 1.1 10 ^ 14 / , where is the magnification factor, likely 11. We report here the redshift identification through CO lines with the IRAM-30m, and the analysis of the gas excitation, based on CO(7-6), CO(6-5), CO(5-4) detected at IRAM and the CO(2-1) at the EVLA. All lines decompose into a wide and strong red component, and a narrower and weaker blue component, 540 apart. Assuming the ultraluminous galaxy (ULIRG) CO-to-H _2 conversion ratio, the mass is 5.8 10 ^ 11 / M _ , of which one third is in a cool component.

gmw_00004317.name: IC 1959
gmw_00004317.overview: IC 1959 is an edge-on dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2309.07919, arXiv:0912.4266] that belongs to the LGG 93 group [arXiv:0912.4266]. It features active star formation across its central region [arXiv:2210.01556] and contains an undistorted disk [arXiv:0912.4266] with a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:2210.01556].

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 1959 in its MUSE dwarf-galaxy sample, requiring two pointings to cover its disk and showing scatter in the resolved metallicity analysis.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

Except for NGC 1487 and IC 1959, all galaxies were covered with one pointing with exposure times ranging between one and four hours. To cover the disk of IC 1959, two pointings were used and by combining the 2021 MUSE data with archival data from 2018 (programme 0100.B-0116, PI: Carollo), we obtained a three pointing mosaic for NGC 1487. It can be seen that overall the high _ * points correspond to high metallicities, but there is significant scatter (e.g. IC 1959).

sources.summary: IC 1959 is viewed edge-on in LGG 93; its disk appears undistorted, prior work describes active star-forming structure, and GALEX colors make it the group's bluest member.

quotes.quote: IC 1959 and NGC 1311 are viewed edge-on. In both galaxies the disk structure appears undistorted. Meurer06 describe IC 1959 and NGC 1311 as galaxies with an elongated shape mixed with gas and a large number of regions of active star formation, also prominent in H _ . The UV images of IC 1959 and NGC 1311 do not show clear evidence of bars, while they are classified as barred in the RC3 catalog.

The five members of LGG 93 have very similar total apparent magnitudes in both UV bands ( NUV_T =14.12 0.42, FUV_T =14.52 0.40) and their (FUV-NUV) colors (see Table 3) show a small dispersion (0.41 0.06), IC 1954 being the reddest galaxy (FUV-NUV=0.47 0.08) and IC 1959 the bluest (FUV-NUV=0.32 0.09). Bars are easily recognizable in the UV bands, although both NGC 1311 and IC 1959, indicated as barred galaxies in the RC3 classification, do not show a bar in our UV images.

sources.summary: The paper says IC 1959 has central star-forming clumps, while its NSC is dominated by old metal-poor mass but also shows low-level central star formation.

quotes.quote: Compared to ESO 59-01, IC 1959 shows much more star formation across its central region (Fig. fig:SFRs ). The NSC is not seen as a peak in star formation, but is located next to star-forming clumps. The velocities of the gaseous and stellar component in its spectra agree, suggesting that the ionised emission lines in the NSC spectrum are originating from low-level star formation in the NSC.

Similar to ESO 59-01, the SFH is strongly dominated by an old and metal-poor component constituting 91 of the NSC mass. Additionally, a minor young and metal-rich component is found. Consequently, also this NSC has likely formed most of its mass through GCs, but the presence of emission lines connected spatially and velocity-space to the NSC indicate additional contributions from low-level central star formation.

gmw_00004318.name: NGC 1796
gmw_00004318.overview: NGC 1796 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits high levels of star formation throughout its disk and bar region [arXiv:2210.01556]. Its nuclear star cluster is dominated by young, metal-rich stars, indicating that it built most of its mass through central star formation [arXiv:2210.01556].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1796 among the tightest metallicity-stellar-mass correlations, while its central bar does not follow the usual low-metallicity high-SFR pattern.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

Figure fig:SFR-sigma_9 shows an overall correlation between _ SFR _ * . Observational limitations do not allow for decisive conclusions in the cases of ESO 59-01, UGC 5889 and UGC 8041. Moreover, the different behaviours within NGC 4592, observed qualitatively in Fig. fig:amazing , lead to significant scatter and no statistical evidence for an overall correlation in this galaxy. It can be seen that the trends are not the same for all galaxies. NGC 1796, NGC 4592, UGC 8041 appear to have the tightest correlations in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1796 as the most massive sample galaxy, with high disk and bar star formation and an NSC mainly built through central star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 1796 is the most massive galaxy in our sample and shows high levels of star formation throughout its disk and bar region. The NSC is located in the bar and while it does not appear as a separable peak of star formation, the velocities of the gaseous and stellar components in the NSC agree, indicating that the star formation occurs within the NSC. The NSC in NGC 1796 is the most metal-rich of the sample and it is dominated by young ages. The fraction of old, metal-poor stars is 19 . For these reasons, we conclude that the NGC 1796's NSC has build most of its mass through central star formation.

gmw_00004319.name: NGC 853
gmw_00004319.overview: NGC 853 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2309.07919] with an asymmetric inner disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy hosts a massive nuclear star cluster dominated by old, metal-poor stars, alongside a younger, metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:2210.01556].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 853 as one of the lowest-mass sample galaxies, with significant scatter and regions showing a metallicity-stellar-mass anti-correlation.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

For the lowest mass galaxies (NGC 853 and UGC 3755), the points present significant scatter, but also a different trend with regions that show a Z_ g _ * anti-correlation, which leads to overall negative correlation coefficients. NGC 1487 introduces the largest scatter of all the galaxies in the sample. Given that this galaxy is undergoing a merger, we propose that the characteristics of this galaxy might complicate the analysis in this case.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 853 as the least massive sample galaxy, with a massive NSC dominated by old metal-poor stars but also shaped by recent enriched star formation.

quotes.quote: According to mass measurements from the literature, NGC 853 is the least massive galaxy of our sample, but has a massive NSC for its mass that constitutes 22 of the total stellar mass (see Table tab:galaxies ). The NSC of NGC 853 is located adjacent to a region with strong star formation, but the gas and stellar velocities show a significant offset of v = 8.8 2.1 km s ^ 1 , suggesting that the emission lines in its spectrum are not originating from currently ongoing star formation in the NSC. The SFH of NGC 853's NSC is complex and extended, with a dominant peak at ages 5 Gyr and low metallicities, but it is not as metal-poor as the NSCs of ESO 59-01 or IC 1959, for example. The old and metal-poor components ( > 2 Gyr, < -1.0 dex) make up 80 of the NSC mass. Additionally, the NSC shows the presence of a young, metal-rich population.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0853 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 0853 : J021141.5-091817 The inner region shows no clear nucleus but many regions, so the centering is done from the outer isophotes. The inner disk is slightly asymmetric with an additional light patch in the western part, therefore mean ellipticity and PA difficult to fix. Almost continuously upbending profile beyond the peak at 15 , which includes all regions, getting flat at 42 .

gmw_00004320.name: UGC 8041
gmw_00004320.overview: UGC 8041 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits spiral arms with active star-forming knots [arXiv:2309.07919, arXiv:2210.01556]. Star formation is also present at the position of its nuclear star cluster, which contains both old, metal-poor and young, enriched stellar populations [arXiv:2210.01556].

sources.summary: The paper includes UGC 8041 from the ESO archive, notes observational limitations in one analysis, but finds it among the tightest metallicity-stellar-mass correlations.

quotes.quote: Our sample contains a total of nine galaxies: ESO 59-01, IC 1959, NGC 1487, NGC 1796, NGC 853, UGC 3755, UGC 5889, NGC 4592 and UGC 8041. The first seven were observed in 2020 and 2021 as part of the MUSE programme 0108.B-0904 (PI: Fahrion) making use of adaptive optics-supported wide-field mode. The last two were retrieved from the ESO Science Archive. The nine galaxies display different morphologies and NGC 1487 deserves a special note given that it is an ongoing merger (see e.g. buzzo_et_al_2021 for a more detailed description of this galaxy).

Figure fig:SFR-sigma_9 shows an overall correlation between _ SFR _ * . Observational limitations do not allow for decisive conclusions in the cases of ESO 59-01, UGC 5889 and UGC 8041. Moreover, the different behaviours within NGC 4592, observed qualitatively in Fig. fig:amazing , lead to significant scatter and no statistical evidence for an overall correlation in this galaxy. It can be seen that the trends are not the same for all galaxies. NGC 1796, NGC 4592, UGC 8041 appear to have the tightest correlations in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 8041 has star-forming knots including the NSC position, and its NSC contains both old metal-poor and young enriched populations.

quotes.quote: UGC 8041 shows knots of star formation along its spiral arms as well as at the NSC position. Since the gas and stellar velocities agree, it is likely that the star formation originates from within the NSC. The NSC SFH exhibits two populations, an older, metal-poor comprising 48 of the mass and a young and enriched population. Based on the ongoing star formation and the presence of both old and young stars, we conclude that both channels have contributed significantly to the formation of this NSC.

gmw_00004321.name: SMM J030227.73
gmw_00004321.overview: SMM J030227.73 is a galaxy at redshift z=1.408 that exhibits active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1302.2145]. Its structure features a bright, compact near-infrared continuum source and a diffuse hydrogen-alpha bridge containing several kinematically offset clumps [arXiv:1302.2145].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMM J030227.73 as a z=1.408 SMG with AGN signatures, compact near-infrared and H emission, diffuse bridged H emission, and kinematically offset clumps.

quotes.quote: SMM J030227.73 (aka CUDSS 3.15) was first detected and identified by webb03a . Optical and near-IR spectra by chapman05 and swinbank04 place this galaxy at z=1.408 , with a high [ NII ] /H ratio ( [ NII ] /H = 1.38 0.07 ; swinbank04 ) suggesting the presence of AGN activity. harrison12 also identify spatially-unresolved ( 3.6 kpc), broad emission (FWHM =900 300 km s^ 1 ) in galaxy-integrated [ OIII ] emission for this object. Furthermore, Spitzer mid-IR spectroscopic follow-up of this object shows a substantial excess in mid-IR continuum, suggesting a significant AGN contribution ( md09 ; see also hainline09 ).

The near-IR continuum map of SMM J030227.73 in Fig. c3_15maps1 shows that continuum emission is dominated by a bright, compact source, with a much fainter secondary component 1.3 ( 11 kpc) to the South-West. With a high [ NII ] /H ratio, this secondary component has been tentatively identified with an AGN by swinbank06 .

In Fig. c3_15maps2 , we show the H line-width ( _ H ) and velocity maps for SMM J030227.73 with overlaid H flux contours. We also show the 1D H spectra extracted from distinct spatial regions: the bright point source and 4 distinct clumps within the diffuse H emission, 3 of which lie in the H bright bridge region extending to the South-West.

gmw_00004322.name: SMM J123549.44
gmw_00004322.overview: SMM J123549.44 is a high-redshift galaxy at z=2.203 [arXiv:1302.2145] that is classified as AGN-dominated [arXiv:1101.4027] and exhibits an excess of hot dust continuum emission [arXiv:1302.2145]. Its compact central region displays broad hydrogen-alpha line emission [arXiv:1302.2145], surrounded by asymmetrical narrow-line clumps that suggest a disturbed structure rather than a well-behaved disk [arXiv:1302.2145].

sources.summary: The paper describes SMM J123549.44 as a z=2.203 SMG with strong AGN evidence, compact CO and H emission, broad-line outflow signatures, and possible merger-related clumps.

quotes.quote: SMM J123549.44 Ultra-deep Chandra observations of this source have revealed clear AGN signatures ( alexander05 ). Furthermore, recent mid-IR spectroscopic md09 and photometric observations hainline11 have revealed an excess of hot dust continuum emission, in agreement with the hypothesis of a significant contribution from AGN activity to the bolometric luminosity. tacconi06, tacconi08 have also undertaken high-resolution CO observations of this galaxy and find CO emission dominated by a compact source ( 0.5 ) with a prominent double-peaked CO profile, which they associate with the orbital motions of gas within a disk close to an AGN. Optical and near-IR (long-slit) spectroscopy set this object at z=2.203 chapman05, swinbank04 .

Fig. 12t1maps1 shows that the H emission for SMM J123549.44 is dominated by a compact source with FWHM 0.24 (3 kpc), though faint traces of diffuse emission extend out to 0.5 ( 4 kpc) to the South-West of the central region. Our observations unveil the H line-width distribution shown in Fig. 12t1maps2 , allowing us to clearly distinguish broad H line emission arising from a bright compact region, with FWHM =1260 210 km s ^ 1 and a velocity offset of 150 50 km s ^ 1 from the redshift reported by swinbank04 .

The velocity map shown in Fig. 12t1maps2 suggests internal velocity offsets of 100-200 km s ^ 1 , consistent with the velocities reported by tacconi08 . Although these velocities could reflect the ordered motions of a gaseous disk, resulting in a total dynamical mass M _ dyn = ^2R/G 4 10^ 10 M _ , the asymmetrical disposition of the narrow-line H bright clumps around the broad-line region suggests that this is not a well-behaved disk structure with an AGN at its center.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SMM J123549+6215 as the AGN-dominated source HDF 76, detected in CO and atomic carbon, with measured 1 mm continuum but no higher-transition carbon line.

quotes.quote: SMM J123549+6215 This source is sometimes also referred to as HDF 76 (S _ 850 m =8.3 2.5 mJy, Chapman et al. \ 2003) and has been detected in CO emission ( J =3 and J =6: Tacconi et al. \ 2006, 2008, J =1: Ivison et al. \ 2010a). SMM J123549+6215 is X--ray bright (Alexander et al. \ 2005a) and its luminosity is classified as AGN--dominated (see also Alexander et al. \ 2005b, Takata et al. \ 2006). We adopt a FWZI of 800 \ for SMM J123549+6215. The \ line is detected both in individual channel maps (Figure 3) and in the integrated line emission (Figure 4, left) with a total flux of 1.1 0.21 Jy .

The continuum at 2 mm is not detected at a 3 flux density limit of S _ 153 GHz < 0.78 mJy (Figure 4, middle). The 1 mm continuum emission is detected at S _ 252.8 GHz =1.7 0.2 mJy (Figure 4, right). This is only marginally consistent with the tentative detection quoted by Tacconi et al. \ 2006 (S _ 213 GHz =2.0 0.6 mJy, the extrapolated flux at 213 GHz would be 1 mJy), and within the expectations based on the extrapolation from the SCUBA measurement (Table 2).

gmw_00004323.name: SMM J163650.43
gmw_00004323.overview: SMM J163650.43 is a high-redshift galaxy merger at z ~ 2.38 [arXiv:1302.2145, arXiv:2004.09356] that consists of a pair of blue and red galaxies [arXiv:1101.4027]. It hosts a Seyfert-like active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1302.2145] and exhibits an arc-shaped hydrogen emission structure [arXiv:1302.2145] alongside strong radio luminosity [arXiv:2004.09356].

sources.summary: The paper presents SMM J163650.43 as an unusually luminous z~2.38 SMG with Seyfert-like AGN activity, complex arc-shaped H emission, multiple components, and velocity offsets among regions.

quotes.quote: SMM J163650.43 This galaxy was first detected by scott02 and ivison02 . Its broad-band near-IR emission has been studied in detail by smail03 . With a bolometric luminosity L _ bol = (3 2) 10^ 13 L _ , this galaxy is unusually luminous even for an SMG ( chapman03 ). Optical and near-IR spectroscopy revealed a redshift of z 2.38 chapman03 , unveiled narrow H emission with an underlying broad H component (FWHM _ rest 310 50 and 1750 240 km s ^ 1 , respectively; swinbank04 ) and a high [ OIII ] /H line ratio characteristic of a Seyfert AGN ( smail03 ).

This galaxy has been resolved into different components, revealing a complex structure smail03, swinbank05, swinbank06 . In particular, based on recent seeing-limited Gemini-North NIFS observations, harrison12 report intricate extensions of narrow [ OIII ] emission ( 100 FWHM 500 km s ^ 1 ) and two kinematically-distinct broad [ OIII ] emission lines (FWHM 1200 km s ^ 1 ). Furthermore, sub-arcsecond resolution ( 0.25-0.5 ) CO observations of SMM J163650.43 undertaken by tacconi08 show a two-peaked CO profile emission restricted to an elliptical region with an intrinsic FWHM size of 0.8 0.2 0.4 0.3 .
As shown in Fig. 16t3maps1 we find H emission in the shape of an arc, extending over 5 7 kpc ^2 and tracing the continuum emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds no compact VLBI emission from J163650.43, but describes it as a close binary merger with strong radio luminosity and other AGN-host indicators.

quotes.quote: J163650.43 was not detected in our VLBI observations with an implied 3- brightness temperature limit of < 3.5 10 ^ 5 K and it is located at a redshift of 2.378 chapman2005 . This source has a S _ 850 m and S _ 350 m of 8.2 1.7 mJy and 45.9 2.9 mJy; this yields a q^ 850 m _ 1.4 GHz and q^ 350 m _ 1.4 GHz of 1.57 0.10 and 2.32 0.04, respectively (see Table t1 ). \ Additionally, both seem to follow the FIR-radio correlation.

The source recovers < 22 of its 1.4 GHz flux; the non-detection on VLBI scales may suggest that a significant fraction of the radio emission is associated with star-formation processes. This is supported by other measurements engel2010 classify this source as a close binary galaxy merger because the CO(3-2) and CO(7-6) data show two peaks separated by 3 kpc.

However, J163650.43 has a total 1.4 GHz VLA flux density of 221.0 16.0 Jy corresponding to a luminosity of 1.02 0.07 10 ^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 . This value can only be reached by an AGN or extremely powerful star-forming galaxies with a SFR of 1.42 0.10 10 ^4 M_ yr^ 1 . The high luminosity suggests that similar to 131225.73, this source may host extended radio jet emission associated with an AGN jarvis2019, muxlow2005 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SMM J163650+4057 as Elias N2 850.4, a CO-studied AGN pair of blue and red galaxies with no detected carbon line but slightly extended 1 mm continuum.

quotes.quote: SMM J163650+4057 This source is also known as Elias N2 850.4 (S _ 850 m =8.2 1.7 mJy, Ivison et al. \ 2002) and first CO data ( J =3 and J =7) have been presented by Neri et al. \ (2003) In Neri et al. \ (2003) this source is referred to as SMM J16368+4057 ( J =1 data are shown in Ivison et al. \ 2010a). Improved CO data are shown in Tacconi et al. \ (2006, 2008, J =3 and J =6). The AGN broad lines of this source (which in fact is a pair of blue and red galaxies, Ivison et al. \ 2002) are discussed in Swinbank et al. \ (2005, see also Takata et al. \ 2006, Men e ndez-Delmestre 2007, 2009).

The \ line is not detected in our data with a 3 upper limit across the expected linewidth ( 1000 ) of 0.7 Jy \ (Figure 5, left, but see caption of Table 2). Likewise, the continuum at 2 mm is not detected at a 3 flux density limit of S _ 145 GHz < 0.74 mJy (Figure 5, middle). The 1 mm continuum emission appears to be slightly extended with S _ 239.1 GHz =3.7 0.4 mJy (peak flux: 2.8 0.18 mJy, Figure 5, right), in rough agreement with our SCUBA extrapolation and with Neri et al. \ (2003) and Tacconi et al. \ (2006) who quote consistent 1 mm flux densities for this source (S _ 230 GHz =2.5 0.4 mJy and 2.6 0.5 mJy, respectively).

gmw_00004324.name: BCG1
aliases_external: Abell 1351 BCG1 / Barrena et al. ID 66
gmw_00004324.overview: BCG1 is the brightest cluster galaxy of the Abell 1351 cluster, located in its northern region [arXiv:1405.4707]. The galaxy is visible in the radio band [arXiv:2207.02850] and closely coincides with the cluster's X-ray emission peak [arXiv:1405.4707, arXiv:2207.02850].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as Abell 1351's brightest cluster galaxy near the X-ray and mass peaks, located in the northern region and associated with the main subcluster structure.

quotes.quote: The position of the brightest cluster member galaxy (ID. 66 with ^ =17.01 , hereafter BCG1) is very close to the peak of the X-ray emission and the peak of the mass distribution provided by dah02 .

We adopted as centre position the coordinates of the brightest cluster galaxy -- BCG1 [ R.A. = 11^ h 42^ m 24 78 , Dec. = +58 32 05.3 (J2000.0) ] , which coincides with the location of the peak of the X-ray emission. However, note that A1351 is an elongated cluster with BCG1 lying in the northern region (see in the following).

BCG2 and BCG1 bright galaxies are placed along the N--S direction. In addition, a few other quite luminous members (IDs. 55, 56, 76, 79) are aligned following the same axis. Moreover, we detected a velocity gradient in the galaxy velocity field roughly in S--N direction and the presence of 3D substructure at a very significant level. The above results indicate that the cluster structure extends along the LOS but is not completely aligned with it. In particular, the two main subclusters, corresponding to BCG1 and BCG2 and detected as two northern X-ray clumps, cause the bimodality of the velocity distribution, low and high velocity peaks respectively (see Fig. figgauss ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as one of Abell 1351's two brightest cluster galaxies, coincident with the X-ray peak, radio visible, and offset from the bright radio edge.

quotes.quote: GMRT 610 MHz Optical study showed the cluster has two main sub-cluster in the northern region surrounding the two brightest cluster galaxies BCG1 and BCG2 ( Barrena_2014MNRAS.442.2216B ). BCG1 also coincides with the X-ray peak. BCG1 and few other galaxies are vissible in radio band, but BCG2 does not have visible radio emission.
The brightest region of the edge is located 470 kpc away from BCG1 and 290 kpc away from source TG. The diffuse emission is more extented in the northern region surrounding BCG1 and BCG2 at 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00004325.name: SWIFT J003119.8+072454
gmw_00004325.overview: SWIFT J003119.8+072454 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1111.3514] classified as a high-energy-peaked blazar [arXiv:1111.3514, arXiv:1110.6868]. The source positionally coincides with a radio counterpart and an optical counterpart [arXiv:1111.3514, arXiv:1110.6868], and it exhibits temporal variability [arXiv:1111.3514, arXiv:1110.6868].

sources.summary: The paper treats SWIFT J003119.8+072454 and its NVSS radio counterpart as the likeliest conventional HBL explanation for the gamma-ray candidate, though a dark subhalo origin remains possible.

quotes.quote: Within the updated positional uncertainty of derived from the 24-month data (Fig. fig:skyplot ), the most likely radio counterpart is NVSS J003119+072456 ( f_ 1.4 GHz = (11.6 0.6 ) mJy), which positionally coincides with the newly discovered hard X-ray source SWIFT J003119.8+072454 ( 1.6 ). Note that corresponding to the notation of Table tab:swift_srcs , the Swift source is flagged with a G in Fig. fig:skyplot . The energy flux observed between 0.2 and 2 keV is 2 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Table tab:swift_srcs ). Additionally, an optical counterpart of the radio and X-ray source is listed in the SDSS catalogue ( = 17.4^ m ), see Table tab:swift_srcs_cp .

The SED is normalised to the radio flux of NVSS J003119+072456 (at 5 GHz). For comparison, the spectral measurements of the optical and X-ray counterparts are presented as well. Within the observational errors and assuming temporal variability, the ray spectrum of is consistent with the model prediction. Furthermore, the spectral index of the X-ray source agrees with an HBL, while its flux is fainter than predicted for an (average) HBL.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J003119+072456 as a viable radio counterpart to the Fermi source, coincident with Swift X-ray and SDSS optical sources, and says its SED and indices favor a high-energy-peaked blazar scenario.

quotes.quote: Within the updated positional information on (see Sect. sect:Fermi_data ), the radio source NVSS J003119+072456 ( f_ 1.4 GHz = (11.6 0.6 ) mJy) provides a viable counterpart. The radio source positionally coincides with the newly discovered hard X-ray source SWIFT J003119.8+072454 ( = 1.6 0.3 ), energy flux 2 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 between 0.2 and 2 keV, and the optical counterpart SDSS J003119.71+072453.5 ( m() = 17.4^ m ). Normalized to the radio flux, the average spectral energy distribution of a high-energy-peaked blazar (HBL, see ) fits the data, assuming standard temporal variability of the source. Reinforcing, the HBL scenario is favored by the spectral indices of the X- and ray signal.

gmw_00004326.name: host of GRB 130427A
gmw_00004326.overview: The host of GRB 130427A is a moderately star-forming, possibly interacting disk galaxy [arXiv:1702.04762, arXiv:1307.5338]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10^9 solar masses [arXiv:1305.6832] and a star-formation rate of about 1 to 2 solar masses per year [arXiv:1305.6832, arXiv:1307.5338].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 130427A host as an SDSS DR12 galaxy, describes it as a moderately star-forming possibly interacting disk, and reports blue color and luminosity.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy suggested that SDSS DR12 galaxy object 1237667431180861948 was the host galaxy of the GRB. This was subsequently confirmed by the close agreement in redshift between absorption lines in the GRB spectrum 2013GCN..14455...1L,2013GCN..14478...1X,2013GCN..14491...1F and emission lines from the galaxy 2013ApJ...776...98X . obtained HST images of the afterglow and host galaxy. They suggested that the host is a moderately star-forming, possibly interacting, disk galaxy and the GRB occured about 0.8 arcsec (4 kpc) from the nucleus. Table tab:host reports our RATIR griZYJH aperture magnitudes from 2016 and magnitudes from the SDSS DR12 image using the same apertures and calibrating stars.

The magnitudes from our image are consistent with the magnitudes from the SDSS image, but have lower uncertainties. We can estimate the rest-frame g-i color and M_i magnitude from our observed Y color and Y magnitude (see Table tabla:bandas for the correspondence between rest-frame and observed bands) assuming a CDM model with a H_0=67.8 km/Mpc/s 2014A&A...571A...1P . We obtain a rest-frame g-i=0.23 0.08 and a rest-frame M_i=-19.91 0.07 .

sources.summary: The paper models the host of GRB 130427A from SDSS photometry, deriving its luminosity, stellar mass, star-formation rate, starburst age, and emission-line metallicity, and placing it within the normal field-galaxy mass-metallicity relation.

quotes.quote: We use the catalogued pre-explosion imaging from the SDSS Data Release Aihara2011 to estimate physical properties of the GRB host galaxy and to build a physical model of the stellar emission. The SDSS ugriz photometry was fitted within LePhare Arnouts1999,Ilbert2006 using stellar population synthesis models from Bruzual2003 as detailed in Kruhler2011 . Based on the model fit to the data (Figure spectrum ), we derive a luminosity M_B = -19.8 0.2 mag, a stellar mass of M_ = 10^ 9.0 0.2 M_ , a star-formation rate SFR_ SED = 2_ 1 ^ +5 M_ yr ^ 1 and an age of the starburst of = 400_ 250 ^ +560 Myr for the host of GRB 130427A.

Host galaxy emission lines are detected above the SN continuum, including [ N 2 ] 6584 in the GTC spectrum, albeit at low significance. This allows us to place constraints on the metallicity of the explosion host environment by using the calibrations in Pettini2004 . We measure ( O/H ) + 12 = 8.43 0.07 and 8.51 0.09 by using the O3N2 and the N2 methods, respectively (statistical errors only), which is at the top-right of the GRB-SN range in the metallicity M_ B, host plane and similar to the cases of SNe Ic-BL without observed GRBs and SNe Ib+IIb (see Fig. 2 in Modjaz2011 ). We note that the host galaxy also nicely lies within the 1 dispersion of the mass-metallicity relation for normal field galaxies Kewley2008 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the host of GRB 130427A as a moderately star-forming, possibly interacting disk galaxy whose luminosity, size, morphology, and burst offset resemble the GRB 980425/SN 1998bw host.

quotes.quote: We find that the burst originated 4 kpc from the nucleus of a moderately star forming (1 M _ yr ^ 1 ), possibly interacting disc galaxy. The absolute magnitude, physical size and morphology of this galaxy, as well as the location of the GRB within it are also strikingly similar to those of GRB980425/SN 1998bw.

The magnitude of the galaxy in a large aperture at late times after the afterglow/SNe has significantly faded is F336W(AB)=22.84 0.07 at a rest-frame wavelength of approximately 2500 . This corresponds to a UV star formation rate of 1.1 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 (error statistical only), broadly in agreement with that inferred from the SDSS observed SED of the galaxy of 2^ +5 _ 1 M _ yr ^ 1 xu13 , and suggesting a relatively (but not extremely) low specific star formation rate by the standards of GRB hosts svensson2010 .

It is intriguing to note that the GRB host galaxy with the closest properties to that of GRB 130427A/SN 2013cq is in fact the host of SN 1998bw. It is also a rare example of a spiral galaxy, in which the GRB occurs at a moderately large offset from the nucleus, and from the strongest region of star formation within the host galaxy. The host galaxy appears to be a disk galaxy of moderate luminosity and star formation rate, whose overall characteristics are consistent with those of the GRB host population at large.

gmw_00004327.name: NIC J141725.66+522652.3
gmw_00004327.overview: NIC J141725.66+522652.3 is an emission-line galaxy at a redshift of approximately z=1.70 [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073]. It appears to be a disturbed system or a close pair of galaxies that may be interacting with its neighbor, NIC J141725.71+522652.9 [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073].

sources.summary: The paper describes this H-emission candidate as a possible two-component disturbed system, close enough to NIC J141725.71+522652.9 that interaction may be occurring.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The naming convention for sources follows IAU recommendations, and uses J2000 coordinates. NIC J141725.66+522652.3

quotes.quote: NIC J141725.66+522652.3: A 3.5 feature at 1.77 m may be H at z=1.70 . The NICMOS direct image of this object (see Figure  imgs1 ) appears to show two components. It is possible that this is a close pair of galaxies, or a single disturbed system. In addition, this galaxy is only separated by 1'' from NIC J141725.71+522652.9 and may be interacting with that galaxy as well.

sources.summary: The paper reports likely H emission at z=1.71 and interprets the galaxy as a probable interaction partner whose high star formation may be interaction-induced.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The naming convention for sources follows IAU recommendations, and uses J2000 coordinates. NIC J141725.71+522652.9

quotes.quote: NIC J141725.71+522652.9: A 6.5 feature at 1.78 m may be H at z=1.71 . This galaxy may be interacting with NIC J141725.66+522652.3. It is possible that the observed H emission may be related to any interaction, and estimated star formation rates in both galaxies are high (assuming no AGN component to the source of ionising radiation). See Figure  imgs1 .

gmw_00004328.name: NIC J141727.34+522647.0
gmw_00004328.overview: NIC J141727.34+522647.0 is a galaxy at a redshift of 1.54 that appears to consist of two components [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073]. It is likely an interacting pair of galaxies, with interaction-induced star formation producing its observed hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073].

sources.summary: The paper says this z=1.54 H-emission galaxy appears as two components, probably an interacting pair with interaction-induced star formation.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The naming convention for sources follows IAU recommendations, and uses J2000 coordinates. NIC J141727.34+522647.0

quotes.quote: NIC J141727.34+522647.0: A redshift of 1.54 for this galaxy, calculated assuming the strong emission feature at 1.67 m is H , is confirmed by a ground-based optical spectrum (the DEEP consortium, private communication). This galaxy is visible in a WFPC2 image of CFRS 14.1496 presented by their Figure 7, the object directly beneath CFRS 14.1496 Gla:99 . The WFPC2 image shows that this object appears to consist of two components. Given the proximity of the components and the presence of H in emission, it is probable that this is a pair of interacting galaxies, with the observed H emission possibly a product of interaction-induced star formation for example Ken:98 .

gmw_00004329.name: NIC J141754.00+522923.9
gmw_00004329.overview: NIC J141754.00+522923.9 is an early-type spiral galaxy with a visible disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073]. It lies approximately 30 kiloparsecs from a nearby galaxy at a similar redshift, suggesting the two systems may be interacting [arXiv:astro-ph/0009073].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a weak H feature, notes a visible disk suggesting an early-type spiral, and suggests association or interaction with a nearby similar-redshift galaxy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The naming convention for sources follows IAU recommendations, and uses J2000 coordinates. NIC J141754.00+522923.9

quotes.quote: NIC J141754.00+522923.9: A weak ( 2 ) feature at 1.31 m may be H at z=0.99 . This is supported by a ground-based optical redshift for this object (the DEEP consortium, private communication). A disk is visible in the NICMOS direct image of this galaxy, and it may be an early-type spiral. Additionally, it is about 5'' from NIC J141753.47+522922.2 at a similar redshift. This corresponds to a separation of 30 kpc.
They may be associated, possibly interacting.

gmw_00004330.overview: 0022+0150 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1003.5600]. Its broad-absorption line features have weakened over time to mini-broad-absorption line status, showing possible velocity-structure changes [arXiv:1003.5600].

sources.summary: The paper treats 0022+0150 as a BAL quasar whose C IV absorption weakened to mini-BAL status and may show possible velocity-structure changes.

quotes.quote: The BAL absorption for one source, LBQS 0022+0150, has weakened and may now be classified as a mini-BAL. In the first case, 0022+0150 (Figure 5d), there are three relatively weak (by BAL standards) absorption features at 1430, 1490, and 1520  . The strongest feature, at 1520  , has weakened over time. Additionally, there is an absorption feature on the blue side of the Ly line (Figure  specAndModelFig ).

If the trough components have truly accelerated in these cases, the change in velocity is 1000 km s ^ 1 over 5.6 yr (0022+0150) and 6.0 yr (1240+1607) for these two sources. The absorption in 0022+0150 did not fully disappear; instead, the absorption weakened to mini-BAL status.

gmw_00004331.overview: The object 1235+1453 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1003.5600]. It exhibits variable, blue ultraviolet continuum emission that appears relatively unabsorbed by its broad-absorption line outflow [arXiv:1003.5600].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1235+1453 as an outlier BAL quasar with unusual blue continuum variability that appears relatively unabsorbed by the BAL outflow.

quotes.quote: Another source, 1235+1453, shows evidence of variable, blue continuum emission that is relatively unabsorbed by the BAL outflow. One of the sources, 1235+1453, is an outlier in several of our analyses. The C IV BAL of this source is dramatically weaker in the Palomar epoch. Figure  plotCompare1235+1453Fig compares the LBQS, SDSS, and HET spectra to the Palomar spectrum of 1235+1453.

The existing photometry and spectra for 1235+1453 are consistent with the presence of a very blue ultraviolet continuum component that declines over time and is not absorbed by the BAL outflow. The differences in the Palomar-epoch BAL are due to changes in the absorption trough depth (by 20--25 of the continuum level), rather than changes in the absorption profile. A variable, blue continuum component would also explain the substantial changes in the (rest-frame) UV spectral shape of 1235+1453 as well as the fainter g band magnitude at the epoch of the SDSS spectrum relative to that of the Palomar spectrum ( 0.6 mag fainter over a rest-frame interval of 4 yr).

gmw_00004332.overview: 1240+1607 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1003.5600]. The object exhibits complex absorption variation, including possible velocity-structure changes in its C IV absorption trough over a period of six years [arXiv:1003.5600].

sources.summary: The paper flags 1240+1607 as a BAL quasar with complex absorption variation and possible C IV velocity-structure changes over about six years.

quotes.quote: The second candidate for absorption acceleration is 1240+1607 (Figure 5j). While narrower absorption features match up well at 1440, 1525, and 1535  , the broader trough from 1490-1510   \ has shifted blueward by about 5  \ over time. In this case, the absorption in the Palomar epoch from 1510-1540   \ also follows a somewhat different pattern than the LBQS and HET epochs, suggesting that the absorption variation in this source may be particularly complex. These examples demonstrate issues that complicate a definitive search for BAL acceleration.
If the trough components have truly accelerated in these cases, the change in velocity is 1000 km s ^ 1 over 5.6 yr (0022+0150) and 6.0 yr (1240+1607) for these two sources.

gmw_00004333.name: SDSSJ0841 + 3921
gmw_00004333.overview: SDSSJ0841 + 3921 is a rare quadruple active galactic nucleus system embedded in a giant Lyman-alpha nebula [arXiv:1505.03786]. The system is located within an exceptionally overdense protocluster environment at redshift z~2 [arXiv:1505.03786].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSSJ0841 + 3921 as a rare quadruple-AGN system in a giant Ly nebula and exceptionally overdense z~2 protocluster environment.

quotes.quote: The giant nebula is only one of the exceptional properties of SDSSJ 0841 + 3921. Our images reveal three relatively compact candidate Ly emitting sources with faint continuum magnitudes V 23-24 , embedded in the Ly filament and roughly aligned with its major axis. Follow-up spectroscopy reveals that the sources labeled AGN1, AGN2, and AGN3 are three AGN at the same redshift as the f/g quasar (see right panel of Fig.  and ), making this system the only quadruple AGN known. Adopting recent measurements of small-scale quasar clustering , we estimate that the probability of finding three AGN around a quasar with such small separations is 10^ 7 .

To test this hypothesis, we constructed a catalog of Ly emitting galaxies (LAEs), and computed the cumulative overdensity profile of LAEs around SDSSJ0841 + 3921, relative to the background number expected based on the LAE luminosity function (see Fig.  ). In addition to the overdensity of four AGN, the high number of LAEs surrounding make it one of the most overdense protoclusters known at z 2-3 .

gmw_00004334.name: AGN1
gmw_00004334.overview: AGN1 is an active galactic nucleus located at the same redshift as a nearby foreground quasar [arXiv:1505.03786]. It exhibits a large velocity offset of approximately 1300 kilometers per second relative to this quasar, indicating extreme gravitational motion within a collapsed structure [arXiv:1505.03786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGN1 as one of three companion AGN at the foreground quasar redshift, with a large velocity offset interpreted as gravitational motion.

quotes.quote: Follow-up spectroscopy reveals that the sources labeled AGN1, AGN2, and AGN3 are three AGN at the same redshift as the f/g quasar (see right panel of Fig.  and ), making this system the only quadruple AGN known. Specifically, AGN1 is offset from the precisely determined systemic redshift of the f/g quasar by +1300 400 . This large velocity offset cannot be explained by Hubble expansion -- the miniscule probability of finding a quadruple quasar in the absence of clustering P 10^ 13 and the physical association between the AGN and giant nebula demand that the four AGN reside in a real collapsed structure -- and thus provides an unambiguous evidence for extreme gravitational motions.

gmw_00004335.name: 2MASX J11595215+5532053
gmw_00004335.overview: 2MASX J11595215+5532053 is an early-type brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1605.02773]. Its stellar mass profile is modeled as a de Vaucouleurs profile with an effective radius of 9.83 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1605.02773].

sources.summary: The paper models the central BCG's stellar mass separately from the fossil cluster halo, measuring its K-band mass-to-light ratio and importance to the mass profile.

quotes.quote: We represent the stellar mass of the cluster using the K band light profile of the BCG (2MASX J11595215+5532053) from the Two Micron All-Sky Survey (2MASS) as listed in the Extended Source Catalog  jarr00a ; i.e., an n=4 Sersic model (i.e., de Vaucouleurs) with r_e=9.83  kpc and L_K=1.03 10^ 12 L_ . Additional (poorly constrained) stellar mass contributions from non-central galaxies and intracluster light are treated as a systematic error in sys.stars .
