The BCG stellar mass component is clearly detected with a K band stellar mass-to-light ratio, M_ /L_K = 0.61 0.11 M_ /L_ , consistent with stellar population synthesis models for a Milky-Way IMF. The detection of a plausible stellar BCG mass component distinct from the NFW DM halo in the total gravitational potential suggests that 10^ 14 M_ represents the mass scale above which dissipation is unimportant in the formation of the central regions of galaxy clusters. The stellar mass of the BCG is clearly required by the model fits and yields a K band stellar mass-to-light ratio, M_ /L_K = 0.61 0.11 M_ /L_ , consistent with stellar population synthesis models for a Milky-Way IMF.

gmw_00004336.name: SBS 1436 + 529A
gmw_00004336.overview: SBS 1436 + 529A is an irregular galaxy characterized by clumpy H II regions [arXiv:2002.00393]. It is described as a merging system with two nuclei separated by 0.9 kiloparsecs, and it exhibits a sub-solar oxygen abundance [arXiv:2002.00393].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBS 1436 + 529A as SN 2019gsc's irregular, clumpy, merging host, gives its velocity and distance, and estimates low host-galaxy oxygen abundance and minimal reddening.

quotes.quote: The SN is located in the irregular galaxy SBS 1436 + 529A (other name: PGC052275), see Fig. fig:map , which is characterized by the presence of clumpy H ii regions, similarly to SN 2008ha's host-galaxy UGG 12682. The galaxy is described as a merging system, with two nuclei separated by 3.8 arcsec, which corresponds to 0.9 kpc h2008 . A heliocentric recessional velocity of 3384 3 km s ^ 1 for SBS 1436 + 529A is reported in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) estimated from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 3 http://www.sdss.org/dr3/products/spectra/getspectra.html observations.

Adopting H_ 0 = 73.2 1.7 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 riess2016 and corrections for peculiar motions due to influences of the Virgo cluster, the Great Attractor, and the Shapley supercluster mould2000 , we obtain a Hubble flow distance and distance modulus to the host of SN 2019gsc of 53.4 3.7 Mpc and = 33.64 0.15 mag, respectively. The NED database also provides a Milky Way reddening in the direction of SBS 1436 + 529A of E(B-V)_ MW = 0.008 0.005 mag schlafly2011 . We do not detect interstellar Na i D lines at the redshift of the host, therefore we assume that the reddening in the host of SN 2019gsc is minimal.
We estimate a sub-solar oxygen abundance for the host galaxy of SN 2019gsc (12 + log _ 10 (O/H) = 8.10 0.18 dex), consistent with the equally metal-poor environment of SN 2008ha.

gmw_00004337.name: NGC 5173
gmw_00004337.overview: NGC 5173 is a gas-rich, counter-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1405.3873] that appears elliptical in the R band [arXiv:1309.5908]. However, it exhibits a patchy ultraviolet structure [arXiv:1405.3873] and clumpy, young-star-forming incomplete spiral arms [arXiv:1309.5908] that contribute to its blue outer regions [arXiv:1609.03179].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5173 as a gas-rich counter-rotating early-type galaxy with blue UV colors, patchy GALEX structure, and an evolutionary path reproduced by SPH encounter simulations.

quotes.quote: We discuss here the UV evolutionary path of two CR-ETGs, NGC 3593 and NGC 5173, concurrently best fitting their global observed properties, i.e., morphology, dynamics, as well as their total B band absolute magnitude and spectral energy distribution (SED) extended over three orders of magnitude in wavelength. These simulations correspond to our predictions about the target evolution which we follow in the color-magnitude diagram (CMD), near-UV ( NUV ) versus band absolute magnitude, as a powerful diagnostic tool to emphasize rejuvenation episodes.

The galaxies studied here, NGC 3593 and NGC 5173, are both ETGs. They show rather blue colors in the rest-frame NUV- versus band absolute magnitude CMD, placing them in the GV instead of on the red sequence as expected for the majority of ETGs e.g. MP14a, MP14b . Both galaxies are gas rich and NGC 5173 shows a patchy structure in the UV GALEX bands. Our chemo-photometric SPH simulations allow us to trace the evolutionary path of these galaxies in a fully consistent way.

Their current ages are 13.1 and 13.8 Gyr, respectively. Their evolution in the rest-frame NUV- versus band absolute magnitude CMD is marked by several rejuvenation episodes, which place them, at their current age, outside the red sequence, where normal ETGs stay.

sources.summary: NGC 5173 appears elliptical in R band but shows clumpy H-alpha and UV structures interpreted as young-star-forming incomplete spiral arms, with possible central UV-upturn contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 5173 NGC 5173 has been classified as an elliptical galaxy, which is perfectly recognizable in the R band image. However, in both the H and UV wavelengths, some non-spherical emission can easily be seen. There are clumps of material emitting in H and UV, tracing young stars and, therefore, star formation. This clumpy emission seems to be part of incomplete spiral arms, better traced in the H image (Fig. fig2 a).

The emission from the centre may not be due to massive star formation and can be related to the UV-upturn phenomenon. As a consequence, the derived SFRs (Sect. sectionSFRs ) may be overestimated for the central part, and for the whole galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5173 as one of the bluest HI-rich early-type galaxies, with blue outer regions, late-type-like SFR surface density, and green-valley or blue-cloud integrated colors.

quotes.quote: It is striking that two rich ETGs, NGC 2685 and NGC 5173, show very blue outer regions (see Fig. fig:link1 ). Within the aperture 1-3 R _ eff or 3-10 R _ eff , the colours of these galaxies fall in the region occupied by star-forming late-type galaxies.

Moreover, the SFR surface density levels of NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 in the first aperture are the same as those of the inner regions of late type galaxies. Since our first aperture is between 1-3 R _ eff , even this region can be counted as an outer region. It is worth to note that these galaxies are the bluest galaxies in our sample and their colour is similar to those of star-forming galaxies. In Fig. fig:ellbyell , we show additional data points (red triangles) for two lenticular galaxies: NGC 404 and ESO 381-47. The values are calculated for the UV/ \ rings far from the optical body of the host galaxies by 2010ApJ...714L.171T and 2009AJ....137.5037D , respectively. We also show a grey hexagon data point in Fig. fig:ellbyell corresponding to a giant low-surface brightness galaxy (LSB): Malin 1. 2010A&A...516A..11L suggest that Malin 1 contains a double structure: an inner high surface brightness early-type spiral galaxy and an outer extended LSB disc. These authors find that the total \ mass is 6.7 10^ 10 M _ and the disc extends up to 110 kpc radii. In addition, a recent deep optical study has revealed that Malin 1 extends up to 150 kpc 2015ApJ...815L..29G . Although the SFR values of Malin 1 are obtained for a large area including the central regions see 2009ApJ...696.1834W , it is remarkable that our results are consistent with that of Malin 1. Since our rich galaxies have bright inner regions surrounded with inefficient star forming low-column density \ discs, we can ask this question: are the rich ETGs related to giant LSB objects such as Malin-1? However, we need more information and analysis to answer this question, therefore we will leave this topic to future studies. So far we have compared \ and SFR surface density within fixed apertures. Here we perform a pixel by pixel as done by previous authors e.g., 2008AJ....136.2846B,2010AJ....140.1194B,2012A&A...545A.142B,2015MNRAS.451..103Y . To do so, we smooth and re-grid the FUV images to the resolution and coordinate grid of the \ images. We show the smoothed FUV images together with column density contours and 3 ellipses indicating 1-3-10 R _ reff in Fig. fig:appendix3 . We focus on regions outside 3 R _ reff , and for galaxy we calculate the average FUV flux in bins of constant \ column density. The bins have a width of 0.3 dex and are identical for all galaxies. For each bin we then take the weighted mean of all the single-galaxy average FUV fluxes. These weighted mean values are indicated by the black markers in Fig. fig:intermsofhi , while the cyan boxes show the scatter of all pixels in each bin. 1.0 para,flushleft Note. Column (1): The name of the rich galaxies. The values in the next columns are for the first and second apertures (1-3 and 3-10 R _ eff ), respectively. Column (2-3): total \ mass. Column (4-5): average \ column density. Column (6-7): integrated star formation rate. Column (8-9): average star formation density. Column (10-11): star formation efficiency. It is clear from Fig. fig:intermsofhi that the _ SFR level is increasing with increasing \ column density (as seen also in Fig. fig:Integrated_SFR and fig:ellbyell ). However, the SF efficiency remains low at all \ column densities in the outermost regions (R > 3 R _ eff ) shown in this figure: the average SF efficiency is 1.3 (0.3 - 5) 10^ 11 yr ^ 1 . This is 5 times lower than in aperture 1. The time necessary to consume all the \ gas (without gas recycling) is 9 (1.5 - 23) t_ H (Hubble time) and 2 (0.7 - 9) t_ H for aperture 2 and 1, respectively. As can be seen from Fig. fig:intermsofhi , the outermost regions of early- and late-type galaxies are consistent with each others. The gas depletion time of the outer regions late-type galaxies is 10^ 11 yr ( 7 t_ H , 2010AJ....140.1194B ). 2015MNRAS.449.3700R analyse the same relation between _ gas and _ SFR in the \ dominated regions of different type of galaxies with a similar method to ours, and therefore we use their results (shown as colour lines in Fig. fig:intermsofhi ) to compare with ours. There is an offset between the lines of 2015MNRAS.449.3700R and our points, and this is most likely due to the fact that we explicitly select the outer regions of galaxies, while they include inner regions too (as long as they are \ dominated). It is worth to note that, for our sample, the scatter in the _ SFR is increasing with decreasing _ gas . Implication for the evolution of these galaxies From the UV-optical colours, we show that several galaxies are blue in the outer regions due to recent star formation related to the presence of \ gas. Their outer colours are so blue that if they were total colours, we would consider those ETGs to lie in the green valley or even blue cloud. Are these outer regions affecting the evolution of the host galaxies? Another way of asking this question is: can the \ gas in outer regions fuel the star formation at a sufficiently high level to rejuvenate them and change the morphology of the host by building a stellar disc? In this section, we will try to answer these questions. As mentioned in the previous sections, NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 are the bluest galaxies in our sample (in both apertures) If we integrate the flux of NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 up to 10 R _ eff , they reside in the green valley and blue-cloud regions, respectively. . The gas depletion time of these galaxies is 10 ^ 11 yr in the second aperture, accordingly, the outer regions of these galaxies will be bluer than the inner regions for a long time. However, given the low-level (inefficient) star formation, the \ gas cannot change the morphology of the host galaxies. If we integrate the total SF over 1 Gyr (assuming constant SF), the contribution to the total stellar mass will be 0.1 and 0.05 percent for NGC 2685 and NGC 5173, respectively.

gmw_00004338.name: SDSS J161513.84+084914.4
gmw_00004338.overview: SDSS J161513.84+084914.4 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=2.46 [arXiv:2206.00684]. Near-infrared interferometric observations of the active galaxy have successfully detected fringes and coherent flux across its hydrogen line [arXiv:2206.00684].

sources.summary: The paper uses GRAVITY Wide to observe SDSS J161513.84+084914.4, detecting near-infrared interferometric fringes, H-line coherent flux, and a tentative BLR differential-phase signal.

quotes.quote: We present the separation and flux ratio of the binary HD 105913A, and the successful detection of fringes across the H line for two z>2 quasars, SDSS J161513.84+084914.4 ( z=2.33 , hereafter SDSS1615) and LAMOST J092034.16+065717.9 ( z=2.46 , hereafter LAMOST09). In Sect. sec:discussion , we discuss the influence of atmospheric turbulence on the new large separation fringe tracking mode with 17 observed FT--SC pairs with separations up to 32 arcsec.

We selected two quasars, LAMOST09 ( z=2.33 , m _K=15.1 ) and SDSS1615 ( z=2.46 , m _K=15.6 ), to be observed with the UT array and GRAVITY Wide. LAMOST09 has a FT star at a separation of 12.8 arcsec with m _K=10.4 while SDSS1615's FT star is 7.5 arcsec away with m _K=10.45 . Observations took place on 18 December 2021, 19 January 2022, and 25 January 2022 for LAMOST09 and 17 April 2022 for SDSS1615 in MEDIUM spectral resolution. For SDSS1615, we had very good weather conditions for the single night of observing and were able to consistently fringe-track and integrate on the QSO over 1 hour.

In the middle panel of Fig. fig:qso , we plot the average coherent flux overlaid on the total flux in the wavelength range of the expected H line for SDSS1615. The detected H line averaged over all four telescopes peaks around 2.28 and has a FWHM of 4300 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00004339.name: LAMOST J092034.16+065717.9
gmw_00004339.overview: LAMOST J092034.16+065717.9 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=2.33 [arXiv:2206.00684]. It has a K-band magnitude of 15.1 and was observed using the GRAVITY Wide instrument [arXiv:2206.00684].

sources.summary: The paper uses LAMOST J092034.16+065717.9 as a high-redshift GRAVITY Wide quasar target, reporting its redshift, magnitude, fringe-tracking reference star, observing dates, and successful fringe detections.

quotes.quote: We present the separation and flux ratio of the binary HD 105913A, and the successful detection of fringes across the H line for two z>2 quasars, SDSS J161513.84+084914.4 ( z=2.33 , hereafter SDSS1615) and LAMOST J092034.16+065717.9 ( z=2.46 , hereafter LAMOST09). In Sect. sec:discussion , we discuss the influence of atmospheric turbulence on the new large separation fringe tracking mode with 17 observed FT--SC pairs with separations up to 32 arcsec.

We selected two quasars, LAMOST09 ( z=2.33 , m _K=15.1 ) and SDSS1615 ( z=2.46 , m _K=15.6 ), to be observed with the UT array and GRAVITY Wide. LAMOST09 has a FT star at a separation of 12.8 arcsec with m _K=10.4 while SDSS1615's FT star is 7.5 arcsec away with m _K=10.45 . Observations took place on 18 December 2021, 19 January 2022, and 25 January 2022 for LAMOST09 and 17 April 2022 for SDSS1615 in MEDIUM spectral resolution. Atmospheric conditions ranged from poor to moderate over the three nights for LAMOST09 resulting in difficulty maintaining fringe tracking especially on 18 December. Still, each night we were able to acquire, fringe-track, and detect fringes with the QSO-star pair resulting in 96 min of useable data.

The raw data were reduced through the GRAVITY pipeline to produce complex visibilities for every DIT (60s for LAMOST09 and 100s for SDSS1615). Due to the low flux level, significant fringe jumps, and a slow drift in the OPD, we chose not to use the standard coherent integration within the pipeline and instead manually ran our own post-processing.

gmw_00004340.name: NGC 3341
gmw_00004340.overview: NGC 3341 is a disturbed disk galaxy merger at z=0.0271 [arXiv:0810.3235] that forms a pair consisting of a compact galaxy and a larger spiral [arXiv:1711.09936]. The system features a triple nucleus, including a Seyfert 2 nucleus offset by 5.1 kpc from the primary galaxy [arXiv:0810.3235].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3341 as a compact-AGN and larger-spiral pair with possible [O III] cross-ionization, though spectroscopy leaves the interpretation ambiguous.

quotes.quote: NGC 3341 : This pair has nearly the ideal geometry from Fig. fig-schematic , with an AGN hosted in a compact galaxy close to a much larger spiral Barth08 . In a multiwavelength study, set very low limits on AGN in the other components of this system. Our emission-line imaging (Fig. fig-ngc3341o3 ) shows a possible ionization cone in [ O III ] southward from the AGN across the larger disc.

However, the spectroscopic data show AGN ionization dominant only close to the AGN, so this structure may be an accident of the spiral pattern and location of star-forming regions. On the other hand, the spectrum shows unusually strong [ O I ] emission for pure stellar photoionization across much of this region, with [ O I ] 6300 /H 0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 3341 as a disturbed disk-galaxy merger with a spatially and velocity-offset Seyfert 2 nucleus, matching expected inspiralling-SMBH signatures.

quotes.quote: An example of this type of galaxy is NGC 3341, a disturbed disk galaxy at z=0.0271 with a triple nucleus, where one nucleus is a confirmed Seyfert 2 with a blueshifted velocity of 190 km s ^ 1 and a spatial offset of 5.1 kpc relative to the primary galaxy BA08.1 .

NGC 3341, a disturbed disk galaxy at z=0.0271 with a Seyfert 2 nucleus at a spatial offset of 5.1 kpc and a blueshifted velocity of 190 km s ^ 1 relative to the primary galaxy BA08.1 , is a local example of a galaxy merger hosting inspiralling SMBHs. Whereas none of the local observations of velocity offsets we explored in Sections gas and outflow would translate into observable velocity offsets at our redshift range, the object would.

gmw_00004341.name: 4C 26.42
gmw_00004341.overview: The radio galaxy 4C 26.42 is a cD galaxy located in the Abell 1795 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0510234, arXiv:astro-ph/9907009]. It is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I source [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342] that hosts S-shaped, precessing bipolar radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0510234] and displays LINER-like nuclear emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342].

sources.summary: The paper models the S-shaped radio jets of 4C 26.42 in Abell 1795 as precessing bipolar jets from a moving radio galaxy interacting with intracluster gas.

quotes.quote: We compare these predictions with observations of the radio jets emanating from the radio-galaxy 4C 26.42 (in the Abell 1795 galaxy cluster). We find that the general morphology of the radio jets can be described by a point-symmetric precessing jet system interacting with a plane parallel wind (i. e., the intra-cluster medium flowing past the galaxy). We also find that our synthetic X-ray emission maps reproduce the observed large scale structures (with sizes of the order of tens of kpc).

Radio observations of the central region of A 1795 show a jet-like emission from the bipolar radio source 4C 26.42 with a ``S'' shape (see white contours in Fig. f6 ). Our models have been computed for parameters relevant for the jets and X-ray filament associated with the 4C 26.42 galaxy in the A 1795 cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1346+26 as a cD galaxy in Abell 1795 with a cooling-flow component and a significant central point source in HRI data.

quotes.quote: B2 1346+26 The source is a cD galaxy in Abell 1795, identified with 4C 26.42. It contains a central cooling-flow component, as discussed in Fabian et al. (1994). HST WFPC2 images of the core of this cooling flow are presented in Pinkney et al. (1996).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C 26.42 with a cD FR I radio galaxy in Abell 1975 and finds double Liner-like emission-line clouds.

quotes.quote: 4C 26.42 . This object has been identified with a cD galaxy, MCG 05.33.005 (Carswell Wills 1967; Olsen 1970; Merkelijn 1972), the brightest member of Abell 1975 (Parma et al. 1986; Pilkington 1964); it is a FR I, Z-shaped radiosource (van Breugel et al. 1984; Ge Owen 1993). Emission lines have been detected in the nuclear region, with \ = 0.4, \ = 0.8 and \ = 0.2 (Anton 1993). These values are similar to the ones usually observed in Liners, but for the low 5007 line intensity.

gmw_00004342.name: 2MASX J11093966-1235116
gmw_00004342.overview: 2MASX J11093966-1235116 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.037 [arXiv:2206.07060, arXiv:1810.03339] that hosted the gamma-ray burst GRB 171205A [arXiv:2206.07060, arXiv:1810.03339]. The galaxy exhibits peculiar neutral hydrogen morphology and kinematics, which are likely explained by a recent interaction with a companion [arXiv:2206.07060].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J11093966-1235116 as the spiral host of GRB 171205A/SN 2017iuk, finding unusual H I morphology and kinematics best explained by a recent satellite passage.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a detailed study of \ in the host galaxy of GRB 171205A / SN 2017iuk at z=0.037 through the observations of \ emission line with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (JVLA) in B-configuration. This is the second nearest GRB associated with a supernova, and is located in the outskirts of its large, spiral host galaxy, identified as 2MASX J11093966-1235116 (LCRS B110709.2-121854) in optical surveys Shectman96-1996ApJ...470..172S, 2017GCN.22178....1I, Wang18-2018ApJ...867..147W .

These observations reveal unusual morphology and kinematics of the \ in this otherwise apparently normal galaxy. High column density, cold \ is absent from an extended North-South region passing by the optical centre of the galaxy, but instead is extended towards the South, on both sides of the galaxy. Moreover, the \ kinematics do not show a continuous change along the major axis of the galaxy as expected in a classical rotating disk.

With all above mentioned candidates ruled out, interaction with a companion remains the most viable explanation for the peculiar structure of \ in the host galaxy of GRB 171205A. While classical tidal interactions are expected to result in gas inflows towards the galaxy centre e.g. Renaud14-2014MNRAS.442L..33R , the penetrating passage of a companion through the disk of the GRB host could drag the gas out of the galaxy and result in the observed features in the \ distribution.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J11093966-1235116 as the nearby spiral host of GRB 171205A, giving its redshift, mass, star formation rate, and photometric contamination.

quotes.quote: The afterglow was tentatively associated with a close-by bright spiral host galaxy at z=0.037 (2MASX J11093966-1235116) even before its optical position was known (Izzo et al. 2017a). 2017), and the confirmation of the association with the host galaxy through the detection of both absorption and emission lines at the common redshift of z=0.0368 (Izzo et al. 2017b). The host galaxy mass is log_ 10 M/M_ = 10.1 0.1 , which is at least a factor of ten heavier than any other low-redshift GRB with a confirmed supernova (SN) counterpart (Perley Taggart 2017), and it has a star-formation rate (SFR) of 3 1 M _ /yr.

Since we observed GRB 171205A superposed on its host galaxy, the count rates we measured are contaminated with light from the host. To measure the contribution of the host galaxy, we measured the count rates for each observation between 6729--6788 ks using the same 3'' source aperture we used for GRB 171205A and performed a moving mean calculation to get an average count rate for the host galaxy.

gmw_00004343.name: VCC 2062
gmw_00004343.overview: VCC 2062 is a gas-rich tidal dwarf galaxy candidate in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185, arXiv:0707.3991] that likely formed from tidal debris expelled during a merger [arXiv:0707.3991]. It is linked to the interacting galaxy NGC 4694 [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185, arXiv:1010.2201] and exhibits active star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0303185, arXiv:0710.0668] alongside a kinematically decoupled velocity gradient [arXiv:0707.3991, arXiv:1509.05404].

sources.summary: The paper argues VCC 2062 is likely misclassified: it lies near interacting NGC 4694, shows blue colors and recent star formation, and is probably an irregular, very gas-rich system.

quotes.quote: VCC 2062 has been studied previously in HI by both Hoffman et al. (1993) and van Driel van Woerden (1989). It is about 2 kpc (0.4 arcmin) away from the irregular galaxy NGC 4694, with which it may be interacting. VCC 2062 was classified as a dE in the VCC, although the DSS image in Hoffman et al. (1993) suggests that this classification is incorrect, and that the galaxy is more likely an irregular. Hoffmann et al. also found very blue colors for this object, with (B-V) = 0.35 and (V-R) = 0.19.

These colors indicate that star formation has recently occurred, as an extremely metal poor stellar population which can also produce blue colors, would have to be less than 700 Myrs old to produce these colors (Worthey 1994). The presence of young stars reveals that this object is probably not a classical dE (i.e., old stars and no gas), despite its classification in the VCC. Due to its fairly high M _ HI /L _ B ratio of 55 this object is also by far the most gas rich member of our sample. The high ratio exceeds that of typical values found for blue compact dwarf galaxies (van Zee et al. 1995) and irregular galaxies (e.g,. Roberts Haynes 1994), another indication that it may be an interacting system.

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 2062 as a Virgo tidal dwarf galaxy formed in HI debris from NGC 4694, with star formation, solar oxygen abundance, CO emission, and a low dynamical-to-luminous mass ratio.

quotes.quote: The case of VCC 2062, also in the Virgo Cluster, provides an example of a Tidal Dwarf Galaxy that has formed out of more prominent tidal material expelled during a merger. Being massive and nearby enough, it allows a detailed study, in particular of its kinematics, revealing the main characteristics of tidal debris.

A Position--Velocity diagram based on the HI datacube reveals the presence of a well--defined velocity gradient (of about 45 km s ^ 1 over 4 kpc) towards VCC 2062 while the SW condensation exhibits broad HI lines and no structured velocity field (see Fig. fig:VCC2062 ). In one case, the HI gas is kinematically decoupled from the rest of the tail, is gravitationally bound, rotating and forming stars like a normal galaxy, in the other it has not (yet) collapsed and has remained purely gaseous. discuss various hypotheses for the origin of the HI--structure around VCC 2062.

The emission lines of the HII regions of VCC 2062 indicate an oxygen abundance of about solar (12+log(O/H) =8.6-8.7 ); a strong signal from the CO(1--0) line has been detected and mapped with the IRAM 30--m antenna. The measure of a high metallicity --- one dex higher than that inferred from the metallicity--luminosity relation --- and the detection of molecular gas via the CO emission is at odds with other star--forming classical dwarf galaxies in the Virgo Cluster, but is consistent with the tidal scenario. These observations indicate that VCC 2062 is made out of material that has been pre--enriched in a larger galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 2062 as a low-surface-brightness Virgo tidal dwarf candidate in an H I tail, with star formation, rotation, high abundance, CO, and low dynamical-to-luminous mass.

quotes.quote: The exception is towards one of its most prominent condensations, where a low surface brightness stellar body is detected. It corresponds to the previously catalogued dwarf galaxy VCC 2062. There, the HI column density --- close to 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 -- is higher than the critical threshold for the on-set of star-formation. And, indeed, the dwarf exhibits star--forming regions as traced by H and far--ultraviolet emission.

In this scenario, the HI structure is a tidal tail and VCC 2062, which was formed in it, a Tidal Dwarf Galaxy. This is further suggested by its relatively high oxygen abundance of about solar and the CO detection. The ratio between the dynamical mass of VCC 2062 --- 3-4 10^ 8 M _ , as inferred from the velocity gradient of the HI --- and the luminous mass --- consisting of HI, H _ 2 traced by CO and stars --- estimated to be only of order 2--3, is much lower than that found for field, dark--matter dominated dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 2062 as an old tidal dwarf galaxy candidate linked to its evolved parent by an HI bridge, with weak attenuation and SED fits indicating star formation began 200-300 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: VCC 2062 has been described as an old TDG candidate, as its parent galaxy has now evolved into a relaxed early-type galaxy duc2007a . It is linked to the latter by a star-less HI bridge. Unfortunately this system does not have any near-infrared image available. As no diffuse stellar component that would hint about the presence of an evolved stellar population can be seen, we assume =2 .

Because of the uncertainties on the fluxes and the lack of near-infrared data beyond the z' band -- which flux is particularly uncertain --, it is hard to have definitive conclusions regarding this star forming region. The fits we have performed indicate that star formation started between t=200 10^6 and t=300 10^6 years ago. The timescale is short compared to the age, at most a few tenths of it.

sources.summary: VCC 2062 is treated as a tidal dwarf candidate or recycled galaxy with high metallicity, CO detection, decoupled kinematics, and modeled internal gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: A long feature stretches to the West of the galaxy for 40 kpc and contains a TDG candidate: VCC 2062 (see Fig. fig:color ). D07 concluded that VCC 2062 is a recycled galaxy based on the following observations: (i) a high metallicity (12+log(O/H) 8.6), (ii) a strong CO detection, and (iii) an velocity gradient that is kinematically decoupled from the debris. Recently, deep optical images have been obtained as part of the Next Generation Virgo Cluster Survey NGVS, Ferrarese12 , revealing that NGC 4694 has a strongly perturbed halo and a prominent tidal feature to the West (Lisenfeld et al. in prep.). This confirms that NGC 4694 is the result of an old merger ( 0.5 Gyr ago). The kinematics of VCC 2062 is modelled in Sect. sec:3Dmodels .

For VCC 2062, the model-cube was not normalized to the observed map because two distinct kinematic components overlap in both space ( R 15'' to 30 '' towards the South-West) and velocity ( V_ l.o.s. 1110 to 1160 ); a simple exponential disc model allows disentangling these two kinematic components (see Fig. fig:VCC2062 ).

gmw_00004344.name: J 0902+2044
gmw_00004344.overview: J 0902+2044 is an elliptical radio galaxy with a wide-angle-tail morphology [arXiv:0907.2306]. Its structure features a central compact component and two bright jets that abruptly change direction to merge into a single low surface brightness tail [arXiv:0907.2306].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 0902+2044 as a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy with bent jets, measured radio fluxes and spectral index, and an elliptical optical counterpart in NSC J090+2043.

quotes.quote: J 0902+2044 has a symmetric WAT morphology, with a central compact component and two bright jets marginally deflected to form an angle of 170^ , at least out to 0.5 ^ from the central radio peak. Then the jets abruptly change direction by 90^ , and merge into a single low surface brightness tail extending East of the source. A compression of the radio isophotes is visible in the North--Western edge of the jets, suggesting that the pressure necessary to bend the radio emission is coming from that direction.

The optical counterparts of J 0902+2044 and J 0902+2045 are two elliptical galaxies with spectroscopic redshift z _ spec =0.083 and z _ spec =0.082, respectively At these redshifts the linear scale is 1.56 kpc/ ^ .

gmw_00004345.name: J 0902+2045
gmw_00004345.overview: J 0902+2045 is a radio galaxy with an elliptical optical counterpart [arXiv:0907.2306]. It features smoothly curved, symmetric twin jets that morphologically resemble a narrow-angle-tail radio source [arXiv:0907.2306].

sources.summary: The paper describes J 0902+2045 as a narrow-angle-tail-like radio galaxy with smoothly curved twin jets, measured radio fluxes, and an elliptical optical counterpart in NSC J090+2043.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy J 0902+2045 is characterized by twin--jets, smoothly curved in a C--shape. The jets appear rather symmetric in morphology and extent, and can be traced out to 1.5 ^ from the central radio peak. The angle between the tails is 120 ^ out to approximately 30 ^ from the centre. Then the jets undergo two more changes of direction, each with an angle of 120 ^ ; after the last bend, they lose collimation and gently curve toward the outer regions. From a morphological point of view, the very smooth and complete bending of J 0902+2045 resembles that of NAT radio galaxies, and indeed the source is strongly reminiscent of the prototypical narrow--angle--tail source NGC 1265 in the Perseus cluster (e.g. O'Dea Owen, 1986; see their Figs. 4 and 5); at the same time, radio sources similar to J 0902+2045 have also been classified in the literature as WAT (Smol i et al. 2007).

The optical counterparts of J 0902+2044 and J 0902+2045 are two elliptical galaxies with spectroscopic redshift z _ spec =0.083 and z _ spec =0.082, respectively At these redshifts the linear scale is 1.56 kpc/ ^ .

gmw_00004346.name: J 1455+2209
gmw_00004346.overview: J 1455+2209 is a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy associated with an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0907.2306]. It exhibits asymmetric jet brightness and has a measured flux density of 91.3 mJy at 610 MHz [arXiv:0907.2306].

sources.summary: The paper presents J 1455+2209 as a wide-angle-tail radio galaxy associated with an elliptical in MaxBCG J223.97317+22.15620, with asymmetric jets and measured radio power.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:wats_2 shows the 610 MHz image of the WAT GMRT--J 1455+2209 (hereinafter J 1455+2209), discovered at 22 ^ from Z 7160 (Tab. tab:obs ), overlaid on the SDSS red optical frame. The sensitivity in this region is 60 Jy b ^ 1 , and the noise level after the primary beam correction is 130 Jy b ^ 1 . The source has a morphology similar to J 0902+2044 (Fig. fig:wats_1 ), however no clear compact component is visible at the source centre, and some level of asymmetry is observed in the jet brightness. The flux density of J 1455+2209 at 610 MHz and 1.4 GHz are S _ 610 MHz =91.3 4.6 mJy and S _ 1.4 GHz = 49.1 2.0 mJy (from the NVSS), implying =0.75^ +0.10 _ 0.12 (see Tab. tab:wats_1 ).
The WAT J 1455+2209 is associated with an elliptical at z _ spec =0.257 At this redshift the linear scale is 3.99 kpc/ ^ .

gmw_00004347.name: J 0154+3627
gmw_00004347.overview: J 0154+3627 is a tailed radio galaxy located in the periphery of the cluster A 262 [arXiv:0907.2306]. The source features a bright, compact central core, two short symmetric radio jets, and a region of low-brightness, amorphous radio emission [arXiv:0907.2306].

sources.summary: The paper discusses J 0154+3627 as a diffuse radio source, known as B2 0151+36, whose core, jets, spectrum, redshift, and morphology indicate a tailed radio galaxy behind A 262.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:diffuse shows the 327 MHz images of the diffuse radio source GMRT--J 0154+3627 (hereinafter J 0154+3627), found in the periphery of A 262. In the literature this source is known as B2 0151+36, being part of the Second Bologna Catalog (B2) of radio sources (Colla et al. 1973), obtained using the Bologna Northern Cross telescope at 408 MHz. However, given the low resolution of that instrument (3 ^ in RA, and 10 ^ in DEC), no morphological classification was provided at that time. The 327 MHz full resolution image, shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:diffuse overlaid on the POSS--2 red optical plate, clearly reveals the presence of a bright and compact component at the source centre, two short symmetric jets (extended 1.5^ ), and a region of low brightness radio emission, with a rather amorphous morphology and a size of 2.5^ 3^ .
In any case, we can safely conclude that J 0154+3627 is a tailed radio galaxy, most likely associated with a cluster at z=0.1177 located behind A 262.

gmw_00004348.name: NGC 7643
gmw_00004348.overview: NGC 7643 is a spiral galaxy located approximately 200 kpc away from UGC 12562 [arXiv:1112.5975]. It is characterized as hydrogen-deficient, and its high deficiency and elevated oxygen abundance may be the result of galaxy harassment [arXiv:1112.5975].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7643 as an H I-deficient Pegasus spiral near UGC 12562, using averaged H II-region abundances and suggesting harassment may explain its high DEF and elevated oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: However, NGC 7643 is approximately 200 kpc away from UGC 12562, the possible effects of which will be addressed in the discussion. Table targettab lists the names, coordinates, inclination-corrected circular velocities, absolute B magnitudes, and effective/isophotal radii of the six spirals observed in this paper paturel03,rose10 . Of these, IC 5309, NGC 7518, and NGC 7643 serve as our hydrogen-deficient sample.

In the case of NGC 7643, for which the H II regions span only a small range of radius, we simply take a weighted average over all values of 12 + log(O/H), and adopt the average scatter around the mean as our uncertainty. As seen in Figure logovr , our measured H II regions for NGC 7643 are close enough to 0.4 R_ iso , and the fitted gradients are shallow enough that this average should be a reasonable estimate.

While this separation is too large by an order of magnitude for tidal interactions to lower the metallicity of NGC 7643 kewley06 , it is possible that ``galaxy harassment'' has contributed to that galaxy's high DEF, and therefore elevated (O/H) abundance.

gmw_00004349.overview: The galaxy 1140 is a starburst dwarf galaxy with mid-infrared emission that agrees with the IRAS 12 micron flux value [arXiv:astro-ph/0510086]. Its spectrum displays conspicuous polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission and a flatter very small grain continuum than other low-metallicity starbursts [arXiv:astro-ph/0510086].

sources.summary: The paper uses ISOCAM spectra of 1140, reporting agreement with IRAS 12 micron flux, prominent PAH emission, and a flatter VSG continuum than other low-metallicity starbursts.

quotes.quote: For 1140 , we find a flux of (96 30) mJy, in agreement with the 12 IRAS flux value from melisse+94 , who measure F_ 12 m = 73 mJy. 1140 seems to be somewhat of an exception (Fig. fig:spec1 ), as the PAHs are rather conspicuous.

The MIR spectrum of 1140 , which has the most prominent PAH emission and obviously flatter VSG continuum, is composed of equal contributions of a PDR component and \ regions (Fig. fig:m17dwarfs ). Since the extended emission is not very well resolved here, there is little difference in the MIR characteristic when inspecting the global spectrum or the peak spectrum.

gmw_00004350.overview: The dwarf galaxy 5253 is an active starburst system [arXiv:astro-ph/0510086]. Its global mid-infrared emission is dominated by very small grains, though weak polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon bands are detectable with small-aperture observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0510086].

sources.summary: The paper compares 5253 with other low-metallicity galaxies, noting active starburst mid-infrared bands, VSG-dominated broad-band flux, and weak PAH bands detectable with smaller beams.

quotes.quote: The LW2 band is dominated by the PAH band emission in all but 2 of the galaxies: the global spectra of the more active galaxies, 5253 and \ have about 1/2 of their total 6.7 (LW2) band flux originating from the wings of the Lorentzians of the fitted PAHs, while 1/2 arises from the small grain continua. In the active starburst galaxies, 5253 and , as well as the starforming reigons, \ and , 85 to 97 of the 15 LW3 flux is originating from the VSG component in the 12 broad band. In the active starburst galaxies, which globally resemble \ regions, 5253 and , almost all of the LW10 IRAS 12 flux is due to the VSG emission.

One example of this point is in 5253 , where the 15 30 ISOSWS beam barely produced evidence for low level PAH bands (Fig. fig:spec1 ), while more recent ground-based observations by alonso-herrero+04 use a beam size comparable to 5pc in 5253 with which they are able to detect the weaker PAH band at 3.3 .

gmw_00004351.name: z1
gmw_00004351.overview: The source z1 is a bright, isolated, and non-stellar galaxy [arXiv:1104.2520]. Although initially identified as a high-redshift dropout candidate [arXiv:1108.2406], spectral energy distribution fits suggest it may instead be a dusty, ultraluminous infrared galaxy interloper [arXiv:1108.2406].

sources.summary: The paper studies z1 as a Herschel-detected dropout candidate, arguing that FIR and SED fits favor a dusty z about 2 ULIRG/SMG interloper rather than a z greater than 7 galaxy.

quotes.quote: Here, we study two galaxies of this sample that are clearly detected by Herschel , namely the sources named 'z1' and 'Y5'. Our goal is to determine whether they could be interlopers and to understand their nature. The redshift probability distributions of these two sources derived by Laporte11 from SED fitting to deep Optical/NIR photometry show a prominent peak at z= 7.6 and 8.6 respectively.

As IRAC/ Spitzer and MIPS/ Spitzer data are available for z1, the source can be followed from one band to the next one by increasing wavelength despite the decreasing resolution. Its identification is therefore robust. A ULIRG template from the 2008A&A...484..631V library, a submillimeter detected galaxy template from the 2010A&A...514A..67M, 2010ApJ...712..942M library and a Seyfert template from the Polletta07 library are able to reproduce the 24 m emission of z1 .

sources.summary: z1 is bright, extended, isolated, and nonstellar; it is detected from Y to 24, but its 24 flux conflicts with both high- and low-redshift template fits.

quotes.quote: z1: This is the brightest source in our sample. It is an extended isolated object, clearly non-stellar. It is detected in all filter bands between Y and 24 except at 5.8 \ where no data is available. Although the best-fit is obtained at high- z , even with a luminosity prior, the 24 \ flux seems incompatible with the high- z solution (see Fig. fig_mips ). It appears even too bright in this band when compared to the best-fit templates at low- z .

gmw_00004352.name: Y5
gmw_00004352.overview: Y5 is a galaxy detected by the Herschel Space Observatory [arXiv:1108.2406]. It is likely a dusty, low-redshift interloper that is detected at 4.5 micrometers and exhibits extreme colors [arXiv:1108.2406].

sources.summary: The paper treats Y5 as a Herschel-detected dropout candidate whose FIR detections and SED fits make it likely a dusty low-redshift interloper, though its optical break remains unusual.

quotes.quote: Here, we study two galaxies of this sample that are clearly detected by Herschel , namely the sources named 'z1' and 'Y5'. Our goal is to determine whether they could be interlopers and to understand their nature. For Y5 there is a larger gap in the SED due to the lack of data between 8 m and 100 m, and due to the fact that it lies at the noisy edges of the 100 and 160 m maps, where it is not detected.

However, Y5 is the only source detected at 4.5 m within a radius of 3 '' (i.e., 1/3^ rd of the 250 m beam radius) around the 250 m peak, its identification with the SPIRE detection is therefore very likely. The source Y5 has a somewhat smaller drop between the near-IR/optical domain. We have examined several possible explanations for the extreme colors of these galaxies but none of them is entirely satisfactory.

gmw_00004353.name: IRAS03056+2034
gmw_00004353.overview: IRAS03056+2034 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1806.03341] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy that hosts hydroxyl megamaser emission [arXiv:1806.03341]. Its central region exhibits circumnuclear star formation [arXiv:1806.03341], while two smaller companion galaxies are located to its north and south-east [arXiv:1806.03341].

sources.summary: The paper maps IRAS03056+2034 as a LIRG/OH megamaser spiral with circumnuclear star formation, mixed starburst/AGN nuclear excitation, and ionized-gas inflow toward the nucleus.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph (GMOS) Integral Field Unit (IFU) observations, Very Large Array (VLA) continuum data and Hubble Space Telescope (HST) narrow and broad band images of the galaxy IRAS03056+2034, which is a LIRG that hosts OHM emission. IRAS03056+2034 (hereafter IRAS03056) is a spiral galaxy (SB(rs)B Vaucouleurs91 ) that presents strong OHM emission. We have obtained GMOS-IFU data covering the central region of IRAS03056 in order to map the distribution and kinematics of the emitting gas and investigate the origin of the line emission in the central region of this object.

Figure fig:merger shows the large scale i-band image of IRAS03056 obtained with HST. This image shows a barred spiral galaxy with two symmetric arms. Two smaller companion galaxies at projected distances of roughly 31 kpc and 34.1 kpc from the nucleus of IRAS03056 are seen to the north and to south-east, respectively.

gmw_00004354.name: SDSS J094700.08+254045.7
gmw_00004354.overview: SDSS J094700.08+254045.7 is a bright super spiral galaxy with a massive stellar population [arXiv:1511.00659]. Its central bulge is dominated by old stars, while its mid-infrared emission is driven by warm dust and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons from active star formation [arXiv:1511.00659].

sources.summary: The appendix analyzes SDSS J094700.08+254045.7 as a bright super spiral with multi-band photometry, an old bulge, massive old and young stellar populations, star-formation dust emission, and a high stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We chose SDSS J094700.08+254045.7 (SS 16) for this analysis because it is one of the brightest super spirals in our sample, with good photometry in many bands, and typical colors. The SDSS spectrum of its bulge is also typical of most super spirals, being dominated by an old stellar population (Figure 9 ).

The galaxy is detected in all GALEX , SDSS, 2MASS, and WISE bands, but is undetected by IRAS . The UV through near-IR data points reveal a massive old stellar population plus a young stellar population. Mid-IR emission appears to be dominated by PAHs and warm dust from star formation. We fit the SED using magphys template fitting dce08 . This gives a total stellar mass of 1.8^ +0.3 _ 0.2 10^ 11 M_ and SFR of 9.9^ +1.6 _ 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00004355.name: 2MASX J13275756+3345291
gmw_00004355.overview: 2MASX J13275756+3345291 is a luminous mid-infrared super spiral and starburst galaxy [arXiv:1511.00659]. It exhibits strong emission from young stars and luminous infrared emission from star formation activity, with an estimated star formation rate of 40.5 solar masses per year [arXiv:1511.00659].

sources.summary: The appendix describes 2MASX J13275756+3345291 as a luminous mid-infrared super spiral/starburst, with broad multi-band photometry, strong young stellar emission, luminous IR star formation, and high mass and SFR.

quotes.quote: We also make a detailed study of 2MASX J13275756+3345291 (SS 05), which is the most luminous (non-QSO) mid-IR source in our sample and has an SDSS nuclear spectrum with strong young stellar component and high-equivalent width H emission (Figure 10 ), characteristic of starburst activity.

We remeasured GALEX (FUV, NUV), SDSS ( u , g , , i , z ), 2MASS ( J , H , Ks ) and WISE band 1-4 photometry for SS 05 (Figure 13 ), using a similar procedure. We also retrieved Spitzer IRAC and MIPS, and Herschel PACS and SPIRE images from the respective IRSA and ESA archives to measure the IR fluxes. We used a circular aperture with 20 0 (156 kpc) radius for most bands.

We present the SED of SS 05 in Figure 14 . The galaxy is detected in all measured bands except the SPIRE 500 m band. In contrast to SS 16, there is a stronger component of emission from young stars, and much more luminous IR emission from star formation activity. We fit the SED using magphys , yielding a total stellar mass of 2.04^ +0.05 _ 0.09 10^ 11 M_ and SFR of 40.5^ +6.5 _ 0.5 M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00004356.name: IRAS F10038 3338
gmw_00004356.overview: IRAS F10038 3338, also known as IC 2545, is a luminous infrared galaxy consisting of a set of interacting galaxies [arXiv:0803.1832]. The system hosts an OH megamaser and exhibits three clear Zeeman detections, including a magnetic field reversal [arXiv:0803.1832].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F10038 3338 with IC 2545, describes its interacting LIRG OH megamaser profile, and reports three clear Zeeman detections including a field reversal.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10038 3338 : Also known as IC 2545 IC 2545 , this LIRG is a set of interacting galaxies at z = 0.0341 . A single-dish spectrum made using the Parkes 64 m telescope is presented by staveley-smithncawr92 showing 1667 MHz OHM emission centered at 10093 km s ^ 1 with a full width at half maximum (FWHM) velocity range of 63 km s ^ 1 and a peak flux density of 315 mJy. Likely due to its low declination, there have been no VLBI observations of this source despite its brightness.

The Stokes V spectrum has an rms noise of S_ rms = 0.7 mJy. There are three clear detections: Gaussian 1 probes a field of 1.8 0.3 mG; Gaussian 2 is fitted by a field of 11.3 1.2 mG; and Gaussian 5 shows a reversal in sign with a field of 1.7 0.3 mG. Since no VLBI observations exist for this LIRG, nothing can be said about the structure of the field reversal.

gmw_00004357.name: IRAS F10173 + 0829
gmw_00004357.overview: IRAS F10173 + 0829 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0803.1832] that exhibits a disky morphology with a highly asymmetric outer disk [arXiv:0906.2797]. The system hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:0803.1832, arXiv:0906.2797] with detected Zeeman splitting [arXiv:0803.1832] and a 1720 MHz satellite line [arXiv:1307.5071].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F10173 + 0829 as a LIRG with clumped MERLIN OH megamaser spots, complex Stokes profiles, detected Zeeman splitting, and suspected profile-model limitations.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10173 + 0829 : The only single-dish observations of the OHM line emission in this LIRG at z = 0.0480 were made with the 305 m Arecibo telescope and are detailed in mirabels87 . There are two distinct peaks in the profile of the 1667 MHz emission with a separation of about 100 km s ^ 1 , the dominant peak having a velocity of 14720 km \ s ^ 1 with a FWHM of 39 km s ^ 1 and a peak flux density of about 105 mJy. The 1665 and 1667 MHz lines are well separated with a hyperfine line ratio R_ H = 14.6 . MERLIN observations made by yu04,yu05 show roughly 50 maser spots distributed into three clumps, labelled east, central, and west, over an area of 1 4 0 6 .

This source represents a case where the profile structure is too complex to be modelled by our straightforward fitting guidelines: we regard the derived components as highly suspect regardless of the quality of the fit. Detectable portions of the Stokes V spectrum are restricted to the strongly peaked I line, where V oscillates rapidly. While it is clear that Zeeman splitting has been detected

sources.summary: The paper reports IRAS F10173+0829 as an OH megamaser with a first detected 1720 MHz satellite line, confined to velocities where the 1667 MHz emission is weaker.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10173+0829. To the best of our knowledge, we present the first detection of 1720 MHz emission in this OHM. Baan1992a noted that Mirabel1987 had previously looked for satellite lines in IRAS F10173+0829, but discussion of this OHM in that paper is limited to the 1667/1665 MHz lines. Our detection of the 1720 MHz line is shown in Figure fig:10173 , along with the moderately stronger 1665 MHz line. The 1720 MHz emission is produced at velocities 14,600--14,700 , corresponding to the weaker of the two peaks in the 1667 MHz emission. There is negligible 1720 MHz emission, if any, at the same velocity as the stronger of the 1667 MHz peaks.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 10173 + 0828 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 10173 + 0828. This z=0.049 ULIRG looks disky compared to most ULIRGs with a highly asymmetric outer disk. It is a OH megamaser galaxy ( OHM ), which is thought to be caused by infrared radiation from the surrounding environment baan85 . Most OHM s are known to be warm (U)LIRGs chen07 although this system is not a particularly warm ULIRG among the sample with f_ 25 /f_ 60 0.11 .

gmw_00004358.name: IRAS F12032 + 1707
gmw_00004358.overview: IRAS F12032 + 1707 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of 0.2170 [arXiv:0803.1832]. It is classified as a LINER-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0803.1832] and hosts an OH gigamaser that exhibits weak Stokes I variability [arXiv:1211.2023].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS F12032 + 1707 as a LINER-type ULIRG gigamaser with extremely broad blended OH emission, compact VLBA emission, and difficult Gaussian decomposition.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12032 + 1707 : A gigamaser discovered at Arecibo by darlingg01 . The host object, a ULIRG at z = 0.2170 , has been classified as a LINER-type AGN veilleuxks99 . The OHM emission spans almost 2000 km \ s ^ 1 with a redshifted high-velocity tail and a mean flux density of roughly 9 mJy. The 1665 and 1667 MHz lines are impossible to distinguish and clearly blended. A very narrow and bright component is seen at 64500 km s ^ 1 with a peak flux density of 16.3 mJy.

The OHM emission from this gigamaser has an extremely wide extent; it is impossible to distinguish the 1665 and 1667 MHz emission. We fit its Stokes I spectrum with 13 Gaussians as shown in Figure fig:iv_12032 with parameters as listed in Table tab:12032 . Due to the complexity of this profile, this is an extremely difficult case to apply our component selection guidelines to.

sources.summary: The paper finds new IRAS F12032+1707 observations broadly consistent with R08, with only weak Stokes I variability and magnetic-field fits matching the earlier Stokes V detections.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12032+1707 : The results of the more recent observations are in general agreement with those of R08, as shown in Figure fig:12032 . The rms error in the difference spectrum is dominated by that in the more recent spectrum, which has 2 mJy channel-to-channel variation. There is weak evidence for variation associated with the brightest, narrowest features in the Stokes I spectrum, as the difference spectrum has small peaks with amplitudes of 5 mJy associated with each bright emission feature, but this variation is nowhere near as dramatic as the nearly factor of two brightening observed in the brightest peak, located at 1371.4 MHz, between the observations of DG01 and those of R08. The Stokes V features remain consistent between observations, and the magnetic fields found by fits to the new spectra are entirely consistent with those published in R08.

gmw_00004359.name: IRAS F14070 + 0525
gmw_00004359.overview: IRAS F14070 + 0525 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0803.1832, arXiv:0906.2797] classified at the Seyfert and LINER boundary [arXiv:0908.4271]. The system is a compact merger remnant with disturbed isophotes [arXiv:0906.2797] that hosts a hydroxyl gigamaser [arXiv:0803.1832] and exhibits broad, blueshifted absorption outflows [arXiv:0908.4271].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F14070 + 0525 as a distant Seyfert 2 ULIRG gigamaser with very broad blended OH lines, diffuse single-dish emission, and weak linear polarization.

quotes.quote: IRAS F14070 + 0525 : Discovered by baanrfah92 , this gigamaser is the most distant OHM at a redshift of z = 0.2644 . darlingg02 redetected this source in their survey. The OH lines are so wide and blended (1580 km \ s ^ 1 at 10 peak flux density) that it is impossible to identify any 1665 MHz emission. The spectral line profile measured by darlingg02 has a peak flux density of 8.4 mJy and shows no significant changes since the original detection by baanrfah92 . darlingg02 suggest that many peaks in the profile are likely the result of many masing nuclei within the host ULIRG, which is classified as a Seyfert 2.

VLBA observations made by pihlstrombdk05 recovered less than 10 of the single-dish flux density and only two of the many single-dish spectral peaks were detected. Most of the single-dish emission is therefore diffuse. The spatial extent of the VLBA emission is confined to 10 10 mas.

sources.summary: The paper places FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary and describes broad, blueshifted absorption whose Mg and Na profiles trace similar outflow kinematics.

quotes.quote: The more recent Kewley (2006) division of these optical-emission-line ratios into excitation types classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN, FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2, FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary, FSC 1630+15 at the LINER/Composite boundary, and FSC 2349+24 as a Seyfert 2.
The FSC1407+05 spectrum presents a broad trough from -200 to -500 .

FSC1407+05: The and 2796 lines in FSC1407+05 are both detected to velocities reaching -750 . The MgII 2803 profile presents emission from 0 to +250 . The MgII 2796 emission is diluted by the MgII 2803 absorption at the same wavelength. The addition of the 2796 emission to the 2803 absorption trough elevates the latter between velocities -750 and -500 . At intermediate velocities less affected by blending, -500 to 0 , the and profiles lie right on top of each other after reducing the depth of the troughs by a factor of 2.0.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 14070 + 0525 as an extreme ULIRG and summarizes its morphology, infrared/AGN indicators, and role among high FIR-to-CO systems in the local ULIRG sample.

quotes.quote: IRAS 14070 + 0525. This compact z=0.264 ULIRG does not show any obvious signatures of interactions or merging. This system however, has disturbed isophotes vks02 and morphologically classified as a remnant by dasyra06b . Its spectrum shows characteristics of Sy2 veilleux99 .

gmw_00004360.name: UGC2885
gmw_00004360.overview: UGC 2885 is an isolated disk galaxy [arXiv:2105.03377] that is the largest and most massive spiral galaxy in the local Universe [arXiv:2105.03377]. It features four spiral arms with active star formation throughout its disk [arXiv:2105.03377], and its nucleus shows spectroscopic evidence of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2105.03377].

sources.summary: The paper tests machine-learning spectra for UGC 2885, identifying it as an extreme isolated disk galaxy with regular rotation, star formation, spiral arms, and spectroscopic evidence for nuclear AGN activity.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we test their prediction for a unique object, UGC 2885 (``Rubin's galaxy''), the largest and most massive, isolated disk galaxy in the local Universe ( D<100 Mpc).

UGC 2885 (``Rubin's Galaxy") is the largest and most massive spiral galaxy ( R_p = 38.1 kpc, M_* = 2.2 10^ 11 M _ ) in the local Universe Rubin80b . It shows very regular rotation with a maximum rotation velocity of 298 km s ^ 1 M_ tot = 1.50 10^ 12 M _ Rubin80a,Roelfsema85,Wiegert14,McGaugh15a , rising from the center, indicating a substantial and extended dark matter halo Marr15a . The galaxy has star-formation throughout its disk Hunter13 , four spiral arms, and a specific incidence of globular clusters that hints at an uneventful accretion history (Holwerda et al in prep.).

Figure f:BPT shows where the observed and the ML predicted line strengths place the nucleus of Rubin's galaxy on the BPT diagram BPT . The line strengths implied by the ML predicted spectrum imply strongly that Rubin's galaxy is an AGN (blue triangle in Figure f:BPT ). A combination of and VIRUS-P line strengths are more consistent with a lower-power AGN, closer to the dividing line between active galaxies and star-forming galaxies (red circle in Figure f:BPT ). The MMT/Binospec line strengths put the nucleus of Rubin's galaxy solidly in the AGN category (green square in Figure f:BPT ).

sources.summary: The paper models UGC2885 as a disk galaxy whose baryonic parameters and rotation-curve flattening are used to test the expanding locally anisotropic metric ansatz.

quotes.quote: As an example of applicability it is modelled a disk galaxy model matching the physical parameters of the galaxy UGC2885 and it is shown that, by fine-tuning the metric functional parameter, the flattening of the galaxy rotation curve is fully parameterized by this metric. It is further analysed the mass-energy density corrections due to the expanding anisotropic background being shown that although there are negative contributions within the galaxy plane the total mass-energy density is strictly positive. Outside the galaxy plane, by considering an anisotropic functional parameter, it is shown that the mass-energy density is also strictly positive.

For exemplification purposes we are modelling the galaxy UGC2885. Experimental photometric data and red-shift measurements for this galaxy are available in the works . We note that within the analysis carried in these references there are discrepancies in the derived physical parameters of the galaxy.

gmw_00004361.name: HRG 2304
gmw_00004361.overview: HRG 2304 is a peculiar ring-like early-type galaxy with a redshift of z = 0.0415 [arXiv:1104.4768]. The galaxy is dominated by an old stellar population and is undergoing a tidal interaction with its companion, AM 1646-795 [arXiv:1104.4768].

sources.summary: The paper studies HRG 2304 as a peculiar ring-like early-type galaxy whose spectra, old stellar population, velocity field, and fine structure indicate ongoing interaction.

quotes.quote: We report the first study of the peculiar ring-like galaxy HRG 2304 (NED02), which was previously classified as a ring galaxy with an elliptical smooth ring. This object was selected to prove that it is a candidate for the Solitaire-type ring galaxies in an early stage of ring formation. The main goal of this work is to provide the spectral characteristics of the current object and its companion AM 1646-795 (NED01).

Along the entire long-slit signal, the spectra of HRG 2304 and its companion resemble that of an early-type galaxy. We estimated a heliocentric systemic redshift of z = 0.0415, corresponding to heliocentric velocities of 12 449 km s ^ 1 for HRG 2304 (NED02) and 12 430 km s ^ 1 for AM1646-795 (NED01). The spatial variation in the contribution of the stellar population components for both objects are dominated by an old stellar population 2 10^ 9 < t 13 10^ 9 yr. The observed radial-velocity distribution and the fine structures around HRG 2304 suggest an ongoing tidal interaction of both galaxies. The spectroscopic results and the morphological peculiarities of HRG 2304 can be adequately interpreted as an ongoing interaction with the companion galaxy.

gmw_00004362.name: AM 1646-795
gmw_00004362.overview: AM 1646-795 is an early-type elliptical companion galaxy to HRG 2304 [arXiv:1104.4768]. The galaxy has a heliocentric systemic redshift of z = 0.0415 and is dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1104.4768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AM 1646-795 as HRG 2304's elliptical early-type companion, with similar redshift, old stellar population, and evidence for tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: The main goal of this work is to provide the spectral characteristics of the current object and its companion AM 1646-795 (NED01). The study is based on spectroscopic observations in the optical band to highlight the characteristics of this interacting galaxy.

Along the entire long-slit signal, the spectra of HRG 2304 and its companion resemble that of an early-type galaxy. We estimated a heliocentric systemic redshift of z = 0.0415, corresponding to heliocentric velocities of 12 449 km s ^ 1 for HRG 2304 (NED02) and 12 430 km s ^ 1 for AM1646-795 (NED01). The spatial variation in the contribution of the stellar population components for both objects are dominated by an old stellar population 2 10^ 9 < t 13 10^ 9 yr. Both galaxies are early-type, the companion is elliptical, and the smooth distribution of the material around HRG 2304 and its off-center nucleus in the direction of AM1646-795 (NED01) characterize HRG 2304 as a Solitaire-type galaxy candidate in an early stage of ring formation.

gmw_00004363.name: SDSS J142914.57+154619.3
gmw_00004363.overview: SDSS J142914.57+154619.3 is a massive, red disk-dominated galaxy [arXiv:1307.5103]. It is located near a faint dwarf companion galaxy that hosted the explosion of a supernova-associated gamma-ray burst [arXiv:1307.5103].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J142914.57+154619.3 as a massive red disk-dominated galaxy near GRB 130702A, likely distinct from the faint dwarf explosion site.

quotes.quote: These authors also noted the existence of a nearby faint =23.01 mag source SDSS J142914.75+154626.0 separated by 0.6 '' from the optical transient, and a bright red ( 18 mag; g- 0.9 mag) galaxy SDSS J142914.57+154619.3, at a separation of 7.6 '' .

The SN-GRB exploded at an offset of 7.6'' from the center of an inclined =18.1 mag red disk-dominated galaxy, and 0.6'' from the center of a much fainter =23 mag object. We obtained Keck-II DEIMOS spectra of the two objects and find a 2 upper limit on their line-of-sight velocity offset of . If we calculate the inclination angle of the massive red galaxy from its axis ratio and assume that its light is dominated by a very thin disk, the explosion would have a central offset, or times the galaxy's half-light radius. A significant bulge or a thicker disk would imply a higher inclination angle and greater central offset. The substantial offset suggests that the faint source is a separate, dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00004364.name: IC4225
gmw_00004364.overview: IC4225 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1505.04567, arXiv:2112.14434] that was previously classified as an S0/a galaxy [arXiv:1505.04567]. It features an exceptionally large dust disk relative to its stellar disk [arXiv:1505.04567] and exhibits a clear far-infrared excess [arXiv:1505.04567].

sources.summary: The paper models IC 4225 as an edge-on spiral whose unusually extended dust disk and remaining FIR excess make its dust energy balance hard to reproduce.

quotes.quote: IC 4225 has an exceptionally large dust disk as can be seen in Table Param.tab . Especially when compared in relative size to the stellar disk, it is almost three times more extended. It is even more surprising keeping in mind that this galaxy was classified as a S0/a galaxy, for which it has been shown that the dust emission should be compact 2007MNRAS.380.1313B, 2009ApJ...703.1569M, 2012ApJ...748..123S . Most likely however, due to the smaller angular size, the morphology of IC 4225 was misclassified. The large dust disk, combined with a total dust mass close to the average of the modelled sample, results in a face-on V-band optical depth of a mere 0.18.

For both galaxies, IC 4225 and NGC 5166, the observed SED shows a clear FIR excess compared to a standard model that is based only on optical images, and hence only contains evolved stellar populations. This is similar as what is found by various teams e.g., 2000A&A...362..138P, 2001A&A...372..775M, 2004A&A...425..109A, 2005A&A...437..447D, 2008A&A...490..461B, 2010A&A...518L..39B, 2011A&A...527A.109P, 2012A&A...541L...5H . However, things change when a young stellar population is included in the modelling: while a substantial FIR excess still remains for IC 4225, the FIR fluxes are recovered almost exactly for our model of NGC 5166.

sources.summary: The paper fits IC4225's observed SED with the Starduster model, finding an edge-on geometry and broadly consistent galaxy properties but an overestimated stellar disk radius.

quotes.quote: As an application, we fit our model to the observed SEDs of IC4225 and NGC5166. Our model can reproduce the observations, and provide reasonable measurements of the inclination angle and stellar mass. However, some predicted geometry parameters are different from an image-fitting study. Our analysis implies that including a constraint at (rest-frame) 40 could alleviate the degeneracy in the parameter space for both IC4225 and NGC5166, leading to broadly consistent results with the image-fitting predictions.

Our model also predicts that both IC4225 and NGC5166 are edge-on galaxies, consistent with the galaxy images. Our predicted galaxy properties are broadly consistent with 2015MNRAS.451.1728D but with some anomalies. For IC4225, the stellar disk radius is overestimated by a factor of 2.

gmw_00004365.name: NGC5166
gmw_00004365.overview: NGC 5166 is a massive edge-on spiral galaxy classified as type Sb [arXiv:1505.04567]. It features an asymmetric dust lane and a large, peanut-shaped central bulge [arXiv:1505.04567].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5166 is a massive edge-on Sb galaxy whose star-dust geometry and UV-submm model reproduce the observed SED and images well.

quotes.quote: NGC 5166 is an Sb type galaxy at a Tully-Fisher based distance of 62.6 Mpc 1991rc3..book.....D, 2007A&A...465...71T . It has a prominent but somewhat asymmetric dust lane and has a larger angular extent of 2.3 by 0.4 arcmin.

NGC 5166 is more in line with those found for other edge-on spiral galaxies. The dust disk is about 50 more extended and about half as thick as the stellar disk, which corresponds to the classical image of edge-on spiral galaxies 1999A&A...344..868X, 2007A&A...471..765B . The dust parameters were constrained with a higher accuracy compared to IC 4225. The model has a bulge that is quite large, relative to the disk. As was found by and can be clearly seen from the residuals in Figure 3 j in 2014MNRAS.441..869D , the bulge has a strong peanut shape, a feature that is quite common when looking at edge-on spiral galaxies 2000A&AS..145..405L .

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC5166's observed SED with Starduster, finding an edge-on galaxy but overestimated dust radius and mass plus MIR/FIR fitting problems.

quotes.quote: As an application, we fit our model to the observed SEDs of IC4225 and NGC5166. Our model can reproduce the observations, and provide reasonable measurements of the inclination angle and stellar mass. However, some predicted geometry parameters are different from an image-fitting study. Our analysis implies that including a constraint at (rest-frame) 40 could alleviate the degeneracy in the parameter space for both IC4225 and NGC5166, leading to broadly consistent results with the image-fitting predictions.

Our model also predicts that both IC4225 and NGC5166 are edge-on galaxies, consistent with the galaxy images. For NGC5166, our results overestimate the dust disk radius and the dust mass by a factor of 3 and 4 respectively. An additional issue is that for NGC5166, our model cannot fit the fluxes in the WISE 22 and IRAS 70 bands.

gmw_00004366.name: DDO 44
gmw_00004366.overview: DDO 44 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1003.0861, arXiv:1104.5318] located in the M81 group [arXiv:1003.0861, arXiv:1806.10141] as a satellite of NGC 2403 [arXiv:2202.10539]. It exhibits spatially separated stellar populations [arXiv:1003.0861] and a 50 kpc stellar stream resulting from tidal interactions with its host [arXiv:2301.13526, arXiv:2202.10539].

sources.summary: DDO 44 lies in the M81 dSph/dIrr transition region, belongs to the NGC 2403 subgroup, and shows metallicity gradients with spatially separated stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The M 81 group dSphs that seem to lie in this apparent transition region are KDG 61, KDG 64, DDO 44 and DDO 71. Out of these four objects, three are classified as transition-types, namely, KDG 61, KDG 64 and DDO 71 (Karachentsev Kaisin ; Boyce et al. ) based on HI detections and H emission.

The most distant dSph is DDO 44 which belongs to the NGC 2403 subgroup, while KDG 61 is the one closest to the M 81 itself. Interestingely, the dwarf KDG 61 which is classified as a transition-type based on HI detections, is the closest to the M 81 itself, with a deprojected distance of 44 kpc (Karachentsev et al. ).

Population Gradients By examining the cumulative metallicity distributions in the middle panels in Fig. sl_figure7 and Fig. sl_figure8 , we conclude that the metallicity gradients are present in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1, while the metallicity gradients are less pronounced or not present in the remaining dSphs. We again separate the RGB stars in each dSph into two samples, where we choose to separate the distributions at the observed weighted mean metallicity. The probabilities from the two-sided Kolmogorov-Smirnov (K-S) test that the two components are drawn from the same parent distribution are listed in Table table3 , column (4). The K-S results are consistent with showing spatially separated populations in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 44 as a dwarf spheroidal near NGC 2403 whose H-alpha knot lies within its optical body, has blue HST-associated stars, and has velocity attributed to DDO 44.

quotes.quote: In contrast to KDG 61, radial velocity of the emission knot in the dSph galaxy DDO 44, +213 25 km s ^ 1 , tells us that this H spark belongs to the dSph galaxy itself. F475W and F814W images of DDO 44 extracted from the HST archive reveal 8 bluish ( B-I < 0.8 ) stars apparently associated with the H knot.

A dwarf spheroidal galaxy DDO 44 is located at 79 arcmin in the direction of NNW from the Sc galaxy NGC 2403. The distance to it, based on the tip of the red giant branch was measured by k99,alo06,dal09 . The mean distance value amounts to 3.20 0.10 Mpc, which almost coincides with the distance estimate of 3.13 0.10 Mpc to NGC2403 from the Cepheids fre01 . The emission knot we discovered in DDO44 with the coordinates 073419.1 +665323.5 (J2000.0) is located in the NE side of the galaxy within its optical boundaries.

Therefore, the radial velocity of the clump in DDO 44 that we measured in no way fits into the kinematics of the peripheral gas disk of NGC 2403, and can obviously be attributed to the dSph system itself.

sources.summary: The paper considers DDO 44 as a nearby dwarf spheroidal whose velocity and interaction timescale make it a possible fly-by perturber of NGC 2403's H I cloud.

quotes.quote: DDO 44 has a single radial velocity determination that puts it at 213 km s ^ 1 kar11 , which is inside the range of velocities found in NGC 2403. See Fig.  fig:field for an overview.  Arbitrarily assuming the same fly-by velocity of 260 km s ^ 1 as given in mapelli (and assuming that this velocity is in the plane of the sky, ignoring any radial velocities), we find the timescale for an interaction with DDO 44 to be 300 Myr. For NGC 2366 we find 750 Myr. The DDO 44 timescale is comparable to the one mapelli found for NGC 891.

The second, closer, galaxy DDO 44 is a dwarf-spheroidal galaxy. In a search for H clumps in the M81 group, kar11 detected an H ii region in this dSph galaxy, containing a number of blue, late-type B-stars. kar11 note that, despite the absence of early-type B- or O-stars, the FUV luminosity of 1 10^ 36 erg s ^ 1 of the detected stars is sufficient to ionise the H ii region.

sources.summary: The paper discusses DDO 44 as the nearby dwarf satellite whose stellar stream, star-formation burst, and inferred orbit support an interaction origin for NGC 2403 filaments.

quotes.quote: Recent deep optical wide-field data presented in carlin19 supports this idea. They observed a 50 kpc long stellar stream originating from the dwarf galaxy DDO 44 and connecting with the NW part of the stellar disk of NGC 2403. This dwarf is likely a satellite of NGC 2403 located about 70 kpc from it. A higher quality re-reduction of the data from carlin19 is given in Fig. fig:ddo44 (J. Carlin, priv. comm) and this shows the stellar stream and the connection between DDO 44 and NGC 2403 even more clearly.

This is also suggested by the star formation histories of the galaxies: NGC 2403 had an increase in its star formation rate about 2 Gyr ago williams13 , in particular, at radii > 20 kpc; while DDO 44 had a burst of star formation from 1 to 3 Gyr ago girardi10,weisz11 . Also, carlin19 calculated from the estimated trajectory of DDO 44 that the interaction with NGC 2403 occurred 1 Gyr ago. These various estimates thus give very similar timescales for the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO44 as a dSph comparison galaxy for [KKS2000] 04, noting its M81-group distance, close stellar/structural analogy, SDSS imaging, and structural measurements.

quotes.quote: The galaxy we have chosen to compare with [ KKS2000 ] 04 is the dSph DDO44 R.A.: 07:34:11.3, Dec: +66:53:10; 1959PDDO....2..147V, 1985A&AS...60..213K . This galaxy is a member of the M81 group and is located at a distance of 3.10 0.05 Mpc 2009ApJS..183...67D . Based on its stellar and structural properties, DDO44 is almost a perfect analogy to [ KKS2000 ] 04. In Fig. fig:ddo44 we show a colour image of the galaxy based on the combination of the SDSS filters g , and i . We have used the SDSS data of DDO44 to explore the structural properties of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 44 as a dwarf spheroidal companion of NGC 2403 with no detected H emission inside its optical boundary and a dubious edge patch.

quotes.quote: DDO 44 . This dwarf spheroidal (dSph) companion of the spiral galaxy NGC 2403 does not show emission in the neutral hydrogen line HI. We have not detected H emission within the optical boundary of DDO 44. However, there is a compact patch (marked by a circle in the figure) at the NE edge of the galaxy. It may be rather a peculiar background galaxy than an emission knot belonging to DDO 44. The H flux presented in Table 2 refers exactly to it.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DDO 44 as an interacting satellite of NGC 2403 and argues that the nearby candidate JD2 is unlikely to be associated with it.

quotes.quote: It has a satellite galaxy DDO 44 (M _V = --12.5), and recently a smaller dwarf, called MADCASH-1 (M _V = --7.7), was discovered by 2016ApJ...828L...5C . Although DDO 44 shows signs of interacting 2019ApJ...886..109C , NGC 2403 itself reveals a remarkably undisturbed HI disk ( 2008AJ....136.2648D ; 2013ApJ...765..120W ). In the case of JD2, it was not selected to be a GC candidate of NGC 2403 as it lies at a projected distance of 75 kpc (and hence greater in physical distance), but it does lie near the dwarf satellite galaxy DDO 44 and might be associated with it (given its mass, a couple of GCs might be expected). Although the dwarf galaxy DDO 44 reveals a tidal stream due to likely interaction with NGC 2403 2019ApJ...886..109C it has a recession velocity of 255 km s ^ 1 and so JD2 with a velocity of 0 km s ^ 1 is very unlikely to be associated with DDO 44.

gmw_00004367.name: HELP J100156.75+022344.7
gmw_00004367.overview: HELP J100156.75+022344.7 is a hyperluminous infrared galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 4.3 [arXiv:2103.08032, arXiv:2405.18130]. Its emission is dominated by an obscured quasar [arXiv:2103.08032, arXiv:2405.18130], and it exhibits a high star formation rate of around 1,000 solar masses per year [arXiv:2405.18130].
