sources.summary: The paper reports HELP J100156.75+022344.7 as a z~4.3 hyperluminous galaxy whose SED is dominated by an obscured quasar with high AGN fraction and luminosity.

quotes.quote: In this work we report the discovery of the hyperluminous galaxy HELP J100156.75+022344.7 at the photometric redshift of z 4.3 . The galaxy was discovered in the Cosmological Evolution Survey (COSMOS) field, one of the fields studied by the Herschel Extragalactic Legacy Project (HELP). We present the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the galaxy and fit it with the CYprus models for Galaxies and their NUclear Spectra (CYGNUS) multi-component radiative transfer models. We find that its emission is dominated by an obscured quasar with a predicted total 1-1000 m luminosity of 3.91^ +1.69 _ 0.55 10^ 13 L_ and an active galactic nucleus (AGN) fraction of 89 .

The catalogue name of the source is HELP J100156.758+022344.739 but in the rest of the letter we will refer to it as HELP J100156.75+022344.7. The HELP photometry for HELP J100156.75+022344.7 we use in the rest of the Letter, and which is the result of the standard default processing by XID+, is listed in Table 1. The source is very faint in the optical and this partly explains why a spectrum is not available.

sources.summary: The paper fits HELP J100156.75+022344.7 as a z~4.3 hyperluminous obscured quasar, comparing torus models and finding high AGN fractions and star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: The HLIRG HELP J100156.75+022344.7 at a photometric redshift of z 4.3 was discovered in the Cosmological Evolution Survey (COSMOS) field, one of the fields studied by HELP. This is one of only a few z>4 hyperluminous obscured quasars discovered to date and, according to the CYGNUS model presented by , it is AGN-dominated with an AGN fraction of 82 per cent. also fitted HELP J100156.75+022344.7 with CIGALE and arrived at a similar conclusion. HELP J100156.75+022344.7 is predicted to have an SFR of 1051^ 109 _ 268 M_ yr^ 1 according to CYGNUS and 991^ 223 _ 223 M_ yr^ 1 according to CIGALE. In this paper we fit HELP J100156.75+022344.7 with the four different combinations of models, in order to quantify the uncertainty in the AGN fraction and SFR.

The CYGNUS, and SKIRTOR models predict a similar solution for the AGN fraction and SFR, although there are significant differences in the ^ 2 _ min, , with the CYGNUS model giving the minimum value of 6.09. The model of gives the worst fit with ^ 2 _ min, =12.03 and predicts a significantly lower SFR compared with the other models.

gmw_00004368.name: AGC 249525
gmw_00004368.overview: AGC 249525 is a gas-dominated dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 1.64 Mpc [arXiv:1702.07282]. It features a stellar overdensity that is exactly coincident with its neutral hydrogen structure, showing ordered motion consistent with rotation [arXiv:1702.07282].

sources.summary: The paper reports an optical counterpart to AGC 249525, deriving a 1.64 Mpc distance, coincident stellar and H I structure, ultra-faint-dwarf-like stellar properties, and gas dominance.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection at > 98 confidence of an optical counterpart to AGC 249525, an Ultra-Compact High Velocity Cloud (UCHVC) discovered by the ALFALFA blind neutral hydrogen survey. UCHVCs are compact, isolated \ clouds with properties consistent with their being nearby low-mass galaxies, but without identified counterparts in extant optical surveys. Analysis of the resolved stellar sources in deep g and i band imaging from the WIYN pODI camera reveals a clustering of possible Red Giant Branch stars associated with AGC 249525 at a distance of 1.64 0.45 Mpc. Matching our optical detection with the \ synthesis map of AGC 249525 from adams16 shows that the stellar overdensity is exactly coincident with the highest-density \ contour from that study. Combining our optical photometry and the \ properties of this object yields an absolute magnitude of 7.1 M_V 4.5 , a stellar mass between 2.2 0.6 10^4 M_ and 3.6 1.0 10^5 M_ , and an \ to stellar mass ratio between 9 and 144.

AGC 249525 shows a smooth \ morphology at improved (60 , 105 ) angular resolution and ordered motion consistent with 15 rotation. AGC 249525, and the aforementioned AGC 198606, are among the highest column density objects in the UCHVC sample, with peak N_ HI 5 10^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 at 105 resolution. adams16 reported a mean \ angular diameter at half-flux level of 8.5 \ for AGC 249525 and an \ mass of 2.4 10^6 M_ , assuming a distance of 1 Mpc.

gmw_00004369.name: BR 0351-1034
gmw_00004369.overview: BR 0351-1034 is a high-redshift, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2110.07065]. Multi-epoch observations reveal that the source is variable in the hard X-ray band, with its variable flux accounting for approximately 40 percent of its total flux [arXiv:2110.07065].

sources.summary: The paper monitors BR 0351-1034 with new Chandra and optical-UV data, noting hard-band variability while emphasizing that it is the faintest, highest-redshift, lowest-S/N source.

quotes.quote: The sources, Q 0000-263 , BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , were selected out of the sample of as having the highest accessible redshifts for economical Chandra observations and having at least two archival \ epochs. In this paper, we present three additional \ epochs for the \ sources (nine \ epochs in total), that allow us to extend our time-series analysis and produce tighter constraints on the X-ray properties of these sources.

The excess variance is consistent with zero, within its uncertainties, in all cases, with the exception of BR 0351-1034 in the hard band, where the variable flux is 40 of the total flux. BR 0351-1034 is our faintest and highest-redshift source. Consequently, observations of the object suffer from the lowest signal-to-noise ratio ( S/N ) of our sources, with the S/N being 2/3 of that of the full sample.

We find that two of our four sources, Q 0000-263 and PSS 0926+3055 , are \ variable in the soft band and three sources, BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , are variable in the hard band.

gmw_00004370.name: PSS 0926+3055
gmw_00004370.overview: PSS 0926+3055 is a high-redshift, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2110.07065]. Multi-epoch monitoring reveals that the active galactic nucleus exhibits variability in both its soft and hard X-ray bands [arXiv:2110.07065].

sources.summary: The paper monitors PSS 0926+3055 across nine X-ray epochs and optical-UV data, finding variability in both soft and hard X-ray bands and stacked imaging over 1300 rest-frame days.

quotes.quote: The sources, Q 0000-263 , BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , were selected out of the sample of as having the highest accessible redshifts for economical Chandra observations and having at least two archival \ epochs. In this paper, we present three additional \ epochs for the \ sources (nine \ epochs in total), that allow us to extend our time-series analysis and produce tighter constraints on the X-ray properties of these sources.

The resulting stacked images of PSS 0926+3055 and PSS 1326+0743 contain observations from \ Cycles 3, 12-15 , and 19-21 , amounting to 40 ks each (spanning 1300 days in the rest frame), and Q 0000-263 and BR 0351-1034 are composed of observations from Cycles 12-15 and 19-21 , amounting to 70 ks each (spanning 700 days in the rest frame).

We find that two of our four sources, Q 0000-263 and PSS 0926+3055 , are \ variable in the soft band and three sources, BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , are variable in the hard band.

gmw_00004371.name: PSS 1326+0743
gmw_00004371.overview: PSS 1326+0743 is a high-redshift, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2110.07065]. Multi-epoch X-ray observations of the active galactic nucleus reveal that it exhibits variability in the hard X-ray band [arXiv:2110.07065].

sources.summary: The paper monitors PSS 1326+0743 as a high-redshift radio-quiet quasar, using nine X-ray epochs and stacked imaging and finding hard-band X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The sources, Q 0000-263 , BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , were selected out of the sample of as having the highest accessible redshifts for economical Chandra observations and having at least two archival \ epochs. In this paper, we present three additional \ epochs for the \ sources (nine \ epochs in total), that allow us to extend our time-series analysis and produce tighter constraints on the X-ray properties of these sources.

The resulting stacked images of PSS 0926+3055 and PSS 1326+0743 contain observations from \ Cycles 3, 12-15 , and 19-21 , amounting to 40 ks each (spanning 1300 days in the rest frame), and Q 0000-263 and BR 0351-1034 are composed of observations from Cycles 12-15 and 19-21 , amounting to 70 ks each (spanning 700 days in the rest frame).

We find that two of our four sources, Q 0000-263 and PSS 0926+3055 , are \ variable in the soft band and three sources, BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , are variable in the hard band.

gmw_00004372.name: J0002+2550
gmw_00004372.overview: J0002+2550 is a quasar at a redshift of z = 5.80 [arXiv:2405.00257, arXiv:2309.03936] with a very steep radio spectrum [arXiv:2309.03936]. It has a low ionizing photon rate [arXiv:2405.00257] and an upper limit on its star formation rate of less than 1,500 solar masses per year [arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper models J0002+2550’s ionising photon rate and radio turnover, finding a low ionising rate, no observed turnover above 1 GHz, and SED fits with compact gas profiles.

quotes.quote: As stated above, only one source, J0002+2550 (WISEA J000239.39+255035.1), had sufficient rest-frame UV data to determine the ionising photon rate. Of the remaining eight, only four had any rest-frame IR–UV photometry and are shown in Appendix C. This is shown in Fig. J0002 , from which we obtain an ionising rate of Q_ = 9.3 10^ 54 s ^ 1 . This is well below the 3 10^ 56 s ^ 1 limit at which all of the gas in the host galaxy is believed to be ionised cw12 and well within the regime where \ 21-cm absorption has been detected cww+08,chj+19 .

Equipped with the expected value of the turnover frequency for a given photo-ionisation rate, we can estimate the turnover for J0002+2550 (Sect. sect:J0002+2550 ). At z = 5.80 , the luminosity distance to the source is D_ L =56 900 Mpc and the finest beam size of 3.4'' , in which the source is unresolved gsi+23 , gives r_ em 10 kpc for the low frequency emission. Correcting the UV spectral index for J0002+2550 gives _ UV = -9.4 , which results in an ionising photon rate of Q_ = 1.0 10^ 55 s ^ 1 , which changes little from the uncorrected value. That the exponential and spherical density models can fit the SED of J0002+2550 for R 100 pc, suggesting that the turnover frequency is indeed below the lowest observed rest-frame frequency of 1 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0002+2550 as a z=5.80 quasar with a studied infrared SED, a star-formation upper limit, and a very steep GMRT radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: J0002+2550 This quasar at z=5.80 was discovered by from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; York2000AJ....120.1579Y, Lyke2020ApJS..250....8L ) and its spectral energy density (SED) shape (from 0.1-80 m) has been studied by , resulting in an upper limit on the star formation rate (SFR) of <1.5 10^ 3 M _ yr ^ 1 . Our GMRT observations show that it has a very steep radio spectrum of =-1.6^ +0.19 _ 0.12 . The possible origin of the steep spectrum will be further discussed in Sect.  subsec:discuss_radio_spec .

gmw_00004373.name: VirgoHI21
gmw_00004373.overview: VirgoHI21 is a starless neutral hydrogen structure located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0707.3278, arXiv:0710.3867]. While initially interpreted as a massive dark galaxy [arXiv:0707.3991, arXiv:0805.1665], subsequent observations suggest it is a denser cloud within a tidal tail expelled from the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4254 [arXiv:0710.3867, arXiv:0802.2100].

sources.summary: VirgoHI21 is presented as a starless HI dark-galaxy prototype whose large velocity gradient is explained instead as a denser cloud in a tidal tail from NGC 4254.

quotes.quote: Among these free-floating low-mass HI clouds, one object located in the outerskirts of the Virgo Cluster has attracted much attention and discussion: VirgoHI21 see Fig. 1 davies04,minchin05 . Despite a HI mass of only 10^8 M _ , this elongated gaseous structure, mapped with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) by hereafter M07 minchin07 , exhibits a velocity gradient as large as 220 km s ^ 1 (see Fig. fig:pv-vhi21 ). Assuming that the observed HI velocities trace rotation, the inferred dynamical mass would be as large as 10^ 11 M _ . The object shows no optical counterpart, even on deep HST images (M07). With such extreme properties, VirgoHI21 has become the prototype for dark galaxies, although its high dynamical mass is atypical even in models predicting the existence of Dark Galaxies.

It reveals that VirgoHI21 actually lies within an even larger HI structure that extends further to the North, in the opposite direction of NGC 4254. This further suggests that the thin and long HI feature is in fact a tidal tail emanating from the spiral and that VirgoHI21 is just a denser cloud within it and thus not a dark galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper argues that VirgoHI21, previously proposed as a dark galaxy, is better interpreted as a bright section of an H I tidal stream connected to NGC 4254.

quotes.quote: A candidate ``dark galaxy'' was discovered and dubbed ``VirgoHI21'' (Minchin 2007 and refs therein and these proceedings). The discovery at Jodrell Bank was corroborated by Arecibo and WSRT observations. VirgoHI21 lies some 100 kpc N of NGC4254, in the NW periphery of the Virgo cluster, projected 1 Mpc from the cluster center. ALFALFA also detects it, but the ALFALFA data strongly suggests a different interpretation for the nature of VirgoHI21.

VirgoHI21 is the bright section of the HI stream extending from 14^ 41' to 14^ 49' . One of the driving arguments for Minchin 's interpretation of VirgoHI21 as an isolated disk galaxy is the gradient seen in the velocity field; ALFALFA data shows that gradient to be just a part of the varying, large--scale velocity field along the stream.

sources.summary: ALFALFA recasts VirgoHI21 from an isolated dark-galaxy candidate into the bright part of an extended HI stream connected kinematically to NGC 4254.

quotes.quote: VirgoHI21 was discovered at Jodrell Bank, corroborated by Arecibo and WSRT observations ( and refs therein). It lies some 100 kpc N of NGC4254, in the NW periphery of the Virgo cluster, projected 1 Mpc from the cluster center and has a relative velocity of more than 1000 with respect to the cluster. Because of its large separation from optical galaxies and the gradient seen in its velocity field, it was interpreted by its discoverers as a dark galaxy. The ALFALFA data suggest a different scenario.

The observations by the previous group did not reveal the HI stream in its full extent: what they called VirgoHI21 is the bright section of the HI stream extending from 14^ 41' to 14^ 49' . One of the driving arguments for the interpretation of VirgoHI21 as an isolated disk galaxy is the gradient seen in the velocity field ; ALFALFA data shows that gradient to be just a part of the varying, large--scale velocity field along the stream.

sources.summary: The paper argues VirgoHI21 is not a dark-matter-dominated dark galaxy but tidal debris expelled from NGC 4254 about 750 Myr ago, with its velocity gradient explained by streaming motions and projection.

quotes.quote: VirgoHI21, in the Virgo Cluster, is by far the most spectacular and most discussed Dark Galaxy candidate so far detected in HI surveys. We show here that it is most likely made out of material expelled 750 Myr ago from the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4254 during its fly--by at about 1000 km s ^ 1 by a massive intruder.

VirgoHI21 is the prototype of Dark Galaxies Minchin05,Minchin07 , being one of the most massive dark objects thus far detected in HI. Its main characteristics are the following: an HI mass of 10^8 M _ ; the absence of any stellar counterpart even on deep HST images and a velocity gradient as large as 220 km s ^ 1 (see Fig. fig:model ) from which a dynamical mass of 10^ 11 M _ was derived.

Whereas the global kinematics of VirgoHI21 and its bridge/tail can be simply explained by streaming motions along a tidal structure, when looked at in detail the model and observations show some minor differences which show up when comparing the observed and simulated Position--Velocity diagrams: the velocity gradient towards VirgoHI21 is locally larger within the HI cloud, while in the model it is not more enhanced at this location than further away near the tip of the tail.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Possible Dark Galaxy as a Virgo dark-galaxy candidate, reports no Swift X-ray detection, and limits extended hot gas and point-source X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: This is the first pointed X-ray observation of , a putative dark galaxy in the Virgo cluster, and no photons were detected from this source. The non-detection of extended X-ray emission within the angular extent of the \ source corresponds to a 99 confidence upper limit of 2.1 10^ 14 \ in the 0.3--2.0 keV band. The equivalent upper limit to the amount of diffuse hot gas associated with \ is in the range 4 10^ 7 2 10^8 \ for a hot gas temperature between 0.1 and 1 keV.

Thus, \ is currently considered the prototypical dark galaxy candidate: a dynamically-massive dark-matter-dominated galaxy with a rotating gaseous disk but devoid of stars. If \ is a massive dark galaxy, then it has important implications for the theory of structure formation.

sources.summary: The paper argues VIRGOHI 21 is a massive dark galaxy: a broad-line 21-cm source imaged as a dark edge-on spinning disk that interacted with NGC 4254.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The structure of VIRGOHI 21 (Fig. 2a) is centred at R.A. 12^h17^m52^s and elongated in the North-South plane between +14^ 41^ and +14^ 49^ .

quotes.quote: Earlier Minchin05 we suggested that VIRGOHI 21, a 21-cm source we found in the Virgo Cluster at Jodrell Bank using single-dish observations Davies04 , was probably such a dark galaxy because of its broad line-width ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) unaccompanied by any visible gravitational source to account for it. Now we have managed to image VIRGOHI 21 in the neutral-hydrogen line, and indeed we find what appears to be a dark, edge-on, spinning disc with the mass and diameter of a typical spiral galaxy. Moreover the disc has unquestionably interacted with NGC 4254, a luminous spiral with an odd one-armed morphology, but lacking the massive interactor invariably responsible for such a feature. Published numerical models Vollmer05 of NGC 4254 call for a close interaction 10^8 years ago with a perturber of 10^ 11 solar masses. This we take as completely independent evidence for the massive nature of VIRGOHI 21.

sources.summary: The review describes VirgoHI21 as a starless dark-galaxy candidate near NGC 4254, but notes that its velocity gradient could instead arise from tidal-collision streaming motions.

quotes.quote: According to a more exotic scenario, star-less intergalactic clouds might reveal the presence of so-called ``dark galaxies", i.e. galaxies embedded in a massive dark matter halo that would contain very few baryons, only in the form of gas see the proceedings of IAU symposium 244 dedicated to dark galaxies and Minchin07 . An example of such objects is VirgoHI21, near the spiral NGC 4254 shown on Figure fig:gas-poor (bottom panel). The elongated cloud exhibits a strong velocity gradient, as if it was rotated and moved by an unseen dark component. But here again, such a velocity field might be explained by streaming motions generated by a tidal collision Bekki05,Duc08 .

sources.summary: The paper cites VIRGOHI21 as a dark galaxy candidate that may be a splash-bridge relic, with an H I disc, no observed stars, and an unusually high mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: Given the extended HI discs of many galaxies, still more off-centre impacts may generate splash bridges, but not a ring galaxy. davies08 outline the detection of a dark galaxy candidate, VIRGOHI21, which could be a relic of a splash bridge. Interestingly, the observations of VIRGOHI21 reveal an galactic disc with a mass of 2e7 M embedded in the 2e8 M tidal bridge. Stars are not observed in this object; a lower limit for mass to luminosity ratio was found to be e6 M / L , which the authors note is significantly higher than a typical galactic value of 50 or less.

sources.summary: The review presents VIRGOHI21 as a debated dark-galaxy candidate: possibly a massive starless dark matter halo, but alternatively gas dynamically stripped from nearby NGC4254.

quotes.quote: Recently Minchin et al. have suggested that VIRGOHI21 is the first discovery of a ``dark galaxy'', a large dark matter halo ( 10^ 11 M _ ) that for some reason has failed to form detectable stars. If correct, this is an important finding for cosmology and galaxy evolution. However, Oosterloo van Gorkom (2005) have argued that VIRGOHI21 might instead be material dynamically stripped from the nearby galaxy NGC4254.

gmw_00004374.name: 8C1435+635
gmw_00004374.overview: 8C1435+635 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at z=4.25 [arXiv:astro-ph/9802139]. The galaxy appears relatively compact and is clearly detected at 350 microns [arXiv:astro-ph/0509364], while its surrounding field shows evidence of weak gravitational lensing [arXiv:astro-ph/9802139].

sources.summary: The paper treats 8C1435+635 as a z=4.25 radio galaxy whose field shows possible weak lensing by foreground groups or a dark cluster.

quotes.quote: 8C1435+635 This z=4.25 radio galaxy has a z=0.24 galaxy projected just beyond the south east radio lobe. This is close to a larger, L_* galaxy at z=0.23 , possibly in the same group or cluster (Lacy et al., 1994). Another group of three galaxies was found at z 0.35 .

With a deep I band image from the WHT, we made a weak lensing study of this field, using the technique of Seitz Schneider (1995). A marginally significant (3.5- ) peak is present in the recovered projected mass distribution, offset by about 50 arcsec from the radio galaxy (Fig. \ 1). Apart from the groups already mentioned there seems to be no obvious optical cluster in the field, and no X-ray emission is seen in this field in a deep ROSAT exposure.

sources.summary: The central radio galaxy 8C 1435 + 635 is clearly detected at 350 microns, appears compact, and has only a weak companion-source upper limit.

quotes.quote: 8C 1435 + 635 Despite being the faintest radio galaxy at 850- m, the central radio galaxy is clearly detected ( 5 ) in the sharc-ii maps ( S_ 350 m = 51 9 mJy). Similar to Stevens et al., who failed to resolve the galaxy at 850- m, there is no evidence of extended emission at 350- m either, suggesting that this source may be relatively compact. The only other scuba source detected in the 8C 1435 + 635 field lies about 60 north-west of the RG and coincides with faint 2 emission at 350- m. We derive an upper flux limit of S_ 350 m 68 mJy.

gmw_00004375.name: 6C0140+326
gmw_00004375.overview: 6C0140+326 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.41 [arXiv:astro-ph/9802139]. Its radio hotspots are gravitationally lensed by a foreground galaxy at z=0.93, which elongates the hotspots [arXiv:astro-ph/9802139].

sources.summary: The paper describes 6C0140+326 as a z=4.41 radio galaxy with candidate high-redshift companions and stronger evidence for foreground lensing of its radio hotspots.

quotes.quote: In the field of the z=4.41 radio galaxy 6C0140+326 we find several candidate z>4 galaxies using the Lyman-break technique, two of which have detectable UV absorption features. Spectroscopy with LDSS2 on the WHT of the 6C0140+326 field has revealed eight objects with low-dispersion spectra consistent with them being z>4 galaxies, two of which have several plausible absorption features placing them at a redshift of 4.02 and 4.16 respectively (see Spinrad, these proceedings, for a discussion of UV absorption features seen in high- z starbursts).

6C0140+326 The evidence for lensing in this source is more secure. Again, a nearby (in projection) galaxy was noted in the discovery paper (Rawlings et al. \ 1996) at z=0.93 . This was sufficient to produce significant lensing on its own, revealed in our 5GHz MERLIN image (Fig. \ 2), where both the hotspots are elongated in the direction expected if they are being lensed by the z=0.93 galaxy. Although the exact magnification factor is hard to estimate due to the fact that the source structure is unknown, a simple singular isothermal sphere (SIS) model can be used to obtain a rough magnification if initially circular hotspots are assumed.

gmw_00004376.name: F565-V2
gmw_00004376.overview: F565-V2 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120]. It features an elongated structure, which may potentially hide an underlying face-on disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120].

sources.summary: MOND cannot fit F565-V2 at its original inclination; a much lower inclination may imply an unseen face-on LSB disk under the observed elongated structure.

quotes.quote: F565-V2 At the original inclination of 60 degrees no fit with (M/L)_ >0 could be made. The inclination needs to be changed to 44 degrees before positive (M/L)_ values are produced by the fitting procedure. The best fitting MOND value is 31 degrees. This might mean that we are missing a face-on LSB disk underlying the elongated structure we now observe as the galaxy. Finding such a LSB disk will be made difficult by the presence of a bright star just outside the region shown (cf. Fig. \ 2 in de Blok et al. \ 1996).

gmw_00004377.name: UGC 1230
gmw_00004377.overview: UGC 1230 is a face-on galaxy with a low surface brightness disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120]. Its rotation curve has been analyzed using modified Newtonian dynamics, which suggests an inclination of 17 degrees for the system [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120].

sources.summary: For UGC 1230, MOND requires only a small inclination adjustment and could constrain the face-on galaxy inclination more tightly than dark-halo fitting.

quotes.quote: UGC 1230 Only a very minor change was needed. The original value derived in van der Hulst et al. \ (1993) is 22 degrees. MOND needs a value of 17 degrees in order to produce a good fit. Note that the quality of the fit for this face-on system depends critically on the inclination. Using MOND to fit the rotation curve of a face-on galaxy could actually tie down its inclination to within a few degrees.

gmw_00004378.name: UGC 5005
gmw_00004378.overview: UGC 5005 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120]. While its original inclination was estimated at 41 degrees, Modified Newtonian Dynamics fits favor a lower inclination of 26 to 30 degrees, which remains consistent with the galaxy's observed optical axis ratio [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120].

sources.summary: MOND fitting of UGC 5005 favors a lower inclination of about 26-30 degrees, with the adopted value still matching the optical axis ratio.

quotes.quote: UGC 5005 The original value for the inclination of this galaxy is 41 degrees. MOND demands an inclination in the range 26 to 30 degrees for the best fits. Here we adopt a value of 30 degrees. Fig. pics shows that this value is still consistent with the observed axis ratio.

gmw_00004379.name: UGC 5999
gmw_00004379.overview: UGC 5999 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120]. Modified Newtonian Dynamics models of the galaxy prefer an inclination of 22 degrees rather than 14 degrees, which causes only a minor change in its axis ratios [arXiv:astro-ph/9805120].

sources.summary: UGC 5999 is the exception among bad fits where distance freedom is not preferred; MOND instead demands only a minor inclination increase to 22 degrees.

quotes.quote: Expect for UGC 5999 we find that a two-parameter fit prefers smaller distances which are typically 60 per cent of the original distance. UGC 5999 MOND demands a value for the inclination of 22 degrees instead of 14 degrees. As shown in Fig. pics this results in only a very minor change in the axis ratios.

gmw_00004380.name: IC 1125
gmw_00004380.overview: IC 1125 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Its radial profile features a small bump at 40 arcseconds that corresponds to outer spiral arms, but it otherwise shows no obvious deviation from a single exponential profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1125 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: IC 1125 : J153305.6-013742 According to the coordinates in NED the companion (KPG 467A) in Karachentsev's Isolated Pairs of Galaxies Catalogue corresponds only to a faint starlike object about 11 away. The small bump in the radial profile at 40 corresponds to an outer spiral arms. Otherwise we see no obvious deviation from a single exponential and therefore the galaxy is classified as .

gmw_00004381.name: IC 1158
gmw_00004381.overview: IC 1158 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1308.5545] featuring a central secondary bar and a small embedded ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. It exhibits diffuse star formation [arXiv:1308.5545], contains 292 H II regions [arXiv:1511.01495], and has experienced comparatively recent stellar-mass growth [arXiv:1511.01495].

sources.summary: IC 1158 is described as a spiral galaxy with diffuse star formation; nearby knots enabled a metallicity gradient and an estimated SN-site oxygen abundance of about 8.5 dex.

quotes.quote: IC 1158 is another spiral galaxy with diffuse star formation, particularly concentrated in its southern and central part (see Fig. sn00cb , top-right panel). The slit, which was placed on the SN explosion site and through the galaxy nucleus, reveals that there is no significant H emission at the SN position. However, two bright knots are located a few arcseconds (the nearest at a de-projected distance of 1.24 kpc) from it and a total of 8 H ii regions fell into the slit (see Fig. sn00cb , top-left panel). This allowed us to map the metallicity gradient, as shown in the bottom panel of Fig. sn00cb and to estimate the oxygen abundance at the SN position to be about 8.5 dex. This is consistent with the result of anderson10 .

sources.summary: IC 1158 was covered with two MUSE pointings, contributed 292 HII regions, had the sample's lowest average SFR indicators except sSFR, and showed comparatively recent stellar-mass growth.

quotes.quote: For IC 1158 also 2 pointings were obtained, and the other 4 galaxies were observed with only 1 pointing.
After these cuts, the number of Hii regions that we are left with is: IC 1158 (292), NGC 6754 (423), ESO 362-18 (91), NGC 7742 (323), NGC 7469 (169), NGC 2906 (241).

IC 1158 is the galaxy showing the lowest values in all indicators but in sSFR. Since the sSFR is the ongoing SFR per unit galaxy stellar mass, it gives an indication on the contribution of the current star formation to the galaxy growth. This indicates that a significant fraction (with respect to the other 5 galaxies) of its mass has been recenlty created.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1158 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: IC 1158 : J160134.1+014228 Galaxy shows clearly a truncated profile with an extended break region, starting approximately at the end of the spiral arms. The center looks like a secondary bar with a small ring embedded in larger bar of size roughly 22 (without any noticeable feature in the final profile). So the break with downbending profile at 60 is well beyond a typical break.

gmw_00004382.name: NGC 1087
gmw_00004382.overview: NGC 1087 is a low-mass galaxy with a center that is not well defined [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682, arXiv:2303.13676]. It features a small central bar-like structure with a double nucleus, an outer disk lacking visible spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682], and nearly flat stellar population profiles [arXiv:2303.13676].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1087 as a low-mass exception to usual negative stellar-age trends and metallicity-slope behavior, concluding that it shows nearly flat profiles.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions are NGC 1087 and NGC 1385, which are both at the low-mass end of our sample (log M _ * /M_ of 9.9 and 10.0 , respectively). This is consistent with previous findings of a larger diversity in the radial structure of low-mass galaxies IbarraMedel2016, Smith2022 .
The figures show flatter, or generally slightly negative slopes, with the exception of the same two low-mass galaxies mentioned earlier (NGC 1087, NGC 1385).
Low-mass galaxies NGC 1385 and NGC 1087 are exceptions, showing nearly flat profiles.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1087 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1087 : J024625.2-002955 An apparent faint low surface brightness (center-less) structure towards the North is only scattered light from a nearby star. The galaxy center is not well defined, it shows only a small bar-like structure with a double nucleus, so we used the slightly lopsided (but symmetric) outer disk for centering, which accounts for the central drop inside 50 in the final profile. There is no spiral structure visible in the outer disk.

gmw_00004383.name: NGC 1299
gmw_00004383.overview: NGC 1299 is a small spiral galaxy with a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Surface photometry of the galaxy reveals a radial profile that helps classify its structure, and spectroscopy has detected a nearby object with a similar velocity [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1299 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1299 : J032009.4-061550 Galaxy is small and close to our high axis ratio limit. The background exhibits a gradient but the galaxy is small enough to avoid fitting it. SDSS spectroscopy detects an object 0.2 away from center with similar velocity.

gmw_00004384.name: NGC 2541
gmw_00004384.overview: NGC 2541 is an unbarred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268] featuring a low surface brightness disk with patchy star-forming knots [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Although the galaxy has a warped structure [arXiv:1909.04048, arXiv:2210.09383], it remains relatively regular and undisturbed by its companion located 120 kpc away [arXiv:2210.09383].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2541 as an unbarred spiral with a zero central major-axis gas gradient, a plateau-like gas rotation curve, and non-zero minor-axis velocities attributed to decoupled off-plane gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 949, NGC 2460, NGC 2541, and NGC 6810 are unbarred spiral galaxies which belong to a sample of 16 bright ( B_T 13.5 ) and nearby ( V_ 3500 ) spirals studied by Coccato et al. (2004) to search for a circumnuclear Keplerian disk (Bertola et al. 1998; Funes et al. 2002). The main properties of the sample galaxies are summarized in Table tab:sample . The major-axis rotation curve of their gaseous component is characterized by a remarkable zero-velocity plateau.

The central velocity gradient along the major axis is zero (NGC 949, NGC 2541, NGC 6810) or at least less steep (NGC 2460) than that we measured for radii larger than 3'' . Further out the gas velocity increases to reach a maximum and then remains constant out to the last observed radius. Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis of all the sample galaxies, in spite of which would be expected if the gas traced the circular velocity in the disk plane.
We attribute the minor-axis velocity gradients to the presence of kinematically-decoupled component which is not rotating in the plane of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes UGC 4284 as an irregular galaxy whose baryonic rotation curve cannot explain its mass, finding its HI dark-matter curve favors cored models while H favors DiCintio.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2002 In this article we investigate the outer and inner mass distributions of the irregular galaxies UGC 4284 and UGC 11861, taking advantage of published HI and H high resolution rotation curves and constraining the stellar disk of both galaxies throughout stellar population synthesis studies. In addition we take into account the gas content of both galaxies deriving the HI + He rotation curve. The deduced baryonic rotation curves (star+gas) are inadequate to account for the total mass of UGC 4284 and UGC 11861, for that reason we examine the possibility of dark matter to explain the incongruity between the observed HI and H rotation curves of UGC 4284 and UGC 11861 and the derived baryonic rotation curves. We consider NFW, Burkert, DiCintio, Einasto, and the Stadel dark matter halos, to analyse the dark matter content of UGC 4284 and UGC 11861. The principal results of this work are that cored dark matter models better reproduce the dark matter H and HI rotation curves of UGC 11861 and the dark matter HI rotation curve of UGC 4284, while, the H rotation curve of UGC 4284 is better reproduced by a cuspy DiCintio DM model.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 2541 has an HI warp, but its anomalous component is smooth, axisymmetric, well reproduced, and has unconstrained EPG scale radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 2541. In this galaxy, too, \ finds a warp in both INC and PA, with magnitudes of 5 10 . Our model seems to reproduce well the emission from the anomalous component, which is fairly axisymmetric and smooth.

For three galaxies (NGC 2541, NGC 4062 and NGC 4414) we find a flat posterior in the R_ g parameter, meaning that the EPG scale radius for these systems is unconstrained. Interestingly, this has little impact on the EPG mass, mostly because of the flux normalisation and because the model is always truncated at the outermost radius measured in the rotation curve (we do not extrapolate the EPG mass beyond that radius).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2541 as warped but regular, with a companion about 120 kpc away whose faint edge-of-cube detection limits structural or kinematic analysis.

quotes.quote: A companion is found at a projected distance of 120 kpc with a velocity difference of only 40 (See Figure fig: ). Even though the galaxy is warped, it appears rather regular and undisturbed. The detected companion is on the edge of the cube and hence its detection is too faint in \ to provide any meaningful analysis about its structure or kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2541 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2541 : J081440.1+490341 Galaxy with low surface brightness disk, having many patchy SF knots and an asymmetric (not well described with an ellipse), lopsided outer disk, which makes the chosen ellipticity and PA of the disk uncertain. The central peak is off by 8 compared to the outer disk where the centering is done, which causes the dip in the final profile close to the center. The downbending break at 140 is still inside the nearly symmetric part of the disk, thus classified as but this should be taken with some caution.

gmw_00004385.name: NGC 2543
gmw_00004385.overview: NGC 2543 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1601.04706] that features a double-barred structure, including a long secondary bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy exhibits radial variations in its spiral pitch angle [arXiv:1309.4308] and shows evidence of recent star-formation truncation within its disk [arXiv:1601.04706].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 23028 as a barred sample galaxy whose pitch-angle measurement differs from previous studies because its pitch angle varies radially.

quotes.quote: The galaxies of our sample are mostly without or with insignificant bars, although some galaxies have prominent bars (e.g. PGC 23028 and PGC 22596).

The only galaxy with significant difference between our measurements and other estimates is PGC 23028. Kennicutt (1981) and Ma (2001) give for this galaxy the angles of 13 and 12. 2 respectively, while our value is 16. 7. We argue that this difference may be due to radial variations of the pitch angle: in the inner regions of this galaxy the value of the pitch angle is about 21 -22 while in outer is only 13 -14 , which is close to values obtained by Ma and Kennicutt.

sources.summary: NGC 2543 has recent SFD star-formation truncation, an expected 33 percent unbarred-disk SF fraction, and the youngest measured SFD population in the four-galaxy barred sample.

quotes.quote: However, significant differences are evident in the ages revealed by our spectroscopic analysis, with the SFD regions in two galaxies (NGC 3185 and NGC 3351) having very old populations, while NGC 2543 and NGC 2712 have evidence for more recent truncation of SF, within the last 2 Gyr.

The resulting fractions are: for NGC 2543, 33 per cent; for NGC 2712, 26 per cent; for NGC 3185, 37 per cent; and for NGC 3351, 15 per cent, with the smaller value for the last galaxy being due to its shorter bar.

The youngest population is found for the SFD of NGC 2543 (the two points lying on the 1 Gyr truncation age in Fig. fig:SFD_emcorpluserrors ); this is classified as an SBb, with quite vigorous SF in the disk outside the SFD region.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2543 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2543 : J081258.0+361516 According to NED there is a dwarf elliptical galaxy (KUG 0809+363) at a similar distance. On the SDSS image (confirmed with DSS) there is an additional, low surface brightness structure visible at RA 08 13 08.5 and DEC + 36 16 35, which is possibly another dwarf companion with an unknown distance. The galaxy is clearly double barred with a R 9 long secondary bar.

gmw_00004386.name: NGC 2684
gmw_00004386.overview: NGC 2684 is a galaxy characterized by a slightly asymmetric disk with a bright center [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. It features an inner ring-like structure that produces a bump in its radial profile, though it lacks coherent spiral arms or an obvious associated bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2684 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2684 : J085454.1+490937 The rich cluster in background, with significantly redder colour, forces a large background mask covering a small part of the galaxy. Slightly asymmetric disk with bright center and no coherent spiral arms makes ellipticity and PA uncertain. Bump at 12 in profile is due to an inner ring-like structure, without an associated obvious bar structure.

gmw_00004387.name: NGC 2701
gmw_00004387.overview: NGC 2701 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a lopsided disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy features an off-centered bar and has a small companion galaxy superimposed on its disk at the end of a spiral arm [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2701 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2701 : J085905.9+534616 According to NED there is a small companion (with similar velocity) superimposed on the galaxy disk towards the South at the end of a spiral arm which is masked here in addition to the bright star on the disk towards the West. The galaxy has a lopsided disk with the bar being offcenterd compared to the outer isophotes, where the centering is done. This is responsible for the dip in the center an possibly for the break at 42 , which is therefore called .

gmw_00004388.name: NGC 2776
gmw_00004388.overview: NGC 2776 is classified as an SAB spiral galaxy, although a central bar is not obvious on its images [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Its disk structure displays an almost prototypical radial profile, featuring a bump from inner spiral arms and outer bumps from very weak spiral arm structures [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2776 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2776 : J091214.3+445717 The galaxy is classified as SAB but a bar is not obvious on the image. The background estimate is uncertain due to a nearby (off-field), extremely bright star. For the final profile we used a round ellipticity instead of the usual value at 1 ( ellipse ) which gives an almost prototypical profile with some minor wiggles, a bump at 25 due to the inner spiral arms, and some outer bumps due to some very weak spiral arm structures in the outer disk.

gmw_00004389.name: NGC 2967
gmw_00004389.overview: NGC 2967 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy features very faint, extended, and symmetric spiral arms inside its outer disk that begin beyond a transition region [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2967 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2967 : J094203.3+002011 The galaxy is close to the edge of the SDSS field in an area with an increased background which is masked. Very faint, extended, but symmetric spiral arms inside the outer disk at 100 are visible, which do not continue inwards but start beyond the bump in the transition region at 75 . The exact break radius is therefore uncertain.

gmw_00004390.name: NGC 3246
gmw_00004390.overview: NGC 3246 is an isolated galaxy that features a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. Photometric observations reveal that the galaxy has a lopsided disk with a central bar region that is clearly off-centered relative to its outer isophote [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3246 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3246 : J102641.8+035143 Galaxy is selected from the NGC catalogue by as being isolated. Their measurements show a lopsided disk which resembles the one on the present SDSS image. The central bar region is clearly offcenterd compared to the outer isophote, which is used for the centering.

gmw_00004391.name: NGC 3259
gmw_00004391.overview: NGC 3259 is a spiral galaxy featuring a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682] and a spatially unresolved central compact object [arXiv:astro-ph/0610638]. The galaxy hosts a Type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1411.2902] and has at least one detected dwarf companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GH 94, also called NGC 3259, as Type II AGN-like, with low-quality spectra that are poorly fit by simple Type I models.

quotes.quote: Type II objects As mentioned above, fits to GH 47 and GH 94 show different spectral shapes compared to the other objects. In the case of GH 47, both a simple power-law and a power-law plus blackbody model produce extremely poor fits (reduced- ^2 > 4.1 in both cases). For GH 94 (also known as NGC 3259), both spectra are of low quality, with the PN spectra having 250 counts in GH94a and 200 counts in GH94b (in the 0.3 - 10 keV range). Even so, fits with a simple power-law are poor (reduced- ^2 > 3.9 in both cases). An acceptable fit can be achieved if fitting a power-law plus blackbody model.

However, in this case the power-law index for both spectra is < 0 , far from the standard index for type-I objects. Visual inspection of the spectra show that they resemble type II AGN X-ray spectra rather than type I spectra.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3259 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3259 : J103234.8+650228 The background is rather disturbed by a bright star, but the galaxy is small enough not to be significantly influenced. Galaxy is isolated according to , but has one SDSS-detected dwarf companion ( 18.0 g^ mag) about 10 away with similar velocity ( v=1744 ) and another possible, dwarf companion (or large region) projected on the south part of the disk (no counterpart in NED), which is partly covered by a foreground star, but also clearly present on the DSS image. The break region of the upbending profile at 55 is fairly extended and curved.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 3259 as the sample galaxy whose colors still favor dust and whose unresolved compact central object differs from other resolved nuclear clusters.

quotes.quote: For only one galaxy, NGC 3259, do the arguments favoring the presence of dust remain supported even when using more realistic star formation histories; for all other galaxies in our sample, the composite stellar population model grids can explain the observed colors of their bulges. It might be worth noting that NGC 3259 is the only galaxy in our sample with a spatially unresolved central compact "object" (at HST resolution; see Carollo et al. \ 2002), in contrast with all other galaxies in which, at similar distance, the distinct photometric nucleus is resolved into a nuclear star cluster.
The use of the CSP or ESFH models would require reddening only for one galaxy, NGC 3259, in an amount similar to the one derived from the SSP models.

gmw_00004392.name: NGC 3488
gmw_00004392.overview: NGC 3488 is an isolated spiral galaxy with a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. A small bar is visible as an elongated isophote on the galaxy's image, and a bright star covers part of its outer disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3488 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3488 : J110123.6+574039 Galaxy is isolated according to . A bright star covering part of the outer disk needs an extended mask. A small bar of roughly R 6 size is visible as an elongated isophote on the image.

gmw_00004393.name: NGC 3583
gmw_00004393.overview: NGC 3583 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:astro-ph/0702434] that features multiple spiral modes [arXiv:2405.10673] and a two-component bar structure [arXiv:0702434]. It belongs to a galaxy pair with NGC 3577 [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622] and possesses a small, superimposed elliptical satellite galaxy to its north [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3583 among five galaxies with multiple spiral modes, consistent corotation-resonance distributions, available rotation curves, and short winding times.

quotes.quote: To investigate this aspect, we focus on five objects from our dataset that could have multiple spiral modes: IC 342, M 58, M 74, NGC 3583 and NGC 5371. The R_c distributions of these galaxies have consistent measurements and demonstrate the evident existence of several corotation resonances. In addition, the rotation velocity profiles for these objects are also publicly available.

Subsequently, we obtained rotation curve data v_c(R) for these five objects from the following sources:  Crosthwaite2000 (IC 342),  Lang2020 (M 58),  Walter2008 (M 74),  Noordermeer2005 (NGC 3583),  Sanders1996 (NGC 5371). Analogous phenomena have been observed in other selected galaxies as well. The figure illustrates that, for all the galaxies in our sample, the winding up process of the spiral structure takes approximately two or three galactic years.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3583 as a barred member of a galaxy pair, with S-shaped isovelocities and symmetric residual-velocity spots indicating radial gas flow.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is a member of the pair of galaxies shared with NGC 3577 (their separation is 5 ), but there is no clear evidence of interaction. A small elliptical companion may be found at a distance of about 1 (see the note in RC2 catalog). The continuum image of the galaxy reveals the presence of a bar which can be traced up to 30 and is aligned with PA = 80 ; it also contains a lot of HII regions (Figs. ngc3583 a and ngc3583 b). Isovelocities close to the bar have a typical S-like shape (Fig. ngc3583 ), the kinematic major axis turns here by more than 20 , which is evidence of a significant contribution of non-circular motion into the line-of-sight velocity field (Fig. ngc3583 d). Indeed, within the bar, in the radius range of 2 18 , the line-of-sight velocities differ from the mean rotation velocity field by (50 - 80) .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3583 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3583 : J111411.0+481906 The apparent companion, a spiral galaxy (NGC 3577) towards the south-west is non-physical with v=5336 . The galaxy shows a stream-like arm extending towards the north-west outside of the galaxy connected to a small superimposed E0 satellite at 0.9 north (not in NED). Due to this structure the outer disk seems to have a slightly different ellipticity and PA.

sources.summary: NGC 3583 is used as a DG-type spiral example with a two-component bar, broad torque peak, and outer bar component showing spiral-like characteristics.

quotes.quote: For example, NGC 3583 clearly has a two-component maximum at < 40'', both in the lower and in the higher Fourier modes, indicating that the bar has both an inner and an outer component. The Q_T peak is also very broad. However the phase of the A_2 component is maintained nearly constant only to 25'', which suggests that the outer part of the bar has some spiral-like characteristics, as is visible also in the direct image of this galaxy.

gmw_00004394.name: NGC 3589
gmw_00004394.overview: NGC 3589 is a spiral galaxy with a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. It exhibits a highly inclined, extended elongated structure and a bar of undefined size [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3589 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3589 : J111513.2+604201 Due to the higher inclination and extended elongated structure the size of the bar is not well defined. The center is also not well defined, but centering on the outer isophote coincides roughly with the brightest pixel. The sky estimate is rather uncertain due to the bright star in the FOV.

gmw_00004395.name: NGC 3642
gmw_00004395.overview: NGC 3642 is a nearly face-on, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1903.04119, arXiv:1408.4070] featuring a LINER nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0702292] and a dusty spiral structure [arXiv:2201.05080]. Its star formation [arXiv:1903.04119], which may be induced by a merger with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1903.04119], is concentrated along its spiral arms [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3642 as a face-on spiral near the absorber, with measurable star formation, possible merger-induced activity, and outflows that could influence gas abundances.

quotes.quote: Apart from the aforementioned possible associations, there is a spiral galaxy, NGC 3642 , at = 142 kpc ( 0.6R_ vir ), and v = +362 , with ( L/L^* ) _g 0.6 and a star formation rate of 0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 , indicated by its integrated H luminosity. This galaxy is nearly face-on, with an inclination of 20.4^ Verdes2002 with respect to the plane of the sky.
Indeed it has been proposed that the star formation in NGC 3642 is likely to have been induced by a merger with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy accreted from its local environment Verdes2002 .

The relative chemical abundances of the gas can be influenced by outflows induced by star formation activity in IC 691 and/or the spiral galaxy NGC 3642 , as well as from merger events and gas stripping of the CGM in the overall galaxy rich environment Chung2007,Yoon2013 .

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 3642 as Disky, with diffuse circumnuclear H-alpha emission, dusty spiral structure, and a broad nuclear spectral component interpreted as a candidate outflow signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 3642 (see Fig. Figure_AB3 ): As in our image, detect a strong nuclear source surrounded by some diffuse circumnuclear H emission. We have classified this emission as Disky , concentrated along the spiral arms, with no outflowing signatures. describe the nuclear region of this galaxy as being elongated, probably due to a dust lane, from where dusty spiral arms emerge. The kinematic study performed by suggest the presence of two kinematic components in the nuclear spectrum, being the broadest component a candidate signature of a nuclear outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3642 as a late-type high-bulge-index galaxy whose fitted bulge-to-disc size ratio and bulge index both decline when the image is artificially redshifted.

quotes.quote: The five Sb--Sc galaxies with n_ b >4 are NGC5430, NGC2841, NGC3521, NGC3642 and NGC4698, with n_ b = 4.2 , 5.7 , 6.7 , 6.8 and 7.1 , respectively.
NGC3642 SA()bc A late-type galaxy for which we measure r_ e,b /r_ e,d = 2.5 and n_ b = 6.7 .
However, by examining the equivalent of Fig. fig:gal1 for NGC3642, we noticed that that both r_ e,b /r_ e,d and n_ b decrease with redshift until z=0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3642 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3642 : J112218.0+590427 The background is slightly inhomogeneous and the galaxy is rather extended. From the nearly round inner disk a single, star forming, spiral arm starts towards the outer disk with slightly more than one revolution, forming the strongly asymmetric, lopsided outer disk. Therefore the ellipticity and PA is determined further inside.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3642 as a LINER nucleus with a radio upper limit, modest 2500-band UV variability, and concentrated nuclear X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3642 - Nagar et al. (2000) placed a 3 upper limit of 0.39 mJy on the 2 cm radio flux from this nucleus, at VLA resolution. M05 measured 8 per cent peak-to-peak amplitude variations around the mean 2500 level, 24.5 10^ 17 ), providing a lower limit on the AGN flux. The 3300 flux was 22.1 10^ 17 ), but not definitively variable.

gmw_00004396.name: NGC 3756
gmw_00004396.overview: NGC 3756 is a spiral galaxy characterized by a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. It is known to possess three tightly wound inner spiral arms and one wide spiral arm [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3756 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3756 : J113648.3+541737 Only partly fitted since 1/4 of galaxy is beyond field. Known three armed spiral galaxy which shows one wide spiral arm starting at 60 extending roughly towards the downbending break at 100 . The extended bump 45 is due to the three inner tightly wound spiral arms and makes it, similar to NGC 3631, difficult to fit the inner region.

gmw_00004397.name: NGC 3888
gmw_00004397.overview: NGC 3888 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a visible disk structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682]. The galaxy features a single malformed, kinked spiral arm across its disk and has a possible dwarf, irregular companion located to the south-west [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3888 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3888 : J114734.7+555800 The background on the image shows a large gradient due to a bright star off the field, but the galaxy is small enough to be not affected. The galaxy has one 'malformed' (kinked) spiral arm across the disk. There is a possible dwarf, irregular companion visible towards the south-west, but without any distance information.

gmw_00004398.name: NGC 3893
gmw_00004398.overview: NGC 3893 is a massive spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290] that forms a pair with NGC 3896 without showing signs of tidal interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. Its structure features a small bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622], three distinct corotation resonances [arXiv:2405.14483], and a maximal stellar disk that dominates its central dynamics [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC3893 consistent with three CRs, flags its first CR as off the flat rotation plateau, and reinterprets reported resonances as overlooked coupling.

quotes.quote: For NGC2403, NGC3893, and NGC5676 we found data to be consistent with three different CRs, and for three other galaxies we find two. Firstly, we note that the first CR in NGC3893 is located before v() reaches the plateau velocity, v_0 . First, the case of special interest is the galaxy NGC2403 and the first resonance coupling in NGC3893. This is not the case for NGC3893, because the first CR is not located within the RC plateau.

As already mentioned, 2011ApJ...741L..14F,2014ApJS..210....2F studied changes of radial velocity sign and found them in many cases, including the presented NGC3344, NGC3893, and NGC5676 (UGC5840, UGC6778 and UGC9366 in their works). For NGC3893, they did not notice the coupling at 63 3 arcsec and interpreted the last point as a separate pattern, when it should be OUHR.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3893 as one of the most massive sample spirals, finding its disk mass fraction well constrained and consistent with a maximal stellar disk.

quotes.quote: We present results from a detailed dynamical analysis of five high surface brightness, late type spiral galaxies NGC 3810, NGC 3893, NGC 4254, NGC 5676, and NGC 6643 which were studied with the aim to quantify the luminous-to-dark matter ratio inside their optical radii. The galaxies' stellar light distribution and gas kinematics have been observed and compared to hydrodynamic gas simulations which predict the gas dynamics arising in response to empirical gravitational potentials, which are combinations of differing stellar disk and dark halo contributions.

For NGC 3893 the disk mass fraction could be estimated quite well, placing this galaxy into the realm of maximum disks. In light of this, Figure allgalcompare shows that a maximal disk scenario applies to NGC 3893 and NGC 5676. Combining the maximum rotation velocity, presented in Table properties , with the estimates for the galaxies' most probable fractions of the stellar disk mass, reveals an intriguing trend: the most massive of the analyzed galaxies, NGC 3893 and NGC 5676, tend to also possess the most massive stellar disks which dominate the dynamics of the central regions.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3893 forms a non-interacting pair, has a rising rotation curve, and shows bar- and local-dynamical perturbations in its H velocity field.

quotes.quote: The galaxy forms a pair with NGC 3896 without any sign of tidal interaction (their separation is 3 9 ). The rotation curve rises all along the radius, having no clear maximum (Fig. ngc3893 d). The turn of the kinematic major axis at <20 is related to the velocity field perturbation caused by a small bar aligned at PA 20 , which is barely seen in the continuum image (Fig. ngc3893 a). The variations of PA_0 at =20 50 may be a spiral arm effect. At the radii of 37 45 in the direction of PA=140 , at the inner edge of the spiral arm, the H emission-line profiles have a double-peaked structure ( see Fig. fig_prof j), the secondary (non-circular) component being redshifted by +50 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3893 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3893 : J114838.4+484234 The background around the galaxy is affected by a nearby, bright star and a close (3.7 away, with similar velocity v=905 , SB0/a:pec), smaller, companion galaxy (NGC 3896), both members of the Ursa Major Cluster. Galaxy exhibits a wide, extended spiral arm like structure towards the east, which could also be a stream or tidal feature. Due to this structure it is not possible to derive the ellipticity and PA from the outer disk, so their values are more uncertain.

gmw_00004399.name: NGC 4041
gmw_00004399.overview: NGC 4041 is an Sbc spiral galaxy with no detected active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0405253]. It features a compact, rotating nuclear disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0405253] and an asymmetric outer disk containing a detached, star-forming spiral arm structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4041 as a non-AGN Sbc spiral with a compact rotating nuclear disk and only an upper limit on any central black hole mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 4041 is classified as a Sbc spiral galaxy with no detected AGN activity. Its average heliocentric radial velocity from radio measurements is 1227 9 becoming 1480 after correction for Local Group infall onto Virgo.

The HST /STIS spectra were used to map the velocity field of the gas in its nuclear region. We detected the presence of a compact ( 0 . ^ 4 40 ), high surface brightness, circularly rotating nuclear disk cospatial with a nuclear star cluster.

Combining the results from the standard and alternative models, the present data only allow us to set an upper limit of 2 10^ 7 M _ to the mass of the nuclear BH. If this upper limit is taken in conjunction with an estimated bulge B magnitude of 17.7 and with a central stellar velocity dispersion of 95 , the putative black hole in NGC 4041 is not inconsistent with both the \ and the \ correlations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4041 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4041 : J120212.2+620814 According to this galaxy forms a kinematic triplet with NGC 4036 and UGC 7009 and is probably member of the Ursa Major Cluster. However, does not list any of these as members. Galaxy shows a detached, extended, star-forming spiral arm structure in the outer, asymmetric disk, similar to NGC 2967, but smaller and only one-sided, which makes the photometric inclination (ellipticity) and PA uncertain.

gmw_00004400.name: HE 0435-4312
gmw_00004400.overview: HE 0435-4312 is a luminous, highly accreting quasar located at a redshift of approximately 1.22 [arXiv:2012.12409, arXiv:2304.13763]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2012.12409] and exhibits variability in both its continuum and Mg II broad-line emission [arXiv:2012.12409].

sources.summary: The paper reverberation-maps quasar HE 0435-4312, measuring its MgII lag, high accretion state, black-hole mass, variability, and usefulness for radius-luminosity cosmology.

quotes.quote: Using the six years of the spectroscopic monitoring of the luminous quasar HE 0435-4312 ( z=1.2231 ) with the Southern African Large Telescope (SALT), in combination with the photometric data (CATALINA, OGLE, SALTICAM, and BMT), we determined the rest-frame time-delay of 296^ +13 _ 14 days between the MgII broad-line emission and the ionizing continuum using seven different time-delay inference methods. The MgII emission is considerably variable with the fractional variability of 5.4 , which is comparable to the continuum variability. Because of its high luminosity ( L_ 3000 =10^ 46.4 erg s^ 1 ), the source is beneficial for a further reduction of the scatter along the MgII-based radius-luminosity relation and its extended versions, especially when the high-accreting subsample that has an RMS scatter of 0.2 dex is considered.

The source HE 0435-4312 ( z=1.2231 ) hosts a supermassive black hole of 2.2 10^9 M_ that is highly accreting with the Eddington ratio of 0.58 according to the SED best-fit of sredzinska2017 . The peculiarity of the source is a smooth shift of the MgII line peak first towards longer wavelengths, while currently the shift proceeds towards shorter wavelengths. This line shift could hint at the presence of a supermassive black hole binary sredzinska2017 .

sources.summary: The paper monitors HE 0435-4312 with SALT, measuring Mg II and UV Fe II reverberation delays and finding nearly co-spatial regions plus symmetric wavelength-resolved behavior.

quotes.quote: The two quasars, HE 0413-4031 and HE 0435-4312 were discovered in the Hamburg-ESO slitless survey wisotzki2000 . These relatively bright quasars, located at the intermediate redshift (z 1), were selected for long-term monitoring with the Southern Large African Telescope (SALT). For these two sources, as well as for the third quasar, CTS C30.10, the time delay of the Mg II line with respect to the continuum was already determined Czerny2019, Zajacek2020, zajacek2021 , and the wavelength-resolved delays for CTS C30.10 were obtained prince_CTS_2022 .
The mean total FeII time delay is nearly equal to the mean total MgII time delay for HE 0435-4312 suggesting a co-spatiality of their emission regions.

For HE 0435-4312, we see a roughly symmetric behavior, particularly in ICCF, ^2 , and zDCF results, with the shortest time delay in curve 4 (see Figure  fig:mean_rms ), most dominated by Mg II and a longer time delay of the wings with a strong contribution from Fe II implied by the spectral decomposition (see Figure  fig:decomp ). In the case of these three methods, the rest frame time delay at the core of Mg II is 225 days while Fe II-dominated wings show 275 days time delay.

sources.summary: SALT monitoring of HE 0435-4312 finds rising Mg II and Fe II emission, a fast Mg II line shift, a non-Gaussian line shape, and separated Mg II and Fe II regions.

quotes.quote: Using the Southern African Large Telescope (SALT) with the Robert Stobie Spectrograph (RSS) we performed ten spectroscopic observations of quasar HE 0435-4312 ( z=1.2231 ) over a period of three years (Dec 23/24, 2012 to Dec 7/8, 2015). Both the Mg II line and the Fe II pseudo-continuum increase with time. We clearly detect the systematic shift of the Mg II line with respect to the Fe II over the years, corresponding to the acceleration of 104 14 km s ^ 1 year ^ 1 in the quasar rest frame. The Mg II line shape is clearly non-Gaussian but single-component, and the increase in line equivalent width and line shift is not accompanied with significant evolution of the line shape. We analyse the conditions in the Mg II and Fe II formation region and we note that the very large difference in the covering factor and the turbulent velocity also support the conclusion that the two regions are spatially separated.

gmw_00004401.name: CXO J133815.6 + 043255
gmw_00004401.overview: CXO J133815.6 + 043255 is an ultraluminous X-ray source located approximately 10 kiloparsecs away from the center of the lenticular galaxy NGC 5252 [arXiv:2001.07927]. It is kinematically associated with NGC 5252 and is hypothesized to be the remnant core of a merging dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2001.07927].

sources.summary: The paper studies CXO J133815.6 + 043255 as a ULX whose NIR counterpart has dwarf-galaxy-like stellar mass, possibly the stripped remnant core merging with NGC 5252.

quotes.quote: The interesting ULX (CXO J133815.6 + 043255) was identified in NGC 5252, a type 2 Seyfert lenticular galaxy. The ULX is 22 \ ( 10 kpc) away from the centre of NGC 5252 and has an X-ray luminosity L_ X-ray 1.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 kim_2015 . Previous studies using spectroscopic data suggest the ULX is kinematically associated with the host with velocity offset of 13 kim_2015, kim_2017 .

Therefore, it is natural to suggest that the ULX is the remnant core of a dwarf galaxy. The dynamical mass of the ULX counterpart as derived from the rotating ionized gas surrounding the ULX, is 10^ 7.5 supporting such a scenario kim_2017 . However, we cannot rule out that the ULX is a recoiling BH. A key physical parameter to answer this question is the stellar mass associated with the ULX. Therefore, in this paper we present near-infrared (NIR) photometric data for the ULX CXO J133815.6 + 043255, and we use it to estimate the stellar mass.

gmw_00004402.name: BD38
gmw_00004402.overview: BD38 is a bright starbursting Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z = 5.515 [arXiv:astro-ph/0507306, arXiv:astro-ph/0612454]. Spectroscopic observations reveal a strong continuum break and interstellar absorption features, but the galaxy lacks Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0507306, arXiv:astro-ph/0612454].

sources.summary: The paper describes BD38 as an unusually bright confirmed z~6 Lyman-break galaxy with a strong continuum break, interstellar absorption features, extended disturbed morphology, and no Ly-alpha emission.

quotes.quote: BD38 This object was also undetected in the LRIS observations, but yields a strong continuum in the FORS2 spectrum. Figure 4202 shows the two- and one- dimensional spectra of the object, along with the ACS and ISAAC imaging. A precipitous continuum break is clearly seen at \ 7900 , reducing the continuum from 27 1 to 0 1 counts ^ 1 . Also, several strong absorption features are found at 8205.77 , 8485.38 , 8498.43 , 8699.72 , 9079.21 , and 9140.84 \ in the spectrum. Associating the break with the UV optically thick \ forest, and the absorption lines with the strong interstellar absorption features typical of LBGs at lower redshifts see Shapley2003,Ando2004 , yields a redshift determination of 5.515 0.003.

BD38 is an unusually bright i dropout. Its z_ 850 band magnitude is 24.3, making this object more than 1.3 mag brighter than any other i dropout in our spectroscopic or photometric sample. In fact, to our knowledge, this object is still the brightest z 6 LBG discovered to date with a confirmed redshift.

sources.summary: BD38 is confirmed as a bright z=5.515 starbursting Lyman-break galaxy with strong continuum, interstellar absorption, no Ly alpha emission, high UV luminosity, and substantial stellar mass.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we report on the spectroscopic confirmation of a particularly bright z_ 850 = 24.3 Bouwens2003 z 6 i dropout candidate: object 1252-5224-4599, hereafter BD38, in the RDCS 1252.9-2927 field (CL1252) Rosati1998 . We show that this large ( r_ hl = 0 29) starbursting galaxy has a Lyman continuum break and interstellar absorption features at a redshift of z = 5.515, but lacks Ly emission. The Hubble Space Telescope Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) observations of BD38 ( _ 2000 = 12^h52^m56 888, _ 2000 = -29 25 55 50 ) involved three orbits of F775W ( i_ 775 ) and five orbits of F850LP ( z_ 850 ).

BD38's observed magnitudes are i_ 775 = 25.76 0.15, z_ 850 = 24.25 0.05, Js = 24.1 0.1, Ks 23.8 0.1, 3.6 = 23.0 0.3, and 4.5 = 22.9 0.4. This demonstrates that BD38 is a LBG at z = 5.515 0.003. This galaxy is brighter than any confirmed z 6 i dropout to date in the z_ 850 band, and both the 3.6 and 4.5 channels, and is the most massive starbursting galaxy known at z > 5.

gmw_00004403.name: 4C04.42
gmw_00004403.overview: The active galaxy 4C04.42 is a gamma-ray-emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1904.12863, arXiv:1509.03030] located at a redshift of approximately 0.966 [arXiv:1904.12863, arXiv:2104.05525]. It exhibits a compact, core-dominated radio structure [arXiv:2104.05525] and displays rapid infrared variability [arXiv:1509.03030].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS J1222 + 0413/4C +04.42 as the highest-redshift gamma-ray NLS1, using multiwavelength data and SED modeling to study accretion, jet emission, and high black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-frequency study of PKS J1222 + 0413 (4C +04.42 ), currently the highest redshift ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 ( NLS1). We assemble a broad spectral energy distribution (SED) including previously unpublished datasets: X-ray data obtained with the NuSTAR and Neil Gehrels Swift observatories; near-infrared, optical and UV spectroscopy obtained with VLT X-shooter; and multiband radio data from the Effelsberg telescope. These new observations are supplemented by archival data from the literature. We apply physical models to the broadband SED, parameterising the accretion flow and jet emission to investigate the disc-jet connection. PKS J1222 + 0413 has a much greater black hole mass than most other NLS1s, M_ BH black 2 10^8 M _ , similar to those found in flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQs).

PKS J1222 + 0413 (RA: 12 22 22.548, Dec: + 04 13 15.75) was identified as a ray NLS1 candidate by and, at redshift z=0.9662 , is currently the most distant example of this class known. From its SDSS spectrum, they determined its mass to be M_ BH =1.8 - 2.0 10^ 8 M _ . Its simultaneous and quasi-simultaneous SED was studied by , although at the time it was not known to be a NLS1.

sources.summary: 4C04.42 is treated as a powerful z=0.965 flat-spectrum radio quasar whose broadband X-ray to gamma-ray SED shows synchrotron and external-Compton components plus a soft X-ray excess.

quotes.quote: We present the broadband analysis of the powerful quasar (z=0.965) observed by and . The 0.2--200 keV spectrum is well reproduced with a hard power-law component ( 1.2), augmented by a soft component below 2 keV (observer frame), which is described by a thermal blackbody with temperature kT 0.15 keV. Using archival data we compile the not-simultaneous Spectral Energy Distribution of the source from radio to gamma-ray frequencies. The SED shows two main components: the low frequency one produced by Synchrotron radiation from the electrons moving in the jet and the high energy one produced through external Compton scattering of the electrons with the photon field of the Broad Line Region.

is a FSRQ, with radio loudness parameter R =S _ 5GHz /S _ 4400 A 1000 kellermann89 . It was detected above 20 keV by \ bird07 with a flux of (7.6 1.5) 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and (18.8 2.8) 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in 20-40 keV and 40-100 keV bands respectively. Throughout this paper we adopt a luminosity distance of d _ L = 6358 Mpc for (z = 0.965), derived for a CDM cosmology with _ m = 0.27, _ = 0.73 and H _ 0 = 71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J122222.55+041315.7 as a distant gamma-ray-emitting NLS1 with broad H emission, strong Fe II, weak [O III], radio/infrared/X-ray variability, and jet-like SED behavior.

quotes.quote: We report on the identification of a new ray-emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy, SDSS J122222.55+041315.7, which increases the number of known objects of this remarkable but rare type of active galactic nuclei (AGN) to seven.
