Its optical spectrum, obtained in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey-Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey, reveals a broad H emission line with a width (FWHM) of 1734 104 km s ^ 1 . This, along with strong optical Fe ii multiplets [ R_ 4570 =0.9 ] and a weak [ O iii ] 5007 emission line, makes the object a typical NLS1. On the other hand, the source exhibits a high radio brightness temperature, rapid infrared variability, and a flat X-ray spectrum extending up to 200 keV. It is associated with a luminous ray source detected significantly with Fermi /LAT.
The spectral energy distribution can be well modelled by a one-zone leptonic jet model. This new member is by far the most distant ray-emitting NLS1, at a redshift of z=0.966 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 1219+044 as a compact blazar reference source, describing its redshift, candidate gamma-ray narrow-line Seyfert identity, compact VLBA morphology, and EAVN image improvements.

quotes.quote: 1219+044 (PKS J1222+0413) is a bright compact blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.966 (1 mas = 8.15 pc, paris2017 ). The source has recently been identified as a candidate of ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert I galaxy ( kynoch2019 ). Previous VLBA images at 15 GHz show an extremely compact core-dominated structure (unresolved up to 442 10^6 _ 15GHz = 8840 km) with a tiny amount of jet emission toward the south ( lister2019 ). Therefore 1219+044 may serve as a useful reference source to check the amplitude accuracy of each antenna.

The EAVN images of 1219+044 shown in figure fig:compK (a)-() and fig:compQ (a)-(b) are characterized by a quasi-unresolved structure at both frequencies, which is consistent with the flat visibility amplitude distributions in the uv domain (figure fig:amp17077K and fig:amp17086Q ).

gmw_00004404.name: NGC 3786
gmw_00004404.overview: NGC 3786 is a spiral galaxy classified as SABa [arXiv:2111.10036] that hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.08062, arXiv:2111.10036]. The galaxy features a dynamically decoupled central minibar [arXiv:astro-ph/0211105] and recently exhibited a changing-look mid-infrared flare likely caused by an obscured tidal disruption event [arXiv:2208.08062].

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets NGC 3786 as having spiral-arm-driven isophotal variations rather than a large-scale bar, undermining earlier double-bar classifications.

quotes.quote: NGC 3786 (Mrk 744) Our disk orientation parameters for this galaxy are in agreement with the results of Afanasiev Shapovalova ( ). Afanasiev et al. ( ) from their imaging data found two bars with semi-major axes of 7'' and 25'' in this Sy 1.8 galaxy. However, our Fourier analysis of the optical and NIR images shows that the line of the maximum of the m=2 harmonic, at >6-7'' , is wound into a regular spiral that coincides with the global two-armed spiral pattern of the galactic disk.

This spiral also appears in the residual brightness maps (Fig. 3786m ). Thus, the radial variations of PA of the inner isophotes caused by the spiral arms have led Afanasiev et al. ( ) to an erroneous conclusion about a large-scale bar in NGC 3786 .

sources.summary: The paper reports an obscured mid-infrared flare in Seyfert 1.8 NGC 3786, linking new broad emission lines and changing-look behavior to a likely tidal disruption event.

quotes.quote: We report an exceptional mid-infrared flare in the Seyfert 1.8 NGC 3786. In the multi-epoch data from the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer, the nuclear mid-infrared brightness of NGC 3786 appears to vary substantially up to 0.5-0.8 mag around mid-2020. However, there is no evidence of significant variation in the corresponding light curve of the optical band from the Zwicky Transient Facility. This implies that the flare may have been heavily obscured by nuclear dust. Through follow-up spectroscopic observations with Gemini-North after the flare, we find that broad emission lines in \ and \ newly appear, while the broad \ emission is marginally detected in the post-flare spectrum.

This reveals that the flare is associated with the changing-look phenomenon from type 1.8 to type 1. Based on these findings, we argue that the flare is likely to originate from an obscured tidal disruption event, although extreme variation in the accretion rate may not be ruled out completely.

sources.summary: NGC 3786 is an SABa Seyfert 1.9 galaxy whose inner region has young, slightly metal-rich populations and strong featureless continuum, surrounded by older, less-metallic stars and rotating disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3786 This galaxy receives the SABa classification, with a Seyfert 1.9 nucleus. According to our analysis, the inner 0 8 are dominated by a young and slightly more metallic SP, co-spatial with a 50 contribution from a FC. This region is surrounded by an older, less metallic region extending up to 1 0 in all directions. Also, except for border effects, the A _ V and are nearly constant inside our FoV. Lastly, the V map is well reproduced by a rotating disk model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3786 as hosting a dynamically decoupled central minibar; Fourier analysis reinterprets a supposed outer bar as a two-armed spiral while gas kinematics support the minibar.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3786, on the scale of the inner bar ( < 6'' ), PA_ dyn deviates from the line of nodes by more than 10^ ; the deviations are in the opposite direction compared with the central isophotes (Fig. 5b). Here, we actually observe a dynamically decoupled central minibar about 2 kpc in diameter. Its existence was first suspected by Afanasiev and Shapovalova (1981) when studying the gas kinematics in NGC 3786.

In this galaxy, however, we cannot speak about a dynamically decoupled secondary bar, because a Fourier analysis of the surface-brightness distribution (Subsect. 3.3) indicates that the two-armed spiral mistaken by Afanasiev et al., (1998) for the outer bar is located in NGC 3786 at = 7-20'' .

gmw_00004405.name: DDO 78
gmw_00004405.overview: DDO 78 is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy belonging to the M81 group [arXiv:1003.0861, arXiv:astro-ph/0208428]. It features a flat surface-brightness profile, spatially separated stellar populations with metallicity gradients, and no detected neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1003.0861, arXiv:astro-ph/0208428].

sources.summary: DDO 78 is grouped among the M81 dSphs with metallicity gradients and spatially separated populations, and the paper describes it as fairly metal rich.

quotes.quote: Population Gradients By examining the cumulative metallicity distributions in the middle panels in Fig. sl_figure7 and Fig. sl_figure8 , we conclude that the metallicity gradients are present in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1, while the metallicity gradients are less pronounced or not present in the remaining dSphs. We again separate the RGB stars in each dSph into two samples, where we choose to separate the distributions at the observed weighted mean metallicity. The probabilities from the two-sided Kolmogorov-Smirnov (K-S) test that the two components are drawn from the same parent distribution are listed in Table table3 , column (4). The K-S results are consistent with showing spatially separated populations in the case of DDO 71, DDO 78, DDO 44 and F6D1.

All of them show either a spatial variation of the centroids of the two stellar populations, as is the case of F12D1 and KDG 64, or that the metal-rich population is more centrally concentrated, as is the case of DDO 71, DDO 44 and F6D1. These findings agree well with the ones from the cumulative histograms, though we should keep in mind that the metal-poor and metal-rich stellar populations involved are differently selected. DDO 78 and IKN are clearly fairly metal-rich while KDG 61, KDG 64 and DDO 44 have prominent metal-poor populations.

Thus, the N_ blends for all the dSphs computed this way is equal to 5 blends, 11 blends, 12 blends and 2 blends in the case of DDO 44, DDO 78, IKN and HS 117, respectively, while in all the other cases the number of blends is less than 1.

sources.summary: The paper describes DDO 78 as a faint M81-group dwarf spheroidal with a flat surface-brightness profile, no H I detection, measured distance, and a globular cluster member.

quotes.quote: DDO 78 is one of the faintest DSphs of the group ( M_ V =-12.83 ). According to Karachentseva et al. (1987), DDO 78 has a very flat surface brightness profile with _ B(0) = 25.1^m/ , r_ ef =32 , and B(t)=15.8 . It was not detected in H I by Fisher Tully (1981) and van Driel et al. (1998). An accurate distance modulus of DDO 78 is (m-M)_0=27.85 0.15 according to Karachentsev et al. (2000). The brightest globular cluster candidate in DSph galaxy DDO 78 has the integrated apparent magnitude V_ t =19.45 , the integrated color after correction for Galactic reddening (V-I)_ 0 =1.07 , the angular half-light radius R(0.5L)=0.3 , the central surface brightness _v(0)=18.0 ^m/ , the linear projected separation of the globular cluster from the galaxy center 0.26 kpc.

gmw_00004406.name: UGC 11178
gmw_00004406.overview: UGC 11178 is a lenticular galaxy that features a ring-like extinction structure visible across all photometric bands [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. This structure likely causes abrupt changes in the galaxy's ellipticity, while its color maps remain featureless [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 11178 has no prior photometric study; the paper reports ring-like extinction structure, abrupt ellipticity changes, positive b4 values, and featureless color maps.

quotes.quote: UGC 11178 : This galaxy has no previous reported photometric study. The extinction map show a ring like structure in all the bands. The ellipticity abruptly changes between 5 and 7 , which could be because of the ring like structure. The b_4 coefficient is significantly positive upto 7 . The color maps are featureless.

gmw_00004407.name: UGC 11781
gmw_00004407.overview: UGC 11781 is classified as an S0 galaxy in the UGC catalog and as an SAB galaxy in the RC3 catalog [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. The galaxy possesses a clear bar structure, which is visible in both direct and extinction images [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 11781 is described as S0 in UGC but SAB in RC3, with a clear bar, ellipticity and position-angle changes, and positive b4 in that region.

quotes.quote: UGC 11781 : This galaxy is classified as S0 in the UGC but as SAB in the RC3 catalogue. The presence of a bar is clear in the direct as well as extinction images. There is a dip in ellipticity profile at 10 , where there is a change in the position angle profile as well. The b_4 coefficient is positive in this region.

gmw_00004408.name: UGC 1250
gmw_00004408.overview: UGC 1250 is classified as an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It features a distorted ring structure surrounding its bulge, a disky b4 profile, and numerous H II regions distributed across its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 1250 is described as an S0 galaxy with a clear distorted ring around the bulge, H II regions across the disk, and a disky b4 profile.

quotes.quote: UGC 1250 : While this galaxy is classified as S0, the B-R color map of the galaxy show a very clear, but distorted ring, just around the bulge. + [ NII ] observations by Pogge Eskridge (1993) show copious HII regions across the disk. The b_4 profile is disky at all major-axis lengths, with the coefficient being larger in the inner region of the galaxy.

gmw_00004409.name: UGC 2039
gmw_00004409.overview: UGC 2039 is a galaxy paired with UGC 2049 that exhibits a rise in ellipticity from 0.1 to 0.5 towards its outer region [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. Its outer region features a disky b4 coefficient suggesting the presence of a disk, and the galaxy has been detected by IRAS at 60 and 100 microns [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: The appendix identifies UGC 2039 as paired with UGC 2049 and finds rising outer ellipticity plus a disky outer b4 coefficient indicating a significant disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 2039 : UGC 2039 is paired with UGC 2049. There is a rise in the ellipticity from 0.1 to 0.5 towards the outer region. The b_4 coefficient is disky in the outer region, which suggests the presence of a significant disk. The galaxy has been detected by IRAS at 60 and 100 .

gmw_00004410.name: UGC 3178
gmw_00004410.overview: UGC 3178 is a galaxy that exhibits a dust feature clearly visible in its B-band extinction map [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. This dust feature is also detectable through the isophotal analysis of B and K' band images [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 3178 is described as showing a dust feature in the B-band extinction map, wavelength-dependent isophotal parameters, and poor V and R signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: UGC 3178 : A dust feature is clearly visible in the B band extinction map of this galaxy, and is also detectable from the isophotal analysis of B and K' band images. The b_4 coefficient, position angle and ellipticity are different in B and K' , which may be due to dust absorption in B . Our V and R band images suffer from poor S/N.

gmw_00004411.name: UGC 3536
gmw_00004411.overview: UGC 3536 is a lenticular galaxy that features a dusty disk and a disky b4 coefficient [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. This disk is visible in B-V and B-R color maps and is associated with a rise and subsequent decrease in the galaxy's ellipticity [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 3536 is described as having a significant dusty disk visible in color maps, disky b4 coefficients, and ellipticity behavior attributed to the disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 3536 : This galaxy has a significant dusty disk and the b_4 coefficient is disky. The disk is also evident in the B-V and B-R color maps. There is a constant rise in ellipticity for semi-major axis length <19.5 , beyond which there is a decrease, which could be because of the disk.

gmw_00004412.name: UGC 3792
gmw_00004412.overview: UGC 3792 is a lenticular galaxy classified as S0 in the UGC and as SA0/a in the RC3 catalogue [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It features a dust lane along its major axis that affects its profiles and creates large color gradients [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 3792 is described as S0 in UGC but SA0/a in RC3, with a prominent dust lane that strongly affects profiles and creates large color gradients.

quotes.quote: UGC 3792 : This galaxy is classified as S0 in the UGC but as SA0/a in the RC3 catalogue, and has no previous reported photometric study. The galaxy has a prominent dust lane along the major axis which greatly affects the various profiles. This dust lane is most prominent in the B band. The b_4 coefficient is positive in the outer region. There is a large color gradient in B-V, B-R and V-K' , presumably because of the dust.

gmw_00004413.name: UGC 4347
gmw_00004413.overview: UGC 4347 is a lenticular galaxy that features a large dust patch near its center [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. The galaxy exhibits a disky profile throughout its observed region, along with faint extinction structures and possible shells [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 4347 is described as having a central dust patch, disky b4 profile, possible shells, and faint extinction structures affecting isophotal coefficients.

quotes.quote: UGC 4347 : Our B-V and B-K' color and extinction maps of this galaxy reveal a large dust patch near the center. The b_4 profile is disky throughout the observed region. Forbes Thomson (1992) have detected possible shells in this galaxy. The extinction images in all bands shows faint structures, which may be responsible for the non-zero values the of b_4 coefficient.

gmw_00004414.name: UGC 4767
gmw_00004414.overview: UGC 4767 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It exhibits differences in its B, R, and K-band profiles at small radii, which are attributed to a central dust patch [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 4767 is described as having B, R, and K-band profile differences at small radii, attributed to a central dust patch also seen in color maps.

quotes.quote: UGC 4767 : The position angle and ellipticity profile for this galaxy are different in the B, R and K' bands for semi-major axis lengths <5 , which could be because of a dust patch near the center of the galaxy. The various color maps also indicate the presence of the dust patch.
In this appendix, we discuss structural properties of each galaxy, whenever isophotes in one or more bands depart from a smooth ellipse.

gmw_00004415.name: UGC 4901
gmw_00004415.overview: UGC 4901 is a lenticular galaxy with a poor signal-to-noise ratio in its B-band image [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. Its V-R and V-K' color maps show no features, but a positive outer b4 coefficient suggests the presence of a faint disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 4901 has a poor B-band image, featureless V-R and V-K color maps, and positive outer b4 coefficient suggesting a faint disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 4901 : Our B band image of this galaxy has poor S/N. The color maps in V-R and V-K' show no features in the galaxy. The b_4 coefficient is positive towards the outer region, suggesting the presence of a faint disk.

gmw_00004416.name: UGC 491
gmw_00004416.overview: UGC 491, also known as NGC 252, is a lenticular galaxy classified as SA0+ or S0 [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:astro-ph/0411026] that is the brightest member of a small galaxy group [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It features an optical and ultraviolet ring [arXiv:1907.07261, arXiv:astro-ph/0411026] with gas excitation driven by young stars [arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 252 as a ringed S0 with a UV ring, young-star gas excitation, an extended fast-rotating disk, stellar corotation, and possible central triaxial potential.

quotes.quote: NGC 252. The galaxy was reported as a ringed one by iralist . Later we noted a UV-ring with a radius about 25^ and reported gas excitation by young stars in the ring ringmnras . Now in Fig. fig_fpi we present an disk extended up to 40^ (15 kpc) from the center and rotating with a very high speed, about 350 . The stars corotate ringmnras ; the lower visible stellar rotation velocity is perhaps due to asymmetric drift -- the stellar disk of NGC 252 may be rather dynamically hot. In the very center, at R<7^ , low-excitation emission line [ NII ] 6583 demonstrates lower rotation velocities than the (Fig. fig_ls ); it implies the possible presence of a triaxial potential though morphologically the galaxy is classified as unbarred, SA0 + .

sources.summary: UGC 491 is described as the brightest galaxy in a small group, classified differently by UGC and RC3, and showing a ring in optical and color maps.

quotes.quote: UGC 491 : UGC 491 is the brightest galaxy in a small group with NGC 0258 and NGC 0260. This galaxy is classified as S0 in the UGC catalogue and SA() in the RC3 catalogue. In the optical images it indeed shows a ring, which is also evident in the B-R and B-K' color maps. Ellipticity and position angle profiles show a change at 21.5 , which may be due to the presence of the ring.

gmw_00004417.name: UGC 7473
gmw_00004417.overview: UGC 7473 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy that forms a pair with NGC 4340 [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It features distinct bulge and disk components, a concentration of dust in its disk, and a rapidly rotating gas disk that is decoupled from the stellar component [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 7473 is described as an edge-on lenticular paired with NGC 4340, with bulge and disk components, a decoupled gas disk, disk dust, and strongly disky isophotes.

quotes.quote: UGC 7473 : This well studied edge-on lenticular galaxy forms a pair with NGC 4340. The surface brightness profile of this galaxy clearly indicates the presence of bulge and disk components. The Mg _2 line strength profiles along the major and minor axis of the galaxy differ dramatically and convincingly indicate the presence bulge and disk components (Fisher, Franx Illingworth 1996). Michard Marchal (1993) described UGC 7473 as having a disk fully embedded in a spheroidal halo. A disk of rapidly rotating gas is present within the inner 3 ; this is decoupled from the stellar component (Fisher 1997).

A concentration of dust in the disk has been proposed by Michard Poulain (2000). Our B-R and B-K' color and extinction maps also reveal a clear inclined disk. The ellipticity rises from 0.2 to 0.61 over the observed region and the b_4 coefficient becomes significantly positive beyond 6 , which reflects the presence of a strong disk.

gmw_00004418.name: UGC 7880
gmw_00004418.overview: UGC 7880 is an edge-on, disk-dominated lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1811.00018] that forms a pair with NGC 4635 [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. Photometric data and color maps suggest the presence of an inner disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026], and the galaxy is located within the Virgo cluster [arXiv:0803.3181].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4638 near M60 and the main Virgo distance, at least 800 kpc farther away than M59 or NGC 4660.

quotes.quote: Taken at face value, the SBF distances suggest that M59 and NGC 4660 lie roughly 2 Mpc to the foreground of M60, NGC 4638, and the bulk of the Virgo cluster. The statistical analysis confirms that the CG is, indeed, the result of a chance alignment of its galaxies, with NGC 4638 lying at least 800 kpc further away (with 99 confidence) than either M59 or NGC 4660. The first two galaxy distances are consistent with M59 and NGC 4660 constituting a tight pair. The dominant galaxy, M60, is at least 440 kpc more distant (95 confidence) than the M59+NGC 4660 pair, and over 1 Mpc (99 confidence) more distant if one uses the broken linear calibration of the SBF distances.
All three SBF estimators indicate that NGC 4638 is at least 800 kpc more distant than M59 and 1 Mpc more distant than NGC 4660 (both at the 99 confidence level).
According to Table minlos , it is highly unlikely that NGC 4638 is part of a dense group or pair containing M59 and NGC 4660 .

sources.summary: The paper finds VCC 1938/NGC 4638 closer than Ton01 reported and argues the NGVS result is reliable because it agrees with an independent ACSVCS distance.

quotes.quote: For VCC 1938/NGC 4638, we find (m M)=31.12 0.15 mag, whereas Ton01 reported (m M)=31.68 0.26 mag, which differs by 1.9 . As shown in Table tab_all , this galaxy also has a measured ACSVCS distance modulus of 31.19 0.07 mag, which agrees with our value. Note that the zero points of all three of these surveys are linked, but the individual distances are independent. Given our agreement with the much higher quality ACSVCS distance, we again conclude that our measurement is likely accurate.

sources.summary: UGC 7880 is described as paired with NGC 4635 and showing ellipticity and b4 behavior, plus color and extinction maps, consistent with an inner disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 7880 : This galaxy forms a pair with NGC 4635, with a separation of at 1.5 . The ellipticity changes from 0.1 to 0.5 upto 10 , and decreases beyond that. The b_4 coefficient is positive upto the point at which the ellipticity begins to decrease. This may be due to the presence of an inner disk, the presence of which is also indicated by the B-R and B-K' color and extinction maps.

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 1938 as an edge-on disk-dominated lenticular, compared to VCC 2095 as a missing link formed by dynamical heating.

quotes.quote: VCC 1938 (NGC 4638). This edge-on lenticular galaxy is a disk-dominated system 2007ApJ...661L..37B . 2012ApJS..198....2K viewed it as a ``missing link'' between S0s and spheroidals like VCC 2095. They suggested that the galaxy was created from a late-type galaxy by dynamical heating due to surrounding galaxies.

gmw_00004419.name: UGC 7933
gmw_00004419.overview: UGC 7933 is classified as an S0 lenticular galaxy in the UGC catalog and as an E1-2 elliptical galaxy in the RC3 catalog [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026]. It forms a non-interacting pair with NGC 4670, and its color and extinction maps show no internal structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 7933 is described as S0 in UGC but E1-2 in RC3, a non-interacting pair member with positive outer b4 and no color-map structure.

quotes.quote: UGC 7933 : This galaxy is classified as S0 in the UGC, but as E1-2 in the RC3 catalogue. It forms a non-interacting pair with NGC 4670 at 5.6 separation. The B band image has poor S/N. The b_4 coefficient is positive towards the outer region. The color and extinction maps do not indicate any structure.

gmw_00004420.name: UGC 80
gmw_00004420.overview: UGC 80 is a barred lenticular galaxy that features a small bar aligned perpendicular to its major axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026, arXiv:1406.7121]. Although it has a very faint interacting companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026, arXiv:1406.7121], the galaxy is considered largely isolated from external tidal effects [arXiv:1406.7121].

sources.summary: The appendix describes UGC 80 as a barred lenticular with a faint interacting companion, disky isophotes, profile changes near 5 arcsec, and a red central feature.

quotes.quote: UGC 80 : UGC 80 is a barred lenticular galaxy, having a very faint interacting companion. The faint bar is almost perpendicular to the galaxy major axis, and is clearly evident in the isophotal profile. The b_4 profile is indicative of a disky shape over the entire major-axis range explored. The ellipticity and position angle profiles show abrupt change at 5 , where there is also a kink in the brightness profile. The B-R color map of the galaxy shows a red feature near the center.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 16 as isolated despite a faint companion, and reports a small bar, hot stellar disc, and intermediate-age stellar component.

quotes.quote: NGC 16 This galaxy has a small bar aligned at a right angle to the line of nodes of the galactic disc and a faint late-type companion, with the magnitude difference toward the host of m_B=4^ m , barway_2005 at 500 kpc from it. The isolation index between the companion and the host galaxy is II=1.9 , but since the mass difference is about two orders of magnitude, we consider NGC 16 as a quite isolated from the external tidal effects. The galaxy has an extended rigid-body rise of the rotation curve of up to 10'' from the center, where the rotation velocity reaches a plateau, and its stellar disc is rather dynamically hot, >100 km s ^ 1 . The stellar component in NGC 16 has an intermediate age, the bulge and the disc being of similar ages in the mean, although individual age estimates in the bins demonstrate large scatter.

gmw_00004421.name: UGC 859
gmw_00004421.overview: UGC 859 is an early-type spiral or S0 galaxy featuring an internal, inhomogeneous ring around its central bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026, arXiv:astro-ph/0311030]. This ring exhibits strong star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030], while a low-surface-brightness disk with spiral structure and disky outer isophotes lies further out [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026].

sources.summary: UGC 859 is discussed as an S0 galaxy with an internal inhomogeneous ring, outer low-surface-brightness disk with spiral structure, IRAS detection, and disky outer isophotes.

quotes.quote: UGC 859 : UGC 859 shows an internal ring, but it is classified as S0 in the UGC catalogue. The B-R and B-K' color maps of the galaxy show the ring as an inhomogeneous structure. A low surface brightness disk with some luminous spiral structure exists outside the ring. UGC 859 has been detected by IRAS and has flux densities of 1160 33 at 60 and 870 319 at 100 (Knapp 1989). The b_4 coefficient is disky towards the outer region.

sources.summary: The survey describes UGC 859 as an early-type spiral with strong star formation in a regular ring around the bulge and unusually strong emission lines.

quotes.quote: UGC 859 shows strong star formation in a very regular ring, around the edge of the bulge (also described by Pogge Eskridge ). This is reflected in the EW curve, which exhibits a deep central dip due to the strong continuum from the central bulge, a peak at the radius of the star-forming ring, and a steady drop at larger radii due to the presence of only old stars outside the ring, to an overall value of about 1 nm. This is typical of moderately star-forming bright spiral galaxies. UGC 12043 shows more centrally-concentrated star formation, with just a central peak in the EW curve and a monotonic decline with radius, at least at the resolution of the present data. The overall EW of 2 nm is high for such an early type galaxy, and both UGC 859 and UGC 12043 are much stronger emission-line sources than were found in the early-type galaxy samples studied by Caldwell et al.

gmw_00004422.name: 3C300
gmw_00004422.overview: 3C300 is a Fanaroff-Riley II high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1404.7499, arXiv:2010.11195] hosted by a compact, elongated elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0712.0595]. The host galaxy has a faint companion and a weak tidal distortion [arXiv:0712.0595], alongside an S-shaped emission-line region extending 60 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2010.11195].

sources.summary: The paper uses JVLA spectral-ageing analysis of 3C300, finding cross-lobe age variations, low-age regions along the jet, and high fitted injection indices.

quotes.quote: Observations were made of 2 bright, nearby FR-II sources selected from the 3CRR sample laing83 ; 3C436 and 3C300. Using the BRATS software, keys model parameters were determined and spectral ageing models fitted on a pixel by pixel basis. The importance of considering spectra on these small scales is immediately evident from the maps of spectral age shown in Figure specagemaps . We see clear cross-lobe variations, particularly in the case of 3C300, where the low-age regions stretch along the length of the jet are likely due to previously unknown jet interaction.
This so-called injection index is commonly assumed to take values between 0.5 and 0.7 ; however, from our analysis using direct fitting of these models, both sources take values in excess of 0.8 .

sources.summary: 3CR 300 is the noisy-profile example: its radial R-K profile suggests a possible color gradient, and after modeling that gradient the authors find no significant nuclear infrared excess.

quotes.quote: 3CR 300 In Fig. color3C300_1 (left-hand panel), the radial color profile for 3CR 300 has been plotted (showing also the effects of the seeing measurement uncertainty, 2 for this source). The profile is quite noisy, and while it is consistent with being constant, a color gradient seems present. For this reason, we modeled the color profile as in colprofApdx_403 (the color profile and the fitted model are shown in Fig. color3C300_2 , left-hand panel). In Table tab5 , the estimated luminosities for 3CR 300 are reported. After modeling the color gradient, no significant IR excess is found (see Fig. color3C300_2 , right-hand panel) and therefore L_ K^ L_ K^ ,scaled .

sources.summary: 3C 300 is an FR II/HEG with an S-shaped, 60 kpc emission-line region, ordered major-axis rotation, extended diffuse emission, and stronger off-nuclear ratios.

quotes.quote: 3C 300: FR II/HEG, z=0.270, 1 = 4.17 kpc (see Fig. 3c300 ). The ELR has an S-shaped structure extending 60 kpc in the EW direction and showing well-ordered rotation along its major axis. More diffuse emission is found on the N and W side out to radii of more than 60 kpc. The / \ ratio is higher in the off-nuclear regions than in the center.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 300 as a double-sided FR II HEG in a compact elongated elliptical host with a faint companion, weak tidal distortion, and candidate jet.

quotes.quote: 3C 300; z=0.270 A double-sided FR II HEG, hosted by a compact, elongated 3 kpc elliptical host galaxy with a faint companion 8 East, and a very faint tidal distortion. The radio axis runs roughly SE-NW, and there is a faint thread of emission along this axis on the NW side, which is a candidate jet. However, it is almost lost in the noise of the NICMOS chip edge.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 3C300 as an FR II radio-galaxy source where the northern lobe jet classification is uncertain and affects the integrated jet flux.

quotes.quote: 3C300 : It is not clear how much of this object's north lobe should be classed as a jet. We omitted the last few arcseconds where there is no surrounding lobe emission. The flux density might be increased by up to 15 mJy if this region were included.

gmw_00004423.name: SGRS J0047 8307
gmw_00004423.overview: SGRS J0047 8307 is a giant radio galaxy hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. It is a triple radio source characterized by asymmetric lobes, emission gaps between the lobes and the core, and nearby companion galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0047 8307 as a triple giant radio source with emission gaps, asymmetric lobes, a core-host galaxy, nearby companions, and a low signal-to-noise optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0047 8307 (Fig 1) In the SUMSS 843 MHz image the source is seen as a triple; there are noticeable gaps in the radio emission between the lobes and the core. The lobe with the smaller separation from the core is also the brighter of the two lobes. The ATCA image shows two compact components in the NW lobe; however, in the southern lobe there is only a weak component close to the peak of the extended emission. The extension seen in the SE lobe at 843 MHz towards NW might be confused with emission associated with a relatively bright, b_ j = 17.8 , elliptical galaxy that is host to a compact S_ 1.4 = 6.4 mJy source.

gmw_00004424.name: SGRS J0143 5431
gmw_00004424.overview: SGRS J0143 5431 is a radio galaxy with a relaxed double-lobed morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. Its compact inner triple structure suggests that the central engine beams may have restarted [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0143 5431 as a relaxed double-lobed radio source whose compact inner triple structure may indicate restarted central-engine beams.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0143 5431 (Fig 2) The SUMSS 843-MHz image shows a relaxed morphology for the two lobes and the higher-resolution 1.4-GHz ATCA image shows no compact features associated with the lobes. The ATCA image (Fig. 2b) shows a radio core and, interestingly, this image also shows two compact features on either side of the core and along the source axis: this triple structure at the core together with the relaxed outer lobes suggest the possibility that SGRS J0143-5431 might be a case where an inner double has been created by a restarting of the beams from the central engine. The host galaxy has a few, relatively faint neighbours within 15 radius and a bright galaxy 30 to the SE. The close faint neighbour 5 to the West is a blue object and the galaxy 12 to the North is a spiral.

gmw_00004425.name: SGRS J0237 6429
gmw_00004425.overview: SGRS J0237 6429 is a double radio source characterized by a weak core and emission gaps between the core and its two lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. The galaxy features compact structures at the ends of both lobes, and its host galaxy has no close neighbors [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0237 6429 as a double radio source with emission gaps, a weak core, compact lobe-end features, and an uncertain noisy optical redshift.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0237 6429 (Fig 3) The SUMSS image shows two lobes with surface brightness decreasing towards the centre and prominent gaps in the emission between the core and the two lobes. The ATCA image shows a weak core and compact features at the ends of both lobes. The closer lobe is also the brighter of the two lobes. The host galaxy does not have any close neighbours.

gmw_00004426.name: SGRS J0326 7730
gmw_00004426.overview: SGRS J0326 7730 is a giant radio galaxy characterized by extended asymmetric lobes and a weak core coincident with a faint host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. Its southeastern lobe contains a bright elongated structure, while its northwestern lobe features a compact component at its peak [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0326 7730 as an unusually structured giant radio source with extended asymmetric lobes, compact lobe features, and a weak core coincident with a faint host galaxy.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0326 7730 (Fig 4) This source has an unusual structure. The SUMSS image shows two extended components of which the SE is significantly stronger. The higher resolution 1.4-GHz ATCA image reveals a bright elongated structure in this SE lobe. Although edge-brightened, there is no compact unresolved feature towards the outer end of this lobe. The ATCA image also shows a compact component at the peak of the NW lobe.

gmw_00004427.name: SGRS J0331 7710
gmw_00004427.overview: SGRS J0331 7710 is a giant radio galaxy characterized by highly elongated, collinear lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. These edge-brightened lobes exhibit extreme asymmetry in both their brightness and separation, with a weak core detected near the northern lobe [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0331 7710 as the largest linear-size source in the sample, with asymmetric elongated lobes and a weak core near the northern lobe.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0331 7710 (Fig 5) This radio source has the largest linear size in the sample and is extremely asymmetric in lobe separation as well as lobe brightness and flux density. Both lobes are highly elongated with high axial ratios and both elongated lobes are collinear. The two lobes are edge brightened; however, the contrast in surface brightness along the lobe axes is relatively weak in this source. In the ATCA image the two lobes are completely resolved and a weak core is detected at the inner end of the northern lobe (Fig. 5b); there is a significant emission gap of almost 600 kpc between the core and the southern lobe.

gmw_00004428.name: SGRS J0400 8456
gmw_00004428.overview: SGRS J0400 8456 is a radio galaxy with a central radio core coincident with a galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. This central core hosts twin jets, while the northern and southern lobes exhibit compact features and bent extended emissions [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0400 8456 as a multi-peaked radio galaxy with a central galaxy core, twin jets, compact lobe features, and bent extended emission.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0400 8456 (Fig 6) The SUMSS image shows several emission peaks that are not all collinear. The ATCA image shows a central source coincident with a galaxy; this radio core has twin jets on either side (Fig. 6b). The SUMSS peaks to the north and south of the central component have compact features in the ATCA images. In the SUMSS image there is an extension from the northern peak towards west that is oriented nearly orthogonal to the inner radio axis. There is a similar bend in the southern lobe towards SE.

gmw_00004429.name: SGRS J0515 8100
gmw_00004429.overview: SGRS J0515 8100 is a giant fat double radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. It features extremely low surface-brightness lobes and a faint radio core coincident with a galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0515 8100 as a giant fat double radio source with very low surface-brightness lobes and a faint radio core at a galaxy.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0515 8100 (Fig 7) This double radio source has extremely low surface brightness lobes that have an extremely low axial ratio; the higher resolution ATCA observations have detected a faint radio core coincident with a galaxy that is located at the inner end of the southern lobe (Fig. 7b). There is an emission gap between the core and the northern lobe. This source appears to be a giant fat double radio source and is being studied in detail by R.

gmw_00004430.name: SGRS J0631 5405
gmw_00004430.overview: SGRS J0631 5405 is a giant radio source classified as a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. It features a pair of radio lobes, a strong radio core, and compact structure at the end of its western lobe [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0631 5405 as a quasar giant radio source with paired lobes, a strong radio core, western compact structure, and a ROSAT X-ray counterpart.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0631 5405 (Fig 8) This giant radio source is a quasar. The SUMSS image shows a pair of lobes; the higher resolution ATCA image shows a strong radio core coincident with a star-like object. The ATCA image shows compact structure at the end of the western lobe whereas the eastern lobe appears resolved. The western lobe has a smaller extent from the core and also has a higher surface brightness.

gmw_00004431.name: SGRS J0746 5702
gmw_00004431.overview: SGRS J0746 5702 is a radio source characterized by a bright, extended central core flanked by much fainter resolved lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. High-resolution observations resolve this central source into a strong core and a jet-like feature directed toward the southwest [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0746 5702 as a radio source with a bright central core, a southwest jet-like feature, fainter resolved lobes, and possible restarted-jet morphology.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0746 5702 (Fig 9) In the SUMSS this radio source appears as a bright extended central source flanked by much fainter lobes. In the higher resolution ATCA image the central source is resolved into a strong core and a jet-like feature to the SW. Both lobes are resolved in the ATCA observations and no compact structure is observed. The jet appears directed towards the fainter of the two lobes.

gmw_00004432.name: SGRS J0810 6800
gmw_00004432.overview: SGRS J0810 6800 is a giant radio source hosted by a quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. The system features a faint radio core and asymmetric lobes that appear directed to the north of the main axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0810 6800 as a quasar giant radio source with asymmetric lobes, a faint core, broad Balmer emission, and weak radio-core emission.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0810 6800 (Fig 10) The SUMSS image shows two lobes; the NW lobe is edge-brightened and has the higher surface brightness. A compact source is detected at the peak of the NW lobe in the ATCA 1.4-GHz image; the SE lobe is completely resolved. The ATCA image at 4.8 GHz (Fig. 10b) revealed a faint radio core coincident with a star-like object that is located on the line joining the outer ends of the two lobes. The lobes do not, however, extend along this line and appear directed to the north of the axis in both cases. The host object has very broad H and H emission lines and is a quasar.

gmw_00004433.name: SGRS J0843 7007
gmw_00004433.overview: SGRS J0843 7007 is a giant radio source featuring a bright central radio core [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. This central component is straddled by two extended, low-surface-brightness lobes, and its host galaxy has a close neighbor located to the north [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J0843 7007 as a giant radio source with a bright central component, low-surface-brightness lobes, a neighboring host galaxy, and relic FR-II interpretation.

quotes.quote: SGRS J0843 7007 (Fig 11) The radio structure of this giant radio source resembles SGRS J0746 5702 in that it has a central, bright component straddled by two extended lobes with low surface brightness. In the higher resolution ATCA image only a central radio core is detected; the two low-surface-brightness lobes are completely resolved. The host galaxy has a close neighbour 10 to the north. This source might be a relic of a giant FR- ii radio source in which the beams from the central engine no longer energize the lobes.

gmw_00004434.name: SGRS J1259 7737
gmw_00004434.overview: SGRS J1259 7737 is an edge-brightened giant radio source with lobes that extend toward each other without any emission gap [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. High-resolution imaging reveals compact hotspots at the ends of both lobes, a weak central core, and a faint host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J1259 7737 as an edge-brightened giant radio source with prominent lobes, compact hotspots, a weak central core, and a faint host galaxy.

quotes.quote: SGRS J1259 7737 (Fig 12) This giant radio source has prominent lobes and is clearly seen to be an edge-brightened double radio source. The two lobes extend all the way towards each other without any emission gap. The 1.4-GHz ATCA high-resolution image detected compact hotspots at the ends of both lobes and later 4.8-GHz ATCA imaging detected an additional weak compact core close to the centre of the radio source (Fig. 12b). The core is coincident with a faint object at the plate limit. The sky region in which the host galaxy lies appears relatively devoid of optical objects suggesting dust obscuration along this line of sight.

gmw_00004435.name: SGRS J1336 8019
gmw_00004435.overview: SGRS J1336 8019 is an edge-brightened double radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. Its compact features trace a weak, two-sided jet, and the central core component coincides with the southern member of a close pair of objects [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J1336 8019 as an edge-brightened double radio source whose compact features trace a weak two-sided jet and whose core coincides with a close object pair.

quotes.quote: SGRS J1336 8019 (Fig 13) In the SUMSS this double radio source appears to have an edge-brightened structure with a high axial ratio. The higher resolution ATCA image shows a chain of weak compact features that trace the chain of peaks observed in SUMSS indicating the presence of a weak two-sided jet along the central line of this radio source. The strongest among the emission peaks is the central core component and this coincides with the southern member of a close pair of objects (Fig. 13b) which were partly blended on the spectrograph slit. The presence of H absorption in the host galaxy spectrum indicates that the northern object is a star. The ATCA image also detects a feature at the leading edge of the southern lobe; however, there is no recognizable equivalent feature at the leading edge of the northern lobe.

gmw_00004436.name: SGRS J1728 7237
gmw_00004436.overview: SGRS J1728 7237 is a triple radio source with a radio core that is coincident with a faint, narrow emission-line galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. The source is asymmetric, featuring large emission gaps between its lobes and the core, with the northern lobe positioned closer to the core than the southern lobe [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J1728 7237 as a triple radio source with large lobe-core gaps, asymmetric lobe separation, and a radio core coincident with a faint emission-line galaxy.

quotes.quote: SGRS J1728 7237 (Fig 14) The source is a triple in both the SUMSS and ATCA images. There are large emission gaps between the lobes and the core. This is an asymmetric source with the northern lobe closer to the core as compared to the southern lobe. The ATCA image detects a radio core that is displaced 7 from a bright star and coincident with a faint galaxy, which has a prominent narrow emission-line spectrum.

gmw_00004437.name: SGRS J1919 7959
gmw_00004437.overview: SGRS J1919 7959 is a giant radio source featuring a pair of lobes with detected internal structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. A weak radio core is located between these lobes, coinciding with a faint galaxy situated behind dust obscuration [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J1919 7959 as a previously observed giant radio source with prominent lobes, lobe structure, and a weak core coincident with a faint galaxy behind dust obscuration.

quotes.quote: SGRS J1919 7959 (Fig 16) This giant radio source was observed earlier by Su96 . The SUMSS image shows a pair of prominent lobes; the ATCA image detects structure in both these lobes. Additionally, the ATCA image from Su96 detects a weak core between the two lobes and coincident with a faint b_ j =22.7 galaxy. Inspection of the digitized plates reveals significant dust obscuration.

gmw_00004438.name: SGRS J1946 8222
gmw_00004438.overview: SGRS J1946 8222 is a giant radio source associated with a faint host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. It features a weak compact core straddled by symmetric, partial inner jets and two elongated lobes that lack compact hotspots at their ends [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J1946 8222 as a giant radio source with a weak compact core, symmetric partial inner jets, elongated lobes, and a faint host galaxy.

quotes.quote: SGRS J1946 8222 (Fig 17) The high resolution ATCA image of this source shows a compact, weak core component coincident with a faint host galaxy; the core is straddled by a pair of partial jet-like features that are symmetrically located on either side of the core. The SUMSS image shows extended emission around this central structure as two lobes that have a high axial ratio. The two ends of the source are asymmetrically located with respect to the core. There are no prominent compact hotspots seen at the ends of the source in the 1.4-GHz ATCA image. The inner partial jets might be a case of restarting beams in this giant radio source.

gmw_00004439.name: SGRS J2159 7219
gmw_00004439.overview: SGRS J2159 7219 is a radio galaxy hosted by a bright galaxy that features a halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055]. The host galaxy is likely situated in a cluster environment with fainter neighboring companions [arXiv:astro-ph/0507055].

sources.summary: The paper describes SGRS J2159 7219 as an edge-brightened source with a central triple structure, compact core at a bright galaxy, halo, companions, and likely cluster environment.

quotes.quote: SGRS J2159 7219 (Fig 18) In the SUMSS this source has an edge-brightened outer structure and a central triple structure with emission gaps between all components. There is a larger emission gap between the northern lobe and the core. In the ATCA 1.4-GHz image a compact radio core is detected at the location of the central component and this coincides with a bright galaxy (Fig. 18b). At this higher frequency and resolution the only other structure seen is the stronger southern component of the central triple. The host galaxy has a prominent halo; fainter companions are seen in its neighbourhood and the host is likely to be in a cluster environment.

gmw_00004440.name: NGC 1189
gmw_00004440.overview: NGC 1189, also known as HCG 22c, is a face-on barred spiral galaxy of classification SBcd [arXiv:1009.3010] that belongs to the Hickson Compact Group 22 [arXiv:1009.3010]. It is an active star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1009.3010] characterized by a patchy appearance with several H II regions [arXiv:0908.2798].

sources.summary: The paper presents HCG 22c as a patchy, HII-region spiral with regular velocity field, kinematic-optical PA misalignment, and a rising solid-body-like rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The monochromatic map of HCG 22c (in Figure maps_hcg22 ) shows several HII regions which belongs to the arms of this galaxy, showing a patchy appearance. Moreover, there is a faint H emission in the intra-arm region. The velocity field seems to be regular, with a misalignment of about 30 ^ between the kinematic and optical PA. The velocity dispersion map does not show any particular features. The rotation curve displayed in Fig. hcg22c (f) shows almost a linear shape, as a solid-body rotator, particularly in the northwest part of the galaxy, steadily rising from 0 to 125 km s ^ 1 out to 30 (5 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1189 as HCG 22c, an SBcd member of HCG 22, and notes blue objects expected from its face-on active star-forming character.

quotes.quote: HCG 22, as originally catalogued by hic82 , consists of three giant galaxies, E2 NGC 1199 (HCG 22a) the Sa NGC 1190 (HCG 22b) and the SBcd NGC 1189 (HCG 22c), with magnitudes in the range from M_B=-20.4 to 18.1 hic89 . An additional smaller member galaxy was latter identified by dec97 , leading to a group mean radial velocity of 2629 33 km s^ 1 and velocity dispersion of 40 28 km s^ 1 rib98 .

It is interesting to note the apparent concentration of blue objects on the NGC 1189 and ``Mid Group'' colour-colour diagrams (bottom row the left and right most panels). NGC 1189, covered by the ``Mid Group'' WFPC2 field, is a face-on, active star-forming SBcd which is the group brightest galaxy in UV wavelengths tza10 .

gmw_00004441.name: HULQ J0002+0239
gmw_00004441.overview: HULQ J0002+0239 is a quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.455 [arXiv:2207.10726]. Although initially identified as a gravitational lens candidate surrounded by four seemingly lensed objects, spectroscopic observations reveal that these surrounding objects are actually separate, unlensed galaxies [arXiv:2207.10726].

sources.summary: The paper studies HULQ J0002+0239 as a QSO-host lens candidate at z=1.455, but Gemini spectra show surrounding objects are separate galaxies, so it is not a QSO lens.

quotes.quote: We present the results of the spectroscopic observation of the first QSO lens candidate from the HULQ project, HULQ J0002+0239, which consists of a QSO host galaxy at z_ d = 1.455 and four seemingly lensed objects in a cross-like configuration. Deep optical spectra of two of the possibly lensed objects with z 24.5 mag were obtained with the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph on the Gemini North Telescope. Their spectra reveal that the objects are newly discovered galaxies at z=0.29 and z=1.11 , and we conclude that HULQ J0002+0239 is not a QSO lens. Our QSO lens search results are so far in agreement with the predicted number of QSO lenses, and we discuss how the future investigation of additional QSO lens candidates could tell us more about the evolution of the black hole mass and host galaxy scaling relations. gravitational lensing: strong -- quasars: supermassive black holes -- galaxies: active -- galaxies: evolution HULQ II.

gmw_00004442.name: companion galaxy
gmw_00004442.overview: The companion galaxy is a marginally resolved source located near the Cloverleaf [arXiv:2209.00012]. It hosts a molecular gas reservoir that is approximately four to five times less massive than that of the Cloverleaf [arXiv:2209.00012].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Cloverleaf companion galaxy as a marginally resolved CO source with a molecular gas reservoir about four to five times smaller than the Cloverleaf.

quotes.quote: Assuming a conversion factor of 0.8 (K \ km \ s^ 1 \ pc^2)^ 1 , typically adopted for high-redshift starbursts Greve:2014 , this companion galaxy has a molecular gas reservoir of 0.9( 0.1) 10^9 . The galaxy is marginally resolved in the direction of the Cloverleaf. In contrast, the Cloverleaf has an integrated line luminosity of 15.8( 0.1) . Assuming a magnification of 11 , as found for both the CO (9--8) and sub-mm dust emission by , we infer a molecular gas reservoir of 4.0( 0.1) 10^ 10 , suggesting that the companion is 4--5 times less massive than the Cloverleaf from the perspective of its molecular gas content (Table tab:line_properties ).

gmw_00004443.name: IV Zw 155
gmw_00004443.overview: IV Zw 155 is an Sa-type spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.063 [arXiv:1810.05181]. It is a star-forming, solar-metallicity system that exhibits two tidal tails and shows signs of interaction with at least three companion galaxies [arXiv:1810.05181].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IV Zw 155 as the tidally interacting spiral host of iPTF 14gqr, measures solar-like metallicity and star formation, and places the transient in its outskirts.

quotes.quote: iPTF 14gqr was discovered at a large projected offset of 24 '' from the nearest apparent host galaxy, a two-tailed spiral galaxy showing signs of tidal interaction with at least three companion galaxies. The association of the transient with the host galaxy was confirmed from the early spectroscopy of the transient, which exhibited flash ionized He ii lines at the same redshift z = 0.063 as that of the galaxy. The offset corresponds to a projected distance of 29 kpc at the redshift of the galaxy.

The host galaxy IV Zw 155 is a spiral of morphology Sa, and has been previously cataloged in the SDSS and the Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) as the source SDSS J233329.84+333848.2 and 2MASX J23332981+3338484 respectively. The galaxy was also detected as a UV source in the Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX) source catalogs as the source GALEXASC J233330.19+333840.3.

The GALEX UV photometry measurements allow us to constrain the best-fitting star formation rate to 2.3 - 7.6 M _ yr ^ 1 . Overall, the host of iPTF 14gqr is found to be a solar metallicity, star forming, spiral galaxy.

gmw_00004444.name: ZF-COS-20115
gmw_00004444.overview: ZF-COS-20115 is a compact, high-redshift quiescent galaxy [arXiv:2010.09738, arXiv:2309.12730] located at a redshift of approximately 3.7 [arXiv:1702.01751, arXiv:2309.12730]. It has very low levels of ongoing star formation [arXiv:2010.09738] and forms a pair with a moderately star-forming companion galaxy named Hyde [arXiv:2309.12730].

sources.summary: The paper measures ZF-COS-20115 as a massive quiescent high-redshift galaxy observed deeply with MOSFIRE, with revised stellar mass, low dust, weak ongoing star formation, and ALMA SFR limits.

quotes.quote: The total exposure times for the four galaxies in our sample are as follows: ZF-COS-20115 had 4.2h and 14.4h in H and K band respectively, and 3D-EGS-18996, 3D-EGS-40032 and 3D-EGS-31322 all had 0.8h and 4.8h in H and K band respectively.
The stellar mass for ZF-COS-20115, originally determined in Glazebrook2017 , was revised after deblending of a nearby ALMA-detected galaxy, reducing the stellar mass by 0.17 dex.

The modeling indicates that the stellar masses are high ( 1-2 10^ 11 M _ ), dust attenuation is low, and the galaxies formed in a brief burst about 0.5-1.0 Gyr earlier, with very low levels of on-going star formation (specific star formation rates sSFR < 0.03 Gyr ^ 1 ) based on SED fitting. This picture is confirmed by analysing the spectra, which show deep Balmer series and CaII H K absorption lines for all galaxies. For one galaxy, 3D-EGS-18996, we find [ OIII ] in emission, and for another, 3D-EGS-40032, [ OII ] emission is weakly detected. Nevertheless, all galaxies have sSFR 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 based on [ OII ] , H absorption alone, or a factor 15 below the galaxy main sequence at this redshift see Schreiber2018 . In addition, ZF-COS-20115 has stringent limits on dust obscured SFR based on a non-detection in deep ALMA imaging Schreiber2017 .

sources.summary: The paper finds ZF-COS-20115 to be a compact high-redshift quiescent galaxy with a very low molecular-gas upper limit, implying little post-quenching accretion.

quotes.quote: ZF-COS-20115 is located at z_ spec =3.715^ +0.002 _ 0.002 and its stellar mass, size, Sersic index, and stellar velocity dispersion are M _ =1.15^ +0.16 _ 0.09 10^ 11 M _ , _ e =0.66 0.08 kpc (0.09"), n _ Sersic =4.1 1.0, and _ =283 52 km s ^ 1 , respectively Schreiber18a,Esdaile . ZF-COS-20115 has been extensively studied as it is part of a pair with a moderately star-forming companion galaxy (Hyde), undetected in H band, located at a distance 3.1 kpc inferred from the 0.43" offset from the HST and ALMA images and the z=0.008 ; hereafter S18a and S18b respectively Schreiber18b, Schreiber18a .

We obtain M _ mol < 3.6 10^ 9 M_ corresponding to f _ mol <3.2 assuming that the entire [ CII ] luminosity arises from cold molecular gas (see Fig. fig:codust ). The upper limit increases to f _ mol <4.5 when adopting the largest value of _ [ CII ] ,mol allowed by the uncertainties in Z18. We note that this is one of the most stringent individual constraints on the molecular gas mass of QGs at z > 2 to date.

An f _ mol < 3 after 0.5 Gyr from the last substantial drop in star formation implies that the galaxy did not experience substantial gas accretion after quenching and that bulge stabilisation Martig09 is not the main maintenance mechanism for its quiescence.

sources.summary: ID 13172 is the paper's spectroscopically confirmed massive quiescent galaxy at z=3.717, with stellar mass 1.7e11 solar masses, no current star formation, and rapid early assembly in a short starburst.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the spectroscopic confirmation of one of these galaxies at redshift z=3.717 with a stellar mass of 1.7 10 ^ 11 M _ whose absorption line spectrum shows no current star-formation and which has a derived age of nearly half the age of the Universe at this redshift. The observations demonstrates that the galaxy must have quickly formed the majority of its stars within the first billion years of cosmic history in an extreme and short starburst. This ancestral event is similar to those starting to be found by sub-mm wavelength surveys pointing to a possible connection between these two populations. Early formation of such massive systems is likely to require significant revisions to our picture of early galaxy assembly.
We note that this source was previously attributed the ID 13172 in earlier papers ^ 15,16 , which were based on a preliminary version of the photometric catalog.

sources.summary: The paper tests whether galaxy-formation models can accommodate the rare z=3.7 quiescent galaxy ZF-COSMOS-20115 and finds simulated analogues with comparable masses and star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: We take advantage of the statistical power of the large-volume dark-matter-only Millennium simulation, combined with a sophisticated semi-analytic galaxy formation model, to explore whether the recently reported z=3.7 quiescent galaxy ZF-COSMOS-20115 (ZF; Glazebrook et al. 2017) can be accommodated in current galaxy formation models. In our model, a population of quiescent galaxies (QGs) with stellar masses and star formation rates comparable to those of ZF naturally emerges at redshifts z<4 . There are two and five ZF analogues at the redshift 3.86 and 3.58 in the Millennium simulation volume, respectively.

The fractions of the ZF analogues in our massive galaxy sample are as low as about 2 3 at the two redshifts, implying that the observed ZF is indeed quite rare. The number of the ZF analogues increases quite rapidly towards lower redshifts: at z=3.06 , there are 410 massive galaxies in the whole simulation volume, while 37 (approximately 9 ) of them have SFR similar to that of ZF, suggesting a rapid formation of massive QGs during a very short timescale at the redshifts z>3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Hyde as ZF-COS-20115's moderately star-forming companion, detected in [CII] with a broad rotating-disk profile offset from the quiescent galaxy.

quotes.quote: ZF-COS-20115 has been extensively studied as it is part of a pair with a moderately star-forming companion galaxy (Hyde), undetected in H band, located at a distance 3.1 kpc inferred from the 0.43" offset from the HST and ALMA images and the z=0.008 ; hereafter S18a and S18b respectively Schreiber18b, Schreiber18a . Hyde is detected in [ CII ] emission showing a very broad, double-peaked profile associated to a rotating disk (v _ rot =781^ +218 _ 366 km s ^ 1 , S18a) and offset from the QG by -550 km s ^ 1 in frequency space. Its [ CII ] spectrum is shown in blue in Fig. fig:spectra .
ZF-COS-20115 is associated to a moderately star-forming galaxy with M _ 0.34-1.28 10^ 11 M_ and SFR _ tot 50^ +24 _ 18 M _ /yr, about a factor of 4 below the MS at its redshift Schreiber22 .

gmw_00004445.name: 3D-EGS-18996
gmw_00004445.overview: 3D-EGS-18996 is a massive quiescent galaxy at a redshift greater than three [arXiv:2010.09738]. It exhibits [OIII] emission, low dust attenuation, and an unusually low dynamical-to-stellar mass ratio [arXiv:2010.09738].

sources.summary: The paper includes 3D-EGS-18996 in a massive quiescent z>3 MOSFIRE sample, detecting OIII emission and noting its unusually low dynamical-to-stellar mass ratio may suggest a top-heavy IMF.

quotes.quote: The total exposure times for the four galaxies in our sample are as follows: ZF-COS-20115 had 4.2h and 14.4h in H and K band respectively, and 3D-EGS-18996, 3D-EGS-40032 and 3D-EGS-31322 all had 0.8h and 4.8h in H and K band respectively.

The modeling indicates that the stellar masses are high ( 1-2 10^ 11 M _ ), dust attenuation is low, and the galaxies formed in a brief burst about 0.5-1.0 Gyr earlier, with very low levels of on-going star formation (specific star formation rates sSFR < 0.03 Gyr ^ 1 ) based on SED fitting. This picture is confirmed by analysing the spectra, which show deep Balmer series and CaII H K absorption lines for all galaxies. For one galaxy, 3D-EGS-18996, we find [ OIII ] in emission, and for another, 3D-EGS-40032, [ OII ] emission is weakly detected. Nevertheless, all galaxies have sSFR 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 based on [ OII ] , H absorption alone, or a factor 15 below the galaxy main sequence at this redshift see Schreiber2018 .

The most extreme galaxies with very low M dyn /M* ratios, such as 3D-EGS-18996, could even point towards a top-heavy IMF, which may be present in galaxies with intense star-bursts Weidner2013 from which MQGs are likely formed.

gmw_00004446.name: 3D-EGS-40032
gmw_00004446.overview: 3D-EGS-40032 is a compact, massive quiescent galaxy at a redshift greater than 3 [arXiv:2010.09738]. It exhibits low dust attenuation, very low levels of ongoing star formation, weakly detected [OII] emission, and a nearby neighbor [arXiv:2010.09738].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3D-EGS-40032 as a compact massive quiescent z>3 galaxy with MOSFIRE spectroscopy, weak OII emission, low star formation, and a nearby neighbor affecting size comparison.

quotes.quote: The total exposure times for the four galaxies in our sample are as follows: ZF-COS-20115 had 4.2h and 14.4h in H and K band respectively, and 3D-EGS-18996, 3D-EGS-40032 and 3D-EGS-31322 all had 0.8h and 4.8h in H and K band respectively.

The modeling indicates that the stellar masses are high ( 1-2 10^ 11 M _ ), dust attenuation is low, and the galaxies formed in a brief burst about 0.5-1.0 Gyr earlier, with very low levels of on-going star formation (specific star formation rates sSFR < 0.03 Gyr ^ 1 ) based on SED fitting. This picture is confirmed by analysing the spectra, which show deep Balmer series and CaII H K absorption lines for all galaxies. For one galaxy, 3D-EGS-18996, we find [ OIII ] in emission, and for another, 3D-EGS-40032, [ OII ] emission is weakly detected. Nevertheless, all galaxies have sSFR 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 based on [ OII ] , H absorption alone, or a factor 15 below the galaxy main sequence at this redshift see Schreiber2018 .

We find excellent agreement with the results of vanderWel2014 , all within uncertainties, except for 3D-EGS-40032 which is has a nearby neighbor and is smaller by 25 . All sources are compact ( _e=0.08-0.3 ) with very high integrated SNR 50-150 (within _e<0.3 ), resulting in small formal uncertainties.

gmw_00004447.name: NGC 2217
gmw_00004447.overview: NGC 2217 is a barred lenticular or spiral galaxy seen almost face-on, featuring a bar, an inner ring, and an outer HI ring [arXiv:1708.02683, arXiv:2304.00515]. The galaxy hosts a LINER-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683, arXiv:1204.6212] and exhibits warped gas kinematics alongside evidence of nuclear outflows [arXiv:2304.13899, arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The paper reports broad nuclear lines in NGC 2217, fits its oxygen doublet with multiple Gaussian components, and associates its diagnostic ratios and broad components with shocks/outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459, a broad is also seen in their spectra. In these cases, we fitted the \ line with the same set of Gaussian functions used to describe the line profile, i.e. we assumed that all Gaussian components of the line have the same kinematics as the Gaussian components found for the line. The fitted profiles for the \ lines of NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459 are shown in Fig. B2 of Appendix B, together with the fitted profile of NGC 4958 taken from 2019MNRAS.486.1138R .

In addition, we fitted the profiles of the 6300, 6363 lines of NGC 2217, NGC 5044 and IC 1459, since these galaxies also present visible broad components in this doublet. For NGC 2217 and NGC 5044, we fitted a sum of two Gaussian functions per line, while for IC 1459 a sum of three Gaussian functions per line was necessary for the profile of the doublet.

For both NGC 2217 and IC 1459, the broad components are narrower and have lower radial velocity values than the broad features detected in . One possible interpretation for these components in the 6300, 6363 doublet is related to nuclear outflows, since shocks may enhance the emission 2006agna.book.....O,2008ApJS..178...20A . Indeed, all three objects present pieces of evidence of outflowing gas, as stated by IFS data 2015MNRAS.451.3728R, 2017MNRAS.470.1703D, 2017ApJS..232...11T .

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 2217 as a face-on barred spiral with LINER-like emission, broad Na D absorption, bubble-like extended emission, warped gas motions, and high velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 2217: This is a barred spiral (SBa) seen almost face-on. It displays LINER-like emission and very deep and broad Na D line absorption suggestive of an outflow. A bar-like nuclear emission region is surrounded by a shaped ``double-bubble" of LINER emission extending right across the WiFeS field ( 38 arcsec). The gas motions suggest a strongly warped disk, with high velocity dispersion seen within the bubble-like cavities. The distribution and kinematics of this ionized gas has been studied by Bettoni90 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2217 as a barred ring galaxy with an HI outer ring, warped extended HI, and resonance radii matching optical and HI rings.

quotes.quote: NGC 2217: The bottom right panels of Fig. fig:mosaic1 show the DSS optical image and 1 velocity map of NGC 2217. The galaxy possesses a prominent bar, an inner ring and an outer ring. In terms of the 1 distribution, we find an 1 ring corresponding to the outer ring. We also observe extension in the 1 gas distribution to the north-east and south-west of the galaxy, which may be hinting at a previous interaction event. This is also evident in the 1 velocity map, wherein we observe a kinematics warp towards the outer parts of the 1 gas distribution. We are able to derive all four resonance radii for this galaxy as seen in the plot showing the Lindblad curves (see Fig. fig:resonance_rings_appendix2 ).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2217 has a compact AGN influence region, large central calibration differences, and SB1 nebular metallicity comparable to stellar metallicity.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2217 the influence region of AGN extends to 0.1 kpc.
But in two of them, NGC 3081 and NGC 2217, these differences are higher (0.4-0.5 dex), and with the peculiarity that become smaller (again 0.1-0.2 dex) for larger galactocentric distances.
If we consider SB1 , nebular metallicity is of the same order than stellar metallicity for NGC 3081 (Sy) and NGC 2217 (LINER).

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 2217 as a ringed lenticular galaxy whose central ionized gas is warped onto a polar plane orthogonal to the bar.

quotes.quote: Further bettoni90 have claimed inner polar gaseous disk in the southern ringed lenticular galaxy NGC 2217. By studying it through long-slit spectroscopy, bettoni90 found visible gas counter-rotation in some slit orientations (not all). Their geometrical scheme for the center of NGC 2217 demonstrated clearly that the ionized-gas disk had to be warped in such a way that in the very center it occupied the polar plane orthogonal to the bar major axis.

gmw_00004448.name: NGC 2962
gmw_00004448.overview: NGC 2962 is a nearby intermediate-luminosity lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261] classified as a barred S0 [arXiv:1101.0903]. The galaxy features a detached outer stellar ring [arXiv:1907.07261], a bluer spiral arm-like structure [arXiv:1101.0903], and two nested, counterrotating inner polar gaseous disks [arXiv:1401.3366].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2962 as a lenticular galaxy with an inner polar disk and a detached outer ring whose gas rotates in the main galactic plane.

quotes.quote: NGC 2962. It is a nearby intermediate-luminosity lenticular galaxy with a detached outer stellar ring at R 65^ 80^ (8--10 ). The galaxy was included into the survey ATLAS-3D atlas3d_1 , and so panoramic spectroscopy of its central part, R<15^ , with the IFU SAURON had provided us with the orientations of the stellar and the ionized-gas rotation planes in its inner part. We found that they are mutually orthogonal polarsau , so the galaxy possessed the inner polar disk.

However, the major axis of the outer ring is aligned with the stellar kinematical major axis, and indeed, our scanning Fabry-Perot data (Fig. fig_fpi ) confirms that the outer gas rotates in the main galactic plane. Our long-slit cross-section along the galaxy major axis (Fig. fig_ls ) reveals both effects -- that the rotation axis of the gas within R=15^ is orthogonal to the rotation axis of the stellar component and that at R 50^ 65^ the gas rotation comes into consistency with that of the stellar disk. The outer ring of NGC 2962 is well seen in the ultraviolet bands FUV and NUV of the GALEX imaging see also our Fig. galex marino .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2962 as a barred S0 whose UV-visible outer ring and spiral arm-like structure are bluer than the galaxy body.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy). Notice that NGC 2974 is erroneously classified as elliptical both in RC3 and RSA, although an exponential disk is evident from the optical and UV luminosity profiles see also Jeong07, Jeong09, Marino10a . In addition, in NGC 2974 Krajnovic05 found, with SAURON observations deZeeuw02 , the existence of non-axisymmetric perturbations consistent with the presence of inner bars.

NGC 1533 UV image shows an incomplete, clumpy ring-like structure, while NGC 2962, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 which have well defined and complete rings, although not uniform, in the FUV and NUV bands. Spiral arm-like structures are visible in both NGC 2962 and in NGC 2974. Both the ring and the arm-like structure appear bluer than the nucleus and the galaxy body.

S0s with rings like NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 are located in the so called green valley, where evolving objects are found, while the ring structures are located in the blue sequence. The blue, star forming rings in NGC 4245, NGC 5636 and NGC 1533 are located at the end of the bar structure. The optical and UV images, shown in Figure  fig1 , suggests that NGC 2962 has two rings, one at the edge of the bar, the other is an outer ring. Only the outer ring in NGC 2962 is visible in UV GALEX images.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2962 as an ATLAS-3D lenticular with a compact inner polar gaseous disk and two nested polar disks that counterrotate.

quotes.quote: Figure n2962 shows a nice example of the newly discovered inner polar disk in the lenticular galaxy NGC 2962 -- a member of the ATLAS-3D volume-limited sample. We have observed this galaxy earlier at the Russian 6-m telescope with the integral-field spectrograph MPFS which field of view was 16 16 , and in the very center, inside R=5 , we saw a compact, fastly rotating, nearly edge-on polar gaseous disk. But with the larger field of view of the SAURON, 41 33 , we are now seeing a switch of the gas rotation sense at R 7 10 : the galaxy possesses two nested polar gaseous disks counterrotating each other (Fig. n2962 ).
Among lenticular galaxies, we can mention NGC 2962 where grossi09 have found 1.1 10^9 M \ of neutral hydrogen in a disk coplanar to the stellar one but extending much farther from the center.

gmw_00004449.name: host of GRB 071227
gmw_00004449.overview: The host of GRB 071227 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1907.10091] located at a redshift of approximately 0.381 [arXiv:1405.5366]. It is a dusty, late-type galaxy [arXiv:1907.10091] with radio emission nearly centered on its bulge [arXiv:1405.5366].

sources.summary: The paper reports radio detection of the GRB 071227 host, deriving a dust-unobscured star-formation rate far above the optical estimate and discussing progenitor implications.

quotes.quote: The host of the short burst 071227 is well-suited as a radio target in this respect. While the afterglow of GRB 071227 is known only from a single detection in the R_C band, and no line-of-sight extinction A_V could be derived, optical broad-band photometry of its host galaxy suggests a high internal A_V ( Leibler2010 ). Also, the galaxy is relatively close-by ( z=0.381 0.001 ; Davanzo2009 ), hinting at its potential detection in the radio band.

We detect the GRB host galaxy in the 5.5 GHz band with a total integrated flux density of F_ = 43 11 Jy. The flux is nearly centered at the bulge of the galaxy. The fact that the radio emission is not exactly centered at the optical center of the bulge (offset 0 4) could be an additional interesting feature.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the GRB 071227 host as an edge-on spiral, dusty late-type SGRB host with measured rotation, stellar age, metallicity, star-formation rate, dynamical mass, and local-cluster environment.

quotes.quote: We investigate the host, a spiral galaxy with an edge-on orientation, resolving its spectrum along its major axis to construct the galaxy rotation curve and analyse the star formation and chemical properties. The integrated host emission shows evidence for high extinction, consistent with the afterglow findings. The metallicity and extinction are consistent with previous studies of this host and indicate the galaxy is a typical, but dusty, late-type SGRB host.

Finally, we have performed spectroscopic analysis of the host of GRB 071227. We find that the host is a spiral galaxy with a dynamical mass of 0.35 10^ 12 M _ . In addition, we have derived a majority stellar population age of 10 Gyr with a small contribution from a younger population, a mean metallicity of 12 + ( O / H ) = 8.6 dex and a star formation rate of 0.2 to 0.7 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00004450.name: KIC 10645722
gmw_00004450.overview: KIC 10645722 is a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.068 [arXiv:1507.08312]. It is characterized by high variability, with the majority of its total luminosity originating from its central source [arXiv:1507.08312].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KIC 10645722 as its most active Kepler AGN candidate, selected for spectroscopy, confirmed as a Seyfert 1, and dominated by central light.

quotes.quote: KIC 10645722 (the most active candidate, Fig. active1 ) was selected to be studied spectroscopically at Lick on the basis of its variability on Kepler and having infrared colors from WISE in the region predominantly populated by AGN EdelsonMalkan2012 . There is an -band CFHT Megacam image of it, and it was imaged in Cycle 21 on HST with the WFC/UVIS (HST proposal 13409) . It is a Seyfert 1 at redshift z=0.068 (K.L. Smith, personal communication) or distance modulus 37.26 (Ho = 72 km/s/Mpc).
