Complemented with archival MUSE and HST data, we analyzed the spatial and spectral properties of the plume and the two main galaxies of the system. Our main findings can be summarized as follows: The DSFG is detected in both and dust continuum, and it shows a compact disk-like morphology. On one hand, dust emisison is fitted by a single 2D S ersic profile with circularized effective radius of R_ eff 0.74 , S ersic index n 1.29 and axis ratio 0.4 . On the other hand, the emission is fitted by 2D S ersic profile of R_ eff 1.13 , n 0.7 and axis ratio .34 .

sources.summary: The paper uses AzTEC/C17 to compare dust and radio sizes, finding a compact FIR radius, larger radio radius, and possible jetted-SMG interpretation for nearby radio structure.

quotes.quote: For AzTEC/C5, we obtain R 1.5^ +0.3 _ 0.1 kpc, and for AzTEC/C17, R 1.4 0.6 kpc. The radius of the dust-emitting region based on this simple analysis of a spherical blackbody agrees within the uncertainties with the observationally determined rest-frame FIR size ( R_ FIR =1.3 kpc for C5 and R_ FIR =1.2 kpc for C17; Appendix D); see also. For comparison, the radius (half the major axis FWHM) of the radio-emitting region of AzTEC/C5 is about 2.3 kpc, and 2.4 kpc for C17.

AzTEC/C17 : The COSMOS2015 catalogue contains a source (ID 841161; ) that lies 0 77 north-west from the additional 3 GHz source, and 1 10 south-west from the SMG's 3 GHz position. The redshift of this source is z_ phot =0.60^ +0.03 _ 0.07 , and if related to the additional 3 GHz source, it is likely to be a foreground galaxy with respect to the z_ spec =4.542 SMG AzTEC/C17. The 3 GHz source configuration appears similar to that seen towards AzTEC/C3a, and a radio jetted SMG could be another explanation.

sources.summary: J1000+0234 has N and S components at the same spectroscopic redshift, a foreground X component, a western companion, a UV bridge, and compact FIR emission on J1000+0234-N.

quotes.quote: J1000+0234 : This system has three main UV components. J1000+0234-N and J1000+0234-S are spectroscopically confirmed at the same redshift Karim et al., in prep 2008ApJ...681L..53C,2008ApJ...689L...5S . J1000+0234-X is a foreground source at z_ spec = 1.41 2008ApJ...681L..53C .

An additional companion is detected West of J1000+0234-S in all the three HST bands, but the HSC images show diffuse features rather than a concentrated source, consistent with 2008ApJ...681L..53C . The North and South components show a connection between them in all the three HST bands, suggesting a merger. The rest-frame FIR emission is compact and associated with J1000+0234-N.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1000+0234 as a z=4.54 starburst, selected as AzTEC/C17, with high infrared SFR and HST components plus velocity offset indicating a merger.

quotes.quote: J1000+0234 (also known as AzTEC/C17) was selected as a V band dropout in the COSMOS field and appears as a 3- detection in JCMT/AzTEC-COSMOS. It is located at z 4.54 and has an IR-estimated SFR of 440 with large uncertainties (Table tab:j1000 ).

J1000+0234 appears in HST images as being composed of two sources with confirmed similar redshifts Capak+2008 . These are visible in the top left panel of Fig. \ fig:j1000 : The first is clearly associated with the emission, while the second is located in the south and is significantly outside the region of the line emission. The source on the west side of the HST image is a foreground galaxy. Because of this peculiar configuration and the large velocity offset revealed by the emission Capak+2008 , J1000+0234 has been classified as a merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes DC 842313 as a peculiar merger system, possibly triple, involving the ALPINE target and the submillimeter galaxy J1000+0234.

quotes.quote: DC 842313 : this galaxy is quite peculiar and was classified as a merger by . The optical maps show the presence of two or three components. Indeed, the ALPINE target is at the center of the maps, clearly visible in the HST F814W image, while a brighter and larger component also detected in [ CII ] is present toward the north, particularly visible in the UVista K_s band map. This is the well-studied submillimeter galaxy J1000+0234 (e.g., Capak08,Schinnerer08,Jones17 ), and it was serendipitously observed in ALPINE Loiacono21 . This galaxy has a large line width (i.e., 1000 km s ^ 1 ) and is separated in velocity by 750 km s ^ 1 from the main ALPINE target.

sources.summary: The paper reports Capak4.55 at z=4.55, confirms that redshift with CO[4-3] emission, and notes that a lightly extinguished QSO template overpredicts its 8 micron flux.

quotes.quote: There was also substantial effort in performing deep optical spectroscopy for optically faint millimeter sources in the COSMOS field using the Keck telescopes by capak2008, capak2011, smolcic2011 , and obtained optical spectroscopic redshifts for Capak4.55, AzTEC1 and AzTEC3 at z = 4.55 , 4.64 and 5.3, respectively.

Subsequent millimeter spectroscopic observations detect of the CO [ 4 3 ] line from Capak4.55 schinnerer2008 and CO [ 2 1 ] , CO [ 5 4 ] , and CO [ 6 5 ] lines from AzTEC3 riechers2010 , confirming their optical spectroscopic redshifts.

The QSO template elvis1994 with a small amount of dust extinction can also fit the 1 m < < 8 m SEDs for AzTEC1, GN20 and GN20.2, but predicts slightly higher flux densities at 8 m for AzTEC3, Capak4.55 and GN10.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1000+0234 as a z=4.542 AzTEC-COSMOS source with CO follow-up, multi-component Ly emission, merger morphology, and molecular-gas and dynamical-mass estimates.

quotes.quote: J1000+0234 is a 3 detection in the JCMT/AzTEC-COSMOS map identified to lie at z_ spec =4.542 based on its Keck II/DEIMOS spectrum ( ), and further CO follow-up with the VLA and PdBI ( ). Capak et al. \ (2008) have shown that the Ly line breaks into multiple, spatially separated components with an observed velocity difference of up to 380 km s ^ 1 and that the morphology of the source indicates a merger. Schinnerer et al. \ (2008) report a molecular gas mass of 2.6 10^ 10 \ assuming a ULIRG-like conversion factor and a dynamical mass of 1.1 10^ 11 \ assuming a merger scenario.

sources.summary: The paper says ALMA-.08 is an unbiased 3 mm detection with a spectroscopic redshift of 4.55, above the original z~1.1 target sample.

quotes.quote: ALMA-.08, ALMA-.15, ALMA-.16: The three sources were detected in different observations of the same project ( 2016.1.00171.S), whose targets are galaxies at z 1.1 . As described in detail in the main text, our galaxies are expected to lie at z 2.5 , and therefore our detections can be considered unbiased. In fact, ALMA-.08 has a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =4.55 (see Table table:catalogue ), significantly higher than the original sample.

sources.summary: The paper reports that DEIMOS COSMOS 842313 is the only ALPINE galaxy matched within 2 arcseconds to a 3 GHz source, but attributes that radio source to a brighter neighbor.

quotes.quote: Only one ALPINE galaxy (DEIMOS COSMOS 842313) is matched to a 3 GHz source (COSMOSVLA3 J100054.49+023436.2) in the 2 \ search. It is known that DEIMOS COSMOS 842313 has an extremely bright neighbor identified as SC 2 DEIMOS COSMOS 842313 in Bethermin2020 , also known as J1000+0234 Schinnerer2008 or AzTEC/C17 Brisbin2017 . This 3 GHz source is closer to SC 2 DEIMOS COSMOS 842313 with a separation of 0.12 , as compared to 1.17 \ to DEIMOS COSMOS 842313, suggesting that the former is the match to the radio source.

gmw_00004480.name: CEERS2 588
gmw_00004480.overview: CEERS2 588 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy with a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of 11.04 [arXiv:2304.06658]. With a UV magnitude of -20.4, it is the most luminous galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift greater than 11.0 [arXiv:2304.06658].

sources.summary: The paper newly determines CEERS2 588's z=11.04 spectroscopic redshift from O II and Ne II lines and identifies it as very luminous for z>11.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of CEERS2 588, which was photometrically identified in 2023ApJ...946L..13F and 2023MNRAS.518.6011D , shows the Lyman break, the Oii 3727 line (5.7 ), and the possible [ Ne iii ] 3869 line (2.8 ), as shown in the middle panel of Figure fig_spec1 . The Oii line feature is also seen in some of the individual frames (Figure fig_spec2_shot ), and they are not affected by an obvious image defect, indicating that this Oii line is real. The rest-frame equivalent width of the Oii line is 100 \ , comparable to that seen in galaxies at z 2-3 2018ApJ...869...92R . We newly determine the spectroscopic redshift of CEERS2 588 to be z_ spec =11.04 based on the Oii and [ Ne ii ] lines. Given its UV magnitude, M_ UV =-20.4 mag, this galaxy is the most luminous galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at z_ spec >11.0 .

gmw_00004481.name: Garland
gmw_00004481.overview: Garland is an irregular dwarf galaxy in the M81 group with a very low stellar mass [arXiv:2107.07775]. It is a tidal system located near NGC 3077 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701465, arXiv:1002.0456] that contains young stars [arXiv:1002.0456] and exhibits an exceptionally high specific star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0701465].

sources.summary: The paper treats Garland as a tidal dwarf system near NGC 3077 with exceptionally high specific star formation and uncertain HI mass because of M81-group filaments.

quotes.quote: We note the dwarf tidal system near NGC 3077, the Garland, to have the highest SFR per unit luminosity among 150 galaxies of the Local volume with known SFRs.

Among the galaxies of the M81 suite, Garland is distinguished by the highest star formation rate per unit luminosity (i.e. specific SFR). Apparently, we observe this tidal structure at the peak of star formation process, in a burst. Among other 150 galaxies of the Local volume, only one BCD galaxy, UGCA 281 = Mkn 209, approaches the Garland in its very high specific SFR.

It should be noted here that from the data by Appleton et al. (1981) and Yun et al. (1994), the central region of the M81 group is filled with HI clouds and filaments, which makes the estimates of the HI mass in BK3N, HoIX, A0952+69, and Garland rather uncertain.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Garland galaxy as the highest stellar-mass-normalized XRB-count outlier, an irregular M81 group member with very low stellar mass and debated origin.

quotes.quote: The galaxy having the highest count is the Garland galaxy. Garland is a member galaxy of the M 81 group Karachentsev2002 . It is an irregular galaxy having a stellar mass of 3.7 10^ 6 \ M_ . Their origin and property are still under debate.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Garland as a young stellar system with CMD age estimates, located near NGC 3077 at a tidal-arm extremity and containing very young stars.

quotes.quote: As discussed in davidge08 , who had also used the MegaPrime/MegaCam imager to investigate the bright stellar content of the M81 group, the ages of the young stellar components estimated for Holmberg IX, BK 3N, and Garland from MegaCam CMDs are consistent with those obtained from CMDs obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope makarova02 , suggesting that blending is not severe, if any.
Interestingly, BK3N and Garland appear to lie at the two extremities of this chain of concentrations of young stars, close to M81 and NGC 3077 respectively.
The Garland structure, at the easter end of the H i tidal arm, however contains stars as young as those observed in Holmberg IX and the spiral arms.

gmw_00004482.name: DG 411
gmw_00004482.overview: DG 411 is a post-starburst galaxy that features a gradient in its hydrogen equivalent width [arXiv:1007.2263]. It possesses a nearby companion galaxy and displays clear evidence of a tidal tail and structural disruption [arXiv:1007.2263].

sources.summary: DG 411 is a post-starburst galaxy with an H equivalent-width gradient, a nearby companion, and the only clear tidal-tail/disturbance evidence among the sample.

quotes.quote: The exception is DG 411 which shows evidence for a gradient in H equivalent width from one side of the galaxy to the other. This galaxy also has a nearby companion evident in the HST image.

With the exception of DG 411, there is no obvious sign of tidal disruption in this sample. The model-subtracted images are shown in the second column of Figure fig:psbmorph and show no evidence of disruption or underlying tidal features -- again with the exception of DG 411 which has a companion and tidal tail.

gmw_00004483.name: DG 338
gmw_00004483.overview: DG 338 is classified as an e(a) star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1007.2263]. It exhibits irregular isophotal profiles, model-subtracted residuals, and [OII] emission that is stronger at smaller radii [arXiv:1007.2263].

sources.summary: DG 338 is classified with the e(a) star-forming subsample, shows central strengthening of [OII] emission, and has significant model-subtracted residuals/asymmetry.

quotes.quote: These three galaxies (DG 338, DG 356, DG 371) are consistent with an e(a) spectral classification within their 1 error bars. We do, however, see a trend in the radial distribution of the [ OII ] 3727 equivalent width in the sense that the equivalent width of the emission line is stronger at small radii (i.e. larger negative equivalent width at small radii). This trend is apparent in all three galaxies.

The model subtracted images show significant residuals for two of the three (DG 338 and DG 371) galaxies and the asymmetry is also illustrated by the irregular isophotal profiles (third column of Figure fig:emmorph ).

gmw_00004484.name: DG 356
gmw_00004484.overview: DG 356 is classified as an e(a) star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1007.2263]. The equivalent width of its [OII] 3727 emission line increases toward smaller radii and toward the plane of the galaxy's disk [arXiv:1007.2263].

sources.summary: DG 356 is one of the e(a) star-forming galaxies; its emission equivalent width strengthens toward small radii and toward the plane of the disk.

quotes.quote: These three galaxies (DG 338, DG 356, DG 371) are consistent with an e(a) spectral classification within their 1 error bars. We do, however, see a trend in the radial distribution of the [ OII ] 3727 equivalent width in the sense that the equivalent width of the emission line is stronger at small radii (i.e. larger negative equivalent width at small radii). This trend is apparent in all three galaxies. In the case of DG 356 (second row in Figure fig:em ) the slit is near to perpendicular to the major axis of the galaxy meaning the increase in emission line equivalent width is toward the plane of the disk.

gmw_00004485.name: DG 371
gmw_00004485.overview: DG 371 is classified as an e(a) star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1007.2263]. It exhibits asymmetric residual morphology, irregular isophotal profiles, and a central concentration of [OII] emission [arXiv:1007.2263].

sources.summary: DG 371 is an e(a) star-forming galaxy with centrally stronger [OII] emission, a more pronounced central concentration in Mask 1, and asymmetric residual morphology.

quotes.quote: These three galaxies (DG 338, DG 356, DG 371) are consistent with an e(a) spectral classification within their 1 error bars. We do, however, see a trend in the radial distribution of the [ OII ] 3727 equivalent width in the sense that the equivalent width of the emission line is stronger at small radii (i.e. larger negative equivalent width at small radii). This trend is apparent in all three galaxies. In DG 371 (3rd row in Figure fig:em ) we see a more pronounced central concentration in the better quality Mask 1 data than in the Mask 2 data.

The model subtracted images show significant residuals for two of the three (DG 338 and DG 371) galaxies and the asymmetry is also illustrated by the irregular isophotal profiles (third column of Figure fig:emmorph ).

gmw_00004486.name: Arp 55
gmw_00004486.overview: Arp 55 is a luminous infrared galaxy merger system [arXiv:1703.07121, arXiv:1907.03574] consisting of two main components [arXiv:1705.10801, arXiv:0806.3002]. The northeast component is classified as a composite active galactic nucleus and starburst [arXiv:1408.4177], while the southwest component is identified as a radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 04881 as a two-component system whose low-signal maps required tapered imaging; the north-east component is more compact and the south-west component resolves out.

quotes.quote: UGC 04881 : This system has two components and its total flux density was recovered by adding the Gaussian fit results of each component separately. The error of this measurement was obtained by adding in quadrature the errors associated to each component (see Section sec:Iflux ). The D configuration map of this system had low signal-to-noise ratio and the quality was not good enough to recover the total flux density. For this reason, we used the final image (with the different array configurations combined, see Section sec:red ) tapered such that we recovered a point-like structure for each component. Even though both components contribute about the same to the total flux density observed at 32.5 GHz, the brightest component (north-east) is more compact.

The south-west component is resolved out at the highest resolution we can achieve. We measured the size of this component from the image we used to obtain the total flux density ( A_ beam =20.3 arcsec^ 2 ) and found an upper limit of A_ 50 =19.8 arcsec^ 2 , i.e., it is unresolved in this coarser map. The brightest component is shown in Figure fig:fig1 . The flux density of this bright component should also be treated as a lower limit.

sources.summary: UGC 4881 is the SN 1999gw host merger system, with irregular kinematics, fan-shaped oxygen abundance, double metallicity peaks, and the supernova near a peak.

quotes.quote: While for the host galaxy of SN 1999gw (UGC 4881), the H velocity distribution is irregular. From the maps of , the host galaxy of SN 1999gw (UGC 4881) has more than one peak, so there is turbulence in this galaxy. Therefore, according to the classification criteria in ya08 , the kinematic type of UGC 4881 is CK, which can also be shown from the image of MaNGA that this galaxy is in merger system.

The host galaxy of SN 1999gw (UGC 4881) is in a merger system. The 2D map of the gas-phase oxygen abundance in this merger system is fanshaped. Fig.  fig.unSN-havd shows that there are double peaks of gas-phase oxygen abundance, one is in the central regions of the field of view of MaNGA (UGC 4881 NED01, marked by plus), and the other locates at the right bottom of the field (UGC 4881 NED02, marked by star). The supernova 1999gw locates close to the peak.

sources.summary: The paper classifies the southwest component of UGC 04881 as a radio AGN, citing steep spectral-index values above 1.1 and spectral ageing despite starburst and composite diagnostics elsewhere.

quotes.quote: UGC 04881 (SW) The map of the SW radio component shows steep values of 0.8, indicating synchrotron emission, that get larger than 1.1. This steepening and spectral ageing suggest an AGN and not a nuclear SB, since the latter have short lifetimes. We note that the brightness temperature is low ( 1.5 10^ 2 K; Table tab:Tb ). Based on the 6.2 m EQW diagnostic, this is a SB, while based on the BPT diagram it is a composite. We classify it as a radio-AGN.

sources.summary: The paper models Arp 55 as an early/intermediate LIRG merger with cold dense molecular gas, a more Galactic abundance ratio, and a low CO-to-H2 conversion factor.

quotes.quote: For Arp 55, an early/intermediate staged merger, the line measurements are consistent with cold ( 10-20 K), dense ( > 10 ^ 3.5 cm ^ 3 ) molecular gas. Since Arp 55 is an early/intermediate stage merger while NGC 2623 is a merger remnant, the difference in physical conditions may be an indicator of merger stage. On the other hand, Arp 55 has a more Galactic \ value with the most probable \ value being 20-30. We measure the CO-to-H _ 2 conversion factor, , to be 0.1 and 0.7 (3 10 ^ 4 / ) \ for Arp 55 and NGC 2623, respectively.

sources.summary: The survey treats Arp 55 as a two-component, little-studied LIRG; both components have HII optical spectra and VLBI data suggest compact starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Arp 55 (UGC 4881, VV 155) Arp 55 is one of the least studied galaxies in our sample. v95 classify the optical spectrum as HII for both components. l93 detected Arp 55 using VLBI observations, but find the source to be consistent with a compact starburst or a group of clustered supernova.

sources.summary: The paper classifies the northeast component of UGC 04881 as AGN/SB, noting outward-steepening radio values and matching mid-infrared and optical composite classifications.

quotes.quote: UGC 04881 (NE) The north-east radio component of a galaxy pair presents flat values of that get steeper outwards, as seen by the histogram (Fig. amaps Right ), but values of barely exceed the value of 1.1, within the uncertainties, thus we classify it as AGN/SB. The mid-IR diagnostics suggest an AGN/SB, and in the optical the BPT diagram gives a composite including both AGN and SB. We see that the map and hist does not help us distinguish between the AGN and the SB.

gmw_00004487.name: J0505.3+0459
gmw_00004487.overview: J0505.3+0459 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of 0.954 [arXiv:2112.08858, arXiv:1807.05057]. The source exhibits strong gamma-ray flaring activity [arXiv:1807.05057, arXiv:2304.13044] and hosts a parsec-scale radio jet with superluminal components [arXiv:2112.08858, arXiv:2204.12242].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0502+049 as a nearby gamma-ray flaring quasar whose jet-component ejections may have contributed to the 2014-2015 IceCube neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: Motivated by this, we studied the parsec-scale structures of TXS 0506+056 and PKS 0502+049 through radio interferometry at 8 and 15 GHz. We identified twelve jet components in TXS 0506+056 and seven components in PKS 0502+049. The most reliable jet components show superluminal speeds ranging from 9.5c to 66c in the case of TXS 0506+056, and from 14.3c to 59c for PKS 0502+049, which were used to estimate a lower (upper) limit for the Lorentz factor (jet viewing angle) for both sources.

The flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) PKS 0502+049 is separated by an angular distance of 1 2 from TXS 0506+056 Johnston_et_al_1995J . At a redshift of 0.954 Drinkwater_et_al_1997 , PKS 0502+049 was identified as a GeV gamma-ray emitter just before and immediately after the period of the neutrino excess in 2014-2015 He_et_al_2018 , so its contribution may not be disregarded.

As the nearby gamma-ray flaring blazar PKS 0502+049 was in the state of enhanced emission at GeV energies during the reported neutrino excess time window, it could have contributed for this event too. Interestingly, we found that this gamma-ray enhancement was accompanied by ejections of two superluminal jet components (C3 and C4) in PKS 0502+049, suggesting that these components could be jet blobs, namely the emitting region in some models of neutrino production in AGN.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 0502+049 as a nearby flaring blazar/FSRQ that contaminates low-energy gamma-ray emission but is disfavored as the neutrino counterpart relative to TXS 0506+056.

quotes.quote: This study is motivated by: (1) the first association between a neutrino alert and a blazar in a flaring state, TXS 0506+056; (2) the evidence of a neutrino flaring activity during 2014 - 2015 from the same direction; (3) the lack of an accompanying simultaneous ray enhancement from the same counterpart; (4) the contrasting flaring activity of a neighbouring bright ray source, the blazar PKS 0502+049, during 2014 - 2015. Our study makes use of multi-wavelength archival data accessed through Open Universe tools and includes a new analysis of Fermi LAT data. We find that PKS 0502+049 contaminates the ray emission region at low energies but TXS 0506+056 dominates the sky above a few GeV.

The Neil Gehrels Swift observatory swift carried out a total of 92 observations within 80 arc-minutes of IceCube-170922A. Of these, 35 were performed before the arrival of the neutrino and were mostly pointed at the nearby flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) PKS 0502+049. The remaining pointings have been carried out either as a Target of Opportunity follow up mapping the error region of IceCube-170922A a few hours after the event keivani , or as part of the monitoring program of the blazar TXS 0506+056 triggered by the IceCube neutrino alert.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0502+049 as a nearby bright blazar with strong GeV flares during the IceCube neutrino flare and higher low-energy GeV flux than TXS 0506+056.

quotes.quote: There is a nearby bright GeV source PKS 0502+049, located at right ascension (RA) 76.35^ , declination (Dec) +5.00^ (J2000 equinox) Gaia2018 , and redshift z=0.954 Drinkwater1997 , with a spatial separation of 1.2^ to TXS 0506+056. Besides these two point sources (i.e., TXS 0506+056 and PKS 0502+049), within the 158-days time window of the neutrino flare IceCube2018neutrinoflare , there is no evidence for any other reliable GeV source within the nearby region around the optimal position of the neutrino flare (see Figure fig:tsmap ). More intriguingly, PKS 0502+049 had strong GeV flares around the time window of the IceCube neutrino flare, while TXS 0506+056 was in a quiescent state (see Fig. fig:weeklc ; please see also http://www.astro.caltech.edu/ovroblazars/data.php for the low radio emission of TXS 0506+056 during the neutrino flare). The two major active phases of PKS 0502+049 appeared in the periods of MJD 56860 - 56960 and MJD 57010 - 57120, which is partly overlapped with the 158-days box-shaped time window of IceCube neutrino flare from MJD 56937 to MJD 57096 IceCube2018neutrinoflare , as is shown in the shaded area in Figure fig:weeklc .

The GeV flux of PKS 0502+049 is significantly higher than that of TXS 0506+056 at the energies lower than 10 GeV, while the spectrum becomes softer at the energies higher than 10 GeV. The spectrum of PKS 0502+049 can be fitted via a power law spectrum with an exponential cutoff (ECPL), i.e., dN_ dE_ E_ ^ p exp (-E_ /E_ , cut ) , with the index of p=2.05 0.04 and the cutoff energy of E_ cut =7.4 1.8 GeV .

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0502+049 around the IceCube neutrino window, modeling its broadband emission and finding a leptonic origin more plausible than hadronic neutrino production.

quotes.quote: ICRANet, P.zza della Repubblica 10, 65122 Pescara, Italy. narek@icra.it Received -; accepted - The origin of the multiwavelength emission from PKS 0502+049 neighboring the first cosmic neutrino source TXS 0506+056 is studied using the data observed by Fermi-LAT and Swift UVOT/XRT. This source was in a flaring state in the considered bands before and after the neutrino observations in 2014-2015, characterized by hard emission spectra in the X-ray and ray bands, 1.5-1.8 and 2.0 , respectively. During the neutrino observations, the ray spectrum shows a deviation from a simple power-law shape, indicating a spectral cutoff at E_ =8.50 2.06 GeV. The spectral energy distributions of PKS 0502+049 are modeled within a one-zone leptonic scenario assuming that high energy ray emission is produced either by inverse Compton scattering of synchrotron or dusty torus photons by the electron population that produce the radio-to-optical emission.

Within the hadronic interpretation, the ray data can be reproduced only when the accretion rate of PKS 0502+049 is in the supper-Eddington regime, as opposed to the leptonic scenario. From the point of view of the necessary energetics as well as considering that the required parameters are physically reasonable, when the neutrinos were observed, the broadband emission from PKS 0502+049 is most likely of a leptonic origin.

sources.summary: The paper processes J0505.3+0459 with two-day Fermi-LAT bins, identifies repeated flare patterns, and discusses its possible role in the 2014-2015 neutrino excess.

quotes.quote: Therefore, for J0505.3+0459, J0509.4+0542 and J0738.1+1742 we processed the Fermi LAT data in the same way as for the top 30 sample, but with two days integration time. By visually inspecting these light curves we identified two repeated patterns of flares in J0505.3+0459 and J0509.4+0542 that are listed at the bottom of Table tab:res and are shown in Figs. fig:0505p1 and fig:0509p1 .

Same inconsistency between the data and the assumption used in our search procedure is found for the first and third repetitions of the pattern in J0505.3+0459. They found that the excess of neutrinos in 2014 - 2015 from the direction consistent with both blazars is coincident in time with the ejection of a new radio knot in J0505.3+0459, which was accompanied by the two strongest gamma-ray flares in this source (see Fig. fig:0505full ). Therefore, conclude that there is a possibility that at least some neutrinos of the excess in 2014 - 2015 arrived from J0505.3+0459 instead of J0509.4+0542.

sources.summary: The review discusses PKS 0502+049 as a nearby flaring blazar whose jet-component timing may explain the 2014-2015 neutrino flare instead of TXS 0506+056 alone.

quotes.quote: A very recent finding and suggestion that has so far received little attention in the literature is based on a detailed re-analysis of the pc-scale jet structures using archival 8 and 15 GHz very-long-baseline interferometry data, not only of TXS 0506+056, but also of another nearby ray blazar, namely PKS 0502+049 . Estimating the ejection times of 12 and 7 jet components in TXS 0506+056 and PKS 0502+049, respectively, they find that the arrival time of IceCube-170922A coincides with the estimated ejection times of two components in the jet of TXS 0506+056, while no such coincidence was found for the 2014 -- 15 neutrino flare. On the contrary, the 2014 -- 2015 neutrino flare was coincident with the ejection time of a jet component in PKS 0502+049 (see Fig. Fig:TXS0506_PKS0502 ). thus argue that IceCube-170922A and the 2014 -- 15 neutrino flare might actually originate from two different blazars.

gmw_00004488.name: SBS 1543+593
gmw_00004488.overview: SBS 1543+593 is a low surface brightness Sm dwarf galaxy at a redshift of z=0.009 [arXiv:astro-ph/0309792, arXiv:astro-ph/0510127]. Its stellar disk is intersected by the sightline to the background quasar HS 1543+5921, producing a damped Lyman-alpha absorption system [arXiv:astro-ph/0309792, arXiv:astro-ph/0104396].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST imaging and spectroscopy of the nearby DLA dwarf SBS 1543+593, measuring star formation, H II regions, and ionized-gas O/N abundances.

quotes.quote: We report new imaging and spectroscopic observations of the damped Ly (DLA) galaxy SBS 1543+593, a nearby dwarf galaxy whose stellar disk is intersected by the sightline to the bright background QSO HS 1543+5921. Hubble Space Telescope imaging observations with WFPC2 in the F450W and F702W bands are used to measure the DLA galaxy's properties and compile a catalog of its (candidate) H 2 regions. Ground-based long-slit spectroscopy of the brightest H 2 region in the galaxy yields estimates of the star formation rate (SFR) and of chemical abundances in the galaxy's interstellar medium. We find that SBS 1543+593 exhibits a SFR 0.006 h^ 2 _ 70 M_ yr ^ 1 , or a SFR per unit area of 1.4 10^ 4 h^ 2 _ 70 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 . We derive gas-phase abundances in the ionized gas of 12 + (O/H) = 8.2 0.2 , which is about 1/3 of the solar value, and (N/O) = -1.40^ +0.2 _ 0.3 .

The main conclusion from the imaging and spectroscopic observations of SBS 1543+593 presented here is that this is an Sm dwarf galaxy of intermediate surface brightness, with properties which are entirely in line with those of other local dwarf galaxies. We have obtained the first determinations of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances in a bona fide DLA galaxy using emission line diagnostics which refer to the ionized gas, rather than the neutral interstellar medium more commonly probed with QSO absorption line spectroscopy. We deduce (O/H) 1/3 solar and (N/O) 1/4 solar; these values are consistent with other properties of SBS 1543+593, including its morphology which shows it to be an evolved disk galaxy with a mix of young and old stars.

sources.summary: The paper presents SBS 1543+593 as a DLA dwarf galaxy where a central quasar sightline enables direct comparison of neutral and ionized gas abundances, which agree within errors.

quotes.quote: recently studied the element abundances of the DLA galaxy SBS 1543+593 in emission and absorption. The background QSO HS 1543+5921 has an impact parameter of 2.4'', and intercepts the dwarf galaxy's disk close to its center. SBS 1543+593 offers an excellent opportunity to directly compare element abundances inferred from cool interstellar gas (DLAs) and ionized gas (SFGs). None of the previously imaged DLA galaxies resolves to show individual HII regions. In none of them, does the sightline to the QSO intercept the disk of the galaxy close to its center, eliminating concerns over disk metallicity gradients. The brightest HII region in SBS 1543+593 yields O/H _ II = -0.54 0.20 \ and S/H _ II = -0.27 0.30 (where the solar S abundance ( 7.20 0.06 ) is referenced to ).

Within the errors these four values are the same. Also, log (N/O) _ II = -1.40^ +0.20 _ 0.30 is within the range of 2.0 (N/S)_I 0.8 , suggesting agreement. On Fig. 3 we show S/H _I for the neutral-gas abundance, and O/H _ II for the ionized-gas abundance. Here is one DLA for which we can demonstrate that in principle, emission- and absorption-line techniques give the same results when chemical elements with similar nucleosynthetic origins are compared at similar locations within a DLA galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies SBS 1543+593 as a resolved star-forming DLA galaxy, comparing its ionized and neutral gas abundances and emphasizing its value for low-redshift metallicity tests.

quotes.quote: We report HST/STIS spectroscopy and Gemini/GMOS-N imaging of the Damped Lyman Alpha (DLA) system toward HS 1543+5921 caused by the host star-forming galaxy (SFG) SBS 1543+593. The Gemini image shows new morphological details of this well resolved DLA galaxy. In combination with previous optical spectra, the new UV spectra enable us to compare for the first time, ionized and neutral gas-phase alpha-element abundances derived from emission- and absorption-line spectroscopy, in a bona fide DLA galaxy.

sl2004a studied SBS 1543+593 using HST imaging and ground based spectroscopy. They classified it an Sm dwarf galaxy with properties that are entirely in line with those of other local dwarf galaxies. Several of 33 HST-discovered HII regions were analyzed with emission-line diagnostics. For the brightest region ( 5) all emission lines necessary to derive the O and N abundances were detected. SBS 1543+593 offers an excellent opportunity to directly compare element abundances inferred from cool interstellar gas (DLAs) and ionized gas (SFGs).

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS1543+593 as a z=0.01 dwarf DLA galaxy projected against HS1543+5921, with emission and absorption abundance diagnostics agreeing within the observational uncertainties.

quotes.quote: I report on observations of the z=0.01 dwarf galaxy SBS1543+593 which is projected onto the background QSO HS1543+5921. As a star-forming galaxy first noted in emission, this dwarf is playing a pivotal role in our understanding of high-redshift galaxy populations, because it also gives rise to a Damped Lyman Alpha system. This enabled us to analyze, for the first time, the chemical abundance of elements in a Damped Lyman Alpha galaxy using both, emission and absorption diagnostics. We find that the abundances agree with one another within the observational uncertainties.

Our experiment indicates that the emission- and absorption-derived element abundances of SBS1543+593 agree within the errors. The diagnostics hypothesis has been falsified . In other words, the hypothesis that QSO absorption lines give lower abundances than HII emission lines for (proto-)galactic systems is not true.

sources.summary: The paper detects 21-cm H I emission from the LSB galaxy SBS 1543+593, derives its systemic velocity and H I mass, and argues similar LSB galaxies can produce damped Lyman-alpha absorption.

quotes.quote: We detect 21-cm emission from the Low Surface Brightness (LSB) galaxy SBS 1543+593, which gives rise to a Damped- \ (DLy ) absorption line in the spectrum of the background QSO HS 1543+5921 ( z_e = 0.807 ). We obtain an accurate measure of the velocity of the H I gas in the LSB galaxy, v = 2868 , and derive a mass of M_ H I = 1.3 10^9 M_ . We compare M_ H I with limits obtained towards two other z 0.1 D \ systems, and show that SBS 1543+593 would not have been detected.

We recently discovered (Bowen 2001, hereafter Paper I) that the nearby LSB galaxy SBS 1543+593 gives rise to a D \ system in the spectrum of the background QSO HS 1543+5921 ( z_ e = 0.807 ). The line of sight to the QSO passes within 2.4 '' ( 0.3 h_ 75 ^ 1 kpc) from the center of the galaxy h_ 75 = H_0/75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , where H_0 is the Hubble constant, and q_0 = 0.0 is assumed throughout this paper , and an H I column density of N (H I) =20.35 dex is measured along the sightline to the quasar. The galaxy has a central surface brightness of _B(0) =23.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 and an absolute magnitude of M_R = 16.5 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 1543+593 as a z=0.009 local DLA galaxy, includes it among z<1 DLA galaxies, and compares its Lyman absorption column with 21-cm emission.

quotes.quote: miller1999 and bowen2001 study the absorption line systems seen in NGC 4203 and SBS 1543+593, at redshifts of z=0.004 and z= 0.009 , respectively. For these systems, the galaxy-QSO alignment was identified before the absorption was found and in one case (NGC 4203) Ly- absorption has not been seen to date, but the structure and strength of observed metal lines resemble those of DLAs. All together, including the z 0 systems NGC 4203 and SBS 1543+593, there are now 20 DLA galaxies known at z<1 .
The DLA galaxy SBS 1543+593 has Lyman absorption measured at 2.2 10^ 20 bowen2001 , whereas the 21-cm emission at the position of the background QSO is measured to be 5 10^ 20 chengalur2002 .

sources.summary: The paper uses SBS 1543+593 as the comparison DLA galaxy-QSO pair, noting its emission and absorption study history and earlier Seyfert-galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: This chance projection of a background QSO with a galaxy at low redshift is reminiscent of the galaxy-QSO pair SBS 1543+593/HS 1543+5921 discovered by . SBS 1543+593 is the only DLA galaxy which has been studied in detail in emission as well as in absorption bowen2005, sl2004, sl2005 . Before pronounced the object a galaxy-QSO pair, SBS 1543+593 was thought to be a Seyfert galaxy with the QSO constituting its active nucleus.

gmw_00004489.name: NGC 3810
gmw_00004489.overview: NGC 3810 is a high surface brightness, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290]. Its stellar disk is characterized as light to medium, balancing the mass of its dark matter halo within the optical disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3810 as a high surface brightness spiral whose stellar disk is light to medium, with stellar and dark-halo mass roughly balanced inside the optical disk.

quotes.quote: We present results from a detailed dynamical analysis of five high surface brightness, late type spiral galaxies NGC 3810, NGC 3893, NGC 4254, NGC 5676, and NGC 6643 which were studied with the aim to quantify the luminous-to-dark matter ratio inside their optical radii. The galaxies' stellar light distribution and gas kinematics have been observed and compared to hydrodynamic gas simulations which predict the gas dynamics arising in response to empirical gravitational potentials, which are combinations of differing stellar disk and dark halo contributions.

For NGC 3810 and NGC 6643 the least squares comparison mostly argued for a light to medium disk scenario. NGC 3810, NGC 4254 and NGC 6643 possess stellar disks that appear to balance the mass of the dark halo inside the optical disk ( f_d 60 ), although for NGC 6643 an even lighter stellar mass component could not be excluded.

gmw_00004490.name: NGC 5676
gmw_00004490.overview: NGC 5676 is a massive, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290]. Its central dynamics are dominated by a heavy, likely maximal stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290], and its structure is consistent with three different corotation resonances [arXiv:2405.14483].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5676 as a massive spiral with a heavy, likely maximal stellar disk whose central dynamics are dominated by stellar mass.

quotes.quote: We present results from a detailed dynamical analysis of five high surface brightness, late type spiral galaxies NGC 3810, NGC 3893, NGC 4254, NGC 5676, and NGC 6643 which were studied with the aim to quantify the luminous-to-dark matter ratio inside their optical radii. The galaxies' stellar light distribution and gas kinematics have been observed and compared to hydrodynamic gas simulations which predict the gas dynamics arising in response to empirical gravitational potentials, which are combinations of differing stellar disk and dark halo contributions.

For NGC 5676 the ^2 analysis mostly argues for a heavy disk scenario, although some simulations suffered from premature terminations. However, as discussed in kra02 , the simulations proceeded to a point where the spiral pattern seemed to have reached a quasi-stationary state, making us confident that the conclusions for this galaxy are quite reliable. In light of this, Figure allgalcompare shows that a maximal disk scenario applies to NGC 3893 and NGC 5676.

Combining the maximum rotation velocity, presented in Table properties , with the estimates for the galaxies' most probable fractions of the stellar disk mass, reveals an intriguing trend: the most massive of the analyzed galaxies, NGC 3893 and NGC 5676, tend to also possess the most massive stellar disks which dominate the dynamics of the central regions.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC5676 consistent with three CRs and argues earlier radial-velocity work missed an important last CR, reclassifying a reported point as OUHR.

quotes.quote: For NGC2403, NGC3893, and NGC5676 we found data to be consistent with three different CRs, and for three other galaxies we find two. As already mentioned, 2011ApJ...741L..14F,2014ApJS..210....2F studied changes of radial velocity sign and found them in many cases, including the presented NGC3344, NGC3893, and NGC5676 (UGC5840, UGC6778 and UGC9366 in their works). Finally, in 2014ApJS..210....2F authors did not detect the important last CR we found in NGC5676 and their 47.2 2.6 arcsec point is likely not another CR; instead it is an OUHR, as Fig. fig:omegas clearly shows. In summary, we show that even in these galaxies, the reported coupling has not been fully recognised.

gmw_00004491.name: NGC 6643
gmw_00004491.overview: NGC 6643 is a late-type spiral galaxy with a weak stellar spiral density wave [arXiv:astro-ph/0212290]. While optical and H-alpha images show a flocculent appearance, near-infrared observations reveal a two-arm spiral pattern [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6643 as flocculent in optical/H images but with NIR two-arm structure and local non-circular H-line anomalies affecting its velocity field.

quotes.quote: According to the classification of Elmegreen \ Elmegreen (1987), the galaxy has a 5th arm class, and therefore is almost lacking an ordered spiral structure. Indeed, both our continuum and H images (Fig. ngc6643 a and ngc6643 b) present a flocculent-like galaxy. However, recent NIR observations by Elmegreen et al. (1999) reveal the presence of a two-arm spiral pattern in NGC 6643 related to an old stellar population and implying a dynamical origin.

Our analysis of the velocity field shows that line-of-sight velocity deviations from the pure rotation field do exist, but they do not exceed 40-50 over the whole disk of the galaxy. These motions are probably related to the spiral structure; but an exact diagnosis will be possible only after a detailed Fourier analysis of the velocity field, which is under way. However there are a few regions in the galaxy where the H emission-line profile cannot be fitted by a single Gaussian function.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6643 difficult to constrain because of its weak stellar spiral density wave, favoring a light-to-medium disk and possible dark-halo balance.

quotes.quote: We present results from a detailed dynamical analysis of five high surface brightness, late type spiral galaxies NGC 3810, NGC 3893, NGC 4254, NGC 5676, and NGC 6643 which were studied with the aim to quantify the luminous-to-dark matter ratio inside their optical radii. The galaxies' stellar light distribution and gas kinematics have been observed and compared to hydrodynamic gas simulations which predict the gas dynamics arising in response to empirical gravitational potentials, which are combinations of differing stellar disk and dark halo contributions.

For NGC 3810 and NGC 6643 the least squares comparison mostly argued for a light to medium disk scenario. The analysis for NGC 6643 was especially difficult since this galaxy exhibits only a weak stellar spiral density wave, leading to a larger uncertainty range for the disk mass fraction. NGC 3810, NGC 4254 and NGC 6643 possess stellar disks that appear to balance the mass of the dark halo inside the optical disk ( f_d 60 ), although for NGC 6643 an even lighter stellar mass component could not be excluded.

gmw_00004492.name: 2MASX J08231289+0333016
gmw_00004492.overview: 2MASX J08231289+0333016 is an E0-type elliptical galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.0897 [arXiv:1606.05016]. It hosts a giant wide-angle-tail radio galaxy with curved jets and trails spanning a projected length of 1.1 Mpc [arXiv:1606.05016].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J08231289+0333016 as an E0 host of a giant wide-angle-tail radio galaxy, measures its redshift, luminosity, radio power, and cluster environment.

quotes.quote: We obtained a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.0897 for the E0-type host galaxy, 2MASX J08231289+0333016, leading to M _r = -22.6 and a 1.4 GHz radio luminosity density of L_ 1.4 = 5.5 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 . These radio and optical luminosities are typical for wide-angle tailed radio galaxies near the borderline between Fanaroff-Riley (FR) classes I and II. The projected largest angular size of 8 corresponds to 800 kpc and the full length of the source along the curved jets/trails is 1.1 Mpc in projection.

We found that the core (C in Fig. fig:2 ) is coincident with 2MASX J08231289+0333016 (SDSS J082312.91+033301.3) and has 175 morphology votes from Galaxy Zoo 2 Willett2013 indicating that the host galaxy has morphological features consistent with type E0 with M _r = -22.6 and M _V = -22.3 . There is no spectrum of SDSS J082312.91+033301.3 in SDSS DR12. We used the Oxford Short Wavelength Integral Field Spectrograph for the Large Telescope (SWIFT) Integral Field Unit (IFU) spectrograph Thatte2010 on the Palomar 5-m Hale telescope to obtain an optical spectrum of SDSS J082312.91+033301.3 on the night of 2013 December 29 UT.

gmw_00004493.name: 4C+01.02
gmw_00004493.overview: 4C+01.02 is a highly polarized, flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.1 [arXiv:1201.4178, arXiv:2112.08871]. The source features a highly variable core [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474] and a one-sided, S-shaped radio jet that extends southward and exhibits strong X-ray emission [arXiv:1201.4178, arXiv:1802.04714].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B0106+013 as a z=2.099 highly polarized quasar with a one-sided, S-shaped 38 kpc jet whose X-ray emission peaks at the first prominent bend.

quotes.quote: PKS B0106+013 is a highly optically polarized quasar (HPQ) at a redshift of 2.099 (1 at the distance of the source corresponds to a spatial scale of 8.4 kpc). Its projected 38 kpc radio jet is clearly one-sided (Figure figxrayimage ), which could be suggestive of a highly relativistic jet on kpc-scales, oriented close to our line of sight. The kpc jet has a gently undulating S-shape around a position angle of 180 .

The X-ray emission follows the one-sided radio jet closely. There is no X-ray emission from the counterjet direction. X-ray emission peaks at the site of the first prominent jet bend on kpc scales (bend 3 in Figure figxfig0106 ).

sources.summary: The paper models 4C+01.02 during flaring and quiescent states, combining SED and spectropolarimetry to separate synchrotron and accretion-disk components and constrain black-hole and jet properties.

quotes.quote: Our model is applied to the multi-wavelength SED and spectropolarimetry observations of the Flat Spectrum Radio Quasar 4C+01.02 ( z=2.1 ) in its 2016 July-August flaring state and July-August 2017 quiescent state, using data from the Fermi Large Area Telescope, the Southern African Large Telescope and the Las Cumbres Observatory network of telescopes. By constraining the AD component, the mass of the SMBH is obtained as M_ . Furthermore, the model retrieves the characteristics of the relativistic electron distribution in the jet and the degree of ordering of the magnetic field.

In this paper, we discuss and interpret observations of the FSRQ 4C+01.02 (also known as PKS B0106+013 ; ICRS coord. (ep=J2000): =01 h 08m 38.77s, =+01^ 35' 00''.32 [ Optical ] ; 2018yCat.1345....0G ), located at a redshift z=2.1 2018A&A...613A..51P . This source exhibited its brightest flare ever recorded in gamma rays in 2016 July--August 2016ATel.9232....1V . Based on this event, we monitored the source at multi-wavelengths for several months, and observed it again in 2017 July--August to uncover the source radiation during a low-state.

sources.summary: The note describes 0106 + 013 as having a southward X-ray jet ending near a terminal hotspot, no HST coverage, and the sample's fastest VLBI motion.

quotes.quote: 0106 + 013 (4C + 01.02) The X-ray image was first published by 2011ApJ...730...92H . The jet extends almost directly south for about 6 . The X-ray emission appears to end in the middle of the terminal hotspot, which peaks at about 4.5 \ from the core. The quasar was not part of our observing program, so we do not have an HST image of it. VLBI mapping shows a jet at a PA of -120 \ with the greatest superluminal motion of the quasars in our sample: 24.4 .

sources.summary: The paper images 0106+013 as a variable core-dominated quasar with 1.80 Jy core emission, 0.15 Jy extended emission, and a 4.5 arcsec double radio structure.

quotes.quote: 0106+013 We measure a 5 GHz core flux density of 1.80 Jy and an additional flux density of 0.15 Jy in extended emission. Comparison with other measurements (B91: 3.47 Jy; G95: 2.08 Jy) indicates strong variability. The radio structure of 0106+013 is a 4.5 arcsec double, with a dominant core coincident with the optical QSO (Fig. 3). Our image is morphologically consistent with the 5 GHz VLA image of Kollgaard et al. ( ) and the lower resolution image of Murphy et al. ( ). The flux density contrast with the Kollgaard et al. data (core flux density 3.87 Jy, at observing epoch 1986.45) is nevertheless striking.

sources.summary: The paper identifies two significant month-long gamma-ray QPO candidates in the distant FSRQ 4C +01.02, with strong CWT significance and fitted multi-cycle oscillations.

quotes.quote: 4C +01.02 The wavelet analysis shows two significant QPO candidates in the light curves of 4C +01.02, a distant FSRQ at z=2.099 (see Fig. fig:CWT0102 (A) and (C)). No previous report of QPO analysis is found for this source in the literature. Both QPOs have month-long periods, with the longer one centered at 286 55 d ( 268 54 d), and the shorter one at 123 26 d ( 122 26 d), in the monthly (weekly) binned light curves. The significance of the longer QPO is over 5 in both time binnings, whereas the shorter QPO has a significance 4.7 for the monthly binned light curve, and larger than 5 for the weekly binned light curve.

sources.summary: 4C+01.02 alternates between gamma-ray flaring states; after MJD 57000 it reaches intense flares, six consecutive bins above 1e50 erg/s, and ranks among the most luminous gamma-ray sources.

quotes.quote: In contrast, 4C+01.02, with a redshift of z=2.099 , experiences alternating periods of flaring activity, with the most intense \ flares observed after MJD 57000. The source entered an active \ emission state starting from MJD 59615.91, when the peak \ flux, measured above 171.79 MeV, reached (2.92 0.50) 10^ 6 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on MJD 59663.19. The other two sources, depicted in panels ) and e) of Fig. strong_gray , also exhibit multiple flaring periods but with only modest increases in their \ flux. Specifically, the peak \ flux of S3 0458-02, measured above 202.55 MeV, was (8.32 1.41) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 57258.92. Similarly, for S4 0917+44, it was (4.27 0.75) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 observed on MJD 57946.70. Especially profound is the luminosity increase in 4C+71.07, PKS 1329-049, and 4C+01.02 (see Fig. strong_gray ). For 4C+71.07, during the flaring periods in MJD 57227-57245.79 and MJD 57296.92-57338.47, the source luminosity exceeded 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 11 times. The highest luminosity of (2.03 0.36) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 was observed on MJD 57335.21. PKS 1329-049 was in an extreme bright state on MJD 55445.23 and MJD 55468.18, with luminosities of (1.07 0.21) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 and (1.39 0.26) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 , respectively. Similarly, 4C+01.02 was in an extreme bright state between MJD 59662.03-59663.55, during which in 6 consecutive bins the flux exceeded 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 , with the highest flux of (2.08 0.48) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 observed on MJD 59662.62. Because of such elevated luminosity, 4C+71.07, PKS 1329-049, and 4C+01.02 rank among the sources with the highest luminosity in the \ band.

gmw_00004494.name: NGC 4923
gmw_00004494.overview: NGC 4923 is a lenticular companion galaxy located southeast of NGC 4921 [arXiv:1601.03047]. It hosts a system of resolved globular clusters that exhibit both blue and red peak colors [arXiv:1601.03047].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4923 as NGC 4921's S0 companion, resolving its globular clusters, measuring its color distribution, deriving its GCLF, and estimating its distance.

quotes.quote: It hosts an AGN, and has an S0 companion, NGC 4923 ((R)SA()0-?), at 2 6 southeast of NGC 4921. Table tab_param lists the basic information of NGC 4921 and NGC 4923.

In this study we resolve the GCs and extended star clusters in NGC 4921 and NGC 4923, taking advantage of the high resolution ACS images produced via drizzling, and investigate their properties in detail. The main goals of this study are as follows. First, we investigate various properties of the subpopulations in the star cluster systems of NGC 4921 and NGC 4923.

The diffuse emission in the south-east region of the field represents the outer region of NGC 4923 whose center is 2 6 to the south-east direction from the NGC 4921 center. Third, the GCs in NGC 4923 show a blue peak color at (V-I) 0.95 , slightly redder than the blue peak for the halo GCs in NGC 4921, (V-I) 0.9 . NGC 4923 also shows a weaker red peak at (V-I) 1.13 .

gmw_00004495.name: RMID 278
gmw_00004495.overview: RMID 278 is a type-1 quasar located at a redshift of approximately z=1 [arXiv:1808.05207]. Repeat X-ray observations reveal that the source has transitioned from a transmission-dominated state to a reflection-dominated state [arXiv:1808.05207].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RMID 278 as a z~1 type-1 QSO whose repeat X-ray spectra changed from transmission-dominated to reflection-dominated, likely from heavy obscuration or coronal fading.

quotes.quote: The QSO RMID 278 ( RA =214.32112 , DEC =+52.29764 ) is part of the SDSS Reverberation Mapping (SDSS-RM) campaign 2015ApJS..216....4S . At z=1.02 , it has a black hole mass of M_ BH 2.0 10^ 8 M_ (based on single-epoch virial mass estimators), a bolometric luminosity of L_ bol 2.8 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 (estimated from L_ 3000 and mean bolometric correction from 2008ApJ...680..169S ), and an Eddington ratio of _ Edd =L_ bol /L_ Edd =0.1 . RMID 278 was first covered in the X-rays by on 2000-07-20-21-23 with a total exposure of 80 ks (ObsID 0127920401, 0127921001, and 0127921201; see 2001cghr.confE..65M ), with 247/178 (PN/MOS) net counts detected in the 0.5--8 keV band.

The repeat X-ray spectra of RMID 278 provide strong evidence for this typical z 1 type-1 QSO switching from a transmission-dominated to a reflection-dominated state. This dramatic spectral change may be caused by either a nuclear eclipse event or a strong decrease of the corona luminosity, leaving the reflected emission from distant material as the dominant component of the source spectrum.

gmw_00004496.name: NGC 4156
gmw_00004496.overview: NGC 4156 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0226 [arXiv:2210.09341]. Historically classified as a LINER based on optical narrow-line emission [arXiv:2210.09341], it hosts a changing-look active galactic nucleus that transitioned from a type 2 to a type 1.5 state in 2019 [arXiv:2210.09341].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4156 as a face-on barred spiral with an optically elusive changing-look AGN, broad-line emergence in 2019, and partial reversal by 2022.

quotes.quote: We report on the changing-look nature of the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in the galaxy NGC 4156, as serendipitously discovered thanks to data acquired in 2019 at the Telescopio Nazionale Galileo (TNG) during a students' observing programme. Previous optical spectra had never shown any signatures of broad-line emission, and evidence of the AGN had come only from X-ray observations, being the optical narrow-line flux ratios unable to unambiguously denote this galaxy as a Seyfert. Our 2019 TNG data unexpectedly revealed the appearance of broad-line components in both the H and H profiles, along with a rise of the continuum, thus implying a changing-look AGN transitioning from a type 2 (no broad-line emission) towards a (nearly) type 1.

In this Letter, we report on the intriguing serendipitous discovery of the galaxy NGC 4156 as a new CLAGN. NGC 4156 is a face-on, barred spiral galaxy (SBc; 1984A&A...131..291N ) at z=0.0226 (for a luminosity distance of 100 Mpc, 2015ApJS..219...12A ), whose AGN nature is implied by X-ray observations elvis1981, 2005A&A...444..119G . Based on its optical narrow-line emission, NGC 4156 has been classified as a LINER (low-ionisation nuclear emission-line region; 1992ApJ...388..310K,2016MNRAS.455.2551N ) galaxy, with no definitive optical AGN signatures.

Here we present the TNG/Dolores observations obtained in 2019 and 2022, which together lead to the discovery of NGC 4156 undergoing a double changing-look transition: first, from a type 2 (in 2004) towards a type 1 (i.e. type 1.5 in 2019), and then backwards towards a type 2 (i.e. type 1.8 in 2022).

gmw_00004497.name: host galaxy of GRB131030A
gmw_00004497.overview: The host galaxy of GRB131030A is an extended source identified in optical imaging [arXiv:1612.06481]. Based on ALMA observations, the galaxy has an infrared luminosity limit of less than 1.11 × 10^11 solar luminosities and a star formation rate of less than 18.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:1612.06481].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB131030A host in Subaru imaging, gives its position, optical brightness, projected offset, and ALMA-based infrared luminosity and SFR limits.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB131030A and optical counterpart of ALMAJ2300-0522 were also identified in the SUBARU image. Based on the deep ALMA limit for the host galaxy, the 3- upper limits of IR luminosity and the star formation rate (SFR) is estimated as L_ IR <1.11 10^ 11 L and SFR <18.7 ( M yr ^ 1 ), respectively.

Figure host (right) provides the Rc band images for the GRB131030A field, and an extended source was identified inside the position error region through /UVOT. By using IRAF centroid, the location of the source was determined at 23^ h 00^ m 16^ s .146 , 05^ 22'05'.18 , which is 0 07 away from the afterglow location. The projected offset of 0.6 kpc is smaller than values for long GRBs (e.g. ). Thus, we concluded that the source was the host galaxy of GRB131030A.

gmw_00004498.name: ALMAJ2300-0522
gmw_00004498.overview: ALMAJ2300-0522 is a faint submillimeter galaxy with a flux density of 0.716 mJy [arXiv:1612.06481]. It has an optical counterpart and is potentially a component of the host galaxy of GRB131030A, with an estimated star formation rate of 147 solar masses per year [arXiv:1612.06481].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMAJ2300-0522 as a faint submillimeter source near GRB131030A, with optical counterpart, possible host-galaxy association, inferred SFR, and uncertain redshift.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure host (left), a source (ALMAJ2300-0522) with 0.716 0.045 mJy was identified in the ALMA image at 23^ h 00^ m 16^ s .326 , 05^ 22'07'.50 (approximately 3 5 from the GRB position). Although several astrometry-related bugs (e.g. phase calibrator coordinate inconsistency) were reported from the ALMA ARC after data delivery, we could not reasonably explain the significant offsets of approximately 3 5 (more than 10-fold the beam size) from the GRB position. In addition, the source also had an optical counterpart in the SUBARU Rc band image (Figure host right, 2.5).

Thus, ALMAJ2300-0522 is potentially a component of the GRB131030A host galaxy. By assuming the same redshift with GRB131030A, we also obtained L_ IR =8.65 10^ 11 L and the corresponding SFR of 147 M yr ^ 1 by using the same template redshifting method (green solid line in Figure hostsed ). The obtained values were comparable or lower than those of observed ultra luminous infrared galaxy host cases (e.g. ). Another noteworthy feature is that ALMAJ2300-0522 is categorized as a faint ( < 1 mJy) submm galaxy (SMG).

gmw_00004499.name: PKS B0941-080
gmw_00004499.overview: PKS B0941-080 is an interacting system composed of two galaxies with blue nuclei [arXiv:0910.3611]. The main radio source is a gigahertz-peaked spectrum galaxy associated with the northern, brighter galaxy [arXiv:0910.3611], and it is the least X-ray-luminous galaxy of its type detected so far [arXiv:0804.1564].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B0941-080 as an interacting compact radio galaxy system with a GPS/CSS-like core, optical-NIR host structure, and detected X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: PKS B0941-080 The source has a radio power P_ 5 GHz =10^ 26.1 , and an overall size of 50 mas ( 177 pc) odea1998 . Imaging with the VLA shows a slightly resolved secondary component 20 '' ( 71 kpc) east of the main one stanghellini1998 . The VLBI morphology of the main component is that of a compact double dallacasa1998 typical of many CSS/GPS radio galaxies. The source was classified as a GPS source and references therein odea1998 , although recent investigation by torniainen2007 did not confirm the GPS-source classification, indicating the object as a steep-spectrum source.

Ground-based optical-NIR observations showed the host galaxy of the main radio source as an elliptical envelope, with magnitude =17.9 and color i=1 , including two cores stanghellini1993 . HST-NICMOS (JK) imaging revealed that the system is actually composed of two galaxies, interacting with each other, and characterized by blue nuclei devries2000 . The GPS source is associated with the core of the northern, larger, and brighter galaxy stanghellini1993,devries2000 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible binary or interacting structure near PKS 0941-080 and describes it as the faintest, least X-ray-luminous GPS galaxy detected so far.

quotes.quote: A radio source in the vicinty of PKS 0941-080 (z=0.228) was identified by stan2005 in their VLA image at about 20 arcsec ( 50 kpc) to the West of the double nucleus galaxy devrie . There is no optical counterpart to this radio source. In 5 ksec Chandra observation of PKS 0941-080 we detected both radio sources, but only 4 counts were detected at the location of the second radio source. This observation was not deep enough to study an X-ray environment of this possibly interacting system.

The GPS galaxy PKS 0941-080 is the faintest f_X(0.5-2 keV) = 5 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and the least X-ray luminous between all GPS galaxies detected so far in X-rays ( L_X(0.5-2 keV = 6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 ) guainazzi2006, vink2006 . No other source in the sample has a second component detected in X-rays.

gmw_00004500.name: IERS B2128+048
gmw_00004500.overview: IERS B2128+048 is a gigahertz-peaked spectrum source located at a redshift of z=0.99 [arXiv:0910.3611]. It is classified as a compact symmetric object [arXiv:0910.3611, arXiv:astro-ph/0108185] with a double-lobed radio structure [arXiv:0910.3611].

sources.summary: The paper presents IERS B2128+048 as the sample’s most radio-powerful GPS/CSO galaxy, with double-lobed VLBI structure, a faint red host galaxy, and X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: IERS B2128+048 (PKS 2127+04; OX +046) This is the most radio powerful source of our sample, with P_ 5 GHz =10^ 27.8 . Early VLBI observations at 18 cm were made by hodges1984 : they found the radio source composed of two resolved components separated by 29 mas (225 pc). More recent VLBI mapping at 6 cm by stanghellini1997 revealed a radio emission extending for 35 mas in NW-SE direction, with the typical scaled-down structure of a Classical Double. At the edges, there are two hotspots/lobes; the central, weak component is assumed to be the core; a jet connects the core to the northern lobe.

The source integrated radio spectrum is convex, fairly stable, and GHz-peaked, and the classification of the object as a GPS source and references therein odea1998 was confirmed also recently by torniainen2007 . The optical counterpart of this radio source is a very faint ( =23.3 ) and red ( i=1.85 ) galaxy biretta1985,devries2000 , located at z=0.99 stickel1994 . The source was then successfully observed by Chandra in 2002 guainazzi2006,siemiginowska2008 with a 2-10 keV luminosity of 4.4 10^ 44 erg ; the da

sources.summary: The paper classifies 2128+048 as a likely CSO with four aligned VLBA components, no clearly flat-spectrum core, hotspot-like outer components, and weak northern polarization.

quotes.quote: 2128+048 : the 15 GHz VLBA image shows an aligned structure with four main components at approximately P.A. 30^ . The outer components are more resolved than the inner ones though the southernmost is rather weak. The comparison with the 5 GHz image indicates that there are no really flat spectrum components. Component B seems to have the flattest spectral index region reaching 0.4 , but this may be caused by poor alignment (which has been done using component A) between 5 and 15 GHz.

The spectral index using the integrated flux densities does not provide any further indication of the location of the core, with 0.9 for component A, and 1.2 for component B, suggesting they are likely knots in a jet. However, based on morphology we can reasonably identify the outer components as the hot spots, and the radio source as a CSO. Polarized emission is detected above the 5 .m.s. noise only in the northern component (C + D) at a 5 level and with an inferred magnetic field perpendicular (E-vector P.A. 37^ ) to the source axis as expected in a typical hot-spot, where the magnetic field lines are compressed against the shock front.

gmw_00004501.name: HCG 97b
gmw_00004501.overview: HCG 97b is a spiral galaxy located within an X-ray bright galaxy group [arXiv:2304.13066]. The galaxy is undergoing ram-pressure stripping, which has created an asymmetric carbon monoxide morphology and a long, extended radio tail [arXiv:2304.13066].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended radio emission and a stripped tail in HCG 97b, attributing its disturbed gas, radio morphology, and possible ULX feedback to ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: We report LOFAR and VLA detections of extended radio emission in the spiral galaxy HCG 97b, hosted by an X-ray bright galaxy group. The extended radio emission detected at 144 MHz, 1.4 GHz and 4.86 GHz is elongated along the optical disk and has a tail that extends 27 kpc in projection towards the centre of the group at GHz frequencies or 60 kpc at 144 MHz. Chandra X-ray data show two off-nuclear ultra-luminous X-ray sources (ULXs), with the farther one being a plausible candidate for an accreting intermediate-mass black hole (IMBH).

The asymmetry observed in both CO emission morphology and kinematics indicates that HCG 97b is undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with the leading side at the southeastern edge of the disk. Moreover, the VLA 4.86 GHz image reveals two bright radio blobs near one ULX, aligning with the disk and tail, respectively. The spectral indices in the disk and tail are comparable and flat ( > -1 ), suggesting the presence of recent outflows potentially linked to ULX feedback.

gmw_00004502.name: B1600+434
gmw_00004502.overview: B1600+434 is a gravitationally lensed system featuring a background quasar at redshift z = 1.59 [arXiv:astro-ph/0106101, arXiv:2108.07293]. The source is split into two images by an edge-on spiral lens galaxy located in a field or group environment [arXiv:astro-ph/0106101, arXiv:1205.4880].

sources.summary: The paper treats B1600+434 as a lensed quasar system, measuring its radio image structure and arguing that scattering or microlensing affects the unequal variability and image sizes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: A single correlation centre was selected at =16^h01^m40.47^s, \ =43^d16^m47.2^s (J2000).

quotes.quote: The gravitational lens B1600+434 was discovered by Jackson et al. (1995). The radio source is a quasar at z = 1.59 and has two images separated by 1.4 arcsec, which are denoted A and B. The main lensing galaxy is identified as an edge-on spiral at z = 0.41 , with an additional lensing contribution from a nearby companion galaxy (Jaunsen Hjorth 1997, Fassnacht Cohen 1998, Koopmans, de Bruyn Jackson 1998).

With the above aims in mind, we observed B1600+434 at 15 GHz using the VLBA with a resolution of 0.5 milliarcsec (mas), to detemine the fine-scale structure and to measure image sizes for A and B. The observations are described in Section 2 and the results are discussed in Section 3. We observed B1600+434 on 2000 September 9/10 (1900 UT to 0500 UT) at 15 GHz using all ten telescopes of the VLBA. The data were recorded in eight 8-MHz channels in dual circular polarization, yielding a total recorded bandwidth of 32-MHz per polarization.

sources.summary: B1600+434 is described as a two-image lens with an edge-on spiral lens galaxy and quasar source; LOFAR image widths give a low differential scattering-measure limit.

quotes.quote: The lens system B1600+434 \ (16 ^ h 01 ^ m 40.45 ^ s 43^ 16^ 47 78 ) is a gravitational lens system that was discovered during the Cosmic Lens All-Sky Survey 1995MNRAS.275.25-29,2003MNRAS.341...13B,2003MNRAS.341....1M . The source is lensed into two images at both radio and optical frequencies, with a separation of 1 4 and a flux ratio of 1.30 0.04 at 8.4 GHz 1995MNRAS.275.25-29 . The main lens is an edge-on spiral galaxy at z=0.41 , and the source is a quasar at z=1.59 1997A&A.317.39-42,1998AJ....115..377F,1998MNRAS.295..534K .

Using jmfit , we fitted two elliptical Gaussians at the positions of the images and found the deconvolved widths of B and A to be 386.83 mas and 287.8 mas respectively. Consequently, we find an upper limit of 258.5 mas on the value of . This corresponds to a differential SM 0.035 kpc m ^ 20/3 ; considering a lens galaxy redshift of 0.41 implies ^ =212 MHz.

sources.summary: The paper treats B1600 as a robust field/group lens galaxy whose lensing mass exceeds stellar mass enough to require an added cold dark component in MOND/TeVeS.

quotes.quote: However, the other four robust candidates (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149) are located in field/group regions, so that a cold component (CDM) would be required even within the MOND/TeVeS framework. However, the other four galaxies (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149) are located in average density regions (either in the field or in small groups). Hence, for these systems the excess of lensing mass can only be reconciled if an additional, cold, component is added, i.e. CDM.

It was found that, for a range of parameterisations of the MOND and TeVeS theories significant quantities of dark matter are present for at least five galaxies: LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600, HE2149 and Q0957, irrespective of the stellar initial mass function adopted. Although it is noted that Q0957 lives in a high density region -- so that the lensing analysis may be probing scales larger than a typical galaxy halo -- still an additional set of four robust candidates are found (LBQS1009, HE1104, B1600 and HE2149) that pose a serious challenge to these theories.

gmw_00004503.name: MCG-02-12-017
gmw_00004503.overview: MCG-02-12-017 is an active galactic nucleus with a line-of-sight column density classified as Compton-thin [arXiv:2305.07705]. However, its average torus column density is estimated to be near or above the Compton-thick threshold [arXiv:2305.07705].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies MCG-02-12-017 as line-of-sight Compton-thin despite a torus column near or above the Compton-thick threshold, with weak reflection constraints.

quotes.quote: MCG-02-12-017 The source was marked as a CT-AGN candidate based on the data of \ and , with log N _ H,l.o.s. =24.25 _ 0.46 ^ +1.06 in cm ^ 2 . For our analysis, we have used the quasi-simultaneous observations of \ and , along with another \ observation which was taken about 15 months earlier with a longer exposure time 34 ks. The cross-calibration ratio between and detector for quasi-simultaneous observations is 75 whereas for the previous NuSTAR observation, it is 85 . This source is very well fitted (see Table Table:best-fit_MCG-02-12-017 and Figure fig:MCG-02-12-017 ) by all three models. All the physically motivated models are in agreement that the observed line-of-sight column density N _ H,l.o.s. =(0.23-0.30) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 is Compton-Thin, in disagreement with the result.

The average torus column density is instead found to be close or above the Compton thick threshold by \ Edge-On and \ (N _ H,tor =(0.9-4.83) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ). The best-fit value of the photon index ranges between =1.94-2.11, considering all the models. The estimation of torus properties, such as covering factor and opening angle in , is found to be difficult since the reflection component is sub-dominant.

gmw_00004504.name: NGC 4180
gmw_00004504.overview: NGC 4180 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2107.07635] that hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.07705]. The source exhibits noticeable X-ray variability [arXiv:2305.07705], and its central ionized gas shows a distorted rotation pattern alongside signs of a nuclear outflow [arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4180 as a bona fide Compton-thick AGN, with variable NuSTAR observations, reflection-dominated emission, and poorly constrained torus geometry.

quotes.quote: NGC 4180 This target was classified as a CT-AGN candidate based on the data of \ and , log N _ H =24.15 _ 0.22 ^ +0.27 cm ^ 2 . For our analysis, we included only the two \ observations, excluding the \ one due to its extremely poor photon statistic (Table tabel:1 ). We used Portable Interactive Multi-Mission Simulator https://cxc.harvard.edu/toolkit/pimms.jsp to convert \ spectrum for E > 2 keV, and found the predicted count rate (1.56 10^ 3 cts/s) for \ is within the error range of the observed one (1.42 0.82 10^ 3 cts/s). Moreover, the cross-normalization ratio between two separate observations (2016 and 2020) of the FPMA detector is 50 , portraying noticeable variability of the source. The source is very well fitted (see Table Table:best-fit_NGC4180 and Figure fig:NGC4180 ) by all models, showing consistent results with each other, having Compton-Thick l.o.s. column density N _ H,l.o.s. =(1.25-6.10) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 in agreement with the results obtained by .

Even the average torus column density, which is more accurately constrained by \ Edge-On and \ in this case, shows N _ H,tor =(0.66-4.56) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , suggesting a moderate CT nature of the obscuring material as a whole. The best-fit values of photon index are 1.40-1.66 , considering all the models. The hard index value shows that the models might not be able to properly estimate the direct power-law component contribution, in absence of soft X-ray data, and therefore cannot fully break the N _ H,l.o.s. degeneracy.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4180's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: In this Sy 2 galaxy, the gas emission peaks 0 2 (40 pc) E of the nucleus and the SER is more extended approximately along the PMA. The gas kinematics shows again a distorted rotation pattern with a KMA apparently tilted relative to the PMA. is largest just to the West of the nucleus, where it reaches 600 that we attribute to an outflow whose orientation is not clear in the the kinematic maps.

gmw_00004505.name: NGC 2788A
gmw_00004505.overview: NGC 2788A is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.07705]. X-ray observations confirm that it is a Compton-thick source with a line-of-sight column density between 1.67 and 2.36 times 10 to the 24th power per square centimeter [arXiv:2305.07705].

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 2788A as a bona fide Compton-thick AGN, with consistent torus-model fits, strong reflection dominance, and line-of-sight CT column density.

quotes.quote: NGC 2788A This source was marked as a CT-AGN candidate based on the data of \ and , with log N _ H =25.55 _ 1.41 ^ +* in cm ^ 2 . For our analysis, we have two \ observations (taken in 2019 and 2020; total exposure 51 ks). To cover the <3 keV energy range, we make use of 12 observations taken from 2008 to 2020 We obtained a joint spectrum using the tool available at www.swift.ac.uk/user_objects/ . Due to very low spectral counts in soft X-ray ( 34 counts; see Table tabel:1 ), we have groupped the spectra from XRT with 1 count/bin and jointly fitted the \ and \ spectra applying C-statistics over the entire range, in . The cross-calibration variability between \ and \ detectors fall within 20 .

This source is very well fitted using the physically motivated models (see Table Table:best-fit_NGC2788A and Figure fig:NGC2788A ). All models show consistent results between each other. The l.o.s. column density N _ H,l.o.s. =(1.67-2.36) 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 shows a CT column density, which validates the result of , although with a significantly lower value.

gmw_00004506.name: NGC 1106
gmw_00004506.overview: NGC 1106 is classified as a bona fide Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.07705]. X-ray observations reveal that both its line-of-sight and average torus column densities confirm this Compton-thick classification [arXiv:2305.07705].
